title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Today most of us accept science

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Science of today, especially Darwins Evolutionary Theory teaches us that the human race has developed from animals and that humanbeings basically are animals among other animals

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Eventhough we do agree with this aspect of science we tend to consider man as the crown of creation with a natural right to treat animals as we please and use them for our own purposes as food, tools and experimentinstruments

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: I belive this is wrong and in this essay I will explain why we morally have no right to exploit animals

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: I am also going to show how meaningless many of the experiments made on animals are

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: To begin with I would like to say that the line we draw today in order to distinguish ourselves from animals is strictly arbitrary

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: There isn't any quality that includes all humanity that empower us a narural position as superiour to animals

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Most humanbeings have unique capacities though, such as language and higher mental abilities but it's important to emphasize that these abilities don't include all humanity, for example they don't include seniles, mentally handicaped or newborns

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: In fact there are some fullgrown vertebrates that have higher mental abilities than seniles

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: The concept "animals" is very wide and include a variety of different creatures from chimpanzees to oysters

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: We are often very conscious about making a distinction between humans and chimpanzees but scientifically it's proved that humans are more psycologically related to chimpanzees than chimpanzees are to oysters

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Personally I think it would be more logical to draw the line between creatures that have consciousness and those who haven't

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: The animals with consciousness have feelings and goals for their existence, they can feel pain and know if a thing goes good or bad

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: All these animals should have the same right as humans to exist for their own sake as individuals and not being valued just for how useful they are to humans

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: For example, just as we wouldn't do experiments on or eat mentally retarded we shouldnt do it to animals since intelectual abilities seem to be the things we base the world hierarcy upon

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: There are laws that prevent ordinary people from beating their dogs to death, but a scientist can do the exact same thing without being punished

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: The difference is that the scientist is supposed to promote science

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: It's hard to understand how people can spent their workinghours putting monkeys in lifelong depressions, heating up dogs until they die or make cats drug addicts

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: It's even harder to understand that the taxpayers allow this to happen

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: I think that one explanation is that people in general have a huge faith in science and that they have no idea what's going on in the labratories

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: A lot of people think that cruel experiments are justified because they are necessary in order to come up with new medicines and cures to save human lives

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: This attitude has very little to do with reality

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: The truth is that most of the time these experiments are trivial and meaningless

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: There are of course some experiments that have led to new knowledge in medicine but in proportion they are very few

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Most of the painful experiments are not made in order to save lives, they are made in order to make new products for the luxery industry, the industry or the cemical weapon industry

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: These are things that we don't really need for our survival

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: There is always a risk translating one species_ reaction to another

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: One example of what can happen is the case of Neurosedyn

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: Scientists tested Neurosedyn on a lot of animals before it was released on the market

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: On the animals it didn't cause any deformity on the foetus but on humans it did

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: I think that in the high technology era of today there must be rather easy to come up with some alternatives to experimenting on animals

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: In this essay I have emphasized that human beings have no natural right to exploit animals the way we do today

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: The way we act towards animals, especially the ones with consciousness, is very discriminating since we can't distinguish ourselves from them in a clear way

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
text: I also think that we have to stop the cruel experiments on animals that are performed today and find other alternatives because they are painful and cause alot of unnessecary suffering, they might also be dangerous to humans

title: We shouldn't exploit animals.
Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: How can we avoid traffic accidents? Unfortunately, we can not, but we can reduce the number of people getting injured and even killed without too much of an effort

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: It would not take much from the driver; a couple of kilometres per hour slower and a tragedy is prevented

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: In my opinion, speed limits are most definitely a life saver and I will in this essay argue for them to be more carefully looked after

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: There has been some debate on this topic lately and politicians, for example, have argued that speed limits should be lower in winter time

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Our neighbour in the east, Finland, already practises this principle

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Speed limits on certain roads are lowered and the result is that fewer people get killed or injured

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: This is something that we here in Sweden must take after

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Every year in the winter the amount of traffic accidents increase due to bad weather

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: The roads may seem dry and safe when they are in fact covered with a thin layer of ice

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: The weather factor and the false feeling of security, almost of immortality, in our new cars are two of the dangers when driving

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Of course, speed limits can not be reduced everywhere just because of the fact that it is winter or that we feel safe in our cars

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: But, for example, on roads that are known for being dangerous the speed limit could be changed from 110 kilometres per hour to 90, or from 90 km/h to 70

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: This procedure would certainly save many lives

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Another good reason for lowering the speed limits is for the sake of the environment

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Cars impose a great threat to nature with their fumes and until more environment-friendly cars are built a slower speed will reduce the damages on nature

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: This fact might sound a bit strange, but it is true that the faster cars go, the more exhaust fumes are spread

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: My third argument on this topic is that people who drive too fast should be more often punished

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Every time I go by car on the motorway to Stockholm, I notice that nearly half of all drivers are speeding; some of them go as fast as 140 km/h

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: That is not acceptable! It is irresponsible because on a motorway, you are not alone and an accident in 140 km/h is a disaster

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: As I mentioned earlier on, there has been quite some debate about speed limits lately and one terrifying fact that came up was that if the driver is driving two km/h too fast, the risk of getting killed increases with 20 per cent

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Thus, the margins are very small

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: So what can we do? We can obviously drive slower, but what if we do not? Should punishments (fines, jail) be tougher? One could hardly argue for that speeding offences should lead to prison, that is taking it a bit too far

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: Fines however are in my opinion a very good punishment

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: People love money and may think twice before speeding again after a fine of a couple of thousand crowns

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: The problem today with fines is that not very many people are stopped by the police and forced to pay

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: There is simply no money to provide for enough policemen to keep after the drivers

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: The question is whether the government should put more money into the police force or not

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: More money would not just make the roads safer but also, obviously, reduce all crimes

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: That is however a totally different story

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: One solution to the speeding problem could be to have camera supervision to register cars that go too fast

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: This way, the need for policemen in the traffic is not so urgent

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: After treating this subject, I see that this is a huge problem and that it can not be easily solved

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: People like driving and people like driving fast

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: The motorways are big and the cars feel safe so there should not be a problem, many seem to think

title: Speed limits- life saver or nuisance?
text: But what if suddenly, a rabbit jumps out in front of the car?

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I've chosen a rather complex topic, the Internet, for this essay

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: It is a topic that is disscussed all over the world

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: There is a ongoing debate about if it is good or bad

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I'm going to argue, as it says in the title, that the Internet is the garden of Eden and not the Hades

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I'm very aware of the arguments against it

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: Everyone of the arguments for the Internet, that I'm going to present, have "dark" sides as well

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I know that there are a lot of arguments for the Internet but I'm only going to use the arguments that I think are the most important

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The most obvious argument for the Internet is the information

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: It is an enormous bank of information and it's not just one kind of information that you can find there

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: There are webpages with information about cooking, music, how to travel in Tibet, how to improve your cleaning of the bathroom and the list can go on for many, many pages

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: A wide range of information have become available for a rather large part of the world's population(for the population of the industrialized countries anyway)

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I think that is good for I belive that people can't have too much information and it is the individual that should decide which information he or she wants to take part of and not the government

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The only time the government should act are in the extreme cases like childpornography and other crimes

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The Internet has made it really easy to look for and find information

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: You only(?) have to have access to a computer and a modem and then you can surf the Net

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: It is much easier than having to go to the library which maybe is located a couple of mils from your home

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The Internet makes it possible for people to find information that they would have a hard time to find anywhere else, especially nowadays when for example many musicians release music over the Net which can't be found anywhere else and if you are a music freak like me then you just got to have access to Internet

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: There is a really social side to the Internet as well even if it seems like a contradiction in terms

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: Many people is using the Net to talk with other people from all over the world

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: You can meet persons that you normally wouldn't meet, I mean how often do I meet people from, for example Singapore, in the "real" world? You can discuss different topics in groups with people that you never met before or discuss with only one person in special chat rooms who are designed for that purpose

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I have seen in the newspapers that people who met over the Internet have gotten married

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I think that's proof enough that the Internet is a good thing

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I mean think of the people who live in unpopulated parts of the world, Internet gives them a better chance to find someone interesting

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I'm of course well aware of the persons that have been injured or even killed when they have met people who they found over the Internet

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: But I think that this have and will unfortunately happen with every kind of medium

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I think that the Internet makes the world a smaller place to live on

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: A related thing is shopping and that has really had a big boom during the last years

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: You can buy just about everything over the Internet like music, books, food, clothes and so on

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The good thing about this is that the stores on the Internet have better prices because they have less expenses than usual stores

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: The competition makes the ordinary stores lower their prices as well and that is very good for us consumers

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: There isn't only shopping which is very big on the Internet, the service side has grown as well

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: There are insurance companies, food-deliverers and "Arbetsförmedlingen" has their own site where you can look at the job-advertisements from all over Sweden

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: If think that this sector is going to grow much bigger on the Internet

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: I think that the Internet will grow much bigger and better in the years to come mainly because of technical improvements like better modems and such

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
text: Internet will have a larger impact on people's lives than it has today especially people in unpopulated areas like "Norrland" in Sweden

title: Internet-The Garden of Eden or Hades?
Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: The Swedish public health in is danger

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: We are getting fatter and fatter thanks to the modern technology that is taking over our world

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: More and more hours are used up in front of our computers, surfing on the Internet, playing games, making bank errands, almost anything is possible, if not to say everything

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: We do not have to take many steps a day to live a very convenient life

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Our children take the bus to school; they walk between the classrooms, when they get home they move between the TV and the computer

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: They do not get the exercise that is essential for a good and wholesome life

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: At the same time the number of hours of physical education in school is decreasing, I see that as a big threat to our children's health in the future

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: If we want our next generation to have a good physical condition, we must give them these extra hours

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Many children are spending their spare time playing video games and watching TV, they are living a more and more sedentary life

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Sport is getting less significant in the computer world that is growing with a tremendous speed today

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: It is important to get our children to understand that physical education is not only about chasing a ball, running the fastest, jumping the highest or throwing the longest

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: But it also concerns the teaching of how our body is functioning, how to eat healthy and to take care of ourselves

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Studies have shown that the average weight for 15-year-olds has increased with 3-4 kilos during this last decade

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: For a growing child it is of a big importance to be physically active

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: In lack of bodily activity, the muscle mass gets reduced; the condition changes for the worse, the skeleton gets more fragile and the flexibility decreases

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Several of our most frequent diseases are related to physical inactivity for example heart and vascular disorders, diabetes, fatness and joint- and back problems

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: The number of children joining local sport clubs is declining, so for many kids and youths the only exercise they get is that what they get in school

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: According to studies made by the Swedish Sports Confederation, the hours of physical education have decreased in 75% of the elementary schools, and in 70% of the senior high schools

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: To compensate for the cuts, the school boards are giving the students the possibility to take sports as an individual choice

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: But this only attracts those who are interested in sport and health, and who are generally already involved in some activity outside school

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: This opportunity does not appeal to those who really are in need for physical education, but hate it

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: They will, of course, choose another curriculum

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: So to reach to those who do not like the subject, it must be compulsory with physical education

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: In school, children get the chance to try on different kind of sports and physical activities

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Things they possibly would not get in touch with otherwise

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: So the more hours of physical education, the more hours for discovering an activity that suits each and every one, and hopefully will lead to a continuing exercising

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Physical education also improves the studying

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: It is difficult to sit in school through six classes of theoretical subjects and stay concentrated

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Many children suffer from headaches

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Often caused by lack of fresh air, bad school environment, eating food that is not nutritious or eating nothing at all

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: If we could make these students see the importance of exercising and eating nutritiously they would do better in school

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: They get healthier, they stay awake during class and it gets easier for them to focus on their assignments

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: Studies show that a good physical condition improves the results in school

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: To take a break from all the facts that are squeezed in one's head, and to do something completely different can only lead to a positive result, students will pay more attention in class and will be willing to learn more

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: I would say that our children's health are very much dependant upon the physical education they get in school

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
text: If we take away from them, these very valuable hours of exercise, the chances for them to get serious illnesses increases, it will get harder for them to be concentrated and focused in class, they will live an unhealthy life, and we do not want that for our children

title: Physical Education - An Investment for Life
To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Dustin Hoffman, Bob Dylan and Chelsea Clinton all have one thing in common and that is the fact that they are vegetarians

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Why have they, and many with them, decided to exclude meat from their menus? We don't know what the reasons were behind their decisions, but there are many reasons why people choose to become vegetarians and I will write about some of them in this essay

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: There are lots of good arguments for being a vegetarian and among the many reasons are beliefs about ethics, culture, health, religion, economics, world hunger, and the environment

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: The first thing that strike me is the way the animals are treated by us humans, and Alice Walker once said "The animals of the world exist for their own reasons

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: They were not made for humans any more than black people were made for white, or women created for men

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: " This is something to think about when we treat animals the way we do

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Do we really have that right? I'll leave that question unanswered, but the way animals are transported is not defendable and you might have seen on the news a few years ago how cruel animal transports were and still are

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: If that wouldn't be enough; many animals are forced to live unnatural lives, where they never see the sky and they are crammed into far too small living spaces, just to have it ended without any form of anaesthetic

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: This isn't always the case, but in far too many cases this is the bitter truth

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: An other thing that might be an important factor for choosing this lifestyle is that research show that vegetarians' health is better, and that they live longer than other people

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: A too large consumption of meat causes fatness, hardening of the arteries, high blood-pressure, several forms of cancer, diabetes, gout, brittleness of the bones among other things

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: You won't get all of this just because you eat meat, but the risk of getting it increases

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: It is absolutely possible to get necessary vitamins, minerals, and protein from non-meat sources

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Sure, the human digestive system is adaptable and can handle small amounts of meats, but my point is that if we choose to become vegetarians, we will be on our way to a healthier lifestyle

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Furthermore, thousands of people die every day from starvation

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: At the same time a third of the world's crop become animal food - in the U

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: S

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: as much as seventy percent

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Fifteen vegetarians can be fed on the amount of land needed to produce a meat-centered diet for one person

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: The food that could be saved if people became vegetarians would be enough to feed the starving people in the world

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: But if we continue as we've done before, the energy is lost that could save the world from hunger

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Sooner or later it's likely that we all have to become vegetarians, either we want to or not

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Another argument talking for vegetarianism is that it's beneficial for the environment

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: We are six billion people on this planet now, and all of us can't eat meat, since nature can't take that much stress

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: In order for people to eat meat; livestock must be fed and housed and the products refrigerated, which requires great amounts of resources

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: Production of a certain amount of energy or protein in the form of meat is harder on the resources than production of the same amount of energy or protein in the form of vegetables

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: A vegetarian diet requires far less water, and decreasing the population of livestock would reduce pollution from manure and animal wastes

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: So it's of my opinion that it's not morally defendable to cause environmental pollution and suffering for millions of animals if we don't have to, and if we can find the key to better health and an answer to the world hunger at the same time that's just great

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
text: But in the end it's of course up to everyone to make their own decisions if they want to be a vegetarian or not, but I hope I have given you something to think about next time you sit down at the dinner table

title: To be or not be (a vegetarian)
UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Many people might think that forcing unemployed people out to do unpaid work for the public sector would be degrading or even cruel, but I think that most people whohave been unemployed for a longer time would infact welcome this opportunity

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Not only because of the obvious fact that they will get some work experience but also to get out and meet people and feel useful

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: For the public sector this would also be posetiv since they at present are terribly understaffed but also have no money to employ more people

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Today I would say that there are two types of people that are unemployed who would benifit from this, first the person who does not want to have a job but thinks that beeing at home, doing nothing and at the same time collecting unemployment benefits is pretty neat

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: In this case I do not think that the person in question himself would benefit so much from having to work unpaid, but instead the society would

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: First for the obvious reason that the person would do a community service but more important: I think that in many cases these people would probably rather go out and try to find themselves a better job and thereby not be dependent upon the government

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: The second type is the ones who might have poor education and little or no work experience

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: For these people I think this is a wounderful solution because the three most common worries for them are; they become isolated from the outside world when they have to stay at home to apply for jobs or wait for telephonecalls from jobs they have applied for

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: They can also feel that they have nothing in common with the people who actually do have a job

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Secondly they feel that they do not contribute to society and to many people, especially they who are a little bit older and who are used to have worked unrelentlessly all their lives, this might be a huge problem

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Last but not least is the problem that they are not improving their situation, by this I mean that the longer you are unemployed the harder it is to get a new employment since many companies nowdays tend to wounder why you have not had a job

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: As far as the public sector itself is concerned hardly a day goes by without you hearing on the news about the lack of people and money

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: This would be easily helped by this system

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: One good example is the hospitals who are understaffed which leads to the maltreatment of patients, why not instead bring in people who might be qualified for some easier work to offload the nurse and let her consentrate on more important duties that requires the expertise she posesses

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Work like making beds, changing bed- pans, easier office-work or maybe just keeping the elderly company for a couple of hours a day

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Same thing applies to the police-force where there is much argument about the police-officers themselves having to do their own paper-work

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: I am sure that there are many unemployed today who have skills enough to do this kind of work and if you do not there are always more unqualified work such as park maintenance and such

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Of course there is problems with this system aswell

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Problems such as the added bureaucracy that this will inevitably bring and which, in my own opinion, is already a huge problem in Sweden today

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: And also a number of questions appear, for instance; what do you do if an unemployed person does not do his job well, do you have the right to fire that person; how long are you suppose to stay at one job if unable to find a regular job; how do you decide who is qualifed for a certain job and who decides this: the employer or the unemployment-agency

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: Another legitimate question is whether it would not be better to give these people a proper job since you have to pay them either way

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: And obviously this would be the solution that I also would prefere but our politicians today seem to think that we can not afford this even though these people are being paid by them either way

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
text: But I say give these people their jobs and thereby also their dignity

title: UNPAID WORK FOR UNEMPLOYED?
Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: When I first moved to Sweden to start at the university I soon got aware of the huge difference between the systems of financing your studies in this country and my own, namely Finland, or the Åland islands

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: I was astonished by the fact that my fellow students were given approximately twice as much money as I every month, and, of course, this was a bit upsetting

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Having had a bit more insight into how the Swedish system actually works, I am, however, relieved to be excluded from it

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: In my opinion the Finnish system is a lot better than the Swedish one

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: The system used in Finland for financing studies is in many ways different from the one used in Sweden

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: One difference is that the amount of money actually given to you is higher, amounting to approximately 2400 crowns a month

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: You also get an accommodation grant which can amount to 1500 crowns

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: In addition to this you are able to loan up to 30,000 crowns a year from the bank since the community stands security for you

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: This is, in short, how the Finnish system works, and I think there are quite a few factors in favour of it

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Firstly, the amount of money granted to you, that is, not loaned to you, is much higher than in Sweden

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Since you get almost four thousand crowns every month you don't have to take as big loans as in Sweden, where you have to loan quite high amounts of money to get by

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: If you have a good sense of economy (and a reasonable rent), with the Finnish system you can even get by without loans, something that is next to impossible in Sweden

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: The second very good thing with the Finnish system when comparing it to the Swedish one, is that if you are going to take a loan you do it directly from your own bank

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: When you have applied for student support you get a paper sent to you granting you money for the coming semester

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: When you want to loan money you simply take this paper to the bank, decide how much money you want, and the amount will be in your bank account the same day

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Consequently, you don't have to get into trouble with any administrative institutions like the CSN in Sweden; instead you have a direct contact with the bank

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: My final argument in favour of the Finnish student support system is also, in my opinion, the most important one, regarding both the individual and the community as a whole

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Whereas in Sweden, students who have taken loans only have to pay off these until retirement, with a fixed percentage every month, students taking loans in Finland are obliged to pay back every penny of theirs

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: As soon as you get a job, or within two years after finishing your studies, you have to start paying back

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: The rate put down every month is dependent on how high your loan is, and on the size of your salary

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: The idea is that you are to pay back the loan within a period twice as long s that of your studies, but you can, in fact, get a few years extra if needed

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: You also get released from the payment if you lose your job, but as soon as you are employed again you have to continue the down payments

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: This system is a great deal better than the Swedish one since the money loaned to students are actually paid back

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: The Swedish system results in tremendous amounts of money "disappearing", since very few students manage to pay back their whole debts

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: Being a temporary "immigrant" in this country I have realized that there are quite a few differences between the Swedish way of things compared to that of my home country

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: One of the most obvious features of Sweden, in my opinion, is the tendency to make everything a bit more difficult than necessary, especially when it comes to administrative areas, and I think this tendency has come to its climax in dealing with student support

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: I know I'm not the only one who thinks the Swedish student financing system doesn't work very well and there is a continuous debate dealing with this problem, so I really hope that, in a near future, someone will come up with a satisfying solution

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland
text: As we have in Finland

title: Financing studies - Sweden vs. Finland

How do you justify executions?
text: Three quarters of all states in America has the death penalty

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Many people think that it is a fair and well functioning punishment, but I think the opposite

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Who are we, ordinary people to decide who is going to live, and who is not? In this essay I will describe how very wrong it is to have a punishment like the death penalty in USA, and what I believe can replace it

title: How do you justify executions?
text: How do you justify taking an other humans life? No matter what crime he or she has committed, no one on earth should have the power to decide about life or death

title: How do you justify executions?
text: There is just no logic here

title: How do you justify executions?
text: People supporting the death penalty claim that if you have taken an other persons life, you do not have the right to live anymore

title: How do you justify executions?
text: But who decides that? Isn't execution proving the opposite? By taking a persons life we show that you are not allowed to kill

title: How do you justify executions?
text: This is as illogic as it sounds, moreover it is a savage act and something that a modern country like America should have abandoned a long time ago

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Sadly the question of death penalty or not have lately been focused on money rather than on human rights

title: How do you justify executions?
text: People advocating the cruel punishment often argue that it is cheaper to execute people than to put them in jail for life time

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Of course it coast money to keep criminals behind bars, however it also coast a lot of money having the prisoner waiting in jail for execution, and for he or she to appeal the sentence

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Often when there are debates involving the death penalty you hear words like money and politics

title: How do you justify executions?
text: People in general tend to forget about the most important issue namely that execution is a cruel punishment

title: How do you justify executions?
text: A convicted felony usually sit a long time in prison before he or she is being executed

title: How do you justify executions?
text: The weeks, months and very often years in the cell are periods of uncertainty and anxiety for the prison

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Sometimes there is a decided date for the execution, but by appealing that date can sometimes be changed

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Life for a person sentenced to death is very much like a roller coaster

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Their situation is inhuman

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Many of those being executed in USA are just teenagers

title: How do you justify executions?
text: The execution of these kids who has had a bad start in life, and never have had the chance to create and participate in a normal life does not solve any problems

title: How do you justify executions?
text: On the contrary it only creates new ones because I think that death penalties and executions helps to brutalise the society

title: How do you justify executions?
text: But what are we going to do with the criminals? Set them free? Off course not

title: How do you justify executions?
text: Many of the prisoners needs to stay in jail, and they need to be watched over 24 hours a day

title: How do you justify executions?
text: However there are many of the prisoners who can be trained to become functioning, well adjusted citizens

title: How do you justify executions?
text: There are rehabilitation programs in USA that have very satisfying results and which have helped a lot of criminals to deal with their problems and to change their lives

title: How do you justify executions?
text: These programs may coast a little more than to just switch on the electricity on the electric chair, but once again money should not be an issue when it comes to preserve human lives

title: How do you justify executions?
text: So, what can we do? Are we going to allow people, very often youths to be executed, meanwhile we know that it exist rehabilitation programs that could help criminals back to a new start in life? Are we going to tolerate that execution is being chosen instead of this because of economic reasons? Humans are certainly not meant to play some sort of gods and when we do that it is wrong, terribly wrong

title: How do you justify executions?

text: It is most upsetting that small children die because no one takes care of them, when there are people who want to

text: For this reason, letting homosexual couples adopt children, would be to act in favour of the children

text: But why should both starving children and people longing for them have to wait for other citizens to accustom themselves to a more openminded society?

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: When I am writing this, it is the beginning of March 1999 and in one of the cities of Sweden, something terrible has happened, something that fills the whole country with horror

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: A twenty-two year old man who was working as a child-minder, has during a few years, abused several children sexually and also produced an enormous amount of child pornographic material

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Of course, what everybody is asking themselves right now is; what kind of a sick creature is capable to do something like that? As always after such a scandal, people start to question the motives of the men working with child-care

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Worried mothers might wonder if he really has to touch the children that much and what are his intentions of following them to the bathroom? I have understood, after reading some interviews in the morning papers, that many of the men working at the day-care centers today are feeling observed by suspicious eyes, as if there would be something abnormal of being a man working with children

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: I find attitudes like that to be in most cases absolutely unfounded and instead I do believe that it is very important to make a greater number of men start working in the child-care system

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Here below I will give an account of my main arguments for thinking so

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Today, the overwhelming majority, or 95 percent, of the people working with childcare in Sweden are women

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: This are usually quite low-paid jobs and I do believe that there is a connection between that and the fact that it is a work sector dominated by women

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Sweden is said to be one of the most equal countries in the world, but still typical female jobs, like nursing and pree-scool teaching are paid less than typical male jobs

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Why is this then? I would suggest that it depends much on the way it traditionally have been; the women were staying at home, taking care of the children while the men were working

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Perhaps the women working in the child-care system today too easily have accepted being low-paid, just because they traditionally have been taking care of the children without being paid at all

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: Since most politicians and employers historically also have been men, they might have been favouring occupations dominated by their own sex

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: I am convinced that if more men were working in the child-care system, it would soon result in demands for higher wages and would in a long term view create a more equal working situation

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: I find the child-care system to be an essential part of our society and therefore I feel that it is of great importance that the jobs are attractive for well-educated people

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: I do believe that more men employed would make the politicians improve the situation sooner

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM
text: My second main argument is that I believe all children need reliable men in their every-day environment

title: MORE MEN ARE NEEDED IN THE CHILD-CARE SYSTEM

License To Pay- the price of state television
text: In Sweden of today people are forced to pay a license for the possession of a television set

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This license is obligatory and you will receive a fine if you do not pay or fail to register yourself as an owner of a television set

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The license is paid per household and there is no difference whether you have one or several television sets or other kinds of receivers, the amount paid is still the same

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The cost of the license is decided upon by the government and is currently around twelve hundred Swedish crowns per year

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The money collected through these licenses are used to fund the state owned television and radio channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This system has been the same for many years and it can no longer be defended as necessary, not even justified

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: There are alternative ways to fund a television channel, most common is the selling of commercial, which should be preferred instead of licenses

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: When this system was initially made into law some decades ago, Sweden had only one broadcasting television channel and it was the only one you could watch

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Therefore at that time owning a television set meant you probably watched that channel

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The easiest way to fund the television broadcasting was then to charge every owner of a television set

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Although, today the broadcasting market is totally different

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: There are up to a hundred different television channels, most run through satellite broadcasting

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: It is therefore fully possible to have and use a television set without ever watching the state owned channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Still, you have to pay the cost of running these two channels, a cost which you might never get anything out of paying

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The other channels available today are usually funded by commercials, which make them dependent on the number of people watching

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: If they have less viewers then they will sell less commercial time, which gives them less funding

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This is not the case for the state owned channels as they are guaranteed their funding no matter how many viewers they have

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This makes the competition between broadcasting companies unfair and in favor of certain channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Instead, it would be much more fair if the state owned channels, as all other channels, also relied on commercial funding

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This system is much more fair towards everyone, both broadcasting companies and watchers

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: It is often argued that the state owned channels are the only commercial free channels and that people want to keep them like that

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This might have been true a few years back, but today the most popular channel is TV4, a commercial channel

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Therefore, commercials on television can not be that unpopular among the majority of today

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The fact that the state owned channels are no longer the most watched channels also creates the problem that the commercial funding might not be sufficient enough

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: This can be solved rather easy though, as a change in the program tabloid of state owned television could easily bring back many watchers which have been lost to commercial channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Through broadcasting more popular shows, enough watchers would be gathered for the ability of commercial funding to be made possible

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Critics might say that this can not be done, as state owned television have a responsibility to broadcast a diversity of shows which will allow for all viewers to watch

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Although, this argument is only justified by the fact that all viewers have been forced to pay for these channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: If no one was forced to pay for them, state television would not have the responsibility of broadcasting such a diversity of shows

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: Rather, they could concentrate on broadcasting shows which the majority would want to watch

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: As state owned television today have the possibility to go over to commercial funding, there is no reason to force people to pay for the funding of state owned channels

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: To take away the possibility of choice from the viewers is a violence of the free will

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The guaranteed funding for selected channels creates an unfair competition situation, which does not belong in a democratic country like Sweden

title: License To Pay- the price of state television
text: The system of television licenses should therefore be abandoned, as it applies to a situation which is no longer present in Sweden

title: License To Pay- the price of state television

Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: In this essay I will argue for a change in our politics dealing with foreigners

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I want Sweden to reduce the number of persons who get the permission to stay each year

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: Of course I don't want this to be a permanent change, just for a couple of years so that we are able to produce a system that works, in the dealing with foreigners

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I think that our community has developed in a bad direction due to the huge number of foreigners entering Sweden each year

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: My opinion is that Sweden at present has a situation we can't handle in the communication between foreigners and those who have Swedish as a native language

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I believe we must do something about this before the problem grows stronger and to reduce the number of entering foreigners for a few years can be one solution

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: One of my arguments for this reduction is that we today can't help refugees to the point I think is necessary for handling such a situation properly

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: This has led to a high criminality among foreigners

text: Many of them come here with heavy memories and difficulties in adjusting to a completely new country, with its language, habits and culture

text: I don't think it's strange that problems arise and lead to criminality, which is the case of many foreigners, especially for young men

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: The fact that many of the crimes in Sweden are made by these people, don't make it easier for Swedes to accept those not born here

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: Negative feelings about foreigners have increased as a result and many in Sweden have developed into racists

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: One frightening aspect of this is the big amount of people who are "invisible" in their disliking of refugees, that is, people who don't go out with their thoughts in public

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I don't think that so much must happen before their feelings increase

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I can refer to myself in this situation because I've become more hostile to refugees in the last few years; due to things I've seen and heard about them

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: The place where I live has changed since we got a refugee camp and there is much more violence now than for a few years ago

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: Another bad aspect with this minority group of criminal foreigners is that they make it harder for the other nice foreigners

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: The well-behaved foreigners get in trouble because of the bad reputation of the criminal ones and can meet resistance in society

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I feel like Sweden is moving in a vicious circle that we don't try to break

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: We now see us in a situation that will soon blow of like a bomb

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: I therefore insist that we must reduce the number of foreigners, who get the opportunity to stay in this country, while we can't take care of those already here

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners
text: Sweden doesn't have the ability to take care of people, who come in despair, fear and hope for a safe place

title: Reduce the number of entering foreigners

Why work?
text: Unemployment is a burning issue in many countries

title: Why work?
text: In Sweden unemployment is the cause of a lot of problems the country is facing

title: Why work?
text: , and is a major issue in every general election

title: Why work?
text: There are a lot of questions I ask myself concerning unemployment in Sweden: Is it really fair that some people are choosing to be unemployed because they make as much, sometimes more money than they would do working? Is it fair that people with jobs that require an education in many cases don't make more money than people with jobs that doesn't require education? It might be easier to get a job if you have an education but what is the point if someone else is making the same money on benefits? A lot of people might think it's wrong to live on benefits but should it really be a question of morale if an unemployed person should try to get a job or just keep living on benefits? Is this really the right way to fight unemployment and motivate people to educate themselves

title: Why work?
text: I don't think so

title: Why work?
text: People in Sweden have a high living standard, and people without serious social problems such as abuse of drugs or alcohol will with or without a job live a rather decent life

title: Why work?
text: At the same time Sweden is a country with huge debts and sometime these debts will have to be paid of

title: Why work?
text: The system with unemployment benefits is costing a lot of money but I believe that the biggest problem with it is that it makes people very unwilling to educate themselves

title: Why work?
text: My father has been in Asia recently and got in touch with a lot of people from that continent, especially people from Japan and China

title: Why work?
text: He was very surprised by their knowledge and their very different attitude towards education

title: Why work?
text: There young students actually wants to learn things

title: Why work?
text: Students over there doesn't have the same possibilities to higher education as we in Sweden have but are much more eager to get educated

title: Why work?
text: My father thinks that the Asians in a near future will take over as the leading "raze" and then the countries in Europe and North America will get much poorer

title: Why work?
text: I don't really share his alarming view at the situation but there might be some truth in it

title: Why work?
text: The mentality the Asians have is not shared by most of us Swedes

title: Why work?
text: I must admit that my own reasons for studying is not only to get an education but to experience all that comes with it

title: Why work?
text: People in Sweden don't have the pressure to get a job or an education as in many other countries

title: Why work?
text: Here the government gives people the option not to work but live with the same standards anyway, and many do that

title: Why work?
text: To care for the less fortunate is a good thing but I don't think that just handing out money is a good way of doing that

title: Why work?
text: Some people would probably be in great danger to get problems with alcohol or drugs if they were just sitting around the house, and I don't think many would be in a rush to get a new job

title: Why work?
text: To get them out working as soon as possible is a better solution

title: Why work?
text: They should not do unpaid work in the public sector to earn their unemployment benefits but instead get paid for the work they do

title: Why work?
text: There are a lot of holes to fill for example in schools and health care

title: Why work?
text: The government could also support companies and motivate them to hire more people

title: Why work?
text: If companies hired more people there would probably be a shortage of educated and skilled workers which would make the wages higher and probably motivate more people to get an education

title: Why work?
text: Unemployment benefits could be handed out for just a few months giving a person a chance to get a job of his own choice and if he was unable to find one the government should provide a job for him

title: Why work?
text: This would motivate people to go out and find jobs themselves

title: Why work?
text: To take away the unemployment benefits and instead support companies so that they can provide jobs in the private sector is a better way of dealing with unemployment

title: Why work?
text: Hopefully this would also make people more willing to get an education

title: Why work?
Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: Some people are obviously against the tradition of having a monarch in Sweden and would prefer to abolish this, as I heard someone call it, "expensive and old-fashioned institution", but the question is not that simple

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: It is obvious that the monarch of Sweden's role has changed radically over the centuries, from being the supreme warlord in the battlefield and sovereign ruler to today's function as a not politically involved representative of the democratic country

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: What would Sweden look like without its monarch? Are its functions out of date? Is the royal institution simply an economical burden to society, a dinosaur of traditions that should be abandoned? The Swedish monarchy of today carry many traditions and only a monarchy can preserve and make such traditions live, and if Sweden abolished its monarchy it would definitely be a culturally poorer and less colourful country

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: As most monarchies, Sweden has grown more democratic through the last few decades, with the consequence that the power of the monarch has decreased

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: But nevertheless, the monarch and his social sphere has been the centre of many important events and developments throughout history, and it is consequently not by coincidence that Swedish history books are mainly concentrated on the history of the country's kings and queens

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: The monarchs have simply been the ones to make and preserve history, and as the case is in today's society, to make history live by staying to traditions and show that we too make history at this very moment

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: The monarchy is the history and represent it

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: On the other hand, some people argue that history is of minor importance when it comes to everyday life and that they would cope just as well "without it", but one must bear in mind that history is a common denominator that brings people together as a people and is an important part of national identity

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: Take for instance the day of independence in Norway, the seventeenth of May, which is celebrated throughout Norway and even by Norwegians abroad, and in which the royal family play a central role

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: In Sweden there are no festivals of that dimension, but there is an increasing interest in the national day of Sweden, the sixth of June, on which the royal family celebrates together with the Swedish people in Skansen in Stockholm and through the media

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: In addition to such festivals there are of course ceremonies which are made more impressive by the presence of a royalty

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: To be rewarded with a medal from the king's hand certainly beats receiving one from some politician; What would the Nobel Prize awards look like if Göran Persson delivered the medals to the prominent price-winners? The fact that the royal family's doings are of national interest and that the presence of its members is very honourable shows more of its importance to Swedish national identity

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: Of even greater importance are the Swedish royalties' doings and presence on certain occasions to the international identity and reputation of Sweden

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: For example, in the summer of 1998 I attended an opening ceremony of an international festival in Gotland where the queen of Sweden, Silvia, made the opening speech

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: The purpose of the festival was to establish and strengthen the trade market between the countries on the Baltic Sea

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: My experience from this occasion was that the foreign participants felt very honoured by the queen's attendance and that they felt that Sweden as a nation cared

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: Another example of this is that when the bosses of major Swedish companies represent Sweden and Swedish industry abroad (this applies especially to Asia and Africa, but to the rest of the world aswell) the company of the monarch definitely help the Swedish industry, and thereby Sweden to profit at length

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: In other words, to represent Sweden on non-political basis is by all means the most important task for today's monarch in Sweden, and I am in no doubt that the four Swedish Crowns that is paid per capita a year to maintain monarchy in Sweden is worth every öre

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars
text: To abolish monarchy in Sweden would not pay since the tasks of the monarch would have to be replaced, presumably by politicians who could not possibly do the tasks with the effect of a monarch

title: Sweden without a monarch would be like Hollywood without its film stars

Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Four years ago there was a reformation of how teachers were supposed to grade their pupils

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The changes that were made were changes only for the worse, I think

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Therefore I am going to argue against the current grading system of Sweden in this essay

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: I will bring up problems both in the grading process and the grading scale

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The area of my essay will be restricted to Upper Secondary education since that is my most recent experience

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: In Sweden today the pupil is completely at the mercy of their one teacher of each course when it comes to grading tests, most essays and the course itself

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: They are judged on a scale of four grades where the lowest one means that you have failed and equals zero

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: So in effect that is the same thing as no grade at all

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The guidelines that the teachers have of how to grade are extremely vague and therefore can be interpreted in various ways

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The criterias for receiving the highest mark is not defined at all

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Lack of inspection, few grades of the scale, and vague or no grading guidelines: these are the three main issues that leads to an unfairness in our grading system

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: I will go in to these problems to explain them and their consequences further

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: My main argent as to why the system of grading is unjust is the lack of inspection: Sweden is one of few countries where the teacher has no obligation to explain and justify the grading of a student to anyone except for the student himself/herself

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: This means that a teacher can set a subjective and unfair grade without anyone doing anything about it

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: I think that there always should be more than one person involved in setting grades, either an inspector or another teacher

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The only way a student can make his or her voice herd is by complaining to the vice principal or principal of the school, but that is not very common since the teacher is an authority to the pupil whom they do not want to stand up to in that way

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Recently though, it has become more and more common that the student starts to argue with the teacher when receiving a low grade

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: This creates a more pressured environment where the teachers sometimes gives in to setting a higher mark which the student really does not deserve so that students who do not argue to get a higher grade feel unfairly treated

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: One of the reasons of why this occurs is that there are too few marks to choose from

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: This is my second argument of why the grading system is unjust

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: There used to be the grades one to five where five is the highest one

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Now the grades are; IG, G, VG and MVG, MVG being the highest one

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: IG equals zero, G ten points, VG fifteen points and MVG twenty points

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Before, underachievers could at least get the grade one or two but now they get zero points

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The IG pulls down the average more and gives the student less self-esteem and less motivation

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Few grades also leads to large variations of the students achievements within the same grade

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: That means that pupils that have worked unequally hard and have quite big differences in test results get the same credit for their work

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Vague definitions of what is required in order to achive a mark is my third argument

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The national guidelines given to the teachers are as I said very unspecific

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: This leads to different policies at different schools and differences between teachers' was of interpreting the guidelines

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Your final grade is both the recite of your accomplished studies and the ticket in to further studies, and your future if you will

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: Therefore it is paramount that they are just and fair

title: Swedish schools have an unjust grading system.
text: The Swedish grading system can in no way affirm this

Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: Why is it, that most of today's people do not live with their old-aged parents anymore? Do you not cherish the time you can spend with your parents? Do you really believe that the pension is well enough to support your parents living through the last days of their lifes and therefore no need to take care of them? Nowadays most old people are being sent away by their children, to live in nursery homes for elderly

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: For this argument, I will below bring up 3 major points I think is worth having in mind before you send your parents off

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: My first point is health

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: By having elderly living with you, you can many times keep them mentally and physically active and healthy

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: Do make them feel useful and needed and keep them busy with some easy daily life chores, such as light cleaning, grocery shopping, execute bank errands but do not forget to keep an active social network

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you
text: (Isnt it a great idea with a household of more headcounts to share those dreadful house chores?) On the other hand, by keeping old people in nursery homes, will many times, bring them to in-activity and boredom due to lack of physical and mental activities but also lack of outside contacts

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you

text: Don't you think it is time for you to show your parents some gratitude and care for their old-aged well being

text: Do understand, that many times old people are just being old, which neccessarily do not mean that they are incapable of taking care of themselves and that full-time devotion is needed

text: All you have to do is to give little time to care for and pay some attention to them

text: Love and compassion is not wasted since you many times, somehow will be re-paid

text: Have in mind that children often take after their own parents, not only the outer and inner features but also by their acts and doings

title: Why don't you have your parents living with you

text: Animals have always been important to mankind

title: Humans need to stop eating anonymous meat and dying their petpoodles pink.

title: Humans need to stop eating anonymous meat and dying their petpoodles pink.

title: Humans need to stop eating anonymous meat and dying their petpoodles pink.
text: For people who live alone or far from families a cat can be not only a kept animal but almost as good as a close friend

title: Humans need to stop eating anonymous meat and dying their petpoodles pink.

title: Humans need to stop eating anonymous meat and dying their petpoodles pink.
