Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The intensity of the debate about whether Sweden should continue to be a monarchy or not has had its highs and lows

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: Today, the debate has somewhat declined and even the most eager monarchy opponents seem to admit that the battle is lost, at least for the time being

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The Swedish Social Democrats have now and then spoken out against Sweden being a monarchy and declared how they want the county to become a republic instead

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: They have an internal problem though, since the majority of their own members are in favour of a monarchy as the form of government in Sweden

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: Of course this may have something to do with the fact that we have a very popular royal family at the moment

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: But there are several aspects of the matter and I will deal with the most common arguments used by the monarchy opponents

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: My intention is to argue why Sweden should continue to be a monarchy

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The most frequent argument against the monarchy is the fact that it is not democratic

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: A monarch is born into the profession and can not be elected by the people

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The people can not choose the person they want to be their foremost representative officially

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The Prime Minister is elected by the people and that is why he also should be the head of State and act as a President, they argue

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: One problem a Prime Minister would have to face though, if he were to be the head of State as well, is the fact that he might not be very welcome in some countries

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: A Prime Minister is foremost a political person that represents specific political and social values that could be offensive in some cultures

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: He would also get two full time jobs on his hands and the Prime Minister part of the job seems to be quite enough for one person to handle

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: A monarch, on the other hand, is a non-political person that remains as head of State during fluctuations of political parties in power

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The monarch must be born into the profession, I believe, otherwise the issue of political voting would be inevitable

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: Another good thing about the law of succession is the fact that in Sweden, like in many other kingdoms, the monarch is trained from birth to act as the head of State

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: In fact the monarch is under constant education during his entire life

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: He learns about his country and keeps himself up to date by visiting schools, hospitals and important industries for example

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: All of this is done in order to represent Sweden in the best possible way and to make important business contacts abroad

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The King is often called a door opener for industrial leaders to meet with colleges from foreign countries

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: This task is a very important one and Sweden has gained a lot from the King's door opening trips abroad

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: However, the monarch is often seen as a too aristocratic, snobby, rich diva that does not know a thing about ordinary way of life, at least the critics think so

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: They argue that we do not need a national role model that costs a lot of money

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: Some people also seem to think that the only thing the royal family does when representing is to arrange and attend parties

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: To tell the truth they actually do arrange and attend quite a lot of parties or representation dinners, but that is a part of their job

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: In terms of working-hours there are few persons in this country that have as many as they do

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: They are theoretically on duty around the clock, every day of their lives

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: People that regard the royal family as too snobby and aristocratic probably refer to the fact that whenever you see them they are dressed very properly and elegantly

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The King must have a certain commanding presence though, and therefore dress and appear properly, to achieve what is expected of him

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: As for the issue of the costs of having a King, Queen and court, it only costs each Swedish citizen 4,50 Swedish crowns per year

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: The royal castles are not included in this budget, but they would be kept and looked after for historical reasons anyway

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: Sweden has a long tradition of being a monarchy and the royal family's work has been well appreciated by the majority of the Swedish citizens for several generations

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: However, I believe that the issues discussed above shows that our monarchy should be maintained not just for historical reasons, but for the fact that Sweden benefits from the work done by the monarch

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
text: We like our King and we need him

title: Long live the monarchy in Sweden!
The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Long before man set foot in Scandinavia, packs of wolves roamed the peninsula which today form the countries of Sweden and Norway

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Then, following the retreating mass of ice, the first humans entered the wild country from the south and conflict arose between the two species

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Both being predatory and hunting the same game, wolf and man long lived as bitter enemies and there were casualties on both sides

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Soon, however, man gained the upper hand due to her dexterity and ingenuity

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: She crafted weapons with which the wolves could not compete and before long the packs found it safest to stay out of her way, as she, as is the nature of her species, continued to colonise the land

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: The situation has changed greatly since that first encounter between humans and wolves in Scandinavia

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Humans have, indeed, colonised the peninsula - and, in fact, the world - and pushed the diminished packs back to the verge of extinction

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: This atrocity was justified by the "danger" that the large packs presented to humans and their livestock, as is depicted in many a fairy tale (for instance the story of Little Red Ridinghood)

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Still in these "enlightened" times, wolves are considered dangerous and some voices cry out for their extermination

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: This essay is a cry for their preservation

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: The main argument one hears against the wolves, comes from the Saami community up in the north

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: They complain about wolves eating their reindeer - which is still their main source of income - and other livestock

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Of course I have sympathy with them for their economic losses, but one must bear in mind that wolves fed on reindeer long before humans and that they, in their diminished packs, often are too few to go after big game such as elks

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: I do not think that we because of this should consider wolves as vermin, to be killed off for the sake of our convenience

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: The wolves in the Sweden-Norway peninsula are the last remnants of a once large population

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: We are not the ones to decide whether they should live on as a species or go extinct merely because of our so-called superiority

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Our own interests as inhabitants of Earth and representatives of life actually demands the opposite: that we should help our furry companions to live on and prosper

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Because, as has been stated by such renowned biologists as Richard Dawkins and Steven J

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Gould, a multitude of species increases the chances of some of them surviving a possible (and rather likely) future catastrophe and those species would then carry with them the seeds of future life on this planet

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Where would we have been if the dinosaurs would have exterminated the little furry rodent that was lurking in the bushes? That little mammal survived the natural disaster that killed off the mighty dinosaurs and went forth to multiply and evolve until, finally, we came along; the descendants of "vermin", if you will

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: A person vaguely familiar with evolutionary biology could here remark that it is only natural that some species go extinct, that it is part of the natural selection process so bravely proclaimed by Darwin

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: This is true and I will not argue with that statement

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: It is, however, not natural that this selection takes place at a rate of fifty to one hundred going extinct every day

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Most of them are insects and had yet to be discovered, but that does not lessen the tragedy of their death

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Another argument with which I have been faced, was that since man is clearly superior to the wolf in intelligence (I have arguments against that statement alone, but I will leave them out in this essay), why should we not kill them? What is the use of having them around? Apart from the reasons I have already stated above, I have yet another to ad

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Compassion

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: We should let them live out of compassion for them as a fellow species

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: Since one keeps hearing the preposterous nonsense about humans "being set apart from the animals", it seems we pride ourselves on our intelligence and complex emotions, since, basically, that is all that really sets us apart from other animals

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: With this in mind the first argument I stated, that the animal life on Earth should be preserved in the interest of the continuance of life, should appeal to our intelligence and the second, to feel compassion rather than hatred, should certainly appeal both to our emotions and sense of moral

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: The Saami community should receive financial compensation to help them cope with the loss of the reindeer, a loss that really is not too great, although I am sure it is keenly felt

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: This compensation could be provided either by the government or a fund especially designated for this purpose

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
text: It is in our power to let them live, therefor I think we should

title: The Preservation of Wolves - a critical issue
ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: There is a lively debate going on in Swedish schools whether to allow pupils to wear caps or whether to force them to take them off in class

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: The custom to wear a baseball cap has become very popular and many pupils do not take the cap off their heads when they enter the classroom

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: What young people wear has always been discussed by adults and teachers have even in the past reacted to how their students are dressed

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Nevertheless, the discussion about the cap is much more upset than previous discussions

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: In some schools the debate is so intense that it has divided the school staff into two groups depending on the opinion on the matter

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: This is a rather complex issue and I will take up just two standpoints for allowing pupils to wear baseball caps in the classroom

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: First, clothing is a part of a young person's struggle for identity, second, the cap itself is not the problem, there are other reasons why teachers get so upset when pupils do not take off their caps

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: In the first place, young people try several ways to find out and show who they are or would like to be

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: For instance, music and sports are often important parts of their lives which they, naturally, have a need to express in different ways

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: In other words, a cap, often with a logotype showing what music style or sports team the wearer likes, is a way to show identity

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: It is no news that teenagers want to dress differently from adults

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Not too long ago this could be done by wearing jeans and a sweater

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: This is not longer possible because it is the way any teachers dress nowadays

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Strangely enough, the reaction to and the feelings about pupils wearing baseball caps in class seem more upset than, for example, the reactions to punk-rockers in the 1980s

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: One explanation of this is that the cap is a religious and social symbol

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Traditionally, men take off their caps in church in order to show respect and generations of simple men have gone "cap in hand" to beg for their survival

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Undoubtedly, this humility is what many teachers seem to want from their students in the 1990s

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: They feel provoked and not respectfully treated by pupils who wear caps in class

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Of course, both teachers and pupils shall be met by respect in school

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: However, it is lack of respect to demand this humility from teenagers

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: School has to look upon every single pupil as an individual and to let this individual develop in her/his way

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: People who do not want the pupils to wear caps in class might ask where this is going to end, what are young persons wearing in2050, are things getting worse and worse? I would say that this is not the problem and it brings me to the next argument for allowing caps in the classroom

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: The reason why teachers get upset when pupils do not take off the cap is unclear

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: It may be argued that it is rude and impolite or that the pupil's eyes can not be seen

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Still, the baseball cap has just become a symbol for more serious problems

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: It is mainly boys in caps that cause the teachers' irritation

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Even this has its explanation in religious traditions, thus, men traditionally take off the caps while women keep their hats on in order to show respect in church

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Owing to this, girls in baseball caps appear less provocative than boys

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Furthermore, boys behave, in general, more provocatively than girls

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: It is not to be said that teachers are careless about the real problems

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: However, some teachers are more likely to deal with the cap-problem than to search the origin to why things do not work out well in the classroom

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: In conclusion, the baseball cap has become a bigger issue than it deserves

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: To wear a cap is, in most cases, a rather harmless way for young persons to show who they are

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: When teachers choose to focus their attention on whether pupils take off their caps or not, they are neglecting more serious problems

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
text: Eventually, in order to deal with the pupils of today teachers have to get rid of old traditions and face modern society

title: ALLOW BASEBALL CAPS IN THE CLASSROOM
A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: A new, big, shiny and powerful car is better

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: That is the statement I will put forward in this essay

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: I will explain the importance of these qualities in a car and why the vehicle needs them

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: There are a number of things to be concerned about when it comes to personal transportation, easy access, safety, environment, to name a few

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: There are also different aspects of these concerns, and I will argue that all are meet in the most satisfactorily way in a new, big, shiny and powerful car

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: So, to start with one of the concerns I mentioned above, the importance or benefit from easy access

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Easy access is one of the major advantages by owning your own means of transportation

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: For instance, very few people live close enough to walk to their work

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Public transportation in any form requires that you have to get to your local bus stop or train station

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: There is a time-table that will tell when transportation is possible, and if these set times doesn't suit me there is nothing I can do

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: By owning a car I can make my own time-table

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: I will never have to walk to a distant bus stop in the rain or in the cold, and for obvious reasons I cannot be late for the car as I always will risk being with the bus

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: My car will be there in the parking lot or in the garage, waiting to take me to work

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Everybody is late sometimes, valuable minutes may be lost and this can very fast create a stressful situation

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: If I have a car powerful enough I will always be able to catch up on those few minutes lost, perhaps in the morning, minutes that probably would cause a disaster if I depended on public transportation

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: I would be late and end up with a very bad start of the day

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: The need for a powerful car in the case of making up lost time is essential, and a strong engine can make such a situation less dangeorus, but this concerns the area of safety which I will now turn to

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: When it comes to cars, safety is always an important issue

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: If, one morning I have to speed up a little bit because I am late, there is always the possibility that I may have to overtake another car

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: The basic rule here is that a powerful engine will make the overtaking of another car safer

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: It is as simple as that

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Every extra horsepower is needed to pass the other car as fast and as smooth as possible

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Being on the wrong side of the road is a danger to you and anyone on the road, and every second there that may be avoided by a stronger engine is an increase in safety

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Size doesn't matter, it is said

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: True or not, it is a big lie in car safety

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: Any investigation on the subject will say that a big car is safer than a small car

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: In the unfortunate event of an accident the metal around me will function as protection and reduce the impact

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: A big car will have larger impact areas to take up the force in an accident, and this will greatly reduce the possibility of the driver ending up with the car engine in his lap

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: I have here talked about safety on the personal level, but safety can also be seen in a much wider perspective, the safety of the environment around us

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: This I will turn to in the next paragraph

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: A new car, built in the last few years is more environmental friendly than an older car

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: The car industry is constantly improving their cars, and every day progress is being made

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER
text: A car today cannot be sold without a catalytic converter

title: A NEW, BIG, SHINY AND POWERFUL CAR IS BETTER

Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: Today in Sweden the monarchy and its representatives, the royal family, are enjoying great popularity

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: But is there actually a place for such an institution in a modern, democratic society? I would say that there isn't, and below I shall seek to explain why this is so

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: A monarch is, by definition, someone who has inherited his or her occupation as the head of state of a country

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: This of course has not only purely professional implications; it also brings with it a certain status and a position as a national figurehead

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: Even a monarch without any actual power or political influence holds an important position as a representative of his country

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: It is therefore quite remarkable that someone is to be born into this function; in a democracy of today a job such as this should not be the private possession of a specific family and because of this the monarchy is in itself undemocratic

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: As this is obviously a matter of principle, it might be argued that such concerns are of little practical importance when it comes to a merely ceremonial regent such as the king of Sweden

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: On the other hand, when regarding it from the monarchs point of view, it is indeed very much a practicality; his personal freedom and his ability to choose his life is actually greatly limited from the very moment of his birth

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: He has the theoretical possibility, of course, to abdicate, but since this is not a very common event it does hardly present a realistic alternative

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: Such a restriction of individual liberty is not usually associated with a modern democracy

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: But, some might object, having a figurehead such as Sweden's ceremonial monarch is indeed important; our king is our countries main representative abroad, a national symbol

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: This is true in as much as having a representative person on an international level could well be said to be important

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: But because this actually is important, it is only natural to assume that we would want to choose for ourselves who is to represent us

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: A monarch, who has only inherited his representative duties, is obviously not necessarily the most suitable person for this office

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: An elected head of state, a president, could on the other hand to a greater extent than a monarch be said to actually represent the people

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: The duties of this elected head of state might still be mainly ceremonial; the president wouldn't even have to be a political figure and there doesn't have to be any danger of too much power being concentrated in one person, as some might indeed fear

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: Are there no advantages of having a monarch, rather than ceremonial president, then? Well, some people would perhaps argue that the institution of the monarchy and the circumstances surrounding it has a value in itself, that it adds a touch of glamour to the country and that reading articles in weekly magazines about the extraordinary lives of the royal family adds an indispensable joy to the dreary lives of the so called ordinary people

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: However, we may very well ask ourselves if such an attitude actually belongs in a modern society, and to this I must answer that it doesn't

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: The media hype surrounding the monarchy in general and the royal family in particular is horrifyingly reminiscent of times long since past, when humble respect towards the regent was demanded of the lower classes

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: The king of today is nothing but a curiosity, but is still sometimes surrounded by the attention bestowed upon his more despotic predecessors

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: This sort of public indulgence in the worship of royalty might be considered comparatively harmless, but does nevertheless preserve an image of a social structure that is no longer relevant

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: One might of course also consider this matter from the point of view of the monarch and his family; constant exposure to the public eye gazing through the distorting lens of the tabloid press is an attention that they have little choice but to accept

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: All together, this shows that a monarchy could not be said to belong in a modern democracy; not only is it undemocratic in itself, it is also unsuitable for the functions it is supposed to perform and severely out of date, perpetuating an old class system

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
text: An elected ceremonial head of state would clearly answer better to the demands of our time

title: Monarchy - obsolete in a democratic society
Racism
text: "Like tribalism, fundamentalism, homophobia and all the other shallow responses of one person to another, racism concentrates on "what" you are, and ignores "who" you are

title: Racism
text: " Timothy Findley This is an argumentational essay against racism

title: Racism
text: Racism has been a problem ever since the humans began to walk on the earth

title: Racism
text: Racism means that you as a racist are looking down on people of other races, and treat them unfairly

title: Racism
text: You see your own race as the best one

title: Racism
text: In our history, the focal point of racism has laid on the whites discrimination of the other races

title: Racism
text: That is partly because they have had the power most of the time

title: Racism
text: Very many problems had occurred based on misunderstandings about other races cultures and the believes that in a race all the people have the same physical characteristics depending of which race they come from

title: Racism
text: In this essay I will try to argue why I am against racism based mostly on the two aspects, unfair treatment of certain races an the belief that some races are better than the other

title: Racism
text: The United Nations resolution about every peoples equal rights, that we here in Sweden are bound to follow, says that we can't treat other races different from our own

title: Racism
text: Unfortunately, even if we have a non-discrimination law from 1994 here in Sweden, this is not followed

title: Racism
text: A immigrant who have the same criteria as a white person is in many cases chosen last when he/she is looking for a job

title: Racism
text: This happens even if they speak very good Swedish

title: Racism
text: They don't get the job because of their appearance and colour of their skin

title: Racism
text: In Sweden fortunately we don't usually have open struggle between races

title: Racism
text: In Sweden there are a lot of scepticism against other cultures than the Swedish but there are only a few groups who tries to hurt people

title: Racism
text: In other countries though, like South Africa was when the Apartheid system was the ruling system, the hole government and authorities tries to discriminate the black people in all different sorts of ways

title: Racism
text: The blacks had no right to vote, they were segregated and tortured and they were not allowed to get any good job

title: Racism
text: The whites were afraid that if the black people got the power the whites would do the same to them as revenge

title: Racism
text: As we see in South Africa today when the Apartheid system no longer exist more black and whites are now living together and the country are now up on its feet again after decades of racism were the whites thought they were the master race

title: Racism
text: This leads us in to the other aspect I thought to write about namely the belief in master races, races that are better than other races

title: Racism
text: Frustration about social situation is one reason why this thought have occurred

title: Racism
text: You have to blame someone that look different and comes from an other culture and meaning that your race is better than his/hers

title: Racism
text: The best example of this is of course what happened in Nazi-Germany during the 30s and 40s when millions of people were murded because of the thought that the Jews an other races were "untermenschen" which means that they weren't worth as much as the Germanic master race

title: Racism
text: Yale biologist Johnatan Morks writes in an article about that he don't belief that races are distinct biological categories created by differences in genes inherit from parents

title: Racism
text: This shows that the racist are wrong about a master race, there are very few genetical differences between races

title: Racism
text: There is no evidence that some races could have hereditary intelligent or mental rudiments or being stronger than other races just because he is from just this race

title: Racism
text: It is of course never proven that everyone of a certain race have the same hereditary draws

title: Racism
text: The best athletes and professionals are from all different races

title: Racism
text: As you could see we are very much alike

title: Racism
text: I think we can learn a lot from other cultures and that we must deal with all the environmental problems we are standing before in the future together

title: Racism
text: Even the food could be a problem in the future where we should cooperate and not kill each other because we look different

title: Racism
text: Money and power used by some races to discriminate others are also a problem that we should solve in the future

title: Racism
text: I don't think that people are so different after all, we all have two ears, one nose, one mouth and are born by a mother

title: Racism
text: So beating other people and thinking that their race incorrectly are better than another, is only racist rubbish we all have to fight against through knowledge and communication

Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: The "elderly" I will be referring to in this essay, are old people that are no longer able to look after themselves

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They can no longer live by themselves and the question is, whether the state or the family should look after them

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: Since I have been working with elderly at a nursing home for four years, I have some personal experience of the accommodation, treatment and care that they need

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: In this essay I have chosen to argue against families looking after the elderly

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: My argumentation is based on three main arguments

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: The first argument has to do with safety

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: How many families today can honestly say, that they can provide proper accommodations for an elderly parent for an unlimited time-period? It has not only to do with having the space needed, but also that the house or apartment should be adequately equipped to accommodate an elderly citizen that is unable to take care of himself

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: A nursing home, on the other hand, is functional and suitable for appropriate care and treatment

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: The beds have protection on the sides to prevent accidents, there are lifts in the ceiling to make sure a disabled person will get to his chair safely and the bathrooms are equipped to make regular baths possible

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: It may sound inhuman to use these kind of facilities, but a lot of elderly people staying at home are confined to their beds all day and wont get a proper shower more than a few times a year

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: That is even more inhuman

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: My second argument has to do with medical service

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: In a private home, someone has to take responsibility for the medication and also make sure that the person is looked after during the day

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: There will also be a need for some sort of alarm-system in case of an emergency during times when he or she is left alone

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: Today, most families need two incomes to support a family

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: Having an elderly relative living in the family would put a further strain on the economy, since one person would have to stay at home or work part-time

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: At a nursing home, the elderly have a close contact with doctors and nurses all around the clock

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: There are also nursing assistants, occupational therapists and physiotherapists

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They will be able to meet the different needs an elderly person might have

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: My third argument has to do with social contact

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: It is important for everyone to have friends and acquaintances

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: One of the biggest fears of getting old is loneliness

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: For the first time in our lives we have all the time we need but no one to share it with

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: The children are in the middle of life, bringing up their children, working on their careers and they don't have time for daily visits

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: Elderly people, who can not as easily visit their friends, are at a risk of being isolated and lonely

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: At a nursing home, they are surrounded by people and have lots of opportunities to make new friends

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They meet other elderly as well as the staff

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: There are a lot of activities; like occupational therapy, newspaper-readings, entertainment and church-services, that they can attend

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: We should not fool ourselves to believe that the stressed-out people of the nineties have the time, money, competence or even the will to take on the responsibility of taking care of the elderly people

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: I'm also sure that the elderly would not want to be in a situation where they are totally dependent on their children

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They don't want to feel that they are a burden to their lives

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They need their privacy, even when they are getting older and in need of assistance

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: Today there are a variety of service buildings, nursing homes and old peoples homes located where people live, so that anyone who wants to can stay living in their own area

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: When families are relieved from the responsibility of the everyday care of the elderly, they can put their energy and efforts on visiting them on a regular basis

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
text: They can bring them home for visits and take an active part in their lives, but they should leave the daily physical and medical care to the professionals, without feeling guilty about it

title: Our Elderly Deserve Qualified Care
Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: The word "euthanasia" stir up many feelings inside of us

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: It originates in the Greek language and means "good death"

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: As soon as death is brought up for discussion we tend to defensively distance ourselves; it is very unpleasant to talk about it

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: But I think it is important that we do discuss it, since we all have very different thoughts and conceptions about death and how we want to face it when it's our turn

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: One aspect to discuss is whether or not we should allow assistance to people who want to committ suicide, but are incapable in one way or another to do so

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Today, euthanasia is only allowed for people living in Columbia, Japan, the Netherlands and the state of Oregon (in the U

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: S

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: A

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: I think this is strange since more than these four states have ratified the UN Decleration of Human Rights, which states in its third article that all individuals have the right to life

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: And, as I see it, death is the end of life, in other words, dying is the last thing we do in life, and thus the right to life indirectly grants us a right to end our life whenever we want to

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: This point of view is supported by most countries, who in fact do not regard suicide as a legal crime

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: As a matter of fact, it would be quite absurd to have a legal procedure against a person who is accused of killing herself

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Everyday we choose between continue living our lives or not to continue living and instead end our suffering by committing suicide

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: By doing so we have not broken any judicial law

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: But if we happen to end up in a hospital bed, unable to perform even the simplest of movements and even less take our lives, the law will have to be broken by others in order to execute our uttermost privilege as human beings

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: I say it is a privilege, because I think that when a person is in such pain, or feels that living is so indignant in her state that she rather dies than tries to continue her fight to live, then it most certainly must be a privilege to have the right to end it once and for all

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: There's really no right or wrong side of this discussion

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: But what I think the opponents of euthanasia disregards far too often, and show little sympathy for, is the wish and rights of the suffering patient

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: They tend to restrict their arguing to a discussion of old-fashioned morals and preachings

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: And since they are a majority, and the laws forbidding euthanasia are in their favor, they thus impose power over the human rigths granted in the third article as mentioned above, in a way they would not be able to if the patients were capable of committing suicide on their own

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: The patients are deprived of their rights to decide over their own lives

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Therefore I think euthanasia should be judicially on a level with suicide

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: This is not an easy issue to take a stand on, or even discuss, since there are so many different opinions and emotions surrounding it

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Of course, this issue is extremely full of emotion to those who have a family member or close relative in a situation where her fate lies in the machine which keeps her breathing and regularly nourishes her, or whose pain wont't leave until she is so full of drugs that she is more dead than alive

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Because of the pain a situation like this causes the patient's family, every effort should be made in order to persuade the patient to reconsider her decision

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: And the routines and procedures should be well developped, as well as the matters of responsibility and who should "turn off the switch"

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: These are of course very difficult questions to which there are no solutions yet

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: What I have tried to illustrate is the importance of our unrestricted right to life and, indirectly, right to death

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: And I have questioned why we have the right to committ suicide when we are "healthy", and deprived of that same right when we happen to be "disabled"

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right
text: Once again I would like to stress the importance of discussing this matter with those we have closely around ourselves and who might be concerned when it is time; it will certainly make things a little easier if we know and respect each others' stands when reality hits us the hard way

title: Euthanasia - A Human Right

High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The aim of this essay is to point out the lack of discipline in Swedish high schools; especially when it comes to being on time

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In Sweden, people have a tendency to think that we have the best school system in the world

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I do agree that we have reached a high level of academic standards, but what about the rest? All through the 90s a wind of student democracy has blown through Swedish high schools, and all rules are gone

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Students fight for their rights in school, however, they forget that they have responsibilities just like everybody ells

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: High school students want to be treated like the young adults that they are, and in my opinion this desire is well met by the staff

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The question is do they behave like adults? In many ways, yes, but there are also many areas, which are open for a great deal of improvement

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In my opinion, Swedish high school students have been given more freedom than many of them can handle

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The problem is that most of them are not even aware of it; they take everything for granted since Sweden is a liberal and free country

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Being on time is, to me, a very simple issue

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Students who are eighteen years old ought to know the importance of being on time

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: What really upsets me, is the fact that many high school students, indeed, do not realize how important this is

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Going to school is a job, and people who do not arrive at work on time find themselves in trouble

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: However, this rule does not apply in Swedish high schools

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In elementary schools it is different; there it is not acceptable to be late

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Swedish students in secondary education do not realize how free and well treated they are

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Perhaps some education in other countries' school systems would help them to understand and appreciate what they have

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I can only compare to the school system in America, which I had the opportunity to experience during a high school year

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In America, the situation is totally different

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: American high schools have often been criticized and analyzed; especially when it comes to the academics

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In my opinion, we should think twice before we criticize America; at least they have some respect and discipline in their schools

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: An American high school student is not allowed to leave the classroom without a signed pass from the teacher, and there is staff in the hallways to make sure that there are no students running around without passes

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Neither are students allowed to leave campus during school hours, and the school hours are fixed; there is no such thing as a free period

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Students are also subject to a dress code (whereas students in Sweden can attend school half-naked)

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: If a student repeatedly comes in late, he or she will, without hesitation, get detention

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: American teachers do not tolerate disrespectful behavior and they are not to be called by their first name

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Let us now take a look at Sweden

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: What happens when students are late for class? Nothing

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In Sweden, students can literally do anything they want to, and nobody cares

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Some students drop in five minutes late, and others arrive when there are fifteen minutes left of the class

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: To me, this is very disturbing and disrespectful

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Of course everybody can be late once or twice, but not every day

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: A person who is frequently late does not give a reliable impression

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Somehow this has to stop

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The 90s are almost over and now it is time for other winds to blow

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Swedish high schools have to obtain some disciplinary rules

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The school is not doing its students a favor by letting them get away with it

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Many students are caught in a bad pattern and they do not see any reasons to change since it seems to be accepted to arrive at any time

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I wonder what will happen when reality strikes

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I honestly thought that it would be different at the university, but I was wrong

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Some people were even twenty minutes late for the important roll call on the first day of school

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: And really, why would things be different? This is what students in Sweden are used to

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I do know that the world outside school does not accept that people are late

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: The train, for example, will not wait for lazy students

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Something has to be done, or my generation will delay the entire country of Sweden

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: I do not mean that teachers have to act like policemen; high schools and universities are not play schools

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: We are talking about adults and the attitude among students has to change

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Abroad Swedes are known to be on time

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: It is alarming that many young adults have no respect for the importance of being on time

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In Sweden everything has to be done kindly and pedagogically, which of course is good, but everything has a limit

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: High school students are young, but yet they are adults and it is time to stop all the coddling with them

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: Let them taste life

title: High schools in Sweden need discipline!
text: In life we all have to take the consequences of being late!

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: This essay is mainly going to discuss whether we can take care of elderly people at home or if the best solution is to keep them in institutions

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Firstly, the main reason of keeping grandparents in institutions is that our family attitudes have changed because of our different way of living, compared to the old society

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Have we forgotten the social aspect with close family relations including taking care of our parents when they become old

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Is this the fact because of lack of moral responsibility or is it a financial problem? These are questions hard to answer but still there are problems that can be solved by financial aid to elderly care and change in attitudes

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Maybe the best way for caring about many elderly people is to put them into institutions

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: This subject will be discussed with the mentioned questions as topics

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: If this will not help you to see which is the best way to take care of grandparents, this can anyway give you suggestions of what could be done about it

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: If we compare our attitude today towards elderly care with how it was in the fifties we notice that the attitude towards old people have changed

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: A question can be asked whether we care less or not about elderly people today when the individual person is more stressed by all the choices and demands in the society

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: This lead to a generation gap which is a huge expanse between parent, child and the grandparents

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: It is said that young people and adults do not know each other

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: They live under the same roof, but they rarely see one another because at least one of the parents go to work before the children are awake

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: In this kind of family where a generation gap exists, it can sometimes be good to have a grandmother at home, giving the children some of the attention the parents do not give

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: On the other hand, if the grandparents are too sick and can not manage without a lot of care it can make the relations worse for this type of family

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: How we treat our old grandparents and children can be related to how we define life quality and good relations

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Life quality today is usually having a good employment, friends, family and be rich enough to buy all the capital goods that an average income earner is expected to use

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: If the aim is to consume then we generally do have a high living standard but what about family values? The society has developed the humans to be more individualistic and self-centered

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: We have forgotten which happiness close relations in a big family can produce

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: We want to live with our nuclear family or spend time with our step-children or step-father

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: With the mentioned family-development there is no room for taking care of elderly grandparents, specially if they are sick

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: The moral responsibility for the family and parents that we used to have in the fifties, is history today in the Western European countries

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: The problem of not having the possibility to keep elderly people at home today is more of a financial problem than a question of responsibility

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Living on one salary can be hard for bigger families if for example three children go to school and the family expenses are high

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Then there are no financial means to spare for taking care of a grandmother

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Institutions are good from an financial point of view, because today it costs often too much to be at home from work or keeping nurses at home to take care of your older parents

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: However, high-income earners should not have a problem with financing their elderly care

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: As long as they spend a lot of time at work they do not have the energy to take care of their grandparents

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Now, as we know these problems for taking care of elderly people at home it makes even more complicated to find solutions to them

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: We can not go back to the fifties and think that we can handle the elderly care like the farmers did

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: The home care of elderly in old days also had disadvantages like poverty, even though it often was more social for the grandparents

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: It is difficult to change peoples attitudes

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: One thing that could be done for both families with middle and high incomes is building blocks of service flats for elderly in the families neighbourhood which could improve the social relations with grandparents

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: This would give the children and parents the possibility to visit them more often and also notice if there is something wrong with the service quality, so they could do something about it

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: The grandparents would also enjoy not to live to far from their children

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: An answer to if we do not have any moral responsibility over our grandparents can be that we are used to live our life on our own with our children

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Nobody expects us to have time for taking care of our grandparents as long as we have service institutions for elderly and disabled

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: One way to make it easier to take care of grandparents at home can be giving financial subsidies to the families

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Another solution can be to lower the fees for families keeping their elderly at service flats in the neighbourhood and letting the grandparents visit their home in weekends, if possible

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: On the other hand, I believe that the children having no interest in taking care of the grandparents should put them into service institutions

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
text: Then there is a major concern that the service quality should be improved, because today there are too little activities for the old people, mainly because of the institutions financial problems

title: Do our living standards require that the state looks after the elderly people?
Adopt or not?
text: There are millions of orphans around the world

title: Adopt or not?
text: In spite of this people keep on bringing new children to the world, instead of taking care of the ones which already exists

title: Adopt or not?
text: This problem could get solved if all the couples whose biological clock has started to tick, should adopt instead of having children on their own

title: Adopt or not?
text: To bear a child is something that is so deeply rooted in the entire mankind, since bringing new children to the world has been necessary in the past to keep on developing the human race

title: Adopt or not?
text: This is, of course, a strong instinct for man, just like it is for all animals

title: Adopt or not?
text: The big difference between man and other animals is that the earth is getting overpopulated by the human race

title: Adopt or not?
text: At the same time as millions of children are dying just because no one wants to or are able to take care of them, the space and the food is running short

title: Adopt or not?
text: Animals regulates overpopulation by diseases, the predatory animals eats weaker animals and herbivores reduces when there is not enough food for them all

title: Adopt or not?
text: Men, on the other hand, have medicines and we do not have to eat other men to survive

title: Adopt or not?
text: Because of this, our population is not regulated in that way

title: Adopt or not?
text: People which adopts children today, are usually people which cannot have children in a biological way

title: Adopt or not?
text: One of the prospective parents could, for example, be barren

title: Adopt or not?
text: The possibility to adopt children from all over the world makes those people able to satisfy their instincts and get children anyway

title: Adopt or not?
text: According to the people which have adopted children, there are no difference between the love to a biological child, and the love to an adopted child

title: Adopt or not?
text: A positive thing with adoption, is that the government controls the prospective parents close and strict

title: Adopt or not?
text: They are controlled in the areas of suitability and how good they might take care of a child

title: Adopt or not?
text: They also has to have a regular and wealthy salary and they have to be married for several years before they can adopt, which should prove that they get on well as a couple

title: Adopt or not?
text: These controls forces people to think careful before they adopt a child

title: Adopt or not?
text: It is not possible to adopt because of a sudden impulse

title: Adopt or not?
text: This is something well thought-out, and it takes good planning to get through with an adoption

title: Adopt or not?
text: Today almost anyone can have a child anytime

title: Adopt or not?
text: It is not rare that the conception is a mistake

title: Adopt or not?
text: In the USA teenage pregnancies is one of the reasons why so many young girls drops out of school

title: Adopt or not?
text: A young teenager can hardly offer a child an adequate childhood, since teenagers seldom have enough money and time for a child

title: Adopt or not?
text: Besides that, it is not unusual that the father is gone by the time the baby arrives

title: Adopt or not?
text: Another reason for adopting instead of having biological children, is that the adoptive parents can choose the babies looks, in rough outlines that is

title: Adopt or not?
text: They can choose if the child should have light or dark skin, and they can even choose the sex of the child

title: Adopt or not?
text: Lots of adoptive parents finds this very positive

title: Adopt or not?
text: There are even people which adopts children just because of this

title: Adopt or not?
text: Scandinavians, for example, are often tired of their light, fine hair and their pale skin, and wishes that their children could get spared from that

title: Adopt or not?
text: In such occasions, adoption is an excellent solution

title: Adopt or not?
text: This could bring a greater mix of different nations and skin-colours without cultural clashes

title: Adopt or not?
text: Even now, it is getting more natural to mix light- and dark-skinned, big-nosed and short people, in schools and at workplaces

title: Adopt or not?
text: Gained adoption of foreign children would probably increase the tolerance towards people which doesn't look exactly like everybody else

title: Adopt or not?
text: Racism might even reduce, since the original, white Viking would more or less get erased

title: Adopt or not?
text: In one single family there could be five different nationalities

title: Adopt or not?
text: Racism and intolerance towards, for example, dark-skinned people could never survive in that kind of society

title: Adopt or not?
text: To make this adopting-theory work, it is required that the government helps some families to finance an adoption, since the costs of adopting a child can get really big

title: Adopt or not?
text: Even an average-family should have the opportunity to adopt, even those families which haven't won the lottery

title: Adopt or not?
text: Adoption have to cost money of course, since the economy is one part of the controls which are made on prospective parents, but it shouldn't have to be about that large amounts

title: Adopt or not?
text: To carry out a decision that would make people forced to adopt instead of having biological children would probably not be possible, since the government can't sterilize every woman in the entire country

title: Adopt or not?
text: Adoption must, for that reason, be a voluntary decision made by every single individual

title: Adopt or not?
text: The only thing that can be done, at this point, is to encourage prospective parents to choose adoption

title: Adopt or not?
Youths need motivation
text: I would like to discuss a problem that is relevant to many young people in Sweden today

title: Youths need motivation
text: Many youths that after graduating from upper secondary school can't get a job and don't live with their parents might get income support from the welfare office

title: Youths need motivation
text: This system with income support is good, but it has some disadvantages when it is combined with a trainee post, which many of these youths get

title: Youths need motivation
text: I will here give you an example, which I think shows what I mean

title: Youths need motivation
text: A year ago a friend of mine was unemployed and received income support, and then he was assigned a trainee post

title: Youths need motivation
text: Since the municipality paid the wages they were very low, almost symbolic

title: Youths need motivation
text: Therefore he received income support to be able to pay rent and food

title: Youths need motivation
text: The welfare office had determined how much money he required during a month and they always gave him what was missing up to this amount

title: Youths need motivation
text: Because of this it didn't matter for him if he went to work or not, he got the money he needed anyway

title: Youths need motivation
text: In fact he saved money if he stayed home, since then he didn't waste it on bus fares

title: Youths need motivation
text: This makes it very tempting to take advantage of the system, which shouldn't be possible and therefore I think the system should be improved

title: Youths need motivation
text: I think the system should be changed so it is profitable to go to work and unprofitable to stay at home and not the other way around

title: Youths need motivation
text: I can see two possible ways of improving this system

title: Youths need motivation
text: One is that they receive more money if they go to work

title: Youths need motivation
text: It only has to be a symbolic sum for motivation

title: Youths need motivation
text: The other is that they get less money if they stay at home, like with a regular employment

title: Youths need motivation
text: However, you can't expect them to live under subsistence level if they have to stay at home because of illness, for example

title: Youths need motivation
text: My opinion is that it is important that the system changes in one way or another so the youths become more motivated to go to their trainee posts

title: Youths need motivation
text: First of all I think it would be good for their health if it was profitable for these youths to go to their trainee posts

title: Youths need motivation
text: Because if they went to work they would meet new people and they would be doing something

title: Youths need motivation
text: This would probably make them feel useful and needed, which would lead to a better self-esteem

title: Youths need motivation
text: This is very good for their health both mentally and physically, since a problem for unemployed might be that the self-esteem gets lower and lower which leads to a feeling of uselessness and maybe even to depression

title: Youths need motivation
text: Second of all I think it is good for moral reasons, because a person who lets other people work and pay taxes to support him (it could also be "her") I would call immoral

title: Youths need motivation
text: That is if he is capable of working and has a job to go to, but refuses only because of laziness

title: Youths need motivation
text: I strongly believe it is wrong to do so

title: Youths need motivation
text: I don't think there is anyone who wants to work and pay a big part of his income as taxes that are used to support someone else who instead of doing his work takes advantage of the system

title: Youths need motivation
text: And I think that these youths that are taking advantage of the system would understand that if they went to work, but as it is now they need motivation

title: Youths need motivation
text: Finally I think that if they took their trainee posts seriously they would get the work experience they need to get a real employment

title: Youths need motivation
text: An argument against trainee posts is that they are only temporary work with meaningless tasks and that there is no time left of the days to search for a real job

title: Youths need motivation
text: But I think it has to be seen from another angle

title: Youths need motivation
text: First of all there are trainee posts with meaningful tasks and the trainees are allowed to search other employment during working hours

title: Youths need motivation
text: And second of all then the youths would get the required work experience and, also, they are still on the work market, which makes it easier to get an employment

title: Youths need motivation
text: Employers often prefer someone who has recently been working instead of someone who has been unemployed and done nothing for a longer period

title: Youths need motivation
text: What I have been trying to say here is that it is very easy to take advantage of the system of income support

title: Youths need motivation
text: I mean it is very easy to think, "why work if I don't have to"

title: Youths need motivation
text: Therefore I think that a change is needed to motivate the youths

title: Youths need motivation
text: A possible way of changing the system is to give the trainees a symbolic sum if they go to their trainee posts and there do what is required of them

title: Youths need motivation
text: Such a change might be possible, even though it costs the society, since there are gains to be made

title: Youths need motivation
text: The youths' mental and physical health would be better, their moral might improve and it would make it easier for them to get a real employment

title: Youths need motivation
text: As I see it, these gains are worth the money it costs

title: Youths need motivation
DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: I do not regret that I voted for EU and not against it but having said that there are many regulations that EU has conceived that I just cannot understand

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: For instance European Union has decided to cease the duty-free sale in the Baltic Sea

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: As far as I am concerned there are not any particular reasons for putting down the most important industry in the Baltic Sea except that this sort of opportunity is said to be unfair for the other EU-countries

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Although mentioned above some drawbacks of EU, I would not have any hesitation to vote for it again but this argumentation is not about EU but the sea trade between Finland and Sweden

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: I have doubts that they have not considered the consequences throughout in Brussels

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: If the duty-free sale finish in 1999 like it has been planned it will be a catastrophe

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: To whom, might one ask, and the answer is to everyone, that is, Swedes and the Finns

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: First of all there has been a lot of emigration from Finland to Sweden and vice versa

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: So for us who has to travel back and forth, these ferries offer us a great possibility to visit our families and friends with a reasonable price

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: There are of course aeroplanes but the difference between the prices is enormous and for example for me flying is absolutely impossible despite the student loan

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: One can ask what has the duty-free sale got anything to do with travelling across the sea

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Well, there is a logical explanation which is that the shipping companies make the profit by selling lots of duty-free products

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Ferries survive because of the duty-free sale opportunity and not because of the ticket sale, tickets cost basically nothing

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: So, what will happen if people will not travel by ferries when losing the possibility to buy cheap items? Unemployment will increase

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Employees lose their jobs, which would be a disaster for approximately 5000 workers

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Shipping companies cannot afford to keep all of their workers if there are no passengers

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: The Baltic sea ferries are popular among the emigrants and immigrants but also among people who just want to relax for few days and cannot afford to travel all the way to Spain

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Taking a cruise is a cheap way to get rid of the normal routine

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Ferries offer lots of entertainment for all age-groups

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: There are for example restaurants, bars and nightclubs

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Passengers can do some shopping, eat well and dance until four in the morning with help of cheap pints and fancy cocktails

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Bearing in mind the employment situation, ferries also offer different sorts of jobs for the musicians and magicians, in one word entertainers

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: The alcohol consuming is quite high in Scandinavia

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Finland and Sweden have their alcohol monopolies and prices are high

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: If EU refuse individual's privilege to buy duty-free alcohol it will lead to a situation that a home distilling will increase worringly

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: Also smuggling will grow which will develope certain problems

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: People will commit crimes first of all by smuggling alcohol and secondly by selling it forward

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: There are not any ways to close down the Baltic Sea trade

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: There are different solutions to keep this duty-free possibility by changing the routes

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: This means that if there is a route from Stockholm to Helsinki, which takes about twelve hours, they will change it in a way that the ferry goes to Tallin first and then to Helsinki

title: DUTY-FREE IS VITAL FOR THE BALTIC SEA
text: In that way people can buy duty-free products because Estonia is not a part of European Union

text: It is about the same thing concerning the study loans

title: Grants instead of loans to go to university
text: You will not be able to borrow the full amount of money if the income has been too high during a year

text: That amount was in 1998 about 55 000 crowns

title: Grants instead of loans to go to university
text: The main problem with taking a loan to finance the studies is that the present system makes it impossible for many students to ever be able to pay back the loan

text: You start to pay back your loan about six months after the finished studies

text: As a consequence of that your loan grows while pay it off

text: A lot of people presumably prefer to start to work after highschool instead of going to university

text: The problem with the loan system is probably one main reason
