text: It is most probable that a lot of people prefer to earn their own money

title: Grants instead of loans to go to university
text: That concerns especially young people who want to get a place of their own to live end become independent from their parents

title: Grants instead of loans to go to university

text: Besides they miss their income during the time they study

text: Certainly, the loan does not have to be paid back in a way that it usually causes people financial troubles

text: But it must certainly be a psychological burden to be indebted and to be aware of that you will probably remain so for the main part of your life

title: Grants instead of loans to go to university
text: Seeing it from another aspect, you can say that a loan is always a loan which has to be paid back

text: It gets more complicated when there is not only the studyloan that has to be paid back, but also other loans

text: The most common is that people take loans to buy an apartment or a house, maybe a car, furniture and other things

text: If then there is also a studyloan which has to be paid off, the financial situation can become very difficult

text: The loans that are given to be able to study are supposed to make it possible for everyone to study

text: But the fact is that a lot of people do not go to university because they simply cannot afford it

text: University students should be given grants, not loans, to finance their studies

We should not eat our friends.
text: To realise something can be very difficult

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Especially when it forces people to change the way they are living

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: It is hard when we decide not to eat our friends

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: But it is the only right decision and I am going to explain why

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: I have been a vegetarian for almost two years

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: I can say I am doing it for many reasons

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: First of all I am a vegetarian for my own sake

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: It is not good for our health to eat too much meat

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Meat contains a lot of fat, which makes it easier to get a heartcondition

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Secondly I am a vegetarian because I care about the environment

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Cattlefood needs strong artificial manuring

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Because of that discharge from factories with animals destroys the subsoilwater and the soil

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: I am also a vegetarian because it is a waste of the world's resources to eat meat

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: A space of land used to produce vegetables can feed at least ten times as many people as if we give the vegetables to the animals and then eat them

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: All these arguments are good ones, especially the last one in this time of overpopulation

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: But it is all about becoming a vegetarian for reasons that in the end, in one way or another, affect us

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: The most obvious reason for becoming a vegetarian is one people tend to ignore

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: It is a truth so obvious it is hard to see

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: We can not eat our friends

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Not because it is not good for our health, bad for the environment or a waste of land, but simply because it is wrong

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Through being a vegetarian we do not participate in killing other creatures for our sake and we also repudiate the horrible conditions most animals live in, in our factories with animals today

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: A life of an animal brought up for the meatindustry today can not possibly be a life worth living

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Most of them live their whole lives in very small areas

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: They are not able to go outside or hardly even turn around in their cages

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: When it is time to kill them it is often made in a brutal way to produce meat as cheap possible

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: People often argue that it is our right to treat animals however we like since we are the most intelligent creatures on earth

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: But does this give us the right to violate and exploit the animals? I have a lot of times been asked what good me being a vegetarian is going to do when almost everyone else eats meat

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: But we have to start somewhere and more and more people mostly young ones, are starting to eat vegetarian-food

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: The more people who give up eating meat the fewer animals need to suffer

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Another argument often heard is that animals do not have feelings

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: A foreign filosopher once said, "the question is not whether they can think, but can they suffer"

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: The point is that we know animals can suffer

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Everyone who has seen a calf part from its mother knows it feels grief

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: He who sees it knows it

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: If people were forced to kill the animals on there own and see their suffering to be able to eat meat, I think a lot of us would resist it

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: But as it is today it is very easy to go into a stoor and by a package of meat without having to reflect on what it is we are really eating

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: We do not have to see the pain in the eyes of the animals we are going to eat and we do not have to know about the conditions they have been living in if we do not want to

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Since we humanbeings can live good lives without eating meat, I can not see other reasons than selfish ones for us continuing to do so

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: There are no more questions to be asked

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Our superior intelligence does not give us the right to make other creatures suffer

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: Not if we don't have to and we don't

title: We should not eat our friends.
text: It is the opposite - we need to let them be

title: We should not eat our friends.
More physical education at school
text: Everybody knows that children are restless creatures

title: More physical education at school
text: When they play their games it is often in a lively manner - they love to run, to jump and to chase each other

title: More physical education at school
text: When children start school though, they enter a sedentary world in which they will have to spend a lot of time for many years

title: More physical education at school
text: For a number of reasons, I would like to emphasise the need for more physical education at the schools today

title: More physical education at school
text: In the last few years this subject has been cut down in the lowest grades as well as in upper secondary school

title: More physical education at school
text: My main arguments will show that we have to look at this subject in a wider perspective

title: More physical education at school
text: In the first place, physical exercise make learning in other subjects easier

title: More physical education at school
text: Secondly, we have to reach the children already at school, to give them a life-long interest for exercising - this for the public health

title: More physical education at school
text: If we give children more physical exercise at school, this would not "only" improve their health - it would also support their learning in other subjects

title: More physical education at school
text: Especially younger children need to develop their motor functions

title: More physical education at school
text: If they get control over their body they will more easily learn skills as reading, writing and mathematics

title: More physical education at school
text: This way to improve other skills is also efficient, because the children will have fun and get self-confidence, instead of maybe losing it in the classroom

title: More physical education at school
text: Still many pupils have performance anxiety and feel bad during the sport lessons, you may say

title: More physical education at school
text: But this is because the subject is based on too much competition and performance

title: More physical education at school
text: I would like to see more exercising that is based on children's needs and more exercising at school in general

title: More physical education at school
text: Furthermore children become more alert and can more easily concentrate if they get some exercise

title: More physical education at school
text: For how long time during a lesson are the pupils really concentrating on their work? Exercising during other lessons than sports could just be the ability for pupils to move around in the classroom

title: More physical education at school
text: To lighten up the ordinary lessons with more creative work as field studies for example, is a good way to get the children more interested and more eager to learn

title: More physical education at school
text: To say that children have recesses when they can play may be right, but how much they exercise then vary from child to child, and when they get older they do not exercise much during the recesses

title: More physical education at school
text: The recesses are also not "real school", because then you are sitting at your desk and have to do as the teacher says

title: More physical education at school
text: In "real school" we need more exercising for children

title: More physical education at school
text: This would improve their learning ability and make the school more creative and fun

title: More physical education at school
text: My second main argument is that the children of today need more exercising to become healthier and stronger and to feel better in mind and body

title: More physical education at school
text: Their interests in the spare time have more and more turned into physical inactive activities

title: More physical education at school
text: Many kids spend many hours a day in front of the television or the computer

title: More physical education at school
text: Children have also been putting on weight and many are lacking in concentration at school

title: More physical education at school
text: Some are totally inactive outside school

title: More physical education at school
text: Therefore we need more physical education at school - to get a lasting interest for exercising and knowledge about a healthier way of life

title: More physical education at school
text: In the long run people would get healthier and simply feel better and the State would save money on the medical service

title: More physical education at school
text: Finally, physical education is socially healthy

title: More physical education at school
text: Athletics and physical exercises make people come together as a team or as a group and they have hopefully fun together and co-operate

title: More physical education at school
text: Most children also think that physical education is fun

title: More physical education at school
text: My conclusion is that we have a lot to win if we add more physical education to the curriculum

title: More physical education at school
text: It is evident that the rising generation would need more exercise as their way of life has changed during the last decades - children sit still too much when they actually need to be out in the fresh air and play

title: More physical education at school
text: We need new thinking at the schools today and a more creative way of learning

title: More physical education at school
text: More physical exercise would in the long run make the pupils healthier and more alert, and their learning ability and self-confidence would improve

title: More physical education at school
The DNA research must be regulated
text: At this very moment scientists, all over the world, are struggling to chart the entire human DNA, in a project called HUGO

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: They hope that they, through this, will find cure to all genetic deceases

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: At the same time other scientists are trying to clone animals, because it would benefit the animal breeding if the best animals could be copied over and over again, and other scientists are manipulating crops

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: The DNA researchers maintain that this is completely harmless and nothing to worry about

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: But is this true? Should we believe them and let them do their work without interference? There are, of course, advantages in DNA research, especially in the field of medicine

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Many severe deceases could probably be avoided or treated with new medicines or methods that could be found with the help of DNA research

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Already today many women who are in greater risk of getting breast cancer can have a DNA test to find out how big the risk is of catching the decease, and whether they should remove their breasts or not

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: This is good, but still there is some very serious dangers about our use of the DNA that we have to pay attention to

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: One hazard is the DNA manipulation of crops

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: The DNA of the crops are manipulated to make the plants get extra big or to make them stand powerful insecticides and weed-killers

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: This brings on a lot of extra insecticides and weed-killers in the fields, which has a negative effect on the enviroment

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: But that is not all

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: All DNA in plants and animals mutate to adjust them to their enviroment

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: This is a very slow process, but nobody knows what happens with a manipulated gen that mutates

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Will it result in an even more powerful plant that out grow other plants and threaten the multiplicity of nature? That is a frightening thought, but it is not unlikely that it happens, since all living things through mutation strive to survive better

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Another kind of DNA manipulation is the new phenomenon cloning

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Not long ago the first example of a DNA manipulated sheep was shown to the world

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: It was Molly, an exact duplicate of another sheep

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: This was a revolution in animal breeding, since it made possible to copy one induvidual's DNA and use it to create another individual with the exact same qualities as the first one

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Now it will be possible to use the most profitable animals over and over again, which, of course, will benefit the farmers

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: But if we only use cloned animals, there will only be a few unique DNA-cromosomes among hundreds of animals, and that will make in-breed very likely

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: That will not be a problem if we steralise all animals and just produce more of them through cloning, but that would not be ethically right

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: We should not pretend that we are some kind of Gods

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: If we also start to clone people, the situation gets absurd

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: And it gets even worse if we, in the future, not just clone people, but also replace the genes that we do not want with better ones

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: Then we might get a society where only people with certain, well adjusted genes are accepted to universities, and where employers check your DNA before they deside whether or not they will hire you

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: That would be a society where your future would almost be determined before you were even born

title: The DNA research must be regulated
text: The conclusion of all this is, that if we do not have a world-wide ethical discussion about DNA, and if we do not get to the conclusion that the use of DNA and its information has to be strictly regulated, we will face a dark future

title: The DNA research must be regulated
Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: In May last year I moved into a student-corridor in Flogsta here in Uppsala after having lived in an apartment for about four months

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: It was quite a big apartment and I had shared it with a friend

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I was used to having a big kitchen and a big living-room for just the two of us, and the possibility to invite lots of friends

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Living in a corridor meant big changes for me

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I would have to share kitchen and living-room with eleven people I didn't know from the beginning

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: If I was unlucky I would get neighbours who stole my food from the refrigerator or used my kitchen-tools

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Now I've lived there for almost a year, and ny now I know that I actually prefer to live like this

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: It suits me perfectly, at least for the moment I would not like to go back to living in an apartment, and now I'm going to tell you why

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: If you live in a corridor with people you like, it's almost like getting a new, big family

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: There is always someone to talk to, if you're feeling lonely you can just go to the kitchen and if there's nobody there right at the moment, they will soon come

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: And if you feel that you want to be alone, you can go to your room and close the door

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: You have a choice that you wouldn't have if you lived alone in an apartment

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I know I wouldn't be able to live in an apartment of my own right now

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I would just sit staring out the window and feel lonely and miserable

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: But in the corridor I've always got some members of my "family" who wants to talk to me

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Of course, I would probably study more if I wasn't tempted to spend so much time in the kitchen, but onthe other hand, if I felt sad and lonely I wouldn't get that much done either

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: What could be a problem, though, is that the people with whom you live know everything about you

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: They know when you leave and when you come home, and if you come home alone

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: There is much gossip in a place like that

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I think veerybody ought to try living this way at some time in their lives

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: You learn a lot about how to get along with other people

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: For me, this is especielly important, since I'm an only child and 'm not used to having to compromise with brothers and sisters all the time

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Now I learn to take my responsibility in another way, for example, I have to clena the kitchen every time I've used it, I can't just leave my dirty plate in the sink, because I know how irritating it is when the others do that

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: If I lived by myself, I wouldn't care that much, because it would only be my own dirty kitchen and nobody would tell me to clean up

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Another thing you learn is of course to share things, and that makes it even more important to wash the dirty saucepans and plates, since someone else will want to use them

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: The way I see it, this kind of living is very good

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I've already mentioned the main arguments, but there are of course others as well

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: If you forget to buy something there is always somebody in the corridor from whom you can borrow it

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: Or if you are ill and can't get out of bed, the others can take care of you and go shopping for you

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: If you live by yourself there is a risk that you can't get anything to eat for days if you're too ill to go out

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I feel that I know quite well what I'm talking about when I say that everybody should live with other people like this for a period in their lives, because I've lived both in a corridor and in a shared apartment, and, before that, all by myself in a very small room when I studied in Paris

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I don't think I can find a better way of living at this period in my life

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I know that I need to be close to other people, or I would be depressed and lonely

title: Corridor or apartment, which is best?
text: I've learned a lot

title: Let's sleep!
text: Some people can be perfectly satisfied with getting only six hours of sleep per night

title: Let's sleep!
text: These people often seem to be feeling good and awake

title: Let's sleep!
text: This, however, surely isn't the case

title: Let's sleep!
text: I believe that when you need a cup of coffee to stay alive or have to pinch yourself to keep your eyes from closing in the middle of a good movie, you're in serious trouble

title: Let's sleep!
text: There are a number of reasons as to why I myself almost always go to bed around ten o'clock in the evening when I know that I have to get up early the next morning

title: Let's sleep!
text: Some of my friends used to have trouble understanding how this habit could make a difference in a person's life

title: Let's sleep!
text: I at the same time didn't really know how to explain it to them until I came across a study that had been done in America

title: Let's sleep!
text: This study showed that a big part of the American population doesn't get enough sleep

title: Let's sleep!
text: This means that most of the American men and women don't spend more than six hours in bed, when they according to this study should be asleep for at least ten hours per night

title: Let's sleep!
text: This is in order to keep away from becoming some kind of zombie who put other people's lives at risk

title: Let's sleep!
text: To me this idea of improving the quality of your own life makes perfect sense

title: Let's sleep!
text: Probably you'll feel the same way after you've read my reasons as to why we all should sleep a whole lot more than we do today

title: Let's sleep!
text: I claim that people who ignore their need of sleep are in fact lethal

title: Let's sleep!
text: This is because when someone is tired and suffers from insomnia there are certain things that this person shouldn't be doing, like driving a car or be responsible for medication and children

title: Let's sleep!
text: Still, this is exactly what's going on everywhere around you all the time

title: Let's sleep!
text: To illustrate this I'll give an example of what could happen on a normal day, around the time when the sun rises

title: Let's sleep!
text: At this time of day it would be possible to spot a little red car on the road

title: Let's sleep!

text: Today it is possible to go shopping in Sweden at practically any hour of the day

text: Many people see this as a positive development, because it is just about always possible to purchase what one needs

text: But while looking a bit further into what the results of this really are, one finds several reasons to change the situation back to how it used to be

title: Shorter Business Hours for Shops!
text: Sweden would gain from this, not only economically but also when it comes to the well-being of the people; those working in the shops as well as those shopping

title: Shorter Business Hours for Shops!
text: The economical aspect is of course an important one, which affects all of us

title: Shorter Business Hours for Shops!
text: When the shops are open longer, people get used to buy what they need at different hours of the day and therefore spread their shopping over a longer period of time

title: Shorter Business Hours for Shops!
text: But they do not necessarily buy much more

Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Do you think that imprisonment is a just and natural penalty for somebody who has committed a felony? Do you think that imprisonment prevents crime and protects the public? You had better reconsider, because this is not the case

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Imprisonment, as punishment in general, is based on certain principles

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: It should, for example, deter people in general from criminality, as well as "cure" criminals, that is, deter them from relapsing

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: It should also protect society from dangerous convicts

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: However, my main argument is that imprisonment serves neither of these purposes

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: I will not discuss the ethical and moral aspects of imprisonment as a punishment, only the practical

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: To begin with, the deterrent effect of imprisonment is not as good as one could wish for

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Of course nobody wants to go to gaol, but still, exemplary punishment does not work satisfactorily, as new crimes are obviously being committed all the time

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: One reason for this is that someone who commits a felony naturally counts on not being caught

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Another reason that can be applied here in Sweden is the remarkably good conditions in the prisons and the fact that most prisoners are released before the stipulated time is at an end

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: I would say that Swedish prisons are the least deterrent ones in the world

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: As to "cure" individual convicts, imprisonment is no great success either

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: When finally released, the former prisoner might actually be revengeful and even more disposed to committing crimes than before

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Another problem is that young criminals in prison are likely to be influenced by older and more experienced prisoners to be more violent and commit more serious crimes

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Being imprisoned, and especially being forced to live under the awfully bad, not to say inhuman, conditions that you can easily find in foreign prisons, might cause you psychological damage

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: It is not unlikely that a prisoner develops a mental disturbance, which of course is a disadvantage to society as a whole as well as to the affected individual

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: In combination with, for instance, a desire for revenge, the prisoner might be a threat to the public when he or she is released

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Besides, even if the former prisoner is entirely sane, readjusting to normal life can be very difficult

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Imprisonment is thus a very destructive punishment

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: But surely it cannot be denied that imprisonment protects the public from dangerous criminals? Yes, it can, because we are only protected against those who are imprisoned for the moment

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Consider for a moment that most of them will eventually be discharged, and add to them all those who commit felonies without being convicted, and the undetected psychopaths that probably exist to some extent in society

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Do you feel secure just because there are prisons? No; on the whole they do not make much difference

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Yet the existence of prisons creates a feeling of security in people, because they can be sure that they will not be subjected to the particular convicts that are imprisoned for the moment

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: They also know that the state takes its responsibility by punishing criminal deeds

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: If people did not have this feeling, however deceitful it is to me, society would face great problems

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: The question is: would the public feel secure and have trust in the authorities if prisons were abolished? I do not think that they could be completely done away with, but they could to a considerable degree be replaced by other penalties

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: So far I have not mentioned the economic aspects of this question, something that in most cases is very important

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Prisons, and again the Swedish ones especially, are expensive to run

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: The state has to provide for the prisoners, and perhaps their families too, by granting social allowances, and also pay for the rehabilitation after the release

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: Imprisonment is thus both ineffective and uneconomical

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: I wish to see more of constructive and inexpensive penalties, such as community service and probation by an electronic shackle, and more specialised treatment of young convicts

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
text: In the long run, society is negatively affected by the existence of prisons

title: Imprisonment - a bad solution
To preserve a language
text: In these times of Anglicisation of languages there are some countries fighting against this development

title: To preserve a language
text: The best known among these are France and Iceland

title: To preserve a language
text: In various ways the people of these nations try to protect their languages against the wave of English and anglicised words

title: To preserve a language
text: I understand the countries defending their languages and I think that what they are doing is right

title: To preserve a language
text: In this essay I will try to show that they are taking a step in the right direction

title: To preserve a language
text: People in other countries think that what the French and the Icelanders are overprotective and too conservative; they say that countries should not be against a natural development of one language - in this case English - becoming a world language

title: To preserve a language
text: But in some countries the opposition to this development creates a very strong nationalism and feeling of togetherness

title: To preserve a language
text: In the case of Iceland, a country with a very small population (only 275, 000 inhabitants), this nationalism is very important to keep the language alive

title: To preserve a language
text: In Reykjavík the government has created a committee charged with making new Icelandic words replace foreign ones

title: To preserve a language
text: France, on the other hand, is not fighting for the existence of its language, but for a top-ranking on the list of world languages

title: To preserve a language
text: This also creates nationalism; a "political nationalism" which in some respects is very extreme in not allowing English words in advertisements and other "public texts"

title: To preserve a language
text: Moreover, the television channels must show French films during at least 40 % of their broadcasting time

title: To preserve a language
text: This may seem very restrictive but I do not think the laws are unusually strict considering the goal: to preserve the language

title: To preserve a language
text: Even though some of these measures taken can be seen as overprotective and too conservative I believe that they are necessary in order to preserve the languages and to avoid words like the half Swedish half English 'shoppa' and 'printa'

title: To preserve a language
text: These half foreign words can, however facilitate the learning of a dominant language like English, because then the learner already has an idea of what the word can mean in the new language

title: To preserve a language
text: But, of course, if being forced to learn both the foreign word and the word from one's own language one gets a broader knowledge of etymology

title: To preserve a language
text: This phenomenon of "invented words" in Icelandic and French can be a help when foreigners learn these languages, because if the learner knows a large number of short common words he is more likely to figure the meaning of longer solid compound words

title: To preserve a language
text: One example of this in Icelandic is the word for 'copying machine' which is 'ljósritari', meaning 'light drawer'

title: To preserve a language
text: In French there is the word for 'snow-plough' which is 'chasse-neige' meaning 'snow chaser'

title: To preserve a language
text: These invented words force serious learners to find out the meaning of three words (ljós, ritari and ljósritari), thus learning three words instead of one

title: To preserve a language
text: Is it worth while for the countries to struggle? I think there are strong arguments for it

title: To preserve a language
text: Iceland not only preserves its ancient language, but is also getting attention abroad for the struggling and its success and in this way spreads the message that it is worth the effort if a country really wants to preserve the originality of its language

title: To preserve a language
text: People in France and the French government's defending against the influx of English - in spite of their perseverance being somewhat more extreme - also gives the wanted result

title: To preserve a language
text: These two peoples concern about their languages will no doubt bear fruit in the future in passing over feeling of pride to coming generations

title: To preserve a language
text: One strong argument for trying to preserve one's language is that so much of the national identity is embedded in the language

title: To preserve a language

HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I have always fancied myself as a broad-minded person ready to accept almost anything in life; so when I was faced with the complex question of whether homosexuals should be allowed to adopt or have children through insemination I realized that I was not able to accept it

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: The questions I want to discuss in my essay are: is society ready to accept homosexuals as parents? How does it affect the children when they are brought up in a family where both parents are of the same sex? According to Swedish law homosexuals are not permitted to adopt children, yet forty thousand children have homosexual (from adoption or through insemination)

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: To be able to adopt in Sweden the person/s/ has to conceal his or her homosexuality which is not fair on the child

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I believe that an adoption has to be approached from the child's angle and not looked upon as a right for the adult, the welfare of the child should always come first

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I do not condemn homosexuals or even question their ability to parenthood, everyone has the right to sexual preference; my main concern however is the psychological wellbeing of the child

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: Society is responsible to provide an environment for the adopted child where he or she has the possibility to develop in the best possible way

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I also think that the psycho- logical development of the child will be put at risk if the parents are homosexuals; even though they would probably provide love, care and show responsibility towards the child

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: However to grow up with homosexuals as parents will be tough on the child since he or she will constantly be confronted with prejudice attitudes towards both male and female homosexuality from other people

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: In my opinion this will be a mental strain on the child

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: Futhermore a child should grow up with the clear understanding that children can only be conceived through a sexual relation between a man and a woman

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: One can argue that would it not be better for a child to be raised in a family with happy, loving and caring homosexual parents rather than in a family of heterosexual parents that are always fighting? Of course the family environment has to be a healthy one; but I am still concerned with the way these children will be treated outside of their homes

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: Homo-sexuality is still not completely accepted in society and these people are often harassed and ridiculed because of their sexual orientation; to let a child be exposed to these prejudiced attitudes all the time must be placing unnecessary emotional strain on the child

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I think the association people have when they see homosexuals is not what they are like as people or parents, but we associate with the actual sexual acts they perform with each other

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: A lot of people find it offensive, vulgar and not normal and this is probably the core of the problem for homosexuals to be treated as other "normal" people in society

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: This could also be one of the main points why society is not ready to accept homosexuals as parents

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: I maintain however that children need both a male and a female rolemodel so they can identify themselves with both sexes

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: It must be very confusing to have either two mothers or two fathers; who is who in the relationship, and who will the child identify him or herself with

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: Iceland is the only country in the world where registered homosexual partners have the ame right as heterosexuals to adopt children

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: So even from a global point of view it seems like the majority of people are not ready to accept homosexuals as parents and I believe it will take a long time to change the attitudes on this subject

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: As I mentioned before this is a very complex issue and one cannot state that homosexuals are not capable of being good parents

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?
text: We live in a world which is full of prejudice and therefore one always has to consider what is best for the child and therefore in the interest of what is best for the children why should we add extra stress to their everyday lives; it might be difficult enough to live with the fact that one has been adopted why then add more emotional strain by having homosexual parents? No we are not yet ready for homo-sexuals as parents if we ever will be

title: HOMOSEXUALS AS PARENTS?

text: Perhaps it is time for some people with conservative attitudes to realise that the computer is a necessity in society of today

Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Before I joined Amnesty International I had a lot of thoughts about the death penalty

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I didn't know whether I was for or against the punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Now I know for sure that I am against the capital punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: My conviction is based on three arguments against death penalty: the state can't show that it's wrong to kill by killing, someone that is not guilty can be convicted and death penalty does not act as a deterrent

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I think that everybody can understand that the relatives of the victim want some sort of revenge on the person who might have killed their family member, but I don't think a state can show its citizens that something is wrong to do by doing exactly the same thing but in the name of the law

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: The Government must think in terms of right and wrong and shouldn't let emotions take over

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: People that is affected by a loss, on the other hand, aren't able to think clearly

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Of course we must understand the feelings those people have but I don't think that the Government should show that they care about their citizens by killing some of them

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: We have to remember that we can't get someone we love back by killing someone else

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I think it's important to stress that people who commit terrible crimes should be punished, but that should be done using other punishments than death penalty for example lifetime punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: One argument you often hear from people who are advocating death penalty is that it is much cheaper to execute a criminal than to keep him or her in prison for life

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: That is not true, though

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: It has been proved that the trial that precede a death penalty is much more expensive than a lifetime punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Even if the trials are very thorough there are still people who are not guilty that get judged

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: In the USA the majority of the prisoners convicted to death are black men who have lived their lives in poverty

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Poor people can't afford the best lawyer and because of that they can be wrongfully convicted

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: The possibility to kill an innocent person is reason enough to be against death penalty

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: The Government can't pay back the loss it has caused the innocent person's family

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: In these cases there is just another victim and no problems has been solved

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: People that advocates death penalty maintain that the capital punishment keeps people away from committing crimes

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: This argument is not true either

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: In countries where they still have death penalty people commit higher amounts of serious crimes than in countries that don't have this punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I think that this fact proves that the capital punishment doesn't work as a deterrent

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I think lifetime punishment is much better than death penalty

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I advocate lifetime punishment because I don't think a victim or its relatives who might have been harassed by a criminal should fear that it could happen again

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I also advocate this kind of punishment because if it appears that the person who is convicted is innocent, he or she will still be alive

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: Even if you can't pay back for years behind bars, the innocent person can live the rest of his life in freedom which you can't let a dead person do

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I think the best thing a society could do is not to scare people away from committing crimes, but to prevent crime

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: As an example this could be done by developing a better and more equal school system

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: This is of course very expensive, but if we put an end to capital punishment I think we could afford it

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: It is not always easy to be against death penalty

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: When you hear about people who rape and kill innocent children it is easy to feel that this person deserves to die

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: I also think it's quite normal to feel this way, but I still don't think the Government can show us what we do wrong by doing exactly the same thing

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
text: There are many more arguments to take a stand against the capital punishment, but for me the three I have presented here are reasons enough to stop this cruel punishment

title: Arguments to take stand against capital punishment
Stub Out!
text: The tobacco industry pleases its consumers by producing approx

title: Stub Out!
text: 5 342 991 000 000 cigarettes for them per day

title: Stub Out!
text: It is not difficult then to imagine what huge amounts of money this gigantic industry turns into cash on its product

title: Stub Out!
text: How can that be, I wonder, when smoking is such an unhealthy and expensive thing to do? How long can it continue like this? Is the fact that tobacco companies are taken to court by individuals for having caused people's illness or even death, a sign of that we somewhere in the future will have a non-smoking world? In today's society, we are very conscious of what we eat

title: Stub Out!
text: To keep your body fit and healthy is of great importance for most people

title: Stub Out!
text: And I think everyone is aware of the risks for diseases related to smoking (both active and passive smoking) and how it affects your health and beauty

title: Stub Out!
text: In spite of this there is yet a message of smoking as accepted and associated with success and glamour

title: Stub Out!
text: Children learn from movies and commercials that it is cool to be a smoker

title: Stub Out!
text: Try smoking is for young people a step into the exciting adult world

title: Stub Out!
text: And of course the advertisers address to this target group

title: Stub Out!
text: In Sweden 80 new smokers per day are claimed, to replace those who have given up smoking or even died from it

title: Stub Out!
text: In the US this number is 5000 per day

title: Stub Out!
text: Then there is the economy aspect of smoking (which is very much connected to the health aspect)

title: Stub Out!
text: It is a very expensive amusement both for the individual and society in large

title: Stub Out!
text: For me as a non-smoker it seems very unnecessary having to count the expense for something that might contribute to shorten your life, in your personal budget

title: Stub Out!
text: The causes of smoking that affect society's economy consequently also affect the economy of people in general

title: Stub Out!
text: Diseases from smoking make people who normally should work, having to be on sickness benefit or even get early retirement pension

title: Stub Out!
text: In the US many states are bringing actions against tobacco companies to get compensation for damages caused by smoking

title: Stub Out!
text: The companies then argue that the individual smoker is responsible for his/her decision and should be aware of the consequences of smoking

title: Stub Out!
text: One can then wonder to what extend the tobacco companies inform their consumers of the risks

title: Stub Out!
text: My third and last argument against smoking is the one that feels most important for myself

title: Stub Out!
text: It is the fact that you involuntary have to spend some time of the day in smoky surroundings almost everyday and often meet a lack of consideration for non-smokers among many smokers

title: Stub Out!
text: The Swedish tobacco law from 1993, says that smoking is forbidden in all places of work, schools and other public buildings

title: Stub Out!
text: (To have special "smoking rooms" is allowed

title: Stub Out!
text: ) I don't think I have noticed any remarkable difference since this law was introduced, though

title: Stub Out!
text: The restaurants' and cafés' non-smoking areas for example, do not often make any sense since you are still in the same room and the smoke doesn't stay in the "smokers' area" just because there is a rule

title: Stub Out!
text: I want to be able to sit in a café without having to bring the nasty smell from smoke with me home in my clothes and hair

title: Stub Out!
text: In many public institutions, for example hospitals, the entrances turn into smoking areas with big stinking ashtrays and when going in you get "welcomed" by the disgusting smell from fag-ends

title: Stub Out!
text: So, what is it then that makes people smoke despite everything, in my view, speaking against it? Perhaps it is a need, a feeling of being something more with that cigarette in the corner of their mouths or/and a lust that is stronger than the question of health and money

title: Stub Out!
text: Whatever it is, it is something that I as a non- (and never-) smoker can't understand it and don't think I ever will

title: Stub Out!

Watching television is not dangerous
text: Recent tragic events involving children's' violent behaviour, such as the death of four-year-old Kevin caused by other children about his age, have once again brought attention to the question whether television is a probable cause to violence or not

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Animated cartoons and various types of action movies are blamed for scaring children and increasing violent behaviour among them

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: It is argued that children not being able to draw a distinction between fiction and reality might copy violence seen on the screen, being unaware of the actual consequences in real life

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Many parents I have spoken to are of the opinion that television indeed affects children in a negative way, and some of them have simply prohibited their children from watching it

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: I strongly object to all these views, believing television is extremely valuable and should not be restricted to adults only

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: To blame television for some children's' violent behaviour is, in my opinion, neither fair nor correct

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Needless to deny, many television programs do contain violence, and I understand some parents concerns

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: It should be pointed out, however, that there are different categories of violence, some more a subject to concerns than others

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: A major worry to parents is the violence in animated cartoons, a worry I argue being unnecessary

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Children watching these cartoons are very much aware of what is real and what is not

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Cartoons are so obviously unrealistic and children very seldom consider them being frightening

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Furthermore, it is even more unlikely that children would copy any of the absurd actions carried out by these fantasy creatures of all shapes and forms

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Compared to animated brutality, violence seen in action movies is of another character though

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: But even if this type is far from as exaggerated as in cartoons, and therefore closer to reality, children of today have great knowledge about filmmaking and accept explanations given about special effects, make-up and artificial blood

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Whether children identify with characters seen on the screen and the fear of that they might act like these in real life, should not be a major subject to worry either

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Naturally children might, and often do, imitate some characters while they are playing, but this identification is not of a more serious kind

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: It should not be confused with an actual belief to be a character

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: When children imitate their favourite super- or action heroes, they know perfectly well that it is only a game, and even though children's games sometimes seem violent to an observer, there is no deliberate wish to hurt a playmate

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Pretending to shoot a friend while playing has nothing to do with actually wanting him dead or hurt in reality, on the contrary; if someone by accident gets hurt while playing, the game almost always comes to its end

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: If the former played conflict becomes a real one, children immediately call each other by real names again, leaving the pretended characters and the game

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Other factors rather than television therefore most often cause real conflicts between children, as the latter inspire to pretended conflicts, not real ones

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Far trickier is it to determine the effect on children that real violence on the screen has, such as it seen on the news

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: This is the violence parents should be concerned about, as it can not be explained as unrealistic or fake

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: I believe this is the kind children might find frightening, realising how awful adults are treating each other for real

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: I do not see, however, any reason for parents to turn off the television when the newscast begins

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Younger children are usually not interested in adult programs like this, they learn early to recognise a "boring adult program" that lack funny voices, bright colours and happy music

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Would an older child show interest in watching the news with his/ her parents, however, I see no reason at all to object

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Apart from being wonderful, the reality we are living in is also very cruel, and I argue it being wrong to pretend otherwise

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: All misery should naturally not be forced on a young person too early and abrupt, but it is important that parents try to answer whatever questions a child might have without glamorise reality too much

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: On the whole, television is a fantastic source from which fantasy is created, playfulness is inspired and experience is made

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Children can both learn and be part of incredible adventures by watching it, and I argue that this opportunity should not be kept out of reach

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Children are capable of handling television to much greater extent than some adults think, and are in addition to that very critical to what they watch

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Any normal child, raised with certain values, has natural limits, which are not to be overstepped

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Violent behaviour is the result of many factors other than television alone

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: What we should be focusing some attention to is in what way our children watch it though

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Children should always have the opportunity to ask an adult about things being unclear or scary

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Television should be watched together, not alone, and should never be used as a babysitter

title: Watching television is not dangerous
text: Having a healthy relation to it, not spending too many hours of our time on it, television is by no means dangerous, but quite good- for adults as well as children

title: Watching television is not dangerous
The choice of geriactic care
text: In Sweden we have chosen to have old-aged care and institutions that look after elderly often ill people

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: But there have not always been like this

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: We can go back to the beginning of the 20th century when we had a large rural population in Sweden

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: People lived close together in families consisting of three generations and the family took care of their members from birth to death

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: When people grew old they were depending on their relatives' goodness

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: There were very few institutions, only poorhouses for elderly and poor people who had no relatives

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: In the middle of the century when people began to move into towns to work for their living, a need of institutions for elderly people rose

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: Families often moved to apartments where there were no place for the elderly generation to come with them

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: The elderly were left back home, and when they could not manage by themselves they must move to institutions

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: Today old people can choose if they want to live at home with help from the geriactic care or in an institution

title: The choice of geriactic care
text: Care of the elderly are in our days questioned because of different alarming reports of bad conditions in institutions and shortage of money

title: The choice of geriactic care

text: That makes the conditions more equal and you do not have to depend on whether you or your relatives can afford it
