THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Since the beginning of time the strong ones has oppressed the weak ones

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It has not always been considered a crime to kill or torture a human being

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: But the time changes and so do mankind

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: This barbarian way became less usual and the killing were considered illegal

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The struggle against capital punishment have been successful but only 100 of the worlds 193 countries have abolished or ceased to carry out the punishment

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Sweden abolished the capital punishment in the year 1921

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: But several surveys in the last couple of years have shocked me and I also believe many others

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Because the surveys show that an increasing number of people, and especially young persons, can consider to reintroduce the capital punishment

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: I think this is terrible and very frightening, and I hope this essay will show you why

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The right to life is the most fundamental part of the Human Rights Agenda

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: To claim the right to life for innocent people is not very difficult

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It goes without saying

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It is considerably harder to defend people with horrible crimes on their consciences; mass-murderers, war criminals, paedophiles etc

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: But that is how universal rights works

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: They applies to everyone, everywhere and during all circumstances

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Argument from the point of view of usefulness, for example that one death of a mass-murderer is better than the future death of ten victims, ignores the most fundamental value - that killing is wrong

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Since killing is wrong, the fact that some States' laws recommend public murders to prevent their citizens to commit murder, seems absurd and full of contradictions

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: How can you show that killing is wrong and try to administer justice by killing? It is totally impossible to achieve justice by killing another person

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: And there is nothing that indicates that the capital punishment prevents criminality

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The crimes of violence follow a curve that is due to social and economical factors at a certain point of time

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It is also independent of if the capital punishment exists or is abolished

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Since the capital punishment was abolished in Canada has for example the brutal crimes decreased

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: But it has increased in the American states where it has been reintroduced

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: In all parts of the world this is a punishment that mostly affects the poor people, minorities and other oppressed or already outcasts of society

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: An execution of a human being is an act that is known to have a brutalising effect

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The capital punishment has therefore a negative influence on the people in the society where it is used

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: If the State consider themselves capable of killing a person in their custody, other people can interpret the killing as something accepted and right in its connection

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: Another argument, which speaks for itself to abolish the capitol punishment, is that every year a number of totally innocent people are executed

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: That is mostly due to meagre and incorrect trials

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: My opinion is that this can never be aloud to happen

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: But it does, obviously, and an abolishment of the capital punishment is the only way to avoid it

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The capital punishment is the ultimate form of torture

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It gives the absolute power to a few men and women to decide if a person will live or not

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: That kind of power can not be accepted in a democracy

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It is the same thing with the right to stop an execution

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: That power often lies in the hands of only one person that don't need to answer for the decision to anyone

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The capital punishment is irreconcilable with the Human Rights

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It is also unnecessary and totally unacceptable in a state governed by law, especially a democracy

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: To speak for abolishing the capital punishment is not to accept cruel crimes

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: It is to say no to deadly violence, even the killing that takes place in the name of the law

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: I think, as I hope this essay have shown, that the capital punishment violates every person's right to live

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: And I think it is an ancient way of punishment, I like to think that mankind has developed

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: The question is if I should have the same view of the matter if somebody would kill a member of my family or one of my close friends

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
text: I hope I will never find out

title: THE CAPITAL PUNISHMENT IS NEVER RIGHT!
Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Introduction The topic I am going to deal with is a very complex one

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: For that reason, there are many facts about the European Union (EU) that I will leave out, and also things that I really haven't got enough knowledge of to be able to say anything about

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: The question in this essay is should Sweden leave the EU? I feel that we should, and therefore I'm going to show a couple of arguments that speak in favour of this

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: There are also some counter-arguments, which I will not get into very deeply, but yet I will touch upon them

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Argumentation One of the greatest disadvantages of the EU, according to me, is that all the decisions that are made in the EU Parliament are harder for the Swedes to influence, than if they were made in the Swedish Parliament

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: The EU ministers have not got as much knowledge of for example Swedish products as we do ourselves, and therefore I think many things should be decided in Sweden instead

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: I believe that some things that the EU Parliament discusses are unimportant issues, which the EU has got nothing to do with

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: One example of this is that the EU wants to control the length of cucumbers, so they all are about the same length

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Another one is that neither the Swedish "snus" (moist snuff) nor "minimjölk" (low-fat milk) was going to be allowed at first, but now I think the EU ministers have changed their minds and that they are going to allow them after all

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Sometimes there has been a conflict, when Swedish environmental legislation has been more restrictive than the EU allows

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: I think it should be encouraged to be restrictive in that sense

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: It really is awful that they want us to ease our rules

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: These and other issues that concern Sweden are important for us to decide upon ourselves

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Of course, some general rules about environmental policy and other issues concerning the whole of Europe are good to have

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: But when it comes to, what I think, silly cucumber rules, and more local matters, the EU should not be able to legislate and regulate everything

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: A counter-argument to this might be that if we leave the EU, we would not be a part of the European Parliament, and thereby it would be more difficult to have an influence on the other European countries' policies

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: But I still think that according to what has been said above, we would benefit from leaving the EU on the whole

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: That was about democracy in decision-making

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Another thing to bring up is commerce

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Before Sweden joined the EU, some people meant that we had to join, otherwise we would be excluded from all the trading in Europe

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: The reason for their concern was that the EU is surrounded by a "wall" with tolls, which means that to buy goods from EU countries is very expensive because of taxes, while there is a duty-free zone inside the "walls", and therefore inexpensive to trade with other EU countries

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: Our export and import would, according to them, definitely benefit from a membership

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: The question I am asking myself is what about the rest of the world? Are the European countries the only countries we trade with? No, I don't think so, but some products might be produced only in Europe and in that case these would of course be more expensive if we left the EU

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: But other ones could perhaps be bought quite cheap from Asia, South America or North America, and then, why be so worried? Conclusion As I wrote in the introduction, I believe that Sweden could manage quite well without being a member of the EU, a membership that costs us too much money, I think

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: If we left the EU, we would be allowed to make our own decisions and set our own rules

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: In Sweden, we strive for that the decisions should be made as near the people as possible, but when becoming a member state of the EU, that goal was obviously forgotten

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
text: We should not have joined the union in the first place, but since we have, our goal should now be to leave it

title: Sweden in the EU - or not?
With rights come obligations
text: Swedish society today is facing a big problem with a rather high rate of unemployment, especially among youths, combined with a poor public sector

title: With rights come obligations
text: Therefore I think society should require people who get unemployment benefit to do unpaid community work in the public sector

title: With rights come obligations
text: There are a lot of lonely people or people in need of some sort of support they can no longer get from the society, as well as there are a lot of unemployed people in need of something new in their lives

title: With rights come obligations
text: So for this reason and for others, as I will try to argue for in this essay, this solution would be in the best interest of both society and the unemployed

title: With rights come obligations
text: In the climate of Swedish society today, with the cut-backs and urbanisation for example, the safety net that used to exist no longer does

title: With rights come obligations
text: In the small villages and the large families people looked out for one another in a different way

title: With rights come obligations
text: Today there are many lonely elderly or sick people, and many children in need of extra attention, that would benefit a lot from something as simple as company

title: With rights come obligations
text: The unemployed have the time to make a difference in these peoples lives

title: With rights come obligations
text: I do not mean that they should do the work of nurses, trained home help or teachers, but simply to be a friend and companion

title: With rights come obligations
text: One might argue that this would take real jobs of the market, but this is not my intention at all

title: With rights come obligations
text: We have to face the fact that cut-backs have been made and more will have to come

title: With rights come obligations
text: I do not want the unemployed to replace the professionals, but it could be a way to make the cut-backs in the public sector somewhat easier on the people affected

title: With rights come obligations
text: The unemployed would, when possible, be a company, read the newspaper or talk with the elderly, or be some kind of special friend to children who needs them

title: With rights come obligations
text: To be a friend requires a certain personal chemistry, they have to get along and I realise that not all are suited to work with people, but these are just examples of ways of doing unpaid community work

title: With rights come obligations
text: And there are a lot of people who are suited to work with people, and it might be stimulating for them to meet someone from another generation

title: With rights come obligations
text: Perhaps it would even give them a different view on their role in society

title: With rights come obligations
text: At the least it would give them daily routines, a reason to get up in the morning, and it would probably be a merit when they apply for a job

title: With rights come obligations
text: A part of the problem of youth unemployment today is that the young are never really let in to the labour market, they can neither get the experience or qualifications needed, and this would improve their CV for future job seeking

title: With rights come obligations
text: The argument "To get a different view on their role in society" might sound a bit naive and grandiose, but I will try to explain why I brought this up

title: With rights come obligations
text: I see a rather scary problem today in that people, especially of my generation, doesn't recognise our obligations to other people, or to the society

title: With rights come obligations
text: We are very clear on our rights, the right to good education, healthcare, elderly care, unemployment benefits and social allowance, but the thought of earning these rights never crosses our minds

title: With rights come obligations
text: What this comes of would take another essay to sort out, but I think it is time to change this attitude and to accept that with rights come obligations

title: With rights come obligations
text: We have to realise that it is up to us to do something about the problems we are facing, we can't blame the politicians for not fixing the problem when we aren't doing anything ourselves

title: With rights come obligations
text: One way of contributing I think would be for the unemployed to do some unpaid community work they feel that they are suited for

title: With rights come obligations
text: To do unpaid community work of the kind I mentioned earlier, to be a companion for an elderly person would serve the attitude change by giving new ideas about values in life and lead to personal development, when the right personal combination is made

title: With rights come obligations
text: The same thing of course could happen when getting to know and taking care of a child

title: With rights come obligations
text: To require recipients of unemployment benefits to do unpaid community work would be one way of changing the attitudes and to get us all to realise what being a part of a society means, and to ask ourselves how we can contribute

title: With rights come obligations
text: So in conclusion I want to say that if the right personal combinations are maid and if the right kind of work is found this suggestion is in the best interest of both the unemployed and of society

title: With rights come obligations
Effects of writing
text: The two main effects of writing - perpetuation of thoughts, and understanding of the topics involved - (although I know that they intertwine) are among the reasons why education in writing in the Swedish schools should be improved, even at the possible decrease of education in certain other topics - the latter being a cost that is controversial, but nontheless necessary, and, as I will show, wise

title: Effects of writing
text: The perhaps most tangible effect of writing is that it has longer duration than speach

title: Effects of writing
text: Somehow one would therefore draw the conclusion that which is being given longer life should be more important, and many pupils do write more carefully than they speak, but a considerate number of them do not make the extra effort because they want to, but because they need to

title: Effects of writing
text: They do not have the means to write what they want in the speed that they would want

title: Effects of writing
text: They lack methods, techiques and tricks of the trade

title: Effects of writing
text: The writing becomes an approximation, much like the usage of a second language differs from a mother tongue

title: Effects of writing
text: Approximations are never quite satisfying, especially if they are made in areas of creativity

title: Effects of writing
text: But the speed and the accuracy are not the only reasons to why this is an important issue

title: Effects of writing
text: It is also a matter of fairness

title: Effects of writing
text: You would not a music teacher teach who cannot sing or play the piano, and therefore it stands to reason that teachers with the task of teaching pupils to write should be able to write

title: Effects of writing
text: This is not the case, however, when most teachers are concerned

title: Effects of writing
text: The result of this is that it is not uncommon for teachers to kill creativity in writing, because of lack of interest and experience

title: Effects of writing
text: I admit that there is less education in music, which perhaps makes it easier to find experienced teachers, but on the other hand, that merely means that the emphasis is on writing

title: Effects of writing
text: The more hours set aside for a subject in the curriculum, the more important is the subject, obviously

title: Effects of writing
text: Writing is considered more fundamental than other subjects, and necessarily so, since our culture is based on writing

title: Effects of writing
text: Every other subject depends on the understanding of writing, and that is why there is an apparant need for more qualified teachers and for more attention to this matter at the school authorities

title: Effects of writing
text: The other main effect of writing is that it solidifies the understanding of the subject

title: Effects of writing
text: Writing is in many ways similar to mirrors

title: Effects of writing
text: They can both be used to discover dirt on ourselves, without the assistance of others, and they can both be used as tools to examine things outside ourselves

title: Effects of writing
text: The former function is about understanding oneself, which is a highly complicated matter, and notions about oneself often do not get their right proportions until they are written down, next to several others

title: Effects of writing
text: This is, for example, what diaries and logs are used for, but also many a story

title: Effects of writing
text: I do think that most people could benefit from writing more, but that habit do not come naturally, and here teachers are somewhat responsible, but they need support and training

title: Effects of writing
text: The latter function, in keeping with the mirror metaphore, can be thought of as a periscope

title: Effects of writing
text: In some cases, writing can be used to understand a subject, as it permits and forces you to look at the topic at hand from different perspectives

title: Effects of writing
text: I notice regularly, for example, that I do not quite grasp the subject completely before I write about it

title: Effects of writing
text: This is probably due to the fact that an idea can be judged more clearly from a little distance

title: Effects of writing
text: Sometimes it is only when you work with a subject, and, for example, write about it, that you discover what kind of information you need

title: Effects of writing
text: This experience is as important, if not more so, than being able to do long divisions

title: Effects of writing
text: This is perhaps more of a change in focus than a set of new ideas

title: Effects of writing
text: It can sound as revolutionary changes, taking a lot of time and effort, but these are sooner small but important changes, for society, the school system and each individual pupil

title: Effects of writing
Take a decision now.
text: In this essay I am going to talk about the position of the European Highway four (E4) in Uppland, and especially in Uppsala

title: Take a decision now.
text: There has been a discussion since the 1960s about moving the highway out of Uppsala and to make a new construction of the road section between Mehedeby and Uppsala

title: Take a decision now.
text: Everybody seems to be agreed that something has to be done but it has not been possible to reach a decision about where to place the new road section

title: Take a decision now.
text: This discussion has to come to an end

title: Take a decision now.
text: My suggestion is that we shall have a referendum in Uppsala County in this issue

title: Take a decision now.
text: In that way the will of the people who are concerned will be showed

title: Take a decision now.
text: In this essay I will concentrate on what I think is the main reasons to get a decision now and why I think so

title: Take a decision now.
text: The main reason of why the highway shall be moved is that people suffers

title: Take a decision now.
text: By that I mean both the people who lives along the highway and those who are involved in all the accidents which happens in this road

title: Take a decision now.
text: The highway in its present condition has a very high rate of accidents

title: Take a decision now.
text: Its standard does not correspond in any way to the large number of vehicles using it

title: Take a decision now.
text: The highway passes through several communities besides Uppsala with reduced passable and large problems of road safety

title: Take a decision now.
text: The length through Uppsala County may today be seen as a bottleneck in its connection between the north of Sweden and the area of Uppsala-Stockholm

title: Take a decision now.
text: This bottleneck often leads to dangerous and irresponsible driving with accidents as a result

title: Take a decision now.
text: These accidents cost a great deal, both in human suffering and purely economic

title: Take a decision now.
text: The costs are another main reason to why I see it as an urgent issue to take a decision now

title: Take a decision now.
text: One can wonder how much all these discussions, investigations and delaying has cost our community

title: Take a decision now.
text: One of the alternatives of a new road construct is the eastern proposition; it would be safer and less expensive than the western proposition

title: Take a decision now.
text: To me the eastern alternative sounds like a natural choice

title: Take a decision now.
text: Other arguments in this discussion are that the new highway in some alternatives is going to disturb eradicate threatened animals and that roadwork is going to take place in one of the greatest areas of ancient remains in Sweden

title: Take a decision now.
text: I do understand these arguments but to me human suffering and safety are more important

title: Take a decision now.
text: One other result of the accidents on the highway is that the police have to watch over the distances between vehicles

title: Take a decision now.
text: This is also a cost and perhaps the police are better needed some other place

title: Take a decision now.
text: This has also become a cost to the whole country now, and that is just because our local politicians cannot decide by themselves where to construct the new highway

title: Take a decision now.
text: This issue is now a case for the government and the Swedish national road administration

title: Take a decision now.
text: This is unfortunate for us in Uppsala County, because these authorities work without any special consideration to our County's wishes

title: Take a decision now.
text: This is the reason to why I suggest we shall have a chance to tell our wishes in a referendum

title: Take a decision now.
text: It may also decline the contempt of politicians that is so common today

title: Take a decision now.
text: In conclusion I would like to say that we have a great deal to win by take a decision now, so we can go through with the construction of a new highway as soon as possible

title: Take a decision now.
text: We need a new road to be able to securely and simply transport goods and people through Uppsala

title: Take a decision now.
text: This new road is going to cost a great deal of money, so it would be stupid to spent any more money on further investigations

title: Take a decision now.
text: Let the people say their wishes

title: Take a decision now.
text: Over thirty years of debate is more than enough

title: Take a decision now.
text: Let us take a decision now

title: Take a decision now.
Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Today the unemployment rate in Sweden is very high

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: This means that we have a great manpower to derive advantage from

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I think that the unemployment benefit should be used as a sort of wage for jobless people working in the public sector

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: It is a very important step in the struggle against unemployment and on the whole in the improvement of the economy

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: First of all there is work to be done in the public sector, in some areas more than other's is

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: It seems to me that the greatest need is in the social sector, the eldercare is understaffed as well is education and the medical service

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: This is a major problem in Swedish society that has become aggravated in the last couple of years

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: When the budget is too low workers is being dismissed and left with unemployment benefit

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Still the need of workforce in these services remains

text: Off course one of the difficulties is that "free" work done by unemployed would enter in to competition with employed manpower

text: I suggest that the unemployment benefit should be seen as a kind of salary when the work is within the public sector

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: An other part of the subject I find substantial is the enhance of the jobless self-image

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The more time a person stay unoccupied the less is the chance of re-employment and obviously this doesn't promote growth

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Workers that have been left without a job need to do something meaningful to maintain their self-esteem

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Having permanent work has always been important, not only to get paid but to be accepted in the community

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: To loose that part of your life might lower your belief in yourself to that extent you don't dare to seek another occupancy

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I really don't think that a person can feel satisfaction doing nothing but eat and sleep

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: We are all humans who need a bit of a challenge and some appreciation each day to survive

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: This you achieve in your work

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Maybe some people wouldn't agree with me and say the authorities shouldn't force you to a certain job

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: That is very true, but my opinion is that if you are not willing to offer your help you should get less compensation

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: One of the problems today is that some people for economical reasons rather stay at home unemployed than take a low paid job

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: To receive benefits shouldn't pay off or is an alternative to employment

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: It should only be a short-time solution

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The solution I am talking about is till the unemployed himself is strong enough to find a regular job

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Maybe a person that has been left without a job for a long time doesn't see an end to it

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: If that same person is offered work he could actually get his own ideas of future occupation

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Trying new areas to work in can lead to an interest in a complete different branch

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: It can also be a good way to make new useful contacts

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: Little by little the former unemployed perhaps could start his own firm or get hired by a company

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: That would be the main goal of this proposal

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I feel strongly for free enterprising and think that it is most important that we act for it

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: My reasoning can seem contradictory when I propagate private business and at the same time wish for work in the public sector as an exchange for benefits

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The explanation of this is that I don't think everybody has the courage to start an own business right after being dismissed, nor do they have the means and knowledge

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I want the exchange of work and benefit to be a push in the right direction and to maintain an available work force

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The reason I first discussed was suffer from being understaffed in the public sector that could be helped by the work of the jobless

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The next motive I mentioned was about the self-esteem and how it could be improved with work

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: The last argument is that of "free" work being a short-time solution and that this would inspire the unemployed to a real work

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I want you to keep in mind that this is a force we have to use, not only for the common good but also for each unemployed individual

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
text: I believe a working man is a happier man

title: Work in return of unemployment benefit
THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: In January 1995 Sweden joined the EU despite the fact that the result from the popular vote the previous year showed a very weak majority with 51% of the population for joining and 48% against

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Joining the EU was a big and important decision and with as many as 48% of the population voting against it, one can wonder why the decision-makers did not wait a year or two to make that decision, during which time they could have arranged another popular vote which might have showed a stronger majority in either direction

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: I could go on criticising the decision they made and also the lack of information before the popular vote and the way in which it was carried out, but I will not go further into that because Sweden has indeed already joined the EU and my primary object here is to elucidate why Sweden should leave the EU

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Certain advantages have been gained from joining the EU, admittedly, but when weighing the pros and cons, the advantages do not make up for all drawbacks and disadvantageous responsibilities

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: The opportunity to work or study in another European country sounds tempting for a lot of Swedish people and is one of the few truly positive things about the EU, but the truth is that we did not have to join to gain that opportunity

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Sweden had already signed the EEC agreement which allowed capital, people, goods and labour to move freely into and within the EU

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: The EEC agreement basically gave us all the opportunities and advantages we need and want, while joining the EU meant that we now have to pay good money for these benefits and that the politicians down in Brussels can tell us how to run our own country

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Sweden is one of the smallest countries in Europe when considering the size of the population, which means that we do not have a lot of power in the European parliament with only 22 seats compared to, for instance, Germany's 99 seats

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Consequently, politicians from other European union countries can make decisions we disagree with, decisions that could affect us greatly

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: At the same time we are one of the three countries which pay the highest membership fees

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: What do we get for that money? Not much

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: The sad truth is in fact that Sweden do not get many benefits compared to other European union countries

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: We give a lot, we get little and we are not even allowed to apply our own laws

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Sweden is known to have strict laws and regulations regarding everything from additives in food to drugs and weapons

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: This is changing and there is nothing we can do about it

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Since we joined the EU our domestic law is subordinate to EU law which in most cases is a lot more liberal

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: For instance, we no longer have the right to demand exhaustive declarations of contents when it comes to food, make-up, shampoo or even paint and other chemical goods

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Our customs supervision has become almost non-existent since the customs officers' right to check passports and luggage has been ridiculously restricted, and this has indeed led to an increase in smuggling, concerning both drugs and weapons

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: It is a brilliant idea to make it easier for people and companies to move across the borders, but why do we have to have the same laws and regulations regarding everything, even within the culinary area? Every single strawberry in Europe has to attain the standard size before it can be sold, every meatball and sausage has to contain a certain amount of meat to deserve its name, and Sweden has to have the genetically manipulated Belgian Blue-meat for sale in supermarkets across the country

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Personally, I have never understood this never ending striving for uniformity within the EU

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Is not that a part of the charm about going abroad, to meet new cultures and traditions, new ways of dealing with things? There is absolutely nothing wrong with collaboration but this EU hysteria for standardisation has gone to far and soon we are even supposed to have the same currency

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Luckily Sweden has not signed that deal yet because it would probably be more difficult to leave the EU if we had done so

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: As it is now there is still time to leave and it is my opinion that Sweden should leave the EU as soon as possible

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
text: Some people argue that it would cost too much to leave because of some absurd penalty fee, but how much does it not cost us now and how much will it not continue to cost us if we stay? We should leave now and spend that money trying to reconstruct our once so prosperous Sweden

title: THERE IS STILL TIME TO LEAVE THE EU
What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: Sweden is, in my opinion a fantastic country

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: We have everything, the best sex equality, legal right of access to private land, freedom for the people and we have a democracy that works

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: We are raised in a free country were everybody is equal

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: We should be very proud of all this things

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: The Swedes are a happy people

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: But there is one thing that is not right with this picture

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy? Here in Sweden we tell our children that all people, irrespective of skin colour, what their parents do for a living, if they are handicapped or not, are equal

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: No one is better than the other

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: What message do we send to our children when it is obvious that the Royal children are smarter than the rest of the children in Sweden? The whole Royal Family is better and has a greater value than an ordinary Swedish family

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?

title: What does the Royal Family has to do with democracy?
text: The Royal Family of Sweden is a relic from the past

text: Since Sweden is a free, modern and equal country should not we be able to decide who we want to represent the country? It is us, the people the King and the Royal Family are representing

text: I am certain that a lot of people would agree with me if I say that we do not need this kind of representation

text: But isn't that all we get out of them in a modern society of today? To be honest, is the Royal Family of Sweden really that necessary?

title: Knowledge costs
text: I will here deal with some of the main arguments why the government should give the university students grants, not loans, to finance their studies

title: Knowledge costs
text: In the modern world, knowledge and education have become keywords

title: Knowledge costs
text: Nowadays we are exporting knowledge as merchandise and each individual country grows with its proficiency of the people

title: Knowledge costs
text: Each man grows intellectual with knowledge

title: Knowledge costs
text: It has become more and more important to be able to communicate with other countries

title: Knowledge costs
text: We are getting closer to people with different cultures and traditions, and to be able to live nearer each other we must understand each other

title: Knowledge costs
text: This can only be achieved throw studying

title: Knowledge costs
text: The medical science are helping people to prevent diseases and sufferings

title: Knowledge costs
text: For the science and for normal living we are using a lot of technical facilities as computers, robots, which needs maintenance by people

title: Knowledge costs
text: This needs knowledge and education

title: Knowledge costs
text: Some people may argue that we could use the robots even for the keeping up, including keeping up themselves, and perhaps also as inventors

title: Knowledge costs
text: In a world like that, the robots are those in authority and not people

title: Knowledge costs
text: Education is expensive

title: Knowledge costs
text: Teachers are needed as equipment and buildings

title: Knowledge costs
text: For all this student don't have to pay

title: Knowledge costs
text: But they have to pay for textbooks, living, a place to stay at and food

title: Knowledge costs
text: The system today offers loan to finance the expenses including a symbolic amount of grant, in all a sum of money that is regarded less than low-income earner

title: Knowledge costs
text: The study loan is more favourable than ordinary loans, but often the debt will grow during the greater part of working life and some people may never be able to repay their debt, especially low-income earner and women

title: Knowledge costs
text: Choosing to study you know that you have to live in fixed economical limits with no possibility to improve your economical situation

title: Knowledge costs
text: In the current system you mustn't make more money than the government have decided that a student should manage with without cut downs

title: Knowledge costs
text: This will influence people with less money to avoid or study as little as possible and it will be of great differences between those who could live at their parent's home with free food and those who have to move to an other city because of their studies

title: Knowledge costs
text: As university studies aren't compulsory you may chose to work instead, to make money and to have a normal living

title: Knowledge costs
text: The system with free education was made to give everyone a chance to study at a university despite of person's background

title: Knowledge costs
text: The intelligence, not money, should determine whether to study at a university or not

title: Knowledge costs
text: But investigations have shown that in spite of free education most of the people who study are those from academic families with a tradition of studying and not working-class people

title: Knowledge costs
text: This system, though based on a compulsory comprehensive schools where all kind of pupils are mixed, won't make intelligent people to continue studying but instead just makes everyone equally bad

title: Knowledge costs
text: More over, studying in Sweden is regarded as a personal development, which means that educated people shouldn't make more money than uneducated people

title: Knowledge costs
text: Further investigations have shown that a person who chooses to work directly after the compulsory school will earn more money during his lifetime than an educated person who in addition has contracted a huge dept

title: Knowledge costs
text: To choose to study you mustn't be afraid of getting into debt

title: Knowledge costs
text: A system with grants instead of loans for university studies would encourage more people to study, which is beneficial for the country

title: Knowledge costs
text: To make progresses for the society you need every intelligent people you can possible find

title: Knowledge costs
text: A decrease in education differences between working-class and people with tradition of studying or people who can afford education would also be achieved

title: Knowledge costs
text: Such system would cost very much for the country, but as an investment the country will get great dividend of knowledge and progress

title: Knowledge costs
text: This investment assume that every educated person will stay in the country

title: Knowledge costs
text: Unfortunately there is a growing trend of high educated people moving abroad

title: Knowledge costs
text: This is a great problem which has to be dealt with

title: Knowledge costs
text: But despite of this, the opportunity to develop intellectual through studying mustn't be limited to some few people but has to be intended for everybody where not money or the fear of getting into debt are limiting

title: Knowledge costs
text: Therefore students at university should be given grants, not loans, to finance their studies

title: Knowledge costs
Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Since the beginning of time one of human beings most important tasks has been to propa-gate their species and bring the descendants up to strong and intelligent young people

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: In my opinion the latter is still one of our main purposes of life and therefore I find it strange that only a few people think that their mission of life is to bring children up professionally as teachers

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Even more strange is that so many are reluctant to work with education

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: I am of opposit opinion and have always known I wanted to teach

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Notwithstanding that firm conviction I often find myself arguing for teaching, and defending my choice of a profes-sion to people around me

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: This is what I will do in this essay as well

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: These peoples first argument always tend to deal with money

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: They find it incredible that anyone with a common sense would study four years at a University for a salary of 15 000 Swedish crowns and with small chances of a great increase of wages

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: This argu-ment both puzzles and alarms me

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: It puzzles me because everyone ought to be aware of the present debate on teachers' wages which sooner or later must lead to the better

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: It alarms me because I think it is awkward that acquaintances of mine are that materialistic and think money is of such importance that they would rather have a boring well-paid job than an interesting less well-paid one, given that they can not have both

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Naturally, eco-nomy is an important aspect of life but such is work, since you generally spend about half of your awake hours working

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: That sums up to quite some hours in a lifetime and therefore I prefer a vocation I really enjoy

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Next counterargument is often that the school has an extremely hectical atmosphere, with pupils behaving like hooligans, which can give anyone a nervous breakdown in a few weeks

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Admittedly such a scene is not impossible but I am inclined to believe that such a situation depends mainly on the teacher

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: If the teacher loves children, enjoy being with them and is experienced as well as prepared of some children being awfully mischie-vous it should not occur

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Personally, I think dealing with all these different unpredictable young individuals is exciting and a charming part of the profession

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Likewise, getting through the barricades and reaching the real restive and disorderly ones is a satisfactory kick without equivalence

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Even though not always feeling satisfied with classroom performance, a teacher always feel needed which is a most important sense

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: While others are tearing their hair because of inability to change what is wrong in society, a teacher can actually do something about the so-called decadence of human morals and society

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: The mere becoming a teacher actu-ally means doing something valuable for the society because of the scarcity of teachers

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Until the year of 2000, 62 000 new full-time teachers must be employed in Sweden

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: 10 000 of these will not be educated

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: That is to say, there will not be a problem finding a job because of the many vacancies and the fact that there is a school - which implies a potential place of work for a teacher - in every inhabited region

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Being attractive on the labour-market, is a reason as good as any to become a teacher, but from my point of view the nonmonotonous and flexible method of working is of greater importance

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: I am aware of that being a teacher includes working long hours in a stressful and loud environment, but I am convinced that it will never get tedious and dull

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: As a teacher every single day is a new challange, one never know what will happen during the day and always need a plan B and C up one's sleeve

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: In conclusion I find numerous reasons for becomimg a teacher, but some people consider them only three: June, July and August

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Undoubtly those are tempting, but the main reason for anyone to become a teacher ought to be the possibility to work with children, follow their development and bring them up to strong and intelligent account-able citizens

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: Teaching is hard work and will not turn anyone economically rich over-night, but I am sure it is a very enriching vocation when it comes to quality of life

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
text: For-tunately, one can always educate to be a teacher and then marry rich

title: Enrich life - become a teacher
BREAKING THE LAW
text: In 1994 I became a member of Amnesty International

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: One of their basic principles is that they are an opponent of death penalty

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: I do agree with their opinion because death penalty is an unfair way to punish people

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Firstly the executed person could have been innocent

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Secondly the law says it is prohibit to perpetrate murder but the law itself is allowed to execute a person

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Finally there are several alternatives to death penalty which are more humane

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: To begin with the perpetrator -what if the one is not guilty? Imagine that a person has been convicted and killed and then it turns out that the person who was sent to death was innocent

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: At this point it is useless to apologise

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: On the other hand, the victim and the relatives would probably want to see the perpetrator dead

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Imagine that a member of your family has been killed, would you not want the murderer killed? Undoubtedly this is a very complex discussion

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Thus there should be a consequent law

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Moreover, the law is not consequent when it comes to capital punishment

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Though the law prohibit a person to perpetrate a murder the law should not be able to murder someone either

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In other words capital punishment is a contradiction against itself

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In some areas of Asia there have been cases when people have been executed without legal proceedings

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: A person who just is suspect to be a criminal could be executed

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: I think that the law should be responsible for the decision of whom is or is not guilty

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: And also all individuals should have the right to investigate their case in court

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Unfortunately the court could not tolerate criminality, it must be emphasised what is right or wrong

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: But a person who have perpetrate a crime once is easily capable to do it a second time

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In addition to this an execution of the perpetrator would be a solution and even a lifetime in prison would prevent this

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: To execute a criminal is wrong, its just like taking revenge, but to send the guilty person to prison for a lifetime is more logical

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In my opinion the criminals deserve lifetime penalties though they have destroyed another person's life or maybe many person's lives

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: On the contrary some people say that lifetime penalty is not severe enough

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: They say that death penalty should frighten people to avoid criminality

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: But statistics show that there are not less crimes in the areas were there have capital punishment

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Other people make propaganda for death penalty based on economical reasons

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: They say it is cheaper to execute a person than to send the one to prison

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In general this is not truth

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Naturally this depends on what kind of executions are chosen

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: However, I do not think it is the right way to see this from an economical point of view

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: It is a more complex problem than that

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: In conclusion I will stress that death penalty should not be acceptable of any reasons

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: That is an opinion I share with many people

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Once I have had an argumentation with a friend of mine who is a relative to a raped person

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: I did realise that my arguments could not even be compared to my friend's arguments full of anger and revenge against the perpetrator

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: I really do understand my friend

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Luckily the attacked person survived but she is hurt though and probable she wont get over it -ever

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: But somehow to create justice there must be a fair law that everyone should follow

title: BREAKING THE LAW
text: Think of it in this way, if someone breaks the law the one should of course get punished, but if the person gets executed one could say that the law is breaking the law itself

title: BREAKING THE LAW
SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: All over the world there is a discussion about narcotic politics

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: On one side there arethose who suggest a liberalisation or even a legalisation of the "soft" narcotics (marijuana and haschish), while people on the other side wants restrictive narcotic politics

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: I agree with the ones who believe in restrictive and preventive narcotic politics, and don't believe in a legalisation of drugs, not even "soft drugs"

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: In this essay, I'll take up some of my main arguments againstlegalisation of "soft drugs"

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: First of all I would like to tear down one argument, used by advocators of legalising "soft drugs"

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: They claim that "soft drugs" are harmless, but it's proved that even small dozes of for example haschish hurt the brain and use of "soft drugs" also lead to further experimentation on other drugs

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: They are often an entrance to heavier drugs

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Secondly, people who want a legalisation of the "soft drugs" can't answer questions on how they are planning to carry this out

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: For example I would like to know by whom the quality of the drugs will be checked, and who'll decide the strength and price of them? If drugs from society are lighter, and perhaps also more expensive than the ones on the street, no one will buy them

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Other questions which the followers of legalising don't have any answers to are for example if the State will have monopoly of drugbusiness or if private corporations will have access to it as well

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: And have they planned to have agelimits, or restrictions for people with sensitive and important jobs like drivers, doctors, teachers and pilots? How much and how often should you be able to buy drugs? Will "drug-shops" be open twenty-four-hour a day? If not, people who want drugs will buy them on the streets anyway

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: I haven't received any answers on these questions, but spokesmen for legalisation maybe have planned to release "soft drugs" without any regulations at all, because regulations only create conditions for a black market

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Besides that the campaign to legalise "soft drugs" gives non-satisfaction answers, or often lack out of answers on how they plan to carry out their ideas, there are more reasons against legalisation of the "soft drugs"

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: One of these reasons is that there's a connection between access to narcotics and use of them

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: If society decides to legalise "soft drugs" it becomes easier for people to buy them, and thereby they might end up in an abuse, because of the increased access to "soft drugs"

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: This also effects the connection between supply and demand of these drugs

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: If there's a combination of easy access to large amount of "soft drugs" with liberal attitude from society about drugs, more persons will try the legal drugs, also those who wouldn't have tried if drugs had been illegal

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: This lead to higher demand and suddenly "soft drugs" have created a legal market with supply and demand

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: To decrease the number of (ab)users there must be a small access to narcotics and difficult to get hold of them

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: If there also are unpleasant consequences for using or dealing with drugs the amount of people who is taking them won't increase in the same way as if "soft drugs" were legalised

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Left is only a group that run the risk of getting into drugabuse from the beginning

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: This group can society reach through support and treatment

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Only then abuse can be defeated

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Society must take responsibility for its citizens

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Abuse have to be seen in a social relation, not in a medical one

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Society must prevent that young people gets into drugabuse, by preventive work, by not legalise drugs and by helping addicts through treatment

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: We must believe in society without narcotics, and not let "soft drugs" be legalised

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: It's when we give up our hope of a drugfree society legalisation movement can win

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: That's why many people supports "harm reduction" movement instead of supporting preventive work

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Unfortunately "harm reduction" alone can't decrease the number of addicts

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: We need for example preventive work among young people to stop them from trying drugs

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: If the number of addicts shall decrease, society can't declare that it's all right to use drugs

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: Then young people get a liberal attitude towards drugs and they are more likely to try them

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: If it also is easy to get "soft drugs" and that it's legal to use them, many more risk to end up in an abuse

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
text: When "soft drugs" no longer are strong enough, addicts will move over to heavier drugs, and this way society never will get rid of drugabuse and narcotics, and social misery will increase

title: SHOULD "SOFT DRUGS" BE LEGALISED?
