7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: Is it a characteristic of art or is it just a way to express Nazi sympathy? In this essay I will argue for the controversial play 7:3 written by Lars Norén and produced by Isa Stenberg

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The play is about John Norén, in fact Lars Norén himself, who is a dramatist

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: He receives letters from prisoners in Tidaholmsanstalten and they ask for material for a theatrical performance

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: John Norén meets the prisoners, records their meetings and starts writing in a documentary way about their meetings

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The play or drama is a balance act between reality and theater, between a sometimes frightened humanist, two Nazis and one member of the military gang

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The prisoners are, in the play, played by themselves

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: John Norén wants the prisoners to open up to him and to tell him about their experiencies and why they think the way they do about a "pure" Sweden, if this has something to do with their childhood or growth

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: But the prisoners do not want to talk about their childhood at all, instead they want to express their feelings about nationalism

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The play has been criticized for not being a real play or drama rather than a Nazi propaganda weaved into a play

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The prisoners are also criticized, and this because they are prisoners in real life and that they in fact are playing themselves

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: They have clear opinions about nationalism and they have declared themselves as New Nazi sympathizers

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The critics say that the play is just a way for the prisoners to reach out to a larger group with their ideas through a famous playwright who likes to cross borders

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: My answer to these arguments, why the play 7:3 should not be showed to people, is simply that it is just a play

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: It is a real drama, about real people and about real feelings

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The main theme is the meeting between an author, John Norén (Lars Norén) and three inmates who want to play theatre

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The story takes place during the winter and the spring of 1998, and it ends when the summer begins

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: This is why I call it a documentary play rather than a propaganda play

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: Everything the actors do is located in the past, and the play is also revised

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: I think that one explanation to why Lars Norén has chosen to use real prisoners in the play and not professional actors, might be that the prisoners are those who really know about life in prison

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: They know everything about the hidden Nazi sympathies that exist and, about what constitutes another theme, the treatment of offenders

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: Another possible explanation is that the author wants to get as close as possible to reality, and since the prisoners contacted Norén in the first place, it is about the wish to act, and therefore they might as well join the play

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: As for the prisoners using Lars Norén to reach out with their nationalistic message, I do not agree

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: When they first contacted Lars Norén they had no idea that he would write a play about them

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: In fact, it is Norén who is using them

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: I believe that Norén has written a play with the aim to reply Nazism on a personal level

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: In their discussions during the story, Norén tries to get the prisoners aware of their views and that these do not correspond with his

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: Norén also tries to show the prisoners that their opinions conflict with our fundamental laws and that this is why they are prisoners

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The title 7:3 alludes to a legal paragraph which includes rules, leaves and the guarding of inmates who are thought to be likely to run away or devote themselves to crime during their time in prison

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The play is made from a documentary basis and it is the first play in Sweden made this way

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: Maybe this is why it has been so criticized

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: We are simply not used to it

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: It is common on television but it has never been seen on the scene before

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: My opinion is that 7:3 is a theatrical performance which needs to be showed

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: We cannot keep running away from the problems that already exist

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: It is better to get them out in the open for discussion and debate rather than keep closing our eyes

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: 7:3 ought to start a political and ideological debate about inmates and hidden Nazism

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: This play is not less important than a book about the Holocaust, but it is nevertheless unpleasant

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
text: The aim with the play is not to laugh and give great ovations, the aim is rather to make you think

title: 7:3, a play by Lars Norén
Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: The twenty-first century is said to become the age of knowledge

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: In Sweden though almost nothing is done to encourage higher education

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Does student financing have to be a burden for the society? The student grant- and loan-system seems to be arranged to deter as many as possibly to take a loan

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: This is a problem and society needs to consider the long-term effects of it

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Both society and individual people suffer from it

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: The present situation is that people who want to study at a higher level a few years have to get into debts that they never will be able to pay back

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Students should be financing their studies with money, partly grant and partly loan, but the former a bigger part and the latter a smaller part, than today

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: I will here present the reasons for why I think this is a good idea

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Since this is a very complex matter and the effects would be far-reaching, I will not be able to comment on all of them

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: The main argument for a better system for helping students to finance their studies is that it would inevitable encourage more people to start studying; more would finish their studies and in the end the level of education would rise

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: For example: I am studying at university to become a teacher, for which there is a high demand in the society right now

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: However, to be able to complete the three-and-a-half-year long education I have to take a loan that I, with the current teacher salary, not even will be able to start paying back

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: If I could have financed my studies some other way it would not have seemed so much as a big step, and then it would have been a much easier decision

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Furthermore, if more people had a higher education that would lead to other positive things

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: It could potentially save the state a good deal of money

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Not at first, of course, but in the long run

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: If we could find the money for this now we would be paid back later

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: When the level of education among the population rises, this would lead to economic growth

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: The state would get tax money from the people who had studied when they started work instead of being unemployed as they probably would have been since most jobs nowadays require a long education

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: People would also do a more effective work after being educated and because they are motivated for it

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: This situation could boast both the national and the individual economy

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Besides if students thought that there was a chance of paying back the loan they would probably consider it worth holding back spending a little bit, because then there would be less to pay back

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Economic growth and money, both good, but what are life really without happiness

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Do more education then create happiness? This is, I think, about personal development

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Everybody should have the right to find their own place in existence and should not be stopped by economic factors, also, everybody should have the opportunity to feel the joy of knowledge and gain a better self-confidence from studying

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: If someone end up with the right job I think that person will be much happier then if he had not

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: If the right place had not been found early in life there did not have to be disaster, there could be further education or maybe something entirely new

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Individual lives and society as a whole would be a lot happier

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: In conclusion, student financing would not have to be a burden for society but could be a tremendous possibility

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: A new system of financing education, guaranteeing the possibility for life-long learning, telling everybody education is good and that society appreciate the efforts done would render us a much better world to live in

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: I would say it is a question about democracy, it is important to give the chance of education to everyone

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
text: Not for the making of a better economy, although that most likely would come as a bonus, and not to make everyone professors; but for nobody to become a slave

title: Student financing: Burden or Possibility?
The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: In this essay I will be discussing the need for an active Swedish national defence

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: I'm fully aware of the complexity of this area and therefore I will focus on a couple of the main points in this debate, more specifically those for and against further cuts in the national defence budget

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: I have selected three arguments against an expensive national defence and I will try to prove them wrong by presenting some essential counter-arguments

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: The arguments I'm going to deal with are: The idea that international relations today are so good that war is unthinkable, the idea that national defence is not cost-effective and finally the proposed solution to these problems: the idea of rebuilding a defence force when necessary

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: The possibly most important argument against a Swedish national defence is that the political situation abroad and our relationship to foreign powers prevent the eventuality of war, at least in the foreseeable future

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Sweden has not been directly involved in a war for a couple of centuries and the possibility of such an event is considered very remote

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: What has been forgotten in this argument is that war is very difficult to predict

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: In the modern world, the intentions of other nations are not always clear and they can change from friendly to hostile in no time

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Today's international relations are not very stable, especially not after the end of the Cold War

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: An alert nation shouldn't be tricked into the "post-war-calm" mentality Sweden has today

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: After World War One, it was argued all over Europe that no more wars could follow after such a horrendous one

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Most nations ignored to maintain a capable defence force and the results of this ignorance are well known today

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Another important point is that war has a tendency to affect larger geographical areas today than before, mainly because of the increased capacity of mass-destruction weapons

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Surely, Sweden can't possibly stay out of a conflict between, for example the U

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: S

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: and Russia, because such a conflict will be world-wide

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Another argument that has gained much in influence these days is that the national defence is a state expense that isn't cost-effective

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: A lot of money is spent each year on training, weapon-systems and maintenance but society doesn't benefit from these activities and purchases

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Indeed, it is hard to point out exactly what we are protecting ourselves from, if it is necessary and why it should be so expensive

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: I'd like to compare this spending to the one you do on your insurance every year

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: You wouldn't even dream of not paying to protect yourself from robberies, accidents or other minor disasters, so why shouldn't the nation protect itself from the most devastating disaster of all - war? I'm sure you'll agree that a couple of millions each year is a low sum to pay considering what you are preventing; war often comes just as suddenly and unpredictably as a fire or a car-accident

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: The final argument I'd like to discuss isn't actually an argument, but more a practical solution to today's defence-budget problems that has been influenced by the anti-defence arguments mentioned above

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Today large parts of the defence forces are cut down permanently, a considerable part of those remaining are "put to sleep" and are not to be revitalised until it is necessary, that is in case of a war being imminent

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: Can you do this to a national defence force and still maintain the effectiveness that has been achieved in years of training and improvement? I'd say no, you can't

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: First of all, it takes several years to create a functioning army unit

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: A brigade (a unit of about 5,000 men), for example, takes some seven years to train

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: It is not very often that you can start training for a war several years ahead, mainly because you can't predict it

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: It would also take several years for the industry to adjust and build all the equipment necessary that we don't build today

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: This "solution" to the problems of the national defence today seems to me nothing but a desperate and irresponsible way to increase national funds

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: As a conclusion I'd like to point out that most of the arguments against a Swedish national defence are not very concerned with the actual safety of the nation, they seem to be more concerned about how much money can be saved by not investing in a national defence

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
text: It is very sad to see that the government of today makes hasty decisions in order to increase funds, decisions which we may have to regret in the future

title: The necessity of a Swedish national defence
Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: This essay will show you why we ought to treat animals not as means to our own ends but as ends in them selves

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: I will start by giving you an explanation to why our attitudes towards animals are the way they are

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: After this I will reject two of the most common arguments of why we need and have the right to use animals as means

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Finally I will give a superior argument which will force you to accept the fact that we have no right exploiting animals just to satisfy our own pleasures

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: I will plea for reason and logic, but this will not be sufficient if you are not ready to change your own attitudes

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: A person's attitudes have a tendency to overrun logic and reason, so please have an open mind, and think of what I just said

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: With this in mind, consider women's position in history

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: In the late 1800's women were men's possession and no one questioned this

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: In fact, Thomas Aquino said that 'a female is a defect male', and he was one of the greatest thinkers in the history of mankind

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Those were the facts, the truth those days, just as it is a fact nowadays that animals do not have any rights to claim towards humans

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: The same parallel could be drawn between the black slaves and the animal's situation today

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: What I want to say is that after every liberation movement people talk about it as 'the last remaining form of discrimination'

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: You should be very wary of talking about 'the last remaining form of discrimination' because, as I have shown, there are always some group of living creatures still being discriminated

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Yesterday it was sexism and racism, today it is speciesism

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: What we today consider morally justified may tomorrow be regarded as a great injustice

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: I assume you can not argue against this historical fact, and hence you have to admit that our attitudes today just might be immoral

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: The strength in my argumentation so far could, to some extent, be questioned if humans needed animals to survive, which is one of the most frequent arguments coming from speciesists

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: I agree there would be no immoral in killing animals and eating them in order to survive, but in many parts of the world this is not necessary, including our own

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: We can very well get all the nutritious substances we need from vegetables, different substitutes and additional vitamins

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: This argument is therefore rejected on basis of its incorrectness

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Could the speciesist defend his position in another way than the statement that humans need animals to survive? Let us consider the second of the most frequent arguments given in favour of speciesism

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: It claims we have the right to use animals as means to our own ends, when it is, at the same time, claiming we do not have the right to discriminate women and black people

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: It goes something like this: 'Women and blacks are members of our own species, animals are not, hence we have the right to exploit animals'

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: If the first argument failed on basis of incorrectness this argument fails on the basis of both relevance and logic

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: If we discriminate animals just because they are animals, we might as well discriminate women because they are women, and black people just because they are black

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: This is called sexism and racism

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: We can not discriminate any living creature on the basis of characteristics such as sex, skin colour, intelligence or species affiliation

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: The question we need to find an answer to is: What is the one capability humans and non-humans share? This question is answered by the Supreme argument, crushing any possible argument defending speciesism

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Any living creature possessing the capability of having feelings of pleasure and pain, has the right of not being used as means but always as ends'

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: This is the Supreme argument

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: On the basis of this, it should be quite obvious why speciesism is wrong, why we do not have the right to use animals as food-machines or experimenting on them

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: Even if the two arguments in favour of speciesism somehow could be defended, this argument could not be rejected

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: It is as simple as that

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: We now have a complete logical reasoning rejecting the speciesism in our society

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: The only obstacle now is your own attitudes

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: It may take some time to change them, and that is all right

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
text: The important thing is that you hopefully have started to reflect on your moral values, and eventually you will no longer find yourself on the side of the oppressors

title: Speciesism -The racism against animals
To live in a student hostel
text: How can you live in a student hostel? My old friends often ask me this question

title: To live in a student hostel
text: They just don't understand how I, since I have lived in different flats and houses on my own or together with my boyfriend of that time for almost ten years, can stand to live in a small room among so many unknown people

title: To live in a student hostel
text: But I think there are several strong reasons why I have chosen to live in a student hostel

title: To live in a student hostel
text: First, it's very economic if you compare with living in your own flat

title: To live in a student hostel
text: You pay less a month and the rent includes costs for electricity - no extra expense for that

title: To live in a student hostel
text: The best of all is that you get two month free rent - in June and July you don't have to pay anything

title: To live in a student hostel
text: The money you save you can spend on a lovely holiday instead

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Those who live in their own flats of course have to pay rent for every month

title: To live in a student hostel
text: But if they want to save their money, since many students take the opportunity to go home and live at their parent's house, they have to give notice of removal from their flat

title: To live in a student hostel
text: And then during the summer they have to spend time in searching for a new flat since they can't be sure of removing to their earlier flat

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Because of there are few available flats it may even be difficult to find a new one

title: To live in a student hostel
text: I don't have these problems since I can afford to keep my room in the student hostel

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Another economic reason is that because of you are twelve persons who live in the student hostel, you can also save money in newspapers and TV expenses - you just split the expenses in twelve

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Next, you will get a lot of friends when you live in a student hostel

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Since there are twelve persons who live together you will always find someone that you will get along with and maybe it will be the beginning of a life-long friendship

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Those who live in a flat often lives alone or have to share it with one or two persons that they really have to cope with, otherwise it will be very tough

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Though there are a bigger chance to make friendships in a student hostel since there are more persons to chose between

title: To live in a student hostel
text: My old friends often wonder if not the friendships will be too tight and if I will be left alone

title: To live in a student hostel
text: But if I want to be alone I just close my door

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Of course I can sometimes hear my friends from outside but it can be a similar insufficiently soundproof if you have a flat of your own

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Since you know your neighbours you will even feel more secure

title: To live in a student hostel
text: I remember when I lived alone in a house on the countryside, I slept with my golf club next to me, in case someone unknown would come and visit me in the middle of the night

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Now that is not necessary

title: To live in a student hostel
text: At last, it's much easier to keep your room and the shared kitchen tidy

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Since you live in only one room it doesn't take that much time to tidy - for me it only takes about half an hour a week

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Earlier when I lived in a big house or a flat I spend many hours on tidying - time that I now can spend in more important matters like studying and training

title: To live in a student hostel
text: The shared kitchen, with respect to my neighbours, makes me washing up the dishes at once

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Earlier my dishes could be left for a week

title: To live in a student hostel
text: When you finally move out from your student hostel you don't have to do the time-wasting and boring work in cleaning the oven, the ventilator and the freezer

title: To live in a student hostel
text: You just have to clean your own room, which of course is much easier

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Thus, it's easy to see why I have chosen to live in a student hostel

title: To live in a student hostel
text: Many people see this kind of living as temporary

title: To live in a student hostel
text: They just live in the student hostel for a short time meanwhile they search for a flat of their own

title: To live in a student hostel
text: But I promise that I will live in my student hostel as long as I will study here in Uppsala because of all the advantages and since this kind of living suits me perfectly well

title: To live in a student hostel
A concert hall - a matter of course
text: There can be very few citizens of Uppsala who have not heard or read about the debate of later years about the building of a concert hall in the centre of the town

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Sadly, the discussion has been going on for decades without any signs from the municipal council of Uppsala of seeing the advantages in such a project

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: I strongly believe that we need a proper concert hall and think that it is very strange that we have not got one when far smaller towns, like Gävle, have

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Undoubtedly, Uppsala with its well - established reputation of a rich and flourishing music life is risking to lose it if nothing is done soon

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: To here list the many musical activities in Uppsala would take up a great deal of space, but we have about 250 choirs, among them OD who has frequently being hailed as the best male choir in the world, and pop- rock- and jazzgroups ( too numerous to count) just to mention some of them

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: However, these activities just as any other form of culture, especially the performing arts, can only exist on physical conditions, and the situation today is that there is a shortage of rehearsal rooms as well as performance halls

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Of course famous and wealthy societies like OD have their own premises but the small ones have to work hard to find any

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Until now many small and independent choirs have been given permission to rehearse in churches but since the state and the church have been separated this has become more difficult

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Moreover, the only two premises we have for bigger concerts, orchestral work for example, are the University Hall and the Fyrishov sports hall, both with terribly bad acoustic properties and none of them built for the purpose of performing music

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: In other words, there is a general demand for a concert hall, and even if the building of one would not be profitable we should support it

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: From an idealistic point of view, culture is good in itself and an essential part of our lives which must be subsidized

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: However, contrary to what many people think, the building of a concert hall in all probability would be economically profitable in the long run

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Studies in Germany have proven that cities which invest in culture improve the situation on the labour market and improve the city's economy in broad outline

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: This applies to Uppsala as well

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: A good concert hall would be a good advertisement for our town, and people from other cities who would come here to go to concerts would also spend money on other lines of business, namely hotels, shops and restaurants

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Furthermore, big companies tend to invest in cultural events, through sponsorships, to get advertisement, and if they are going to move the business they seem to chose cities with a cultural profile

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: As I already have mentioned, this is all profitable in the long term, and to avoid heavy expences in the beginning it is possible to build the hall with the Nanna school as the base as have been suggested several times before

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Apart from being a place for musical performances and rehearsals, the concert hall could also serve an academic and commersial purpose

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Every year Uppsala University organizes several conferences and one of them, which I worked at, was a scientific assembly held in the summer of 1997

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: It comprised more than 1300 participants and would definitely have needed another big lecture hall

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: For non-academic conferences and meetings, available premises today are one only; the Atrium hall

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: It does seem obvious that increased supply would increase the demand, and the nearness to Arlanda would make Uppsala even more attractive

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: To sum up, the current situation bears no proportion to the musical life in Uppsala and have a restraining influence on the young generation of musicians

title: A concert hall - a matter of course
text: Only a rudimental knowledge of the practising of music leads to the conclusion that Uppsala is in immediate need of a concert hall, and the fact that it would not have a negative effect on the municipal economy, rather the contrary, makes it even more difficult to understand why we still lack one

Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The situation of the parents and the children of today is precarious

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The parents are working so much that they feel insufficient for their family

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: At young age, children are left at the day nursery several hours and therefore not feeling loved

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The current situation of Swedish day nurseries is a shame

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: As it is today all day nurseries for children under the age of four ought to be closed

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Day nurseries are so crowded that no real pedagogic activity is possible

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Children of this age group are particularly sensible because they need one main person to turn to

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Today an employee has not got enough time to give proper attention to each child

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: In addition the employees are so worn-out that they often need to report themselves sick, and several substitutes come and go

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: There are three main arguments why day nurseries for children under four should be closed

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The first has to do with children's need of developing a self-esteem, the second with learning rules to feel safe and the third with their health

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Small children like to play by themselves therefore they don't really need a lot of children around to play with

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: They don't understand why they been left and that their parents will come back to pick them up later

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Some children believe that they've been abandon forever

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Under the circumstances of today's day nurseries no child can develop a good self- confidence

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The setting of a personality starts already from the first day of life

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: To establish a good self-esteem children need to have one adult who's always available

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The adult ought to have a clear set of rules and give continuous feedback and interest in the children's behaviour

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Having one person to get attached to means that children learns to develop a relationship with another individual

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Not having one influence the child negatively for the rest of their life

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The capability of dealing with situations later on in life is determined during his or her first three years of life

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Children need the help of an adult to learn and understand proper social skills and everyday rules

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Children who have not yet learned social skills tend to make up their own, particularly if they spend most of their time with children of their own age

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Their own way of solving a problem often includes violence

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: If no adult make the time to teach them alternative ways of communication, children can go on becoming socially disabled up in high age

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Different rules and persons at day nursery and at home can severely confuse small children

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: To understand the environment makes the individual feel safe

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The employees at the day nurseries do not have a chance to see to that each child follows their own day rhythm

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: To eat when hungry and to sleep when tired is important for their health

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Small children's immune defence is not yet entirely developed and they are therefore more vulnerably to diseases

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Being continuously exposed to several illnesses, being tired and staying in humid buildings has a negative influence on their health

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Many children develop different kinds of allergies under these conditions

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Suggestions how to make it financially possible for one parent to stay at home with their children are many

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: If the Government would close down all day nurseries for children under four there would be some money to replace

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: That money should be used to pay the parents

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Parents who are staying home from work should continue to get the same ATP* as if they were working

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The first three years of minding the parents should also get maintenance payment grounded on their working salary

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Single parents should however have the possibility to send their children to a day nursery

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Their children would gain from this solution also because the day nurseries wouldn't then be as crowded as they are today

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Special laws would have to be passed to make it smoother for the parents to return to the labour market

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The whole society would gain from this solution

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Parents would know their children better and the children would become more self-confident and have fewer tendencies to use violence

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: Better relationships mean fewer divorces and fewer crimes

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: The health of many children and future adults would also improve

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
text: *ATP: national supplementary pensions scheme

title: Abolish day nursery for children under four.
The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: My family means the world to me, especially my grandmother

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: She is soon to be eighty-nine years old and she is without doubt the healthiest, most spirited and alert eighty-nine year old I have ever met

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Six days a week a woman from a home for aged people comes and visits her

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: She tidies up and vacuum-cleans my grandmother's flat and does her laundry

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: She also brings my grandmother the food she needs, so she does not have to do anything that could be dangerous to or jeopardize her health

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Her husband, my grandfather, recently died, and our whole family were convinced that she would be terribly miserable living on her own, with no one to talk to during the day

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: My mother and her brother were worried that they would be forced to decide whether to put grandmother in a home for elderly or to let her live with one of them

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Unfortunately, none of them were happy about the possibility that their mother should live in one of their homes

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I felt overcome with anger and rage against their attitudes, but I realized that there were not much I could do about it

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Surprisingly, my grandmother was very determined when she told us that she would rather die than to be put in an institution, as she called it

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Neither did she want to intrude upon her daughter's and son's family life and she rejected the offer to live with either of them

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I actually think that she was more anxious about her own privacy than the fact that she was going to intrude on our

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: My family is lucky to have such a vital grandmother, but not everyone is so fortunate as we are

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: The most important issue, when it comes to elderly care, is the question whether to take care of the older and let them stay home with the family, or to let the state look after them

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: It was not my place to tell my mother what was right or wrong in her attitude against elderly care, but I know that I could never put my parents in those "institutions"

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I guess I would be forced to, if they were ill and could not cope on their own

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: But if the they would stay healthy, that is, if they would not need hospital care, and just were too old to cook and clean and shop for groceries, I would certainly let them live with me

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: The house might be a bit crowded, but I prefer a crowded house, than a crowded home for aged people

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I think the family bonds are extremely significant and important for the human race and for life itself and I would do anything to keep my family intact

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I also think that it is quite important for the youth of today to live with and among the older generation

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: As a none-practising teacher, I still have met hundreds of young students that are very disrespectful towards their parents and towards older people

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I am convinced, that if every family let the old members live with them, sharing their lives and ideals, the world would be a much better place

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Young ones today, hardly see their grandparents, but for perhaps three times a year

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: That is a shame

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Old people have experienced a great deal and are full of valuable knowledge that they can, hopefully, influence the young with

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: There are a too wide gap between the youth and the elderly today and I strongly feel that this situation has to change

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: If we let our older family members live with the rest of the family, I am sure that the violence that are spreading among youths in Sweden today, would decrease radically

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: I have only mentioned the advantages the younger generation could experience when and if they live with older people, but I think the elderly also can experience more happiness sharing their lives with the young members in the family

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: That is, they can feel useful when they take part of the interaction between family members

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Life is fragile and so are family bonds

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: It is of great importance to keep those bonds strong and fresh

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
text: Therefore it is far beyond my ability to comprehend that some people prefer to put their old ones in homes for aged people, rather than taking care of them themselves

title: The family, not the state, should look after the elderly
The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Sweden has traditionally been a monarchy ruled by a sovereign Monarch

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: This did not change until the 19th century when Montesquieu's ideas were adopted to the Swedish constitution and the king had to share his powers with the politicians

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The question whether a democratic country like Sweden should be a monarchy or not has been increasingly debated throughout the past century and culminated with a reform of the government in 1974

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Then it was stated that the Monarch should be Sweden's Head of State

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: This means that his powers are very limited and mostly of symbolic value

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The monarch's greatest power is that he is the first representative for the Swedish defence force and that he holds the highest grades of the defence

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: One could argue that the Monarch does not have any real political power and is therefore merely to be seen as an ambassador who travels round the world as a representative for his country

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: But there are many aspects of the monarchicalsystem and even though the Monarch's powers are very limited his or her position as the Head of State is of principal matter

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Because the Monarch is given his title by heredity and is consequently not chosen by the people

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: This is the antithesis to the main thought of democracy, that the power emanates from the people

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Another disturbing factor which interferes with the thought that all individuals are equal is that the Monarch historically has been looked upon as a divine ruler, that is a ruler chosen by God

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: And he does still today in our modern society stand above the grey mass of working- and middle class people

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The Monarch is part of and in a way a symbol for the class society which is advancing rapidly

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: He does not have to prove that he has any kind of qualifications at all for his office

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: And it takes at least eight years and two votes in the Parliament if a monarch is to be removed from his office

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Another aspect that is related to the Monarch's lack of qualifications is the economical part

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The grant from the state to the royal household is as much as 37 million kronor each year

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: This sum is supposed to cover the costs for the Monarch's representation and the 75 persons who are employed by the Earl Marshal office and the divisions for computers, economy and press

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Furthermore does the tax payers pay for the Governor office, that is administration of the royal castles and parks, and the Royal Chamber of Household Utensil, which includes administration and preservation of equipment and art collections

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: These two offices receive about 35 million kronor every year

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The main reason to why a majority of Swedes are in favour of the monarchy is the enormous amount of attention that is paid on the royal family in the press

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: Members of the royal family are to be seen on the front covers of various magazines and newspapers

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: But the headlines are not always flattering, whit the scandal press digging deeper and deeper trying to find out anything that presumably would sell

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: And this not, and has never been, the purpose of the monarchy

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: The monarchical system is in many ways archaic and does not have validity in a modern, contemporary and democratic society

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: It can not be denied that the Monarch has a symbolic value which is of benefit to his country

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: But this does not mean that this uniting role can not be shouldered by someone else, like the politicians

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: It would definitely be more democratic if the Prime minister, who is chosen by the people, would be the head of state for the period that he or she is elected

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: This would open up the possibility for the people to remove a Head of State that does not have the people's symphaties

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
text: And the sum of 72 million kronor for the Monarch's costs could be used in sectors like education, health- and elderly care to benefit the whole society

title: The monarchy - an archaic system
