The usless art of debating
text: Politicians seem to argue about just everything

title: The usless art of debating
text: Sometimes you wonder does debates between politicians lead somewhere? Do politicians of different opinions ever agree? No they don't according to me

title: The usless art of debating
text: I would say that almost all debates, political or non-political are pointless

title: The usless art of debating
text: Why is that? Because of logic or rather lack of logic in debating arguments

title: The usless art of debating
text: You often hear people say: "That's logical

title: The usless art of debating
text: " Is it really? Arguments that are logical can't be contradicted

title: The usless art of debating
text: Six divided by two is greater than one plus one is logical, because six divided by two equals three and one plus one equals two and three is as we know greater than two

title: The usless art of debating
text: That argument can't be contradicted

title: The usless art of debating
text: It's logical

title: The usless art of debating
text: The argument "Liberalism is better than socialism because personal freedom is more important than the common good" is not logical

title: The usless art of debating
text: You could argue that it is and I would agree if you could logically prove that personal freedom is more important than the common good

title: The usless art of debating
text: I would say that you had mixed your feelings and values with your logic

title: The usless art of debating
text: You value personal freedom higher than the common good and your conclusion is therefore based on your values not strict logic

title: The usless art of debating
text: You could never convince a devoted socialist that personal freedom is better than the common good, he would laugh at you

title: The usless art of debating
text: He would agree that six divided by two is greater than one plus one though

title: The usless art of debating
text: So what does all this logic imply you wonder? Well, take a look at abortion for an example

title: The usless art of debating
text: Those that are against abortion often argue that it violates life which is holy

title: The usless art of debating
text: "Life is holy? Why is that?" I would ask

title: The usless art of debating
text: An atheist would probably not have an answer at that question

title: The usless art of debating
text: A religious person would probably answer that it's God's will, the Bible says so or he would refer to another holy scripture

title: The usless art of debating
text: "But I don't believe in those scriptures

title: The usless art of debating
text: I don't believe in God" I would answer

title: The usless art of debating
text: "Can you prove his existence for me?" The religious person probably tries to convince me of God's existence and wouldn't succeed in his attempt, because I'm a devout atheist, if you can be a devout atheist, that is

title: The usless art of debating
text: If the religious man can't prove God's existence he can't prove that human life is holy

title: The usless art of debating
text: Therefore he really can't use the argument to convince a person who is pro-choice

title: The usless art of debating
text: Had he found some logical reason to why abortion should be banned he could convince his opponent of his point

title: The usless art of debating
text: The life-is-holy argument is often used in the debate of Capital Punishment

title: The usless art of debating
text: As I already have proven the argument isn't logical and can of course be used, but it won't change the minds of the most stiff-necked supporters of Capital Punishment

title: The usless art of debating
text: There are other arguments against Capital Punishment that are better

title: The usless art of debating
text: For an example "in a democracy, if it's forbidden to take a human life how can the state commit murder without being prosecuted? For if the state is under the rule of law the same rules should apply to both the state and the citizens

title: The usless art of debating
text: " That argument is more logical in its nature and therefore better to use to convince someone that Capital Punishment is something that you should be against

title: The usless art of debating
text: The problem with this argument is that it can't be used in a state that isn't a democracy

title: The usless art of debating
text: That may seem strange, but it isn't if you see it from a logical point of view

title: The usless art of debating
text: If a dictator orders a person to be executed, the dictator hasn't broken any rules, because he decides the rules and isn't bound by them

title: The usless art of debating
text: But dictatorship is wrong the common man would say

title: The usless art of debating
text: If that man should be asked why dictatorship is wrong he probably can't give a logical answer

title: The usless art of debating
text: Answers like "people must have freedom" aren't logical they're expressing values, something fundamentally different from logic

title: The usless art of debating
text: As I explained earlier in this essay logic can't be contradicted

title: The usless art of debating
text: Values can

title: The usless art of debating
text: You can argue about abortion, capital punishment or dictatorship, but you often express values or feelings instead of logical arguments

title: The usless art of debating
text: You can't really prove your point

title: The usless art of debating
text: You may be against something you may be for something

title: The usless art of debating
text: It doesn't matter

title: The usless art of debating
text: The debate have been a waste

title: The usless art of debating
text: You probably still have the same values as before

title: The usless art of debating
text: The one you have been debating with probably still have his values intact

title: The usless art of debating
text: If people looked for more logical arguments they hopefully would question their own ideas and values and discussion would become more than a pastime

Silence
text: The first sheet of paper was not forgotten, it was a gift

title: Silence
text: I wanted to give you a second of silence, of no words, of empty paper

title: Silence
text: It could be the most valuable part of this essay, because what I want to say is that we need these small moments of tranquility

title: Silence
text: I have been walking around in silence for some hours now, among silent trees and sleeping mountains, and I am filled with air and emptyness

title: Silence
text: Back inside, I looked at "silence" in Longmans Dictionary, and found the sentence "The complete silence of the forest at night"

title: Silence
text: It made me wonder -Has everyone experienced that complete silence? Maybe most people has not experienced complete silence at all, and maybe they do not miss it

title: Silence
text: But still I think that all of us needs to empty ourselves from time to time, like a vacuum cleaner, you need to make space for new thoughts and views

title: Silence
text: People usually agree with this, complaining about overcrowded aching heads, but they do not do anything about it

title: Silence
text: It is easier to just go on, talking, arranging, making the small things work and hiding from the big things in the noise

title: Silence
text: These big things and questions are droven away down to the stommach, where they can lye murmuring

title: Silence
text: And if no one hears their murmours, it will rise to screams and gastric ulsers

title: Silence
text: There are a lot of gastric ulsers walking around in the world today

title: Silence
text: Not all of them, but some, may be longing for silence

title: Silence
text: Not only physical silence, but inner silence

title: Silence
text: Watch out, we are getting into the dangerous "peace in mind areas", not far from horoscops and crystals

title: Silence
text: Normal people have to be careful these days

title: Silence
text: Silence is an issue for New agers and "letters-to-the-editor-ladies"

title: Silence
text: Local papers need to have one or two letters a week about the immoral, noisy youth, but people of power will not notice them

title: Silence
text: Maybe we would need to put a price on silence, to make it fit into the buisness pages

title: Silence
text: It is discussed to put a price on air, so why not? Silence has to have a market value, get a profile, fit in on Internet, maybe some silent silence commercials? Or just wait in silence

title: Silence
text: Wait and see what will come

title: Silence
text: I often do so before I go to bed, just listening to my breath

title: Silence
text: There are a lot of things flying around me and I give them some time to come down

title: Silence
text: Eventually there is only the breath left and I can go to sleep

title: Silence
text: I think many people are meditating, contemplating, without knowing

title: Silence
text: Why do you think parents of small children spend so long time in the bathroom? The Corean boddhist master Song San is also meditating

title: Silence
text: When he got his enlightment after ages of meditation, his teacher told him to be quiet for three years to make it deeper

title: Silence
text: He says that talking takes a lot of energy, so if you really want to concentrate on something, try to be quiet

title: Silence
text: But it is not easy to be quiet when there are so many words attacking incessantly

title: Silence
text: Everyone wants to send out their message, their opinions, their views, and there are not enough ears to catch all the empty words

title: Silence
text: There are not eyes to read all the essays that has already been written a thousand times before, I think that schools has got some responsibility in all this, when they keep filling their students with things that they do not want to hear

title: Silence
text: "Knowledge is the lightest burden to bear!" is their mantra, but I do not agree

title: Silence
text: It is heavy for me to carry around knowlwedge of logarithms, that I will never need

title: Silence
text: Some lessons of silence could have been more interesting

title: Silence
text: I am not suggesting a voiceless society, I love to talk and I would go crazy among only Song Sans

title: Silence

title: Silence
text: Things could turn up behind the deperate need of keeping the conversation

title: Silence
text: I have produced even more words now to fill the poor, noisy world, just to suggest you to give yourself and your ears some quiet seconds

title: Silence

Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Every year, usually in the end of May, millions of people gather around their TV sets to rejoice in a spectacular cacophony of lighthearted music- the Eurovision Song Contest

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: For decades, this festival has enchanted viewers all over Europe, and also in Turkey and Israel

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Not because the music in itself is particularly good, but because the Eurosong (or "schlager") genre is such a fascinating phenomenon

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: We have seen many a memorable costume and heard catchy tunes galore through the years

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Since the fall of Communism and the birth of all the new nations- the Baltic states and the Balkan countries, the contest has grown bigger than ever

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Everybody wants to join the festival of shimmering, glamourous western splendour

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Although the Eurovision Song Contest is supposed to be unpolitical, it often reflects society in one way or another

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In the affluent eighties, the songs were mostly happy, frolicky little numbers

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In the nineties the songs have been generally more serious

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In 1990, several songs dealt with the Berlin Wall- Norway's song was called "Brandenburger Tor", Switzerland contributed with "Keine Mauer Mehr", and Italy won with "Insieme 2000", a hopeful vision of a future united Europe

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In 1993, Croatia, Slovenia and Bosnia-Hercegovina participated for the first time- all three songs dealing with the Balkan war

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: When the Bosnian jury delivered their results over the phone directly from Sarajevo, the cheering of the audience was deafening

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: This shows what an important role the festival can play, in spite of its superficiality

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In the last few years, however, it has all started to change

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In quite a negative way, I am afraid

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: The rules of the contest used to be simple

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Every artist or group were to sing in one of the official languages of their nation, although they were allowed to include eight foreign words in their lyrics

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: The song should preferably mirror the culture of the country

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Independent jury groups from every participating nation selected the winner

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: These groups were able to listen to the songs several times, thus examening them carefully

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Recently, though, the rules have been dramatically changed

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Since 1998, the winner is selected by the viewers themselves, through telephone voting

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: This was supposed to be a more democratic way of choosing the victor, and also a way to attract more viewers by making them interactive

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Personally, I find this new rule utterly horrible

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Since the artists now have to appeal to the TV audience, looks, sexual allusions and spectacular effects have become much more important

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Is there anyone who truly believes that Sweden's Charlotte Nilsson won in 1999 because of her lovely singing voice? Moreover, the unique Eurosong genre is slowly dying, as the artists try to resemble contemporary MTV-pop-singers

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Last but not least, this method is not very democratic at all

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: People very seldom choose their favourite song in an objective way

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: For example, Turkey did extremely well in 1998, although their song was really rather dull

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Why? Well, they got thousands of votes from Germany, were there is a considerable Turkish minority

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: An even more epoch-making change was made in 1999

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Every country was now free to choose any language they wanted for their lyrics

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: This was, of course, another attempt to make the contest more democratic

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Undoubtedly, the English-speaking countries have had a great advantage, since almost everybody understands, and can relate to their lyrics

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: For instance, in 1993 Ireland won, closely follwed by the United Kingdom and Malta

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Furthermore, some people have emphasized the injustice of the fact that a country like Switzerland has got four languages to choose from whereas, for example, Denmark has only got one

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: It has also been argued that the recurrent failure of certain countries like Finland and Cyprus is due to the fact that their languages are not rhythmically suitable for this type of music

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: (In Finland's case, though, this is apparently not true, since Estonia has been tremendously succesful although the two languages are very closely related

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: ) In 1999, all the Nordic artists sang in English, Bosnia-Hercegovina's contribution was in French, and the German group chose to sing in German, English, Turkish and Hebrew in the same song- thus appealing to the voters in all these countries

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: I find all this very sad

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: I have always enjoyed learning a few phrases in strange languages by watching the Eurovision Song Contest

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Now, the cultural aspect of the festival has disappeared completely

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: In 1999, Norway's contribution was performed in English, by a black hip-hop-boy from the Netherlands

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: I have got nothing against a multicultural society, but in the world of the Eurosong, national characteristics have to be preserved

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Besides, Eurosong lyrics are very seldom profound and well-expressed

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: On the contrary, they are often terribly banal, which is all right all long as you do not understand them

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: I think the importance of the Eurovision Song Contest is often underrated

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: It is a unique event, where the European states can meet and learn about each other, regardless of political ideologies or religious disagreements

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: It proves that we can get together in the name of peace and fun

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
text: Such a festival must not be commercialized! Therefore, I truly hope that the arrangers will listen to reason and go back to the former system- for the sake of love, peace and the good old Eurosong

title: Ding-A-Dong... The Bell Tolls for the Eurosong.
Capital Punishment
text: In my essay I would like to argue about, if a person or the authority of a state has the right to decide weather a criminal should be deprived its life or not, and how this method does effect ordinary people and the society

title: Capital Punishment
text: I will also discuss what is the difference between criminals and the authority of a country that use capital punishment, as a legally punishing method, and what I thing would be a better alternative instead of this penalty

title: Capital Punishment
text: Capital punishment is the execution of a criminal who's sentence of death has been decided by a court

title: Capital Punishment
text: Capital Punishment was once a common penalty throughout the world

title: Capital Punishment
text: And it was inflected for a large number of crimes

title: Capital Punishment
text: However nowadays this kind of punishment has reduced more or less, as a result of people's struggle against old systems and old regimes

title: Capital Punishment
text: The World is now divided between countries that have abolished death penalty like Sweden, and those countries that have retained it, for instance Iraq, Iran, many deferent Muslim countries and USA, the later one has employed this method more than any other countries in the whole world

title: Capital Punishment
text: Before arguing for or against this penalty we have to take in consideration that every single person has the right to be treated faire in his/her life

title: Capital Punishment
text: It is every individual's basic human right, no matter what

title: Capital Punishment
text: Justice in a person's life could signify two factors, that is, no man has the right take life of another person's, and that a murder should or deserve to die

title: Capital Punishment
text: Those who have abolished death penalty have long time ago come to the conclusion, that this method is wrong and not effective in the long ran

title: Capital Punishment
text: For instance in the USA, the crimes are increasing more and more in spite of using capital punishment

title: Capital Punishment
text: Still there are a lot of countries that use this extremely cruel and grim way of punishing to preserve the justice in a society

title: Capital Punishment
text: In contrast I personally don't believe that capital punishment has that much power to deter people from committing crimes

text: In contrary those who have killed a person, it is said, that are rarely committing any crimes when released or paroled

title: Capital Punishment
text: Otherwise there is evidence that those who have committed other crimes such as burglary, drug crimes and those who abuse children sexually are committed the same crime over and over again, as son as they are realest

title: Capital Punishment
text: I'm of strong opinion that capital punishment gives the wrong impression to the ordinary, likewise it even could effect their life in domestic matters

text: I thing also that people's behaviour reflect a country's policy

text: Specifically if we take USA as an example, that have retained capital punishment, has the highest rate crimes involving a person's death

title: Capital Punishment
text: As I understand people in this country believe that they have the right to protect themselves by possessing guns

text: In this case they act like the government of their country

title: Capital Punishment
text: I believe that there must other effective penalty than capital punishment

text: Violence never solve any problem

title: Capital Punishment
text: Although the first one, that is the authority of a country, is doing it legally, for the sake of justice, in purpose of deterring people, and to create a better society of safety

title: Capital Punishment
text: This is what I think, that they think is the benefits of having capital punishment

text: I believe that countries, that have maintained capital punishment, argue also that this way of punishing has a unique power to scare people from committing crimes

title: Capital Punishment
text: I am of strong opinion that this is not the case

text: I think capital punishment is used because it is cheap and it might avoid vendetta and also because the criminals are sometime considered as dangerous to the society

title: Capital Punishment
text: Thus I consider this whole system completely wrong and unnecessary when I think better of it

text: There must be other methods that not contend to the right of human, namely imprisonment for long time or imprisonment for lifetime

text: It doesn't gain a country to get ride of criminals

Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Are you one of those who work eight hours a day five days a week and come home almost exhausted in the evenings? More and more of working people are under stress at work and several even have to take psycho-drugs to cope with it

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Not many are able to work until retirement age, our need of rest is not provided for

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: While many working people are suffering from burnout there are also about four hundred thousand people that are out of work

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: A solution to these problems could be to introduce a thirty hours' working week in Sweden

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Thirty hours work a week would be six hours a day, which is two hours less than today

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: These two hours the employees could spend with their family or by themselves to rest

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: With shorter working-hours there would be more time to look after close relationships and families would have time to other things together than the usual everyday duties

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Time is a commodity in short supply in many families, which affects the children above all

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Less working hours would perhaps even contribute to prevent divorces when people have more time for each other

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: More spare time would give people opportunity to devote themselves to hobbies or to exercises as well

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: When people feel that they have time to do other things than duties they will become more positive in general and we would get a more harmonious and healthy people

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Less working hours would also contribute to increased equality in society since it would even up the difference in working hours between men and women

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Today women often voluntary work part time to have time to take care of the housework

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Over eighty per cent of part time workers are women

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: If six hours' work a day will become standard in Sweden, men and women could do equal work at home and women would get better positions on the labour market

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Labour market would also offer more jobs that form from shortening of work

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: If all three million working people would shorten their workweek with ten hours, seven hundred and fifty thousand new jobs would be available

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Those who have been out of work could then get a job and lighten the work burden for the others

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: It is obvious that work shortening favours the individual, but what about the employers and the society? It might be difficult to count the gains of work shortening in money, but in the long run I think everybody will make a profit on it

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Just think about the redundant twenty per cent of the population that are unemployed and would get a chance to get into the labour market

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Another example is that less tired and healthier employees lead to fewer people being put on the sick-list

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: This would result less sickness benefits having to be paid by the company

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: The employees will presumably also find more pleasure in their jobs and work more efficiently

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: During the short day they would not have time to be so tired or bored

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Working hours and occupational injuries are connected

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Most occupational injuries take place in the later part of the day when people are most tired

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: Many occupational injuries never pass and result in high costs for society in form of medical treatment and rehabilitation

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: A shortening of working hours would very likely imply that accidents at places of work would reduce and fewer people would be absent due to long time illness

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: The environment would also benefit by work shortening

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: To transform Sweden into a more environment friendly country requires time and commitment from the individual citizen, time that would be gained from work shortening

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: People might consider they have time to take the bus instead of the car to work, or sort garbage at home

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: It is about time that shorter working weeks are introduced in Sweden, it would be a gain for society in general in form of reduced costs for unemployed, early retired and sick people

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
text: For the people less working hours would signify quality of life, time for the family, increased equality and a chance to contribute to a better environment

title: Increased Quality of Life-Something for You?
Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Ever since the Beatles in the 1960:s there have been boy bands, which millions of girls all over the world worship, even though, in the beginning it was quite innocent and the music was still in focus

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: In the last decade there has been a dramatic change and the music is no longer the main issue when it comes to boy bands

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: What makes them popular now is their image and looks

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: It has many times been debated whether these bands are good for teenagers and children, who devote their lives to their idols, or if they can be damaging

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Boy bands keep popping up, some of them are independent and make it on their own, but most of them are completely created by a record company, who spend a huge amount of money on them

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: The artists themselves rarely write their own music and in many cases they are not even very good at music

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: All that matters to the company is money and they do their best at making as much money as possible out of their new boys and this industry is constantly growing

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: The age of the boy band fans is now lower than ever, it varies from about six to fourteen - fifteen years old

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Is it natural for little girls of six or seven to adore boy bands to the extent that they dress and act like teenagers, and stand at the front row at concerts, almost being killed by the enormous pressure? Some people might say that at that young age girls don't take it very seriously and that they know it's not real "love", but I would like to say that as soon as children start growing up too soon and missing their entire childhood, it is indeed unnatural

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Millions of girls, and of course some boys, spend many years following their favourite boy band on tours and media performances and buying every magazine and every poster with their "darling"

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: This summer I went to a Boyzone- concert in Oslo and heard about an English girl at the age of 11 or 12, who had been to over 200 different Boyzone performances during the last couple of years

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Her mother came with her on her trips around the world and seemed to think this was perfectly normal

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: At every concert they would be standing right in front of the stage, screaming when the boys appeared and singing along in every song

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Of course, one could say that the girl was given the opportunity to see many different places and meeting new friends all over the world, all with the same interest as herself, which is very positive, but I don't think it's healthy to devote all of your time and attention to members of a boy band

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: What happens for example if a boy band splits up? When the biggest boy band at the time, Take That, split up a few years ago, many girls tried to commit suicide and therapy groups and hot lines had to be set up in England and many other countries, because of many girls' disappointment and frustration

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: This devotion is not only psychologically unhealthy, it can also be physically dangerous

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: To stand in front of the stage with thousands of hysterical fans around her can be lethal to a young girl if she's not careful

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: If she falls she might be stepped on, or the huge pressure and the heat, along with the excitement, can make her faint

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Everybody who has ever been to one of these concerts knows what I am talking about

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: They have all seen the girls being picked up from the screaming crowd and carried out unconscious

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: The chance of a boy band fan's biggest dream (that her favourite member will get to know and fall in love with her) to come true is so little, it hardly exists and the day that she realises this she's bound to be incredibly disappointed

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: However, boy bands do offer girls many nice experiences at concerts and fan gatherings, but is it really worth it? Is it worth all the money, the risk of getting hurt and the lost childhood years? I think parents of young girls should be aware of the dangerous aspects of boy band concerts and make sure that their children don't lose themselves in the big "boy band circus"

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Everybody should also think about how we like the music industry to be

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
text: Music ought to be about people enjoying themselves and making other people happy, without a great big music company taking advantage of little girls and making it into strict business

title: Boy bands - for better or worse?
Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: I have been given an essay assignment in my A-level composition class I am taking at Uppsala University

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: The assignment is to write paper in which I argue for or against something

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: I have chosen to argue for something that sooner or later will concern us all

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: People feel safe and secure in an environment they know well and have lived in for a long period of time, which is often the case for senior citizens

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: When you grow old you want to be close to family and friends and that continues to be of great importance when you can't live alone and take good care of yourself any longer

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: Therefore I think that the family, not the state, should look after the elderly

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: I think the main reason for taking care of our older family members, like parents and grand parents, should be like returning a favor

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: The older generations took care of us, the younger, and fed us, loved us, sheltered us, shared their wisdom with us and helped us to become the persons we are today

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: When a person grow old and maybe can't care for himself/herself in a good way any longer, it is the younger generation's turn to look after his/her best

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: The family shouldn't feel obligated to do so, but think of it as a way of showing gratitude and appreciation

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: In my opinion the best way of showing this is to take the elderly into our homes and make them feel important in our lives, instead of putting them in an institution, where they easily become just one in the crowd

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: Two words of great significance in this discussion are the words important and safe i

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: e

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: to feel important and to feel safe

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: Elderly often feel a little useless and forgotten when they don't have a daily job, where they made a difference, to go to any more

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: To take that person into your home can make him/her feel needed and important

title: Let The Elderly Stay At Home
text: He/she can help look after the children, if there are any, cook and help with other house-hold chores; this would make the elder feel that he/she is playing an important role in the family

Slavery is not dead!
text: Although slavery is outlawed by several of the most widely ratified international Conventions and by legislation in almost every country, slavery is not dead

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Estimates working for ILO (International Labour Organisation) and the United Nations put the number of child slaves into tens of millions

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Children are regularly found working in manifestly hazardous and arduous situations

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: They haul wagons underground in coalmines, they draw molten glass in stifling temperatures, they squat on the workshop floor for many hours making carpets for example

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: This form of slavery, children working with there own lives at stake to almost no money at all, has to be stopped for several different reasons

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: One reason is the fact that to hard work ruins a child physically

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Children are susceptible to all of the dangers that are faced by adult workers when placed in the same situation, but they are more seriously affected because they differ from adults in their anatomical, physiological characteristics

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: The health effect can be devastating, causing irreversible damage to their physical development

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: To use unnatural positions or carry heavy loads at work can permanently distort or disable growing bodies

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Children suffer more readily from chemical hazards and radiation than do adults, and children also have less resistance to diseases

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Children are less aware of their rights and they have never got a chance to learn how to defend themselves, which makes it easier to hire children to do dangerous and laborious work But children doing hard work in poor working conditions to a disgraceful salary do not only suffer physically

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: They are also subjected to psychological suffer

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Children suffer deeper psychological damage when they are denigrated

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: The expectations of the children working are often high and they work under hard pressure

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: In most cases far to high regarded to their age

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: With all the information above in mind you ask your self why and how can so many children today, in 1999, be exposed to hazardous work? Poverty emerges as the most compelling reason why children work

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Poor households need the money

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: But it can not be said, however, that poverty necessary causes child labour

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: The picture varies

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: There are regions in poor countries where child labour is extensively practised while in other equally poor regions it is not

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Kerala State in India, for example, though poor, has in fact abolished child labour

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: So irrespective of poverty in a country is it important that a lot of efforts are put to eliminate hazardous child labour, independently of poverty reduction

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: It is important that the children, with help from the government working through local organisations, are offered a choice

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: Otherwise it easily gets into at bad circle

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: If a child for example do not has the chance to attend school the possibility for him/her to get a well-paid job is almost impossible

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: The child grows into an adult trapped in unskilled and badly paid jobs

title: Slavery is not dead!
text: He/she later get children that he/she cant provide for

text: But that was just the beginning

text: When you get missions to complete you will have to co-operate and then you automatically get to know the rest of the group

title: To haze or not to haze, that is a question.

title: To haze or not to haze, that is a question.
text: I would almost dear to say that many of the ones who are sceptical or critical have never been part of a hazing themselves

text: They have only heard rumours about it

title: To haze or not to haze, that is a question.

text: Behind the children's behavior are socio-economic factors, their behavior is only a symptom

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: But is there anything practical we can do today, to move towards a peaceful classroom? I'm going to argue in this essay that introducing the school uniform can be a good idea

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I want to make clear though that introducing school uniforms isn't the one and only solution

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: School uniforms is one small, positive step

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I would like to raise teacher's salary and involve parents even more, but that's a different story

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I'm going to argue for school uniforms for pupils up to the twelfth grade

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: The most common argument against school uniforms is that it doesn't allow pupils to develop their personality

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I don't believe so, because human beings can express themselves in other ways than through clothes

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I want to promote a very underestimated channel of expression - the verbal one

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Today's society is too obsessed about appearance and the way we look

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: It is treated as if it was the most important thing many times

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I think it would be great if the school could help children focus on what's important - who they are INSIDE and the whole point with school; education, not what they wear

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: One other thing that seems to be forgotten about is the fact that schooldays DO END (even if it doesn't appear so sometimes)

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: We are only talking about a couple of hours five days a week

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: On their free time, holidays, weekends and so on, children can wear whatever they like and develop their own style in clothes

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: What harm can a few hours do? Is it really that big of a deal? Does it mess up the child's personality? I don't think so and I think it is quit absurd to claim that

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: What do we gain? The best thing about the school uniform is that it erase all class differences

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: You can't tell the rich from the poor

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: The fashion industry is getting tougher and they are aiming at younger and younger children

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Is a seven-year-old expressing his/hers personality through Nike-shoes? Or is it the parents personality? Or the fashion industry's? Children are becoming more and more aware about what's hip and what's not

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: The poor parents are not gonna be able to buy their children the brand-clothing that's "in" right now

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Class differences are clear today

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: What we can gain with the school uniform, except from the erasing of class differences, is a sense of belonging, gemenskap

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: School colors and their own symbol, "We in this school"

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: School uniforms are also a dividing line between home and school

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: The classroom today looks a lot like a playhouse

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I think that school uniforms signals that "now it is a little bit more serious"

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: It signals work, in the adult world we have our jobs own "uniforms"; policemen, politicians, mechanics, basically every workplace has one

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Why shouldn't the school have one? I personally think that a uniform would bring some order to the classroom and maybe even respect

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: My final argument is that the school uniform makes the teacher to take a closer look at his/her pupils

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: The teacher is a human being and human beings tend to judge before they take a look inside (if they bother to look further)

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I personally experienced that teachers assumed that I was a bad kid because I liked to dress in black at an early age

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I wore heavy metal-t-shirts and was immediately assumed to be a troublemaker

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Even if I didn't do anything that idea seemed to tag along

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Everything is not in your clothes, but it is easy to judge anyway

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: With school uniforms the teacher would treat the individual pupil after a better criteria

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: Final comment I think that the school uniform deserves a better name than the one it has today

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: People seems to think of the army or snobbish, English public schools when you start to talk about it

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
text: I don't see it as a status object, it makes the classroom more equal and fair to the pupils! It does not pose a threat to the personality of the pupil and it helps the pupils to focus on what is important - their future

title: Is the individual freedom in danger?
Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Psychologists have a very important function in our society

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: They support people in grief, help teenagers with their love-problems and do their best to cure patients with various psychological disorders

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: To become a psychologist you have to study for five years at the University and you have to have top-grades to get accepted to the Psychologist-program

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This means that most Psychologists probably are quite intelligent, but it does not necessarily mean that they are the most suitable persons for this job

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Instead the Universities should adopt a different admission system, admitting people on basis of their grades combined with an interview element

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Psychologists spend most their working time meeting and talking to people

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: The persons they talk to are presumably depressed, sad, unhappy, desperate or perhaps even suicidal

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: In these situations the Psychologist has to carry a great deal of responsibility, as it in fact might be a question of life or death

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: It is of absolute importance that the Psychologist is sensitive to the needs of the persons and also that he/she is able to approach them in a suitable way

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: An arrogant or careless person is therefore most unfitted for this proffession

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: The importance of the character and personality of the psychologist can't be stressed enough

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Of course he or she has to have some academic abilities to be able to learn different methods and approaches to identify and treat different kinds of psychological disorders

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: However, it doesn't matter how well they know these things if they can't approach the person in need for help, in an appropriate way

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This fact is not taken into any consideration by the admission system of today

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: The Universities simply admit people just looking at their grades without knowing anything about the person in question or about the qualities this person possess

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: He or she might even be a psycho-path for all they know

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This is perhaps going to the extremes, but in reality this can actually happen

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Intelligence and good grades are of course not a disadvantage for a psychologist

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: In contrast, they can absolutely be an advantage as in most professions

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: The thing about adding the interview element is that you take both the grades and the personality into consideration

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: So the persons getting admitted would probably still have very good grades as well as they would be more motivated and more suitable

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: The University can still have a cut-off limit, saying that you have to have a certain average to get the opportunity to qualify for the interview

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This means that the persons accepted will be able to handle the long and quite demanding education

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Looking at all these advantages you might wonder why the presented admission system isn't used (although some actually do use it, like the University of Linköping for example)

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: There is of course a reason for this and this reason is as always money

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: It is more expensive to adopt this kind of admission system, there is no doubt about that

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: However, it is rather short-sighted to think that way

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Not only would we get more suitable psychologists by adding the interview element, but it would also result in more motivated students

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This would in return mean that less people would drop-out from the education

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Since it is such an expensive education the University loses a lot of money, if the students just drop-out

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: So this would actually mean that the Universities could gain some money by adopting this system

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Not to mention how unfair it is for the more motivated and eager students, who could have entered instead of the ones dropping out

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: To sum up, using this admission system combining both the students' grades and results on the interview, would provide the society with better and more suitable psychologists

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This is something our whole society should be interested in

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: This does not mean that unintelligent persons get accepted, since the grades still are considered

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Of course it would be more expensive using this method, but that is looking short-sighted at the issue

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
text: Not only would society gain on it in the long run, but drop-outs would be less and thereby some money is actually gained

title: Admission to the Psychologist-program-The Importance of Suitable Psychologists
Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Two years ago, I worked extra in an old people's home

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The place filled me with anxiety

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The pensioners spent most of their time in their rooms, sleeping or reading (those who still had an eyesight good enough)

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Some sat looking out the window for hours

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: I caught myself hoping I would never be so old that society would have to take care of me

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: And when it came to my parents, I would of course take care of them myself

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: They could look after my children when they got home from school, tell them stories and play with them

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: It would be just like in the good old days, when the family took care of the elderly, without intervention or help from society

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Thinking about it now, however, I realize that the issue if far more complex than what I thought back then

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In the society of today, taking care of your elderly yourself would be very difficult, if not impossible

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: First, I would like to reveal some of the myths about the "good old days"

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: We tend to think that the elderly always have had a high status in the past

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: They enjoyed great respect, and their wisdom was widely known

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: However, this was not necessarily the case

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In almost all societies, old people have had a low status

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Of course, not very many people turned that old in those days, but those who did could expect having to struggle hard for their bread and butter

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In some mountain districts of Japan, it was common that the elderly drew back up in the mountains to die when they felt that they were getting weak

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: If they did so by their own free will is questioned by anthropologists

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Presumably they were subject to strong social pressure

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In other cultures where resources were limited, the elderly might even be killed when they became a burden to society

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Turning to our own country, old people have not been treated much better

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Those who came from relatively wealthy circumstances were usually put in the "exception cottage" while the oldest son (usually) took over the main house

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: If you did not have any children, or if they were incapable of taking care of you, you could look forward to being sold at an auction as a pauper

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Another option was to walk from farm to farm and do as much work as you were capable of in exchange for food and shelter

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Still, there were of course cases in which the family looked after their elderly in a way that worked well for both parties

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Why could this not be possible today? Well, there are two main reasons

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: First, the aging process is not the same today as it was then

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Today, medicines cure infections that were terminal a hundred years ago

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: People live longer for each generation, but the number of years they manage on their own has not increased very much

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Taking care of an Alzheimer patient is a full-time job today

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: It is admirable that senile persons were taken care of within the family a hundred years ago, but one has to bear in mind that the sufferer did probably not survive for as many years then as he/she may now

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Nutrition might be poor, and cuts and wounds the person got as a result from the disease often led to terminal infections

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The second reason, which is perhaps more important, is that society has changed

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In the old peasant community, people worked on the farm or at least not very far from it

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The families gathered at meals, and the women worked at home most of the time

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Thus, there was someone around who could have an eye to the elderly every now and then

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: This is rarely the case today

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: There would not be much use of having your elderly parents living with you if you were not there to mind them

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: In order to be able to take care of our elderly at home, we would have to adapt to a different standard of living

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Let us say that the average Swede needs some kind of help when he/she reaches the age of 70

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The children are then probably around 40-45, that is, they are at the top of their career

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: They would have to quit their jobs or start working part time to be able to look after their parents

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Presumably, this would be the woman's duty in most families, which would be setback for equality

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: It would also lead to a reduced income for the family

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Even with the parents' pensions included, it would be difficult financially to move to a larger apartment

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Thus, most families would have to adjust to living in overcrowded conditions

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Moreover, families are spread all over the country, or even the world, today

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: Many elderly would probably oppose having to move to a new city and leave their well-known surroundings

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: To me it is obvious that the state, not the family should look after the elderly

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: It might not be the ultimate solution, but it is probably the only possible one in this society

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: If we want to take care of our elderly ourselves, we have to start by making a thorough change of society as a whole

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: And what would that change look like? Going back to the peasant community if the 19th century is probably not a good alternative

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: I think state-funded facilities for eldercare are necessary in the society of today

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: To me, the question is not whether they should exist or not - what we need to discuss is how to improve them

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
text: The system we have today may very well be invalid, but to simply put the responsibility on the families will not do much help

title: Who Should Look After Grandma?
GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Giant transnational companies are carrying out a dangerous global experiment by introducing large numbers of genetically engineered foods into our diet

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Genetic manipulations can result in unanticipated harmful effects, and because genetically engineered foods are not tested sufficiently, this experiment not only jeopardizes the health of individuals, but could also lead to global food shortages and extensive ecological hazards

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Due to genetically modified food's unique and unknown nature, there is potential for adverse health side effects

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: It is impossible to predict the impact of genetic food, it is a matter of waiting for and observing future consequences

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: The cross-species transfers being made, such as between fish and tomatoes, would not happen in nature and may create new toxins, diseases, and weaknesses

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: An example of a possible health detrimental effect is resistance to widely used antibiotics

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: A new type of maize is currently being produced in the UK

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Maize is attacked by the corn borer mite, that chews through the stalks

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Genetically modified maize has been produced to be repellent to the mite

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: The marker gene in the new maize is resistant to ampicllin, an antibiotic

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Scientists are concerned that when this maize is eaten, the gene will come in contact and be passed to bacteria living in the human gut

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: These bacteria would then be resistant to the antibiotic

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: There is only a slight chance of this occurring, but there is always a first time

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Transferring animal genes into plants also raises important ethical issues for vegetarians and religious groups

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: It may also involve animal experiments that are unacceptable to many people

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Once a genetically modified organism has been released into the environment it can reproduce, move and even mutate

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: After its release, it cannot be recalled

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Unlike chemical or nuclear contamination, genetic pollution is perpetual

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: It can never be reversed or cleaned up; genetic mistakes will be passed on to all future generations of a species

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: The huge areas of genetically identical crops will influence the evolution of local pests, plants, wildlife, and disrupt surrounding ecosystems

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: There is no way of stopping genetically engineered plants from breeding with wild plants and contaminating them with foreign DNA strains

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Pollen from genetically engineered crops can be transferred to cultivated and wild plants up to a mile away

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: What would happen if the genes for insect and weed killer resistance, that have been introduced to crop plants, found their way into weeds? The result could be 'super weeds' which would force the development of more toxic herbicides

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Traditionally, farmers around the world would save some of their harvest to use as next year's seed

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: This practice would be prevented with genetically engineered crops under the terms of a license

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: There is also a possibility of companies selling sterile seeds

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: The companies developing genetically modified plants and animals wish to legally own these new life forms in order to protect their investments

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Supporters of genetic engineering say the gene splicing process is accurate and precise

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: In fact, the process of inserting genes is quite fortuitous and can damage normal genes

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Many weaknesses in plants, animals, and humans have their foundations in minute flaws in the genetic code

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Consequently, the random damage resulting from gene insertion will inevitably result in irreversible damage

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Genetically modified food activists promise gene technology will supply plentiful amounts of food to starvation stricken areas in the Third World

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Genetic engineering is profit driven rather than science driven

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Biotechnology research serves the desires of the rich rather than the needs of the poor

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Biotechnology is primarily a commercial activity that determines what is investigated, how it is applied and who are to benefit

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Biotechnology will concentrate power into the hands of a select few, which in turn will enhance farmers' dependence and force them to pay inflated prices for seed-chemical packages

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: Genetic engineering poses the greatest danger of any technology yet introduced

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: The damaging effects of genetic engineering are irreversible, but we have the capability to prevent a catastrophe before it occurs

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: We must take precautionary action if we are to protect ourselves, and all generations to come

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
text: We must refuse genetically modified food immediately, if we want to prevent an avalanche of deviant foods from permeating the market and placing the world at risk

title: GENETICALLY ENGINEERED FOODS: RISK TO HUMANITY
The Union: friend and foe
text: Fifty years ago the European Union was formed to prevent future wars in Europe and ensure the safety of democray

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Today it is a bureaucratic labyrinth

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: It's powerstructure is known for it's secrecy and the European people are at best sceptic and at worst uninterested

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: How are we supposed to make things better if no takes an interest? This paper deals with is the Swedish people's uninterest towards thr lack of EU democracy

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The first basic principle of Swedish democracy is that all power originates from the people

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: When Sweden joined EU there was a general feeling that we had given up on democracy as we know it

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The main worry, as I see it, was that of actual power

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The highest authorities in EU are The Ministry council and The Commision

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: These are not electoral bodies

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: They remain the same, uneffected, by elections to the EU parliament

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: You can not dismiss the Ministry council even if they've done something wrong

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Each country can, by general elections, dismiss a single member of the Council

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: But the body as a whole is uneffected

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: This obvious anti-democratic way of organizing the European Uninon is problematic

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The main thing is that the responsibility does not lie with the actual power

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: We, as individulas, are not allowed to view documents from the Council's or the Commision's proceeding nor can we hold them responsible for their actions

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The only electoral body in EU is the Parliament, here delegates are chosen by general elections in each country

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The parliament is, however, not a decision making body and can sometimes seem inconsequential

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: What I am concerned with is that people don't seem to care

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: People must be made aware that change is always possible

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: But it must be initiated, it does not happen by itself

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Is there then, no way for the people react to decisions made by these high authorities? Fortunately, there is

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: First we have the most drastical one

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: If a majority of registered voters in Sweden feel that the Council's or Commison's decisions are not in agreement with their own ideals then it is their right to vote for a swedish withdrawal from the European Union

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: This kind of extreme measure is, of course, a last resort but it should be kept in mind

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The next is one of internal investigation

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: In this case there is even a historical precedent

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The Commision before the present was subject to an investigation of that kind

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Due to some rumors, concerning corruption in the Commision, the EU Parliament initiated an investigation

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: On the basis of that investigation the Commision resigned

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: It could not be dismissed by Parliment but the political confidence for the Commision was so hurt that it became impossible to go on like before

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: This shows that there are still ways to reform the power structure

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: To point out these things is to make the people aware

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: One of the exciting things about EU is that it holds a tremendous opportuninty to renew ourselves as democratic fighters

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Sweden's principle of public access to official records has for a very long time have, for a long time, been taken for granted

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The result has been that almost no one takes adavantage of it

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Now things have changed

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: We can't investigate EU that easy, so we have to become more active

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: I do not refer directly to the public but rather to professionals like journalists and reporter

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: There is, however, a need for an increased general interest in EU matters

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: We have to become aware that the present structure of the European Union is not an unchangeable one

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Summing up I find that the European Union has a power structure that to us Swedes, and many other Europeans, seem undemocratic

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: But to change this we have become more active as citizens

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: There is today a predominant uninterest towards the Union and that uninterest is on of the greatest threats to our future society

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: We have to be aware of the possibilities of reform that are open

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: Furthermore that we have a new chance to fight for democracy

title: The Union: friend and foe

title: The Union: friend and foe
text: The Union remains a friend to have and a foe to fight
