title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: The fact that we don't hear anything now, doesn't mean that we won't hear anything in the future

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Another explanation is that the "civilizations" that are at a reachable distance from earth have not yet developed a technology that makes it possible to transmit signals

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: The possibility of extraterrestrial intelligence sometimes urge people to search for "little green men", riding around in their space crafts and hassling earthlings

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Would it be conceivable that at least one of all those possible civilizations is so technologically advanced that they have the ability to travel through space? I think so

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Unfortunately, people tend to put UFOs in the same category as ghosts and other supernatural occurrences

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: We know that there is nothing supernatural about a Space Shuttle, but to people in the Middle Ages it would have seemed magical or supernatural

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: So, UFOs could probably best be explained as an extrapolation of where our own technology might be thousands of years from now

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: UFO sightings have been reported all over the world, but one particular incident is well documented and rather well known

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: It is the incident in Roswell, New Mexico in the year 1947

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Colonel William Blanchard, Commander of the 509th Bomb Group, issued a press release stating that the wreckage of a "crashed disc" had been recovered

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Within hours, a second press release was issued from the U

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: S

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: government saying that the wreckage in fact was a weather balloon

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: People who knew William Blanchard say that he would never have made a fool of him self and the Air Force by ordering a press release about something so out of the ordinary without being certain he was correct

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Credible witnesses, including retired generals, have testified that the original press release issued by Blanchard was correct

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Why would the government cover-up a story if it only concerned a weather balloon? It had to be a space craft that crashed at Roswell

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: There are three possible reasons for a cover-up: fear of mass panic, national security, and concern about offending religious groups

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: First and foremost, we have been living with AIDS, terrorism and violent crimes for such a long time, that a confirmation of extraterrestrial intelligence would hardly create a mass panic in this day and age

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: In conclusion to these facts I would say that there has to be life on other planets

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: This is not merely my own belief, but there have been far too many credible witnesses, like U

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: S

title: Life on other planets:fiction or fact?
text: Air Force pilots, reporting UFOs, too much government secrecy and too many mathematically proven possibilities of extraterrestrial intelligence, that the only possible explanation to all of this is that there is life in space

text: Mercy killing, or euthanasia, the Greek word often used for it, is a topic lively debated

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: Is it right to interfere with the course of nature? If yes, to what extent? To define the expression thoroughly from the very beginning, it can be divided into two forms: active or passive euthanasia

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: An example of the passive form would be for instance to turn off a life-sustaining apparatus like a respirator, while if a doctor would give a patient a lethal injection that would be an action classified as active euthanasia

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: It is of great importance to understand the difference between those two when looking at the legislation in different countries

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: In Sweden, only passive mercy killing is allowed, whereas in the Netherlands active mercy killing has been lawfully practised for more than 25 years

text: What I would like to state is that the Swedish legislation of today is insufficient - I think we should permit active euthanasia, but within strict guidelines

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: Those strict guidelines are of course part of the main problem here, raising important questions about how we can ever work out and agree on where to draw the lines

text: Picture a close relative lying helpless, just waiting for death to arrive and relive his or her pain

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active
text: Morally, I don't see the wrong with giving this person a lethal injection if he or she desires that

title: Yes to euthanasia - passive and active

text: In our time and culture humans generally consider themselves to be the most intelligent species on earth

text: In the States they have done some researches on which it is that will be convicted to death and it turned out to be those incapable of defending them selves

text: It turned out that courts have a tendency to look upon the prosecutes race, religion, social status and so on

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?
text: 1977 to 1998 500 people got executed and of the 81

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?
text: 8 % had killed white even though black and white are victims of the same amount of crimes

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?
text: So capital punishment is also a matter of race discrimination

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?
text: One of my favourite arguments against capital punishment is that it is against the law in Sweden

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?

title: Capital punishment in Sweden?
text: Statistics from other countries shows that brutal criminal acts come as a result of political instability, poverty and other disruption in our society

Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: "Once upon a time a Divine God came down to earth

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: He said to the people: 'You shall work eight hours a day

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: This is never to be changed

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: ' And then he disappeared again

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: " When you listen to the Swedish debate about shorter working-hours, you are sometimes inclined to believe that the opponents of this necessary reform actually hold the fairy-tale above as a truth

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: However, the eight-hour working-day was not a divine decision, but a political one which was a result of the nineteenth century's great changes in society

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: In fact, the eight-hour day is just the latest step in a long line of working-hour reforms, all of which have created better conditions for the people

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: So what could be more natural than to shorten the working-day even further? Shorter hours will first of all enable vast improvements concerning people's health

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: As we all know, the society of today puts a lot of pressure on its citizens

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: There is much evidence of that

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: A few examples: A lot of people suffer from different kinds of stress diseases, like gastritis, and a growing problem is burning out, especially among women with low-paid, stressful jobs

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Some 250,000 people in Sweden take psychoactive drugs just to be able to cope with daily life

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: It must be clear to everyone that something has to be done about these alarming problems

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Setting a new norm for working-hours is the easiest, the quickest, and the most effective means for that

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: However, it is important to stress the fact that shortening of the working-day is not just a health concern for certain groups, but will lead to a better life for everyone

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: If we work less, we will have more time to spend with our family and our friends, and more time to take care of each other

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Parents will have more time and energy to look after their children, which will eventually help preventing youth criminality and drug abuse in the future

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Shorter hours will also lead to better possibilities for equality between men and women

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: At present, both women and men generally work at least eight hours a day, which leaves very little time for domestic work

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: It turns out that in the end, it is usually the woman who takes the main responsibility at home, on top of her professional duties

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: If both the man and the woman worked less, they would more easily find ways to cooperate and to share home duties equally

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: What about the costs of this reform, then? Opponents argue that the Swedish State won't be able to pay for it unless people's salaries are lowered together with their level of working-hours

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: And as they assume that most people will be reluctant to shorter hours if it implies lower salaries, they claim that the reform is unrealistic

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: But there are several mistakes in that argument

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: First of all, it might be that people don't want lower salaries, but most people would gladly accept less rise in pay, if it leads to better working conditions as a whole

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Secondly, the finances of the State are much better today, in the year 2000, than they were ten years ago

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Thirdly, recent studies have shown that even if there will be a certain initial cost for the State, so much benefit will result from this reform in the coming years, that we could on the contrary not afford to spare it

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: A lot of countries have already begun the process of shortening the general working-day below the former eight-hour standard

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: For instance, France and Italy have fixed the new norm to 35 hours a week, that is, a seven-hour working-day

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: In Germany, metal industry has adopted the same norm, and there is a present discussion going on about the possibility of a 32-hour week

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: Inquiries show that a majority of the Swedish people already say yes to shorter working-hours

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: So why should Sweden refuse to join this sound development? No, of course it is just a matter of time before we step into the future and work less than today

title: Shorter Hours: Just a Matter of Time
text: It is about time to work less and live more!

title: Why believe in God?
text: "In the beginning God created heaven and earth

title: Why believe in God?
text: " Genesis 1:1 These are the first words in the Bible

title: Why believe in God?
text: Christianity, our world's most widespread religion, circle around this statement

title: Why believe in God?
text: But what are the reasons for us to believe in these words? I will in this essay give three different arguments for Gods existence and according to them why it is logical for people of today to believe in God

title: Why believe in God?
text: My first argument for Gods existence is an argument based on religious experience

title: Why believe in God?
text: Many individuals and groups have claimed religious experience so why should we not believe them

title: Why believe in God?
text: These people are what one might be called trustworthy citizens

title: Why believe in God?
text: We believe them when they testify in a court trial so why should we not believe them when they bear witness about an religious experience

title: Why believe in God?
text: The problem with this argument is that people also testify to have seen UFOs and other unnatural phenomenon's

title: Why believe in God?
text: But the sheer number of individuals who testify to have been subject to miracles and other religious experiences gives weight to the argument

title: Why believe in God?
text: It is highly unlikely that so many people all over the world should be wrong

title: Why believe in God?
text: But I have to admit the possibility that there might be an global mistake when it comes to this argument

title: Why believe in God?
text: Still the fact is - the number of believers give weight to the fact that there is good reason to believe in God

title: Why believe in God?
text: Cosmos - my second reason comes from the fact that we, our planet and the whole universe exist

title: Why believe in God?
text: The question that must be asked is: Why is there a universe? Everything inside the universe is depending on something else for the reason for its existence

title: Why believe in God?
text: For example - The reason for my existence is my mother and father, but what is the ultimate reason for my existence? It is not my mother and father because the reason for their existence is their parents and so on back trough history

title: Why believe in God?
text: So the answer to the question must be something else

title: Why believe in God?
text: If one accepts Darwin's teachings about the evolution of the species we will still in the end come to a point where we run out of reasons

title: Why believe in God?
text: One might then answer the question with Big Bang

title: Why believe in God?
text: But what was the reason for Big Bang? The answer has to be something outside our universe, a transcendental being - God

title: Why believe in God?
text: My third argument for God's existence is what might be called a design argument

title: Why believe in God?
text: It takes its stand in the fact that our world is a marvel of orderly function

title: Why believe in God?
text: Everything inside our world is working in harmony with everything else

title: Why believe in God?
text: This argument is old and not a specific Christian argument from the beginning

title: Why believe in God?
text: It was first recorded of Xenophon in 390 BC when he quoted Socrates as saying "With such signs of forethought in the design of living creatures, can you doubt they are the work of choice or design?" The argument is fairly simple, the intricacies of our world are in them self proof for the fact that our world is a creation of a higher being

title: Why believe in God?
text: If one open a pocket watch and look inside one realise that this miracle of order just cannot exist out of pure chance there have to be a mind behind its construction

title: Why believe in God?
text: It is the same thing whit our world and all things inside of it

title: Why believe in God?
text: The answer to the question of who the designer is is therefor logical - God

title: Why believe in God?

title: Why believe in God?

title: Why believe in God?

Women - persons or objects?
text: Attractive female bodies are being used everywhere to sell almost anything

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Women are exposed half-naked in all kinds of comersials in order to sell items and services that has nothing to do with these women

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: This exposure of women's bodies results in many negative side effects

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Women become more and more insecure about themselves

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: They degrade themselves

text: They will never become equal to men as long as we put up with this flaw in our culture which tell us: A women is solely a sex object

text: Women are being exploited to such an extent that it makes them feel very insecure about themselves

text: Girls start to worry about their apperance when they are very young and put themselves on diets when they in fact are skinny as can be

text: Teenage girls suffer from aneroxia more than ever

text: When massive underwear campaignes are going on, with perfect half-naked women wherever you turn, the problems accelerate

text: Another effect that comes from women's insecurity is the fact that they have become more jealous and competative toward their own sex

text: The sexiest women are being miscredited by other women, because they get the attention from men that insecure women fight so hard for

text: A very serious outcome of our culture, where so much emphas is being put on good looks and youth, is that women have started having breast implants and plastic surgery

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: It is not only expensive but it is also dangerous

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Being this insecure about themselves, many women tend to degrade themselves in order to attract men's attention

text: Music and magazines are being marketed by female bodies and in the movies most characters for women imply showing off their bodies and not so much their talent

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: The more women let themselves being exploited, the more men objectifies the opposite sex

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: When men are attracted to, or lust for, a woman she gets attention which does not really help her build her self-esteem, but it gives her some sort of worth

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: This sense of worth is very deluding

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Since a woman, if caught in a bad circle like this, needs a constant refill of attention to feel good about herself, and since she lets other people decide her value for her she can never feel content

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: It is a constant struggle for women to become equal to men

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: The enormous exposure on women as sex-objects makes this struggle even harder

title: Women - persons or objects?

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Other women disrespect them as well, because of envy, and seek to thwart more attractive women's plans to advance

title: Women - persons or objects?

title: Women - persons or objects?
text: Good looks and youth are no longer something to admire and enjoy but are the only qualities in a woman that really counts

The right to bear tear gas
text: Sweden is a leading land of woman's rights, yet women lack the self-confidence to go out for a walk after nightfall

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Why is this so? Apart from the fact that there are even less law enforcement officers employed at the same time the crimes against women are on the increase

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: In a land that was once considered open and relatively free society women have become prisoners of there own homes

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: That is why I think it is so important for legalization of tear gas as a deterrent and self-defense mechanism against the increasing violent attacks against our sister, mothers and wives

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: It provides a sense of security, physical equality, and is a cost effective alternative

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Tear gas is more than personal protection; it gives women a sense of security and a feeling of control in their lives

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: In a recent survey conducted by one of Sweden's largest unions, Industriarbetarförbundet, women were asked if they felt safe going out after dark

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The results were astounding, more than 70% of the women surveyed replied that they did not feel safe going out after dark and most of them surveyed said that they avoided going out after dark due to fear of attack

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: They were, asked if having some form of protection, such as a dog or tear gas, would make them more inclined to go outdoors after dark without company

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Over 50% of those surveyed answered that they would feel more comfortable given some sort of protection

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Many of the single women that were included in the survey gave protection as one of the reasons for having a dog

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: If tear gas were legal in Sweden many big city women would presumably choose to have a tear gas instead of a dog! Tear gas gives women physical equality, women should have the possibility to be equal in strength to men

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Men in general are physically stronger than women are, given a few exceptions

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The legalization of tear gas would allow a woman to defend her self from a naturally physically stronger person

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: In Canada, where tear gas is legal, men and women carry tear gas when out walking or trekking in nature simply to prevent the attack from a grizzly bear or other types of wild animals

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Should not the attackers of innocent city women be treated as animals? Then one can argue that the tear gas would also become available for the perpetrators as well

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: There is already illegal trade of tear gas and other self - defense weapons in Sweden today

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The majority of people that deal in this type of trade are men; the large majority of them have criminal backgrounds

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The perpetrators already have access to these types of weapons, why can't innocent women and elderly have them as well? There is not enough police force to make women feel safe on the streets today

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The lack of police in our big cities makes the legalization of tear gas even more necessary

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: According to Leif GW Persson, professor in criminology at the University of Stockholm only 5% of all reports on physical abuse and assault result in convictions in court

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Money is of major concern in many peoples' lives, not to say the least for single women

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Tear gas is a cost effective alternative to purchasing a dog

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Many women would like to buy a dog for security but simply can't afford the eventual cost that an animal can bring to the household

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: In the survey by Industriarbetarförbundet, another reason women did not get a dog was that they already felt like they were tied to their homes

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Having a dog would mean even less freedom for them since they would have to walk, feed and keep it company

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: The legalization of tear gas would allow women access to the night as well as the day! Tear gas does more than provide a sense of security, physical equality and cost-effective alternative it provides a choice, not to be a victim

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: These three reasons are just a few of the explanations, which are important to discuss in the argument of legalizing tear gas

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: These thoughts are not mine alone, but represent many of the women in our society

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: With tear gas women wouldn't have to learn ugly methods, or difficult maneuvers to defend off an attacker, such as eye scratching or kick to the genitals, which only the young and physically capable women can do

title: The right to bear tear gas
text: Violence doesn't beat violence; tear gas provides a chance to get away before becoming a victim

title: The right to bear tear gas

Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Western society is becoming more and more superficial

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: People, women especially, are more often judged by their physical appearance than by who they are

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: This affects women in many ways, none of which is positive

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: I believe the media, in their often stereotypical picturing of women, could be blamed for having a great part in this

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Therefore I think the media should change their approach to how they show women

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: In this essay I will discuss how women are portrayed, which message this gives and the effects of it

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: The potrayal of women in the media does not very much correspond to reality

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: The female characters in films and TV series, as well as the models shown in magazines, send out a distorted picture of the perfect woman

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: These people are supposed to be good role models, but the only characteristics they have in common are that they are all very skinny and very good-looking

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Other qualities, such as intelligence, strength of character or kindness, do not seem as important

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: In the magazines the beautiful faces are accompanied by headlines about how to lose weight with the new diet, or how to make yourself more desireable with the latest fashion and make-up

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: It is very seldom that a plump girl or a mature woman, with wrinkles, are seen on the covers of these magazines

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: To make things worse, the pictures are retouched to make the models even more beautiful

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Flaws, like wrinkles, moles or anything unwanted, are easily removed to make the perfect picture

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: If the top models are not good enough the way they are, how are the rest of us supposed to be? This message, of the importance of beauty, is very disturbing

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: By constantly giving advice on how to lose weight, what make-up to use or which exercises to do to get "the perfect body", the magazines are more or less saying: You are not good enough, you are not thin enough and you are not beautiful enough

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Why this focus on looks? Why is it so important to be attractive? Women tend to say it is for their own sake, because it makes them feel good, and they probably believe this to be true

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: But the underlying reason why is because they think that is how the men want them

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: It is what the media tell them every day

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: I do recognize that there are a few newspapers, magazines and TV shows that have a better approach, but this is not nearly enough

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: The majority of the media still need to change their attitude towards women, because what they tell us today is that a woman needs be thin and beautiful

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Only then can she find a man and be socially accepted, happy and successful

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: This distorted picture of women are having severe effects, especially among young and insecure girls

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Not realizing that what they are pesented with are products, manufactured by the media, and not real people, they think they are expected to look like the models and the actresses

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Not too long ago a junior school in Stockholm had to impose a ban on cosmetics; the seven-year-old girls kept turning up late for class, as they "had to" put on their make-up

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Another already common problem is young girls going on diets, and starving themselves

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Sadly enough illnesses like anorexia and bulimia are becoming more frequent, and are affecting younger and younger children

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: Older teenagers and women are also influenced, spending thousands of pounds on cosmetical surgery; breast enlargements, liposuction and facelifts

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: However, these operations seldom make very much difference on what is important: The feeling of not being good enough, which probably caused the desire to change the appearance, will still be there

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: I believe if the media showed a more diverse and less superficial picture of women, these problems would not be as common

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: As the media have have so much influence on society, they also have a moral responsibility towards it

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: In presenting a stereotypical and superficial image of women, the media are giving the message that the physical appearance is what is important

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: This generates a feeling of inadequacy among many girls and women and creates many problems for them

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media
text: The media can, and therefore should, change this by taking a different approach to how they portray women

title: Young, beautiful and successful: The superficial portrayal of women in the media

THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: I am going to argue that it's good to have the Austrian Freedom Party in the Austrian government and I am going to do it out of a democratic point of view because the AFP has been accused for being undemocratic

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Listen to the people My strongest argument for the AFP's right to be in government is that the election in Austria was free and democratic in every possible way and that the AFP became the second largest party with 26

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: 9 percentage points and that's why I feel the party has every right to be in the government

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: One could argue that 73 per cent didn't vote for the party and that it shouldn't be in the government, but I feel that people holding this opinion forget two very important things about Austrian elections

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: First point is that it is very rare for a party to get more than fifty per cent of the votes which means that there is always going to be people whose voices are not heard

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: For the second, it is important to see where AFP's votes came from and one notices that it was mostly young, less educated men who voted for AFP, which means that the party managed to activate those who don't usually vote for any party and I see AFP's role in the government as an important sign for these people that it is possible to affect things in a democratic way, which I am going emphasize in the following chapters

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: AFP needs to show where it stands Many argue that the Conservative and Social Democratic parties should have had built a coalition in order to block the AFP from entering the government, but I think that's not the way to act in a democracy

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: I see the AFP as the Austrian people's revolt against old parties that have had the power for the last fifty years and in a democracy it is very important to respect election results and in the latest election the biggest winner was actually the AFP and ignoring this expression of discontent would have made, I think, the AFP even more popular and would've enabled them to continue their policy of populist soundbacks, while in government they have to take more responsibility for their comments because the party now represents Austria

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Another thing is that it is easier for the other parties to counter the AFP when it has power and a position to think about, but it also gives the AFP a chance to show what they stand for and after this, are Europeans but especially Austrians entitled to draw conclusion of the AFP and not before the AFP got in power, a mistake that the EU made

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: It made it maybe because it's afraid that the same kind of development is also possible in other European countries

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Freedom of opinion I think that a democratic country has to have a freedom of opinions, no matter what they are because no one has the wisdom of what's right or wrong and I think that democratic parties have to have the capacity to counter "dark forces" with their democratic values, otherwise there is something very wrong in the democracy

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Many have suggested that the AFP should be forbidden because it's undemocratic

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: I think it would be very undemocratic to forbid a party with the support of thirty per cent of the population, forbidding would only make the party even more popular, the popularity of the AFP is a sign of people's malaise

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Different freedoms are democracy's biggest strength and its biggest weakness too, but it is impossible to have a democracy and consider forbidding something as illegal without proof

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Conclusion My conclusion is that it is positive to have the AFP in the Austrian government and this because of three points

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: First if all, it's very good for the country to show that voting means something and that people actually can change things in a democratic way

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Secondly it's a good because the AFP has to show where it stands and can't act irresponsibly without losing its support

title: THE AUSTRIAN FREEDOM PARTY IS DEMOCRACY IN PRACTICE
text: Thirdly it shows that freedom of opinion means freedom of all opinions and not only those that might be politically correct, the AFP works as an alarm clock for all democracies in Europe that they have to fight against racism with democratic values

text: We hear a lot of negative things about immigrants these days

text: We use terms like "we and them" and in some cases we only mention immigrants when problems are being discussed

A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: An often-debated topic in Swedish schools, is capital punishment

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Even thought this topic has been thoroughly discussed, concerning the aspects of moral, economic issues, and that it is cruel, inhuman, and degrading punishment

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I would like to highlight some other aspects

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: My opinion on the topic is neither black nor white, but somewhere in the greyarea

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I hope to clear, why I am not sure of my position and one of the reasons, is surely: An aspect that I have just recently heard of against the death penalty in the United States is that some states are discriminating on racial issues, which to me is scaring

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Judges and attorneys, who are elected or appointed, they should be blind, like the statue of the judicial system claims to be, especially in a democratic country, which everyday claims not to judge by race, sex or religious beliefs

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: This does not only accrue in the US, but what is more frightening is, that a rich country like the United States of America cannot guarantee a fair trail

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Another aspect of capital punishment is that of children, by that I mean humans under the age of 18 years old, who are sentenced to death

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: More than one hundred countries have laws specifically excluding the execution of child offenders

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Although six countries have executed children in the 1990's

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Among this countries are Saudi Arabia, Iran, Pakistan, which are all ruled under Islamic laws, which means they follow the rules written down in the Qu'ran

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: There it does have a deterrent affect

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I think you cannot compare these countries with for instant the USA! The States have executed ten children in the 1990's

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I do not believe that a person of the age of eighteen years is mature

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Therefore, I think if you are going to have the death penalty, you must not have it as a mandatory sentence

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Should we not protect and help or treat children, who have committed a crime and give them a second chance? A third point of view is that almost half of all the countries in the world still have the death penalty in their lawframes, though not all of them use it

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I would like to focus a bit on Thailand and Singapore

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: In 1996, the first execution took place in nine years in Thailand

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: This execution was supposed to set an example for other inmates, according to the Ministry of Interior

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Scientific studies have consistently failed to prove that the death penalty deters crime more effectively than other punishments

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Moreover, as for the deterrent affect in Thailand in 1996 120 people under sentence of death had their sentences commuted to life imprisonment in a Royal Pardon

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: However, this did not include people convicted of drug offences! On the contrary, Singapore has since 1994 executed well over 150 people for drug offences, including many foreigners

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Despite the mandatory use of the death penalty for those convicted of drug trafficking, drug addiction has increased in Singapore

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: Drug addiction rose with 30 % between 1990 and 1993, according to Singapore's 5 rehab-clinics

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: According to, Amnesty International Singapore probably executes more people than the reported figures and that they do not get a fair trail before being executed

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: To summarize my essay I would say, I am basically against the death penalty, but when I try to put myself in to the context of me getting abuse or killed, or anyone close to me, I start to wonder if I wouldn't want that person to be executed? Just a few days ago, I discussed the death penalty with some friends

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: One of them was against it in every aspect, the other one was for the death penalty for serve crimes, which she categorized been serial killings, where a person had committed more than one murder

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: I do not believe that the death penalty is actually a penalty for these people, because I do not think they cherish life that much

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: However, in the subjective position I think if the offender was over 25 or so, I would probably think he deserves death, if he did not have any mitigating circumstances like a terrible childhood or mental illness

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: So I would say that I am for the death penalty, if we could have perfect circumstances and being 100% sure, through technical evidence

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
text: However the world will never be prefect, therefore I think we should abolish the death penalty and find a better solution

title: A NEVER CEASING DEBATE
