title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Immigrants need faster answers regarding the possibilities for them to stay in Sweden

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: It takes to long before they get an answer to if they can stay in Sweden as it is today

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: There have been immigrants who have lived in Sweden for five years before the authorities decide that they can't stay

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: That is not acceptable

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: We have to take a stand and make a law that limits the amount of time that the authorities get to make their decision

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Such a law would make the lives of the immigrants much easier and reduce the amount of stress that uncertainty causes

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: They would also gain their self esteem back as they would be allowed to work again

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Immigrants shouldn't have to stay in Sweden long enough to see it as their home before they get their answer from the authorities

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: It is better to find that you can't stay after only a few months than finding it out after several years in Sweden

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Not knowing if you can stay in Sweden or not causes mental stress and suffrage

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Some immigrants don't even have the strength to learn the Swedish language because they find that it is not worth the effort since they don't know what lies in their future and if they can stay or not

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Many immigrants get depressed because of the uncertainty that they are living in

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: The fact that they often have gone through severe traumatically experiences due to war and other terrible circumstances, doesn't make it any easier on them

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Ad to that the constant fear they have that one day, just as they are beginning to feel secure about getting to stay, someone comes knocking on their door to tell them that they have to go back to the hell which they barely managed to get away from years ago

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: They have tried to build a new life here in Sweden and now as that world falls apart too, who knows if they have the will or the strength to try again? As for the Children, those who are young when they first come to Sweden or who wereborn here adapt our culture and our language

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: To send them away to the country they came from, after as long time in Sweden as most immigrants spend today, is not to send them Back to their home country, but to send them Away from their home country

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: Can you imagine what a psychological horror it must be for a parent to tell his/her child that you have to leave all of your friends, your school, your entire life to move to a country that you can't even remember? How do you explain that to a seven-year-old child? A law regarding time limits for the authorities to make their decision would prevent that kind of suffering

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: There are people who would argue that a law of this kind would make it hard for the authorities to investigate each case properly, others might argue that it will cost to much money, but we have to see to the humanity part of it

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: It is not human to let people wait for years only to tell them "sorry, you can't stay", and then force them to change country and culture again

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: It can't be accepted that it sometimes takes up to five years to find out if a certain immigrant's reasons to stay in Sweden qualifies as legit or not

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: We have to speed up the process and I think that the only way to do so is to make a law which shows that we won't tolerate the mental abuse that immigrants go through to day while waiting for an answer

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: I would also argue that a law of this kind will actually save money, since the immigrants who get to stay will get a working permit much faster, which means that they will start paying taxes and provide for themselves much sooner than they would otherwise

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: When it comes to those immigrants who won't be allowed to stay in Sweden, we will actually save money by making fast decisions there too

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: By making fast decisions we only have to support them for a few months instead of several years before we send them back to where they came from

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: A time limiting law for the authorities will therefor benefit not only the immigrants'mental health and social life, it would also benefit Sweden's economy and future growth

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
text: This is why I feel that it is of extreme importance that we stipulate this law as soon as possible

title: HUMANITY BY LAW IN SWEDEN?
FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Through history people in most cultures have tried their best to deny the very existence of gay people and the possibility of those non-existing gay people having children were thus totally unimaginable

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Today we know that homosexuality is simply a natural variation of the human species, something we can find in many other species too

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: However, far too many people choose to ignore science when it is at odds with their own prejudices and when it comes to gay couples having children this is more true than ever

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: This issue is often made into a question of equality between gays and heterosexuals

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: I think we should instead focus on the best interest of the children

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: I find it hard to believe that people objecting to gays raising children base their opinion on anything but their own prejudice misconceptions of what homosexuality is about

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: For reasons I will state further on I am convinced that their misdirected goodwill causes great harm to the children they claim to protect

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Millions of children have no parents

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: They are left all alone and far too often they end up in the hands of strangers who use them cruelly as underpaid labour or even worse as prostitutes

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: With this in mind, I think it is preposterous that "well-meaning" people want to deprive these children the opportunity of getting a loving family and a chance of a good education just because their self-appointed benefactors, whom they have never met, says that their could-be parents are not fit to raise children just because they're gay

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: I can't possibly see why it would be better for a child to grow up in the gutter than be living in a good home with parents, gay or not, who love and provide for it

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Interestingly enough these people often base their standpoint on the idea that children in gay families will have a hard time in society because of other people's prejudices against their parents lifestyle

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Isn't it strange that they are totally unable to identify themselves with the ones that are most likely to constitute this threat to the children? It's a simple fact that other children do not constitute any real threat in this respect

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Numerous studies have been carried out both in the US and in the UK, and to some people's surprise it has not been possible to establish any scientific proof that children with gay parents are victimised more often than other children

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: It is also important to note that when we pass legislation that deem some children to a life in poverty and oppression based on some dubious, and indeed also unscientific, idea that gay parents would only be a burden to them and wouldn't be able to provide them with anything better, we also show no concern whatsoever for all the children who are already living with gay parents

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: When a society starts passing laws based on prejudice ideas we are in on very deep water

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Legislation that doesn't allow gays to adopt children also has a negative effect for all the children, and there are many, who are already living in gay families

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: It breeds a notion that the love, care and security these children get from their parents aren't worth as much as if their parents were heterosexual

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: Sadly, this is in part true but simply because the legislation effectively neglects giving these children the same legal security as the children of heterosexual families

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: If the biological parent in a gay relationship dies, the partner has no legal right to get the custody of the child even if she/he has in reality been a parent to the child since its birth

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: A result of this it could very well be that a blood relative might step in and claim the child if the biological parent dies

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: This means that a child who have already lost one parent risks loosing its other parent with the full consent of the law

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: I fail to see how this could possibly be in the best interest of that particular child

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: If we are to act in the best interest of the children we will have to put our own prejudices aside

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: There are thousands of good and decent people out there who wants nothing more than to give a child a loving home

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: There are others who just wants to be able to truthfully promise their children that they will always be there for them even if their biological parent should die

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
text: It is not a human right to have children, but it is my firm conviction that it is the right of every single child to have good and loving parents, may the parents be hetero-, bi-, or homosexual

title: FAMILY TIES: CHILDREN'S RIGHT TO LIVE IN GAY FAMILIES
All the spongers out to work
text: It is much too easy for unemployed people to get money from the state nowadays

title: All the spongers out to work
text: I am sure that there are a lot of people who receive unemployment benefit by force of circumstances in our society, but I am convinced that there are even more people who remorselessly take advantage of this easy way to get money

title: All the spongers out to work
text: In my opinion people receiving unemployment benefit should absolutely be required to do unpaid work in the public sector

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It would partly prevent other social problems in society, partly it would make the unemployment benefit system more purposeful

title: All the spongers out to work
text: The unemployment benefit system can't be particularly good when it makes work uneconomic

title: All the spongers out to work
text: If you are offered equal or even better pay without working than if you were working who would not accept it and be grateful? The system should encourage people to find a new job and go back out to work, but that is not the case and the system is impudently misused

title: All the spongers out to work
text: There are rules to limit this misuse, for example you have to look for a job all the time in order to be entitled to the benefit

title: All the spongers out to work
text: Even so, that rule does not seem to work too good

title: All the spongers out to work
text: People have to apply for jobs and they have to go to job interviews, but it is easy for them to make themselves not suited for the jobs if they want to by just giving an unsuitable image to employers

title: All the spongers out to work
text: To require these people to do unpaid work in the public sector, to demand them to earn their benefit, would encourage them to seek and also to receive proper employment

title: All the spongers out to work
text: There is even ruder way to take advantage of the unemployment benefit system

title: All the spongers out to work
text: There are people who sign up themselves as unemployed and at the same time they work on the side

title: All the spongers out to work
text: In other words they get both the pay from the state and a wage for their jobs on the black market

title: All the spongers out to work
text: To employ these abusers by public sector against the benefit would stop them working on the side

title: All the spongers out to work
text: High unemployment is reflected in many social problems in our society

title: All the spongers out to work
text: There are a lot of drinking problems, mental deseases, domestic violence and other problems among unemployed people

title: All the spongers out to work
text: The fact that work is so dominating factor in whole society also makes it an important part of our self-esteem

title: All the spongers out to work
text: Unemployment is generally considered as something negative

title: All the spongers out to work
text: Unemployed people, especially the long-term unemployed, are often afraid of imprinting, something that easily leads to self-accusation and depression

title: All the spongers out to work
text: They feel that they are not good enough, that they are not competent -not worth a job

title: All the spongers out to work
text: The longer people are unemployed the higher the thresold into working life will be

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It is dangerous because a part of these people eventually fall outside of the society's solidarity

title: All the spongers out to work
text: To offer these people work against the unemployment benefit would keep them in touch with working life and give them a feeling of belonging to society

title: All the spongers out to work
text: In that way it would further prevent many of those social problems that we now are struggeling with

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It really is time to establish the workfare system and get all society's spongers out to work

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It would put an end to the bold misuse of the unemployment benefit system and prevent the misuse in future

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It would simply make the whole system more purposeful, focusing on encouraging people to get back to work

title: All the spongers out to work
text: Above all the workfare system would reduce, and in future prevent, the great number of the various social problems resulted from the high unemployment

title: All the spongers out to work
text: It would make many people, who now are living in constant anxiety, self-accusation and depression, to feel considerably better, and in that way contribute to whole society's well-being

title: All the spongers out to work
Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: The Department of Education in Sweden has suggested that the amount of PE lessons at school should be reduced

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: The arguments are that there is not enough money, and the money there is should be spent on the more important theoretical subjects

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: These points, however, seem quite odd to the fact that during the recent years, many alarming reports have told us that people in today's high-standard society get too little physical exercise

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Due to this, fatness and heart diseases have increased, and general condition and health has worsened - even among young people

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Below, I aim to pick out some points that must be considered before any cuts are made on the PE subject

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: First, it must be emphasised that we as human beings are made to activate ourselves

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: As children, we are out at play many hours a day

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Then, suddenly, at the age of seven, we are required to sit still, concentrating, in a desk at school

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: And what happens? Children not used to long schooldays tend to get restless and unconcentrated if they cannot fulfil their needs of exercise

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Of course, there are breaks, but is 15-20 minutes sufficient? Not all schoolyards can provide good opportunities to play; far too many only consist of dull pavement

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: The result is that many pupils sit still even during the breaks, and thus it is easy to see that the PE lessons are unvaluable times for physical exercise

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Here I can mention an article I read about a week ago: In a school in southern Sweden, a plan is carried out in which the pupils have one hour of PE every single day

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Though the schooldays are now 45 minutes longer, the teachers say that the children are more alert and concentrated now

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: 1 Does not this show us that a reduction cannot be sufficiently justified? However, one can never avoid the fact that there will always be pupils who really dislike sports in all forms, maybe especially at higher levels when sports often tend to become competitive and connected with performance anxiety - it is not a game any longer

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: There will probably be a number of students who will skip PE lessons if there are "too many" of these per week

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: But although the fact that many students do not put a great value in PE may seem like an argument for reducing the amount of hours, it is not

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: On the contrary, it is probably these students who need exercise more than others

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: The important thing is, as I see it, that there is time for activities, whatever the sort, during schooltime: a quick walk or a swim, for example

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: PE is important, but must not become a burden! School must realise their great responsibility of young people's general health

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: There are many families that never come in contact with any sports activities or other physical exercise, and, if PE and health education is further reduced from the school schedules, where shall the children of such families get their understanding of the importance of good health from? Undoubtedly, school sports are a natural way of getting in contact with physical exercise, and this is getting even more important nowadays, when a great deal of the highly esteemed, prestige-filled occupations are to a large extent sedentary

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: People use their cars even if they only are travelling a short distance, and consequently, this mentality is many times transferred onto the children

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: Furthermore, reports are showing us the increasing number of overweight people, and fatness can lead to heart diseases and reduced life length - all due to lesser exercise

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: This alarming development can only be stopped if we realise the need of a general understanding of health

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: And is it not just as important that school provides young people with that general understanding, as with general knowledge in subjects like language, social studies and science? So what I would like to say is that although an understanding of different sports and general health issues such as smoking, alcohol and drugs (which are a part of basic education in Sweden) is not directly significant to an education in other subjects, it is still the school's responsibility to provide students with general knowledge at all levels

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: After all, people who are at school today are going to take care of tomorrow's society

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
text: And how are we supposed to be able to take care of our society if we cannot take care of ourselves? 1 "Dagens lek morgondagens hopp", SvD tue

title: Public health in danger - please don't reduce our PE lessons!
Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: We live today in a country that claims to be a modern democracy, but still we seem to support the very conservative idea of having a royal family

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: This is something I feel very strongly about, since it has to do with the picture of us that we give to foreign countries

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Do we really want it to consist of posh people running around in chic dresses at chic parties? But even more important, are we willing to pay with tax money for these parties or for Crown princess Victoria's going out evenings at fashionable Stureplan? Well, I am most certain about that I am not the only person in this country that do not wish to pay for such things

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: I will not be surprised if some people get upset when reading thoughts similar to the introductory lines above, since I know that a lot of people care and look up to the royalties and, more strangely to me, see them as a natural part of our country's profile

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: However, it is a fact that our country is going through a rather tough period at the moment although recent studies keep telling us that economy is getting better and better

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: What we need to ask ourselves is the following: better compared to what? The present situation in certain areas in society are not good at all at the moment

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Figures show that things are getting better, but real life tells us the contrary

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Schools have to cut down their costs; there are more and more students in each class, there are more and more pupils per teacher

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: The teaching is getting worse since it cannot provide with the most basic material

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: The reason is said to be money

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Statistics from heath care show us terrifying numbers; the queues to get proper care are enormous and the trend is going towards that each patient has to pay more and more of the care on his or her own

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Although these facts seem to be obvious to everyone most people would probably say that: yes, of course I want to keep our Monarchy! It does not matter if the person being asked has two children in a school which the whole family agrees on is cutting far to much on expenses so that proper education cannot be offered

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: He or she may even be one of those nervously walking around waiting for heart surgery which cannot be offered because of the economic situation

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: You may say that what I have mentioned above does not have to do with the royal family, but I would have to say that it does

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: If our country cannot afford proper education and health care for it's citizens then it seems rather strange to me that it is possible to support the royal family with tax money

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: You may also say that each citizen only pay about four Swedish kronor by the year to support the royal family, but I say that this is too much in hard times like this

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: I agree on that the four kronor are not a lot, but imagine what 4 times 8,6 million a year could do

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: It most certainly would make it possible to employ another doctor, another teacher, it would make it possible to buy material for hospitals and schools

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Maybe this money could make all those general improvements possible that there is never money enough for making

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Schools and health care are two things that I, personally, feel could use the tax money a lot more than the royal family, but what about the actual function of the king

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: The King, as head of the state, could we do without him? My answer is yes, definitely

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: There are plenty of countries around the world that seem to get a long very well even if not being monarchies

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: A president could most certainly deal with the work that our king has today

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: The royal land areas and the royal residences could be sold and bring money to the state or transformed to other useful buildings for society, like libraries or reading-buildings for students

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: I think that what I have said above says about everything what is necessary to say when it comes to the question of keeping the royal family or not

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: And I cannot see any reason to keep them

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Our country needs to save all money that is possible to save

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: The work and the duties that our king has today could be made by a president instead, so why spend tax money on a royal family when it instead could help improving education or health care

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: Why keep capital tied-up in huge palaces when they could be sold and bring money to the state? My answer to the title is that, no, it is not defensible with monarchy and, no, we cannot afford it either

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
text: It is my opinion that we should consider how to get rid of the royal family as soon as possible to save money and the reputation of our country

title: Sweden - a monarchy, but is it defensible and can we afford it?
Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Sweden is today a country with almost 300 000 unemployed people, more than a tenth of which are young people between 18 and 24

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: This is a big problem of course and much effort has been put in to diminish these figures

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: The suggestion to make people who recieve unemployment benefit do work in the public sector is not something that would imediately contribute to this

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: It would rather be a way of lessen the damage that can come out of being unemployed for a long time and make the step back out in the working life a bit smaller

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: It seems to me that many people in Sweden today, especially young people, are taking advantage of the system of unemployment benefit

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: They find it easier to live on the government than to go out and find a job themselves

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: It is also the case that some people are officially unemployed and recieve benefit but work illegally without paying tax which gives a nice little income

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: If people have to work in order to get benefit they will be kept busy during the day and they won't get the same opportunity to work illegally at the same time

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Some people might argue that they would feel taken advantage of and be used as free labour

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: But as it is now, who is using who? And why not look at it as a paid job

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: It shouldn't really matter who pays the wages, the company you work for or the government

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: My mother is one of the people who have been working all their life, sometimes more than full time

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: At the end of last year she was between assignments for two months and decided to allow herself these two months of unemployment receiving benefit

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Now, the very thought of her having to go out and do work in the public sector is of course ridiculous

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: This proposition should only apply to people who have been unemployed for a long time, for example a year

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I think it is those people who are at risk of giving up and need to be reminded of what it is like to work and be out in society

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: So it should only be compulsory for long-term unemployed but everybody should be given this opportunity

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I myself have experienced some shorter periods of unemployment, not more than two months in a row though, and I know I would have appreciated having the possibility to do some kind of work

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I find it very depressing not having anything to do

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: At first sight working in the public sector might not be that appealing

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: But in the long run I know I would have felt better doing something rather than just being at home all day

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: This leads me over to the health aspect of this matter

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Well, who says working is good for your mental or physical health considering stress, responsibility, injuries at work etc? It is a problem, but no one is really supposed to get injuries at work and mental problems because of stress, then the position itself is the problem so that is really not the issue here

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: What I want to point out is that it is better healthwise to get up and out and do something during the day

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: There are always exceptions of course but I think that in general people would agree that an active life is better than a passive, this latter which the life of an unemployed tends to become

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: The mental health aspect is even more important here

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I've read somewhere that people that loose their job go through different stages

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: At first they might actually enjoy a bit of time off without any responsibilities when they can do whatever they like

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: They see it as only temporary and they apply for jobs with great confidence that they'll soon get one

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: But after a while they enter the stage of pessimism and they sort of give up and stop applying for jobs

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Depression and loss of self confidence is common and some even take to alcohol and drugs and become very isolated from society

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: This is what obligatory working would prevent

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: People need responsibility and routines and to feel useful and needed

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Many aspects remain to be discussed and a great deal of questions need to be answered regarding the actual realization of this

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I have here given my opinion as to why I think of it as a good idea

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: In general I think that if people recieving unemployment benefit, and having done so for a long time, have to go out and do unpaid work in the public sector it would be a good solution for everybody and I can only hope for such a proposal to be accepted in the future

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: I realize of course that it's not so easy

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Many questions remain to be answered concerning the actual genomförandet of this

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: Where can they work? etc

title: Compulsory work; solution for unemployed
text: And what good has ever come out of forcing anyone to do something?

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: INTRODUCTION

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Sweden has a long history of centralised power, even since Gustav Vasa and the other autocratic kings into present times with our strong central government in Stockholm

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Surely the situation with the very long period of social democracy in governmental position prepared the ground for the comprehensive school system we have today

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The intention of providing all pupils with an equal and good education caused the government to impose the comprehensive system in the 60's

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The government's aim was not bad

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: They wished that more people should be able to attend further and higher education

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Earlier that was impossible for a lot of persons

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Since a more conservative government got the power in the 80's the uniformity of the situation has altered

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: We now have a totally different situation

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: I wish to discuss why it is absolutely essential that the parents and not the government have the power and right of decision about what school their children shall attend

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The same arguments are valid about the importance of parental freedom to decide upon what kind of child care they prefer

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: WHY, THEN, IS IT SO IMPORTANT THAT PARENTS CAN CHOOSE SCHOOL - AND CHOOSE ANOTHER SCHOOL THAN THE STATE SUPPLY? First of all, it is a human right to choose school according to your religious or philosophical belief

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: This is clearly stated in the United Nation's declaration of human rights and also ratified by the Swedish state

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The elementary schooling should also be free of charge, says the same declaration

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: But of course, this is not the only reason why it is so important to have a possibility to select

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Another reason is that when people feel that they can affect this aspect of their childrens lives, they feel responsibility too

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: They feel urged to attend parents meeting at school and take a greater interest in their childrens schooling

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: CAN'T THE FREEDOM TO SELECT A FREE SCHOOL CREATE A SEGREGATED SOCIETY? This is a very interesting thought you often hear from opponents to free schools

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Could it be that such schools would consist only of rich and successful people's children? And perhaps - a even worse thought - only of white people, no immigrants and their descendants? No, I would say, whit the present system when the money follows the pupil every pupil has a chance to go to an independent school

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: When the money comes from the tax revenue, justice is maintained

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: It should NOT be a question of money if a parent wants to send his child to a non-state school

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The segregation in Sweden depends on housing policy

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: This is a greater problem for the state comprehensive schools since wealthier people just can move from a problem area while others just have to stay and have their children in a sometimes bad school - if they don't have a choice

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: CAN'T THE FREEDOM TO CHOOSE ANOTHER SCHOOL IMPOVERISH THE STATE SCHOOLS? This is a common question among defenders of state schools as the only alternative

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: I would say no, again

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: On the contrary, since competition will force the state schools to be better or at least as good as independent schools it will benefit the whole society

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: Monopoly has never been a good option

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: In a initial stage an independent school could cause a small increase of the education costs in a local place, if the classes are very small

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: But too small schools do not get permission to start

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: CONCLUSION

title: THE FAMILY, NOT THE STATE, SHOULD DECIDE WHAT SCHOOL AND CHILD CARE THEIR CHILDREN ATTEND!
text: The main reason why parents should be able to choose schools for their children is that this is a human right and a part of the freedom a citizen should enjoy

Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Now that we have entered the 21st century, the situation for gay people has never been better, at least not in the Western World, as people, generally speaking, are more open-minded towards homosexuals

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: However, as soon as one problem seems to disappear, man immediately focuses on another, which ultimately creates "problems of luxury"

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Adoption for gay couples is one of these problems, and in this paper, I would like to discuss the pros and cons of this matter, by approaching the most common objections raised against this issue

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: The focus is here, quite naturally, entirely on the child, so I will discuss the problems of gender identification, sexual orientation, teasing and general problems of identity that may affect a child who is adopted by a gay couple

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: The first remark that people usually make is that as two people of the same sex cannot conceive a child together, they should not be allowed to adopt children

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: That assertion is easily dismissed by the fact that adoption in itself, in most cases, would be useless if two people wanting to adopt a child could have one in a "natural" way

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: People then usually retort that the child will be confused about gender roles, as it will not have a mother and a father around, but two of either sort

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: A simple reminder of all the single parents who raise children should be enough to put an end to that myth

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Gay people do have friends and relatives who have an effect on the child's perception of gender roles, and growing up with gay parents can actually help the child to get a more nuanced view of the traditional gender roles created by society

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Another myth concerning gay parents is that they would subconsciously influence the child to become a homosexual too

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: The fact that such remarks are made shows how little people know about homosexuality

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: People do not just become gay; it is a part of a person's identity, and most homosexuals say that they were born that way

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Another good rejoinder is that if homosexuality depended on your parents' sexual orientation, there would be no such thing as gay people

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Therefore, there is absolutely no indication that there would be a flood of homosexuals if gay couples were allowed to adopt children

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: A more relevant problem is that of teasing, which can afflict the child when coming into contact with other children, mainly at school

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Children can be cruel, and if somebody is different, he or she usually gets to hear at least some kind of remark about it

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: However, children who have got a negative view of people being different, in this case homosexuals, usually have got that view from somewhere, in most cases from their parents

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: If parents are open-minded enough to learn that homosexuality is not a bad thing, and to pass that on to their children, there should be no immediate problem when a child with gay parents starts going to school

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Nevertheless, an important point to make is that gay parents have to take the first step by informing teachers and other parents of the situation, in order to avoid rumours and gossip that may occur if they try to conceal their life style to other people

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Lastly, it can be argued that adopted children have got enough battles to fight as it is, and that their having gay parents could only contribute to their confusion

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: It is true that adopted children, sooner or later, start thinking about their origin and about the fact that they were once, in some way, rejected by their biological parents

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Sometimes the adoptive parents have to act as punchbags to their child's frustration, and the fact that they are gay can be seen as yet another way of complicating things for their child

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: However, I think we can all agree that people who adopt children do it partly in order to help a disadvantaged child, and not to cause trouble

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: If people do not accept children adopted by gay couples, the only ones who can change this are people in general

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: We have to start somewhere in order to have a more open-minded climate when it comes to homosexuality, and letting gay couples adopt children is one way of getting closer to that goal

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: To sum up, I would like to stress that homosexuals are just as good parents as heterosexuals, and that they too can raise good citizens

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
text: Adoption always implies some kind of difficulties for the child, but these obstacles can be overcome if we all work together towards a more comprehensive and open-minded society

title: Adoption for gay couples: towards an open-minded society
Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Moral panic is a Swedish expression

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: It can be defined as a contagious anger or fear that arises when something "evil" is considered so devastating that society is thretend and actions must come to re-establish social order

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Classical issues that have caused moral panic in Sweden over this century are for example the books written by Nick Carter in 1908-1909, the dancing-floor misery in the 1930's and the video violence in 1980 (1)

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Debates that have touched upon the conseption of moral panic are aids, abortion, computer-games and rave-parties

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I will here give you my look upon the discussion going on today about violent pornography

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Few people seem to have known that this excist in Sweden before a documentary were shown on the Gothemburg film festival on February 3:rd

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: The film gives examples of violent pornography that come in to Swedish homes by cabel-TV

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Although the cabel-TV-networks are pointed out as guilty of agression on wimen, I think that this is a to simple explanation to a serious problem

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: The pronography debate have arose from time to time during the years, and it is something that people often have an oppinion about

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: My belief is that pornografphy is something that is here to stay, and so has been for a long time

text: Today pornography is seen by both singles and couples as a source of excitement

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: If this weren't provided by Swedish magazines and cabel-TV the need would still be there, but the source seeked elswere

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: The advantage of having it through Swedish magazines and cabel-TV-distributers is that we have the possibility to maintain a good control

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I think it is very important not to disregard this chanse

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: A few yers ago it were a similar discussion going on about child-pornography

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Thre was a long time of looking in to it in every possible way

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: In the end it led to a change of the law that now make it illegal to possess child-pornography

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: There is argued now that the law need to be changed this time as well

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I think it is important to make an equally deep investigation this time

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Althoug we have a law today that ought to make it possible to stop violent pornography, many critics argue that the law isn't working

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Since it is so many feelings involved in discussions like this, I think it's important to shade off the discussion and step back and see if the law really needs to be changed, or if it's just the interpretation that needs to be looked upon

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: When moral panic breaks out it is immediately demanded that a scapegoat shall be pointed out

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: This often lead to a superficial discussion that is dangerous because it reduces an important topic to feelings instead of facts

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I hope that this discussion will go on and that all the facts will be looked upon to make it possible to make a change to prevent violent pornography in Sweden

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I would now like to come back to the statement I made in the beginning about that it is a simple solution to accuse violent pornography for all agression on wimen

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: These agressions are something very serious that needs to be prevented

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: It is possible that pornography plays a role in it, but I think it is so much more

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: If you have a solid belief in yourself, a social network and good models as you grow up, it is possible to seperate correct behavior from a destructive

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: Most people don't go out and pick a fight on a Saturday nigt just because they've just seen a violent movie, and you don't depravate wimen because you've seen a violent pornographic movie

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: It is my deep belief that it is a profund human right to be spared from depravation, which is what the discussion about violent pornography in my oppinion comes down to

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: I hope that the discussion going on now will leed to a wider look upon what can be done about deprevation taking place every day, as well as a stop to the showing of violent pornography both in movies and magazines

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: 1) Nick Carters novels were made scapegoat for that times youth violence wich led to that no one sold his books, but no change in the right of freedom of speach

title: Violent pornography, todays cause of moral panic
text: The dancing-floor misery were seen as a threat to the youth since "drinking and fucking" were happening there, but the dancing-floors survived this attac

text: Why did Sweden join the European Union in the first place? The answer is that a referendum was held in our country and the population voted in favour of the Union even though the result was almost 50/50

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: History tells us that all attempts to put people together with different ethnic backgrounds, different languages and religions, is a failure

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: My strong but negative standpoint against the EU is not only from an ideological point of view but also from a historical view

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: We should learn from history, but unfortunately it seems as if history is going to repeat itself

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: My opinion is that we should leave the union and I would like to bring to light the problems with the Union and to examine whether the Union is good for Europe or not

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: We should learn from the history

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: In the last years we have seen cruel and violent wars for independence in a few of our closest neighbour countries: Russia and Yugoslavia

text: These are two examples of countries with ethnic, religious and linguistic differences that could not live together

text: Everything ended up in chaos and a lot of people died a completely unnecessary death

text: We can just look at ourselves and our history

text: I am of the opinion that the EU will end up in a disaster, some countries will not accept certain decisions made within the European parliament and withdraw their membership

text: The romantic view of the European Union is the desire to create a peaceful and prosperous Europe among the 15 member countries

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: The true objective though, is to create a powerful military and political Union in order to be able to rule the world

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Both the EU and EFTA were an attempt in the beginning to create a smooth system of transporting goods between the European countries

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: EFTA abolished the trade tariffs and supported a common agricultural cooperation and I am in favour to this kind of trade union

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: The difference between EFTA and the EU is that the European Union continues and continues to develop itself

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: EU tends to expand and wants more member countries, a common defence and a common monetary union

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Today the EU account for 40 per cent of the world trade and I see a tendency of megalomania

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: The EU is not only a free-trade area, as the intention was in the beginning, but the fact is that the cost of the membership starts to retain each country's national rights and independence

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Sweden is a small country

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Europe has been involved in two World wars in the last century

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Sweden has always been neutral and my opinion is that we should remain so

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: EU law is superior to Swedish law and, in certain matters that might be important for our country might not be biased for other member states such as Greece and Portugal

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: So who are we? Swedes? Europeans? In my opinion there will occur and identity crises among our population, and it is dangerous to loose that sense of not having your roots somewhere

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: We are not in the same situation as the United States of America

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: At least they speak the same language, they built up their country together and they have a common history from their independence at the end of the eighteenth century

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: The European Union had a basic aim which I found quite reasonable but I am still very sceptical

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: I always look backwards when there is talk about the future, not because I am nostalgic but because we should study our history, on that basis we should make our decisions for the future

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: Today we do not know the outcome of the European Union but I hope it is positive of course

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: How it will come out and reach that aim, no one knows

title: Sweden - out of the EU
text: The only thing I know is that the Union might be good for Europe in the short term, but in the long run it will end up in a disaster

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: I am probably not the first one to notice that Swedish is not what it used to be

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Most people would agree that English has affected speakers of Swedish

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: What I am wondering is if people really have understood the extenuating effect the growing Anglicism have had on the Swedish language

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In the last decades Sweden has experienced an invasion of the English language through music, television, film and the new information technology

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In this paper I am going to draw your attention to what this invasion has done to the Swedish language

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: I will start with a few examples to support my following thesis about the risks I see

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: My first example of Anglicized features in Swedish has to do with morphology

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In the Swedish language compound words are written together as one single word as opposed to English where they are usually written separately

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Take for example the word, or rather the expression, standard language in English

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: A correct translation into Swedish would be, standardspråk

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: However, in many cases you find "standard språk" (two separate words instead of one compound)

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: This might seem like a minor problem because in this particular case it doesn't really change the meaning, it is more a question of orthography

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: However, there are cases where it does change the meaning of a word

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: For instance, one time I ran across stark öl1 written on a sign outside a restaurant

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The natural way to interpret that is that stark (strong) is a modifier of the noun öl (beer) and not that it is the compound noun starköl Another linguistic field in which we also find evidence of a growing Anglicism is the syntax

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: I am thinking about the English apostrophe genitive

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In both Swedish and English adding the letter s to the word forms the genitive

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: One important difference between the two languages that seems to have escaped a lot of Swedes is the fact that only English uses an apostrophe before adding s

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: As a result I often run across the English apostrophe variant in the Swedish language

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: This phenomenon is common in the naming of shops and restaurants for example, Åsa's Salong och Berra's Sportfiske2

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The third example shows that also the Swedish lexicon has been affected

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: This trend probably started with the innocent occurrence of borrowing an English word in order to express some nonexistent term in the Swedish language

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The growing computer language is an example of that

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Swedish linguists have come up with translations to most of these terms but it has not resulted successful, since people don't use them

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: This phenomenon is not what is upsetting

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: After all, loan words are a very common feature in most languages

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: I am talking about the growing tendency of replacing useful Swedish words with English equivalences

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: There are tons of examples but I will just mention a few

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The Swedish word Kafé is nowadays often replaced by the English corresponding word coffee shop and you don't hear anybody saying that they are going away to a kurort, instead it's spa nowadays

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Another example is the English word airbag, which totally has replaced the Swedish equivalent Krock-kudde

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In this case I even claim that the Swedish word is better, semantically speaking

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Airbag is definitely more arbitrary than Krock-kudde3, which as oppose to airbag tells you exactly what it is

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Now that I have provided you with the evidence it is time to take a look at the risks with linguistic Anglicism

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Naturally one could argue that changes like these are inevitable since languages are dynamical, but the danger I see is that Swedish seems to have lost the potential to develop independently from English

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: If we take a look at the last of the above examples we can draw the conclusion that speakers of Swedish has lost an important quality, namely creativity

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: To simply adapt new English words and ignore the possibility of a Swedish translation or an already existing Swedish equivalent word seems both lazy and negligent

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The appalling result of this growing trend is that people nowadays state that Swedish is a poor language and that it hasn't got a large enough vocabulary

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: In fact, nothing could be more wrong

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: The Swedish vocabulary is not poorer than the average languages'; instead I would say that Swedish speakers that claim this have a poor knowledge of their own language

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: They simply aren't aware of the possibilities in Swedish

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Doubtlessly Sweden lives in the shadow of our model English speaking countries

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Not only do we copy the American lifestyle and share the weather with The British Isles, we are also abandoning our own language for their more prestigious one

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Isn't it time we stop to think about the consequences of the growing usage of English? The native tongue could be said to be the soul of a people's national identity, if we eliminate that our nationality becomes crippled

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde
text: Let us turn the attention towards the enormous potential the Swedish language possesses! Under the surface of anglicized words, expressions and structure lies a rich and useful idiom that suffers of undeserved poor reputation

title: Airbag vs. Krock-kudde

The school in our time
text: People in general have very different opinions about whether the private sector's be or not to be

title: The school in our time
text: I myself find it very appealing in some areas; especially when it comes to educational matters

title: The school in our time
text: It is a privileged resource that everyone should appreciate and give a warm welcome

title: The school in our time
text: Privatization in schools is essential to be able to attain student achievement, based upon their own motivation to learn and interest in their subjects

title: The school in our time
text: These schools have a curricula that presuppose from the childrens' needs and meet them at their level of development

title: The school in our time
text: There are a wider selection of subjects that demand a certain imaginative and creative mind; which challenge the children

title: The school in our time
text: Rewarding challenges that lead to good knowledge to use in the future to come

title: The school in our time
text: You can always try to find flowing creativity, motivation and happiness in an unpleasant environment, but most certainly you will not find it

title: The school in our time
text: A lot of times this is unfortunately a fact when it comes to schools outside the private sector

title: The school in our time
text: You gather too many children in a much too small room, and call this a classroom

title: The school in our time
text: In this room the head of the class, that is the teacher, is trying his/her best to give the children a valuable education

title: The school in our time
text: And that is with far too few books, and other pedagogical material that children need to be able to receive knowledge

title: The school in our time
text: Comparing to this I should think the private alternative is evident

title: The school in our time
text: The classrooms are perhaps not larger but the number of children in the same class is not even close to the number of children in Public schools

title: The school in our time
text: When walking into a classroom in a private school you meet a motivation and creativity that is unfortunally not very common in Public schools

title: The school in our time
text: You can also be pleased with the fact that all children have their own new books, not old inherited ones

title: The school in our time
text: That is; when they need books

title: The school in our time
text: In many subjects in the private schools the children work with other materials, as in biology they use the nature as knowledge resource

title: The school in our time
text: Their facilities are more modern over all

title: The school in our time
text: Private schools do not just have more modern facilities, than Public scools; they also have more modern, and motivated teachers that are interested of the childrens'best

title: The school in our time
text: With modern teachers I mean that they a lot of times have attended further educational training

title: The school in our time
text: This makes them aware of new didactical and pedagogical knowledge

title: The school in our time
text: It is more rare to find teachers of the same educational experiences in Public schools; mainly because of the economical situation

title: The school in our time
text: Schools in the private sector are a lot of times sponsored by different kinds of companies; that are providing them with books, computers and other essential facilities

title: The school in our time
text: These schools therefor have more money to spend on their teachers'further educational training, and other necessities that makes them high-quality schools

title: The school in our time
text: Mentioning high-quality scools brings me to think of the differing in grade scales, between Public schools and Private schools

title: The school in our time
text: The latter, see to that the childrens' personal qualities come to its right

title: The school in our time
text: Instead of Public schools, where the students receive a piece of paper with the letters: U, G, VG, MVG, the children get their grades described in detail

title: The school in our time
text: This means that either if you are a high-level student or a low-level student your personal qualities are shown and matter

title: The school in our time
text: This is an essential difference

title: The school in our time
text: The students put a lot of hard work and effort in their years of studying and should get shown valuable credit for it

title: The school in our time
text: This system demand a lot of the children which makes them more motivated and detemined to struggle to get through with good grades

title: The school in our time
text: People that are more positive to Public schools than to Schools in the private sector might use this utterance as a "weapon" against Private schools

title: The school in our time
text: They seem to be of the opinion that grades seem to be the most essential aim in these schools and they tend to dislike the demands Private schools have on the children

title: The school in our time
text: These schools do have demands on the children and they are aiming to get high-level students; but this is also what these children are going to meet in the "real world", outside the protected school

title: The school in our time
text: If society wants well-educated, motivated, creative and ambitious students; Private schools are a good educational alternative

title: The school in our time

Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: When The Spice Girls entered the public eye in 1997, they seemed to consider themselves as a new kind of definition of feminism, with their Girl Power slogans, high-kicks and pushy attitude

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: But despite the Spice Girls' increasing popularity, it seemed like their slogans didn't quite work

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: In the debate around the book "Fittstim" in 1999, it became clear that feminism was something that a surprising number of young people didn't want to be associated with

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: My aim with this essay is to explain why the term feminism still is so important, and why we should hold on to it

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: I have realised that feminism doesn't mean the same thing to everyone, and in order to avoid confusion, I'm going to give my own definition of the word: To me feminism has always meant equality, and nothing else

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Equality and justice, and not "Girl Power" or matriarchy or any other form of female domination

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: In other words, basic humanitarian values that most people can relate to

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: But in that case, why not simply call it humanism? If equality and justice are the key-words, then why use a term that excludes half of the population, ie

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: men? The most obvious reason for this is that no other group in the world, be it blacks, homosexuals or different religious groups, have suffered from so much oppression and violence as women have

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Fifty percent of the world population are women, and in almost all societies and cultures, since the beginning of time, women are or have been seen as weaker, less important and inferior to men

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: It is vital that we actually see things in this light, with the focus on women, to actually be able to change this reality

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: When it comes down to equality, it has always been a case of women wanting the same rights as men, and then by nature it becomes a women's movement, even though it concerenes men just as much

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: However, this doesn't mean that feminism is an excluding term

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: It is in everybody's interest that we get rid of the patriarchy, which is almost as opressing for men as it is for women, with its fixed roles of masculinity and strength

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: It can be argued that opression and unequality might exist, but not in the Western society of today, where the feminist movement in the 1960s made sure that women and men were given equal rights

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: But this is not entirely true, for many reasons

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: First of all, even if we were equal in Western society, that doesn't mean we should forget about the less fortunate women who are still living in opression in traditionally patriarchal societies

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: It is our responsibility to show that we care

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: But we also have to ask ourselves what we have actually achieved in our society in the terms of equality? Looking at statistics over domestic violence and rape, you start to realise that we've got a long way to go before we can talk about true equality, even in our society

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Women still earn less than men in the same positions, and a glass-ceiling prevents women from getting top jobs in companies

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: The covered-up discriminiation is present in every level of society, and even though it might not be as obvious as in other cultures, we still live in a patriarchy

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Now, more than ever, it is neccessary for feminism to become a part of society, and a part of peoples vocabulary

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: And even though the goals of the early feminist movement haven't yet been achieved by far, we have to be able to see what actually has happened, and to acknowledge the struggle that has lead us to where we are now

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: By bringing feminism into our speech and into our daily lives, we also pay tribute to the women and men who paved the way for our generations

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: We have to remember why the suffragettes chained themselves to the Houses of Parliament in the early 20th century; that less than one hundred years ago, the right to vote was reserved for men only

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Feminism is not a dead movement, and we mustn't let it die

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: There's still lots of work to be done, all over the world, and a way to start would be making feminism an accepted term

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: To convince people that it has nothing to do with the Spice Girls and Girl Power, or turning the world into a matricarcal dictatorship

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
text: Feminism means giving people the same opportunities and equal rights, and until these goals have been achieved, we can't afford to get rid of the word, let alone the movement

title: Spice Up Your Vocabulary: Why Feminism Is Still An Important Word
