ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: In what grade should Swedish students be introduced to English? That is right now a big issue

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Everyone have different opinions about what is best

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Some think it is better to start with English as early as in first grade, while many people still think that fourth grade is the best time to start

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: There are of course many, more or less, good arguments for both alternatives

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: An argument against English in first grade is that it might be confusing for the students with too many new subjects

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: They should have a chance to learn Swedish before they start with a foreign language

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: An answer to that is that English is not as new to the students now as it may have been before

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: In the society we live in today, we get a lot of influences from English speaking countries, mainly America

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: The students have probably met English earlier, either on vacations or on TV and in music

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Even if they do not know what everything means they have heard the sound of the language

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Probably most children know how to count and say a few sentences too

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: It could be something they have heard in a song

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: You can start on different levels depending on which grade you start with English

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: If you start in first grade, it of course will be impossible to read texts while many students can not read in Swedish yet

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: That does not mean that I think it is better to start later with English

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Students learn easier, the younger they are

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Even in first grade you can bring in English in other subjects

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: If you talk about the different seasons or animals, the students often want to learn those words in English

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Reading and writing in English is easier to learn if you already now the meaning of the words and how they are pronounced

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Also I think it is more motivated for everyone to feel that they can use the language in speaking rather than reading and writing in the beginning

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: When I went to school, we started with English in third grade

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: My sister started school two years later, and than they tried to start with English in first grade

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: It did not work at all, but I do not think it had to do with anything else but the teacher

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: If the teacher does not believe in an early start, he/she probably will have a hard time motivating the children

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: They had to start over in third grade again

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: When starting with a foreign language, in this case English, with small children it is good to start playing with it

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Rimes and songs, which are easy to remember, are a good start

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: The children probably will say the words awkwardly, but still it is an easy way for them to remember

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: The teacher just will have to be extra clear on those words

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Pictures are good to bring in to the classroom as well

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: The children can try to explain them to each other

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: I also think it is important to start with short sessions of English, maybe 20 minutes each time

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: A lot of repeating, not new things every time will help the children not to loose their patience

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: As you can see, I am positive for starting with English as early as possible

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: I think it is good because most students at that age will be interested in English

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: It is better to start before many children starts to act out thinking everything is boring as they often do in fourth and fifth grade

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: Then it is cool to hate school and skipping homework

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: The main thing is of course what the teacher believes in

title: ENGLISH, WHEN IS THE RIGHT TIME?
text: If the teacher do not think it is good to start too early, he/she probably will not be as good as a teacher as one who believes in an early start

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE
text: We can also see this in the spread of the wages in the teaching-profession today which is not wide enough

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE
text: There are lots of secondary school teachers who hardly have any higher wages than preschool teachers

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE
text: And this I think also should be compared to the different lenghts of educations, since secondary school teachers in fact read a year and a half longer than preschool teachers

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE
text: Even the demands of the preschool education is quite different to the demands of secondary school education

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE

title: EDUCATION MUST BE PROFITABLE

THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Even though we all know how important nature is to us, and for other living creatures, we tend to ignore that because of our own laziness

text: It is a shame to see all this people sitting in their cars, alone, in a traffic jam and pollution lies like a blanket of the sky

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: If we do not take care of Mother Nature, who will? And a good way to help is by travelling together

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: There are so many reasons to why commuting is a much better alternative than travelling by car for example

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: I have chosen to bring out some of them, to convince you that I am right

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Firstly, one of the reasons that commuting is cheaper is that you do not have to pay parking fees

text: In cities these fees are expensive and if you also add the petrol costs and maintenance you get away very cheap by commuting

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: If you leave your car at home you also avoid putting other people in a position that might be dangerous

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Due to the fact that the number of accidents are closely related to the number of vehicles on the road, a safe and strong recommendation should be to travel together with other people

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Another aspect of why it saves you money is all the exercise you get for free

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: In other words, the expense for a gym-card is not necessary

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: As I mentioned earlier, if more people would commute, we will get less pollution and you will then breath more fresh air, when you walk between two bus stops

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Secondly, the foremost reason, except that it is cheaper, and saves the nature, travel together with other people, is the great chance to socialise

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: In stead of clutching the wheel tight, half listening to the radio Stockholm, you hear, for example on the bus, get the opportunity to talking with others about this and that

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: However, sometimes you step upon people with a strange attitude, and maybe you get a little bit annoyed but in the end, they always makes you laugh and that is really what life is all about

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: To have a good laugh and meet all kinds of people

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Finally, it is important to bring out how much you develop as a person, thanks to all the books and music you have time to explore when you travel

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: To start the day with a exciting novel on your way to work instead of being stuck in a traffic jam, that, I would say is a nice beginning of the day

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: Suddenly you might find yourself in the middle of the jungle with all sorts of different animals, or perhaps you are out in the sea on a big boat just floating around, having a life experience, who knows? When you commute, all this is possible, just use your imagination, and explore the world of the novels, and music

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: In other words, travelling together with other people makes you develop as a human being

text: This is done by all the intellectual work you get to process

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: I also want to underline the social experience

text: The commuters are friendly, with some exceptions that I mentioned earlier, and one must always take the chance to talking with others

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF COMMUTING
text: However, the greatest reason for commuting is to saving Mother Nature

text: There are of course other things that effects pollution, but exhaust fumes are one of the largest source to increasing it

text: By now you have probably realised what I am pointing out, that is, the importance of commuting

Elders in the families?
text: Is it possible to move the responsiblity of care for the eldery from the state onto the family? What would such a reorganization mean? What are the advantages and disadvanatges? Taking care of old people, especially old people over a certain age, requires a lot of hard work

title: Elders in the families?
text: My sister has been working in a home for old people for a lot of years during her summer holidays, so I know from her experience that the case is really so

title: Elders in the families?
text: Old people in bad condition needs attention more or less 24 hours a day

title: Elders in the families?
text: Moreover, I have a grandmother who is 92 years old and who is living in a home for old people

title: Elders in the families?
text: Even though she is entirely taken care of by the personnel in the home, my mother has to run some errands for her and of course go and visit her a couple of times a week

title: Elders in the families?
text: My mother, who works full time, has difficulties with coping with this rather limited amount of work regarding my grandmother

title: Elders in the families?
text: Thus, it seems to me very difficult for people to entirely treat elders at home in their families

title: Elders in the families?
text: Take for example a family of two adults and two small children

title: Elders in the families?
text: Managing full time jobs and taking care of their children seems to me as more than enough work for these adults

title: Elders in the families?
text: Imagine these two in addition having to take care of an elder

title: Elders in the families?
text: If the elder isn't that old and in realtively good health perhaps the workload will not be insurmountable

title: Elders in the families?
text: If the elder on the opposite is in bad health, perhaps unable even to go to the bathroom by his or herself, the situation seems unstable

title: Elders in the families?
text: If the elder needs day-time attention one of the providers will have to stop working full-time or perhaps stop working entirely

title: Elders in the families?
text: Of course there is always the possibility of hirering a nurse to take care of the elder but, evidently, far from all families have enough money to afford that

title: Elders in the families?
text: Single people obviously will be having even bigger problems treating the elder in their home

title: Elders in the families?
text: Living off only one half-time salary seems very difficult, if not impossible

title: Elders in the families?
text: Furthermore, treating an elder in bad physical condition requires a certain amount of expertise, concerning medicine and such

title: Elders in the families?
text: Obviously authorised personnel could inform people how to do this, but would that be enough? In addition, if the elder is in such bad shape that he or she cannot walk or stand by his or herself, evidently the treatment of him or her will include really heavy lifts, which obviously will be difficult for physically weak individuals

title: Elders in the families?
text: A combination of lack of time and limited knowledge could lead to bad treatment of the elder, despite the best intentions

title: Elders in the families?
text: An inevitable part of the treatment of elders is death

title: Elders in the families?
text: If the elder should die in the home and not in a hospital, I believe this could lead to feelings of guilt and bad conscience

title: Elders in the families?
text: Did we do enough? Did we do anything wrong? Isn't there any advantages of treating elders in the homes then? Of course the state (or local authorities) will save money, surely large amounts of money, by moving the care of elders to the families

title: Elders in the families?
text: Furthermore, under the right circumstances the treatment of the elder could be much better in the family than in a home for old people

title: Elders in the families?
text: Perhaps it is possible for families to look after the elderly, but it doesn't, seem like a good idea to me, as may stand clear

title: Elders in the families?
text: The advantages are few - the disadvantages are many

title: Elders in the families?
text: My main point is that the care of an elder takes to much time of peoples lives - I think that many people, who have any experience of care for the elderly, would agree with me

title: Elders in the families?
text: The system we have now is the best one for everybody, I believe, even though the standard of many homes for old people ought to be improved considerably

title: Elders in the families?

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.
text: In this case a person that could have lived a long and happy life does not get the chance just because we think he/ her is guilty

text: Is it possible that a person with a lot of money is worth more than a poor

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

text: Just the thought of taking another persons life to let justice be done make me questioning the future of this world

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.

title: An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.
text: If we want to put an end to the violence that exist in society then society can not be as bad as the criminal

Learning English at an early age
text: Nowadays it's becoming more and more common that Swedish pupils start to learn English in school at a very young age

title: Learning English at an early age
text: It provides the pupils with a better understanding and basic ground for the language since they get to practise it in a playful way, and they can slowly let their language abilities mature

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Pupils understand a lot more English than we think they do since English is very common in today's society, in for example films, music and television

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Starting early with English will also help the pupils to get a greater knowledge about the language in a natural way

title: Learning English at an early age
text: I will now look into some advantages in learning English at an early age

title: Learning English at an early age
text: One major advantage is that when you are younger you learn more easily

title: Learning English at an early age
text: It doesn't take very long for children to comprehend

title: Learning English at an early age
text: They are willing to learn and they also think it's fun to learn new things, which is really important

title: Learning English at an early age
text: This can be related to that children now can start school at the age of six instead of seven

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Some children are ready to start learning, in a playful way, at the age of six

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Of course it varies from child to child

text: The pupils need to be stimulated and my opinion is that it all depends on how you do it

title: Learning English at an early age
text: They don't have to be able to spell every word they know at once, just get familiar and comfortable with the language

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Later on, when the pupils are comfortable and ready for it, writing and reading can be introduced

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Another major advantage is that the pupils gain a lot of self-confidence

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Being able to speak and understand some phrases and words makes the pupils very proud and they can grow as individuals

title: Learning English at an early age
text: Being self-confident, knowing that you can do and comprehend things, makes the entire learning process easier

title: Learning English at an early age
text: It will probably develop and make them stronger in other subjects too

title: Learning English at an early age

title: Learning English at an early age

Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: In November 1999, the Student library in Uppsala made the decision that all borrowing of course literature should be stopped, in order to save money

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: This upset many students, who would now have to get hold of their literature in other, less efficient ways

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: This, I think, is not something that should be allowed to occur at a big, renown university like Uppsala, and in my opinion the permission to borrow course literature should be reintroduced

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: To only be able to read the books at the library is not a good solution for all students

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: One point that argues against the fact that books should only be read at the library, is that not all students have the possibility to go to there during opening hours

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Parents with small children may have difficulties being away from home more than a certain period of time a week, and students who live far away from the university, maybe even in another city, or those who have lectures during opening hours, are all unable to access the books

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: For these students, the possibility to have books on loan and the opportunity to read them at home, may for them be essential in order to pass the course

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Those in favour of the prohibition maintain that more people will be able to read the books when not only one person has got access to them during a longer period of time

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: This may be true, but still, for students who don't have the possibility to go to the library regularly, it is unfortunately not a solution that is in their interest

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: The lack of enough seats at the library for all students, is something that also argues for the fact that the library should allow students to borrow books

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: If the argument that more people will have access to the books is true, then presumably this will also mean that the number of students that will need a place to study, at the library, will increase

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Since the amount of places will not be increased, the number of students that compete for a seat will get higher, and then not all books available to read will be, even though there are several copies on offer

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Also, those who need to use the books the most will not have the opportunity, because they will not be able to compete for the copies in the way that students who have regular access to the library can

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: A way of saving money is borrowing the course literature

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: And even though many like to buy their own literature, course books are often very expensive and since the study allowances are not that high, a popular alternative is borrowing

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Many courses have got a large amount of books that are included in the obligatory literature that students will need for their course

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Because of that, many depend on the fact that they have always been able to borrow their books from the library, take them home, get to read them properly, and take notes

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Additional literature and handbooks are often seen as an extra cost, and are therefore more often borrowed than bought

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: To many people, borrowing course literature is very important

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Not all have the possibility to go to the library during opening hours, and the lack of seats will also make this impossible for the large amount of students who will be needing a seat at the library

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: Money, of course, is another argument, and I believe that the possibility to borrow course literature should be reintroduced in order to give the students a wider choice in how they get hold of their literature

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: After all, the university in Uppsala is supposed to offer their students the best, and the Student library should therefore be able to have course literature that is available for home loan

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: The saving of money should try to be made in other areas and not at the expense of the students

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature
text: A library should not only be a place where people can sit and read books, but also an institution from which books can be borrowed

title: Reintroduce the borrowing of course literature

text: Antagonists of the right for homosexuals to adopt children often use arguments like: the child might run the risk of being bullied in school because of that he or she has homosexual parents; they will raise the child into homosexuality

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: The latter argument is definitely absurd

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Ones sexuality is nothing one can be brought up into, it is something one is born with

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: And I do not understand the harm of children growing up into homosexuals

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: A child with homosexual parents might, according to the first common argument, be bullied because of the parents sexuality

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: But this can possibly be prevented if the parents, before their child starts school, contact and inform the teachers about their sexuality

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Homosexual parents could also visit the school and let their child's classmates know that it is quite normal to be homosexual and that they are just like other parents

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Children of homosexuals might be bullied in spite of all attempts to prevent it

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Nevertheless someone must take the first step

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: It is not the homosexual parents' fault if their child is bullied but society's view, that certainly needs a change

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Prejudices towards homosexuals can be prevented if we start doing something about them

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
text: Permitting homosexuals to adopt children is one step further towards freedom from prejudice

title: Homosexual's right to adopt children
Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Unemployment benefit must exist, but should one be able to live on them for a long time without being required to do something? I do not think so

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: I believe that most unemployed want to get a new job, but I also know that there are people who are quite content with being unemployed since they still get some money, and that without lifting a finger

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: I do believe that it would be a reasonable demand that unemployed should do unpaid work in the public sector

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: I do not think that it should be seen as a way of punishing the unemployed but as an opportunity for them to get new experiences

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Maybe it sounds hard that they have to do unpaid work but it is not unpaid is it?! All people have to work to get money so why should the unemployed be able to lie in bed all day and collect money

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Perhaps it can feel unfair to work with those who are employed at the public service and know that they get more money for the same job

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: But they probably have education or experience that helped them get the job, and is it not as it should be that those who have education or experience should have a better salary? And as a "unpaid worker" you would not work fulltime since the society still wants you to be able to find a "real" job, and therefore you must have time to look for a new job and have the opportunity to go to job interviews

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: But in the meantime I believe that it is for the best for the unemployed to be able to work in the public sector

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: It is a part of the Swedish mentality that you should work, if you do not the guilt comes crawling and says that you are not worth anything

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Most people have to feel that they accomplish something to feel that he/she is worth something

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: I think that the self-esteem can touch rock bottom if a person is unemployed too long

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: If it is so that people cannot get a job for different reasons is it not better in those cases that they get a temporary job at the public service? I believe that those who think that unpaid work in the public sector is a punishment are those who are content with watching television all day and get "paid" for it, and is it them we should be listening to? Should we not be glad that we can support the society in which we live by working in the public sector instead of being home doing nothing? If you look at it, those who were formerly unemployed now have a "job" and the public sector gets "free" personnel that they really need

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: There are no losers

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: You may think that the losers are those who are not fitted to work in the public sector, but there are many different kinds of occupations in the public sector and I am sure that they can find at least one kind of job that they would consider doing

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: For of course the unemployed must have some say in what they are willing to work with

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Not everyone is suited to work at a day-care centre, and we have to respect that

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: Another aspect of unpaid work is that those who are without a job has an opportunity to get work experience and maybe later, the possibility to get a "real" job in the public sector or elsewhere

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: As I see it, it is only positive that those who are unemployed should be required to do unpaid work in the public sector

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: It is good for a person to know that he/she has accomplished something and that improves the self-esteem

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: The unemployed have the opportunity to get work experience, which can never be a bad thing, and perhaps they get to try something new

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: They also get the opportunity to help the society in which they live, and they still get the same amount of money as they would have if they were just hanging around at home growing apathetic

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
text: I strongly believe that it is an opportunity to do unpaid work, not a punishment

title: Unpaid work - a punishment or an opportunity?
Cap - on or off in school
text: One of the most intense debates in Swedish schools at the moment is weather students should have the right to wear a cap or not in the school during class

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The use of caps even inside the schools has led to tussles

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: For many teachers and other adults the cap is seen as very provocative

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: What I don't understand is why this specific article of clothing is so loaded and for what reasons so many adults think that it is inappropriate to wear it inside

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The cap has become a subject of debate that on some schools take a lot of time and energy away from the education

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I believe that the way that teenager dress and behave always has been and always will be a subject constantly discussed among adults

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: However it is obvious that exactly this action, not taking the cap of inside the school, has got a quite special loading to it and is by many adults seen as altogether provocative

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Headgear has always been a piece of clothing full of symbols

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The Christian man still takes his hat of in church but the Jewish man on the other hand puts his kippa on before entering the Synagogue

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: In both cases the action is a way of showing humility for the Lord

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Is it possibly the heredity demand on humility that explains many teachers' hot reaction towards the caps? Earlier when both men and women used to wear hats, it was a breach of etiquette for men not to take their hats off when they came inside

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Women on the other hand didn't need to take their hats off

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: These are old rules of etiquette but I believe that this is something that many elder people still take very seriously and therefore consider it ill mannered to wear a hat inside

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Still I believe that these rules should be revised, since things change in our society all the time

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Maybe the teachers only want the students to show that they are not only physically present but also psychically; that the brake is over and that the work has begun

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I don't believe that we should need a change of clothing to create intervals during the day

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: A good teacher should be able to make it clear to the students when the class starts without a "dresscode"

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I absolutely don't believe that the cap would make a student less receptive or motivated with a cap on

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: As long as the students can show that they can handle to participate in class, I see no reason to forbid them to wear a cap

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Some professionals mean that a cap could be compared with sunglasses or even a Walkman

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I find this comparison foolish since the cap doesn't have any effect on a student's vision or hearing

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I cannot understand how a cap can be an obstacle to students learning

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The teenagers today are often wearing their caps on days when they haven't had time to do their hair or when the hair is unwashed; it is very important to them to look fresh

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The cap is also a part of many teenagers search for their identity and it also shows which group they belong to

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The caps and sweaters are also tied to different sports and music

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Logotypes show which team they are supporting

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Teenagers always show what they like and it is very important to them to be different from other groups and above all from adults

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: Earlier teenager could wear jeans and T-shirts to differ from the adults, but that is no longer possible since adults dress the same way that teenager do today

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I believe that a teacher who tries to stop this only is going to get trouble

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: I think that they should accept the caps and not get upset about the trifling matter that it actually is instead of trying to get rid of them

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: There are several far more important matters in school that the teachers could put their time into than this ridiculous struggle back and forward

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The cap doesn't have any impact on the students learning abilities

title: Cap - on or off in school
text: The resistance against the use of caps is only based on old rules that has been handed down to us from a earlier generation that lived in an other society that we do today and therefore it has to change

title: Cap - on or off in school

No sex until marriage
text: In this essay I will argue in favour of not having sex before marriage

title: No sex until marriage
text: By this I hope to convince the reader how much better the world would be if everyone lived under a "law of chastity"

title: No sex until marriage
text: I will first discuss it from a religious perspective, but my main focus will be on the social aspect of this matter

title: No sex until marriage
text: Almost everyone has heard the seventh commandment "Though shalt not commit adultery" (Exodus 20:14), but not everyone interpret it the same way

title: No sex until marriage
text: Many Christian congregations don't just interpret it that you shall not cheat on your spouse, but also that you must be faithful to your future spouse, meaning that you shall not have sex until you are married

title: No sex until marriage
text: But why is it wrong with premarital sexual relationships? We read in 1 Corinthians 6:13 and 18-19: "

title: No sex until marriage
No sex until marriage
No sex until marriage
text: Now the body is not for fornication, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body

title: No sex until marriage
No sex until marriage

text: Flee fornication

title: No sex until marriage
text: Every sin that a man doeth is without the body, but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body

title: No sex until marriage
text: What? Know ye not that your body is the temple of the Holy Ghost, which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your own

title: No sex until marriage
text: " Here we can clearly see that by using your body for the wrong purposes you commit a terrible sin

title: No sex until marriage
text: When God said "be fruitful and multiply, and replenish the earth" (Genesis 1:28), he meant that this should only take place within the sacred bonds of marriage

title: No sex until marriage
text: "Marriage is honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge

title: No sex until marriage
text: " (Hebrews 13:4) I will now proceed to explain why you shouldn't have sex before marriage from a social point of view

title: No sex until marriage
text: I read in Sydsvenska Dagbladet that Malmö had the highest rate of teenage abortions in the Nordic countries, and the girls who are having an abortion are getting ever younger

title: No sex until marriage
text: Also the cases of chlamydia had more than doubled among teenagers

title: No sex until marriage
text: These are shocking facts! Almost every day we can read in the papers about abortions, rapes, pornography and similar things

title: No sex until marriage
text: Pornography is getting grosser and grosser and many experts think that the many gang rapes nowadays are due to the pornography several young men pore over

title: No sex until marriage
text: Some seem to think that what they see on film and in magazines can be transferred to real life

title: No sex until marriage
text: It appears that teenagers are put under a lot of pressure over the fact that they are virgins and think they are the only ones in this state

title: No sex until marriage
text: They have their sexual debut much earlier than ten years ago, some at the scandalous age of twelve! I think this is due to the excessive pornography which you have access to nearly everywhere; on TV, video, Internet and in magazines

title: No sex until marriage
text: No doubt you get the impression that everybody is having sex and then you feel that you must have it too

title: No sex until marriage
text: In the wake of this comes sexually transmitted diseases, abortions and most tragic, rapes

title: No sex until marriage
text: Just because you are fooled into believing that sex is something you must have

title: No sex until marriage
text: "Be there (that is: do it) or be square" so to speak

title: No sex until marriage
text: Now, these things would not be a problem if everybody lived under a "law of chastity"

title: No sex until marriage
text: If we taught our children from the beginning that they don't have to have sex, but that it is perfectly OK to wait until you're married, much unhappiness would be eliminated

title: No sex until marriage
text: I think that fewer people would have premarital sex if they didn't feel the pressure from media, friends and even the sexual instruction in the biology class in school

title: No sex until marriage
text: At an early age children are taught that when they come of age it is natural to begin to have sex

title: No sex until marriage
text: There is no doubt that if you are not member of a religious society that claims that having premarital sex is wrong, you will probably not get the idea that you would wait to have it

title: No sex until marriage
text: Sometimes you get the impression that if you want to wait to have sex you are odd

title: No sex until marriage
text: But the fact is that many of my friends tell me that they wish they had the courage to go against society's norms and defer sexual relations until they have entered holy matrimony

title: No sex until marriage
text: I hope that I have been able to convince you how advantageous it is to wait until marriage before engaging in sexual intimacies and that there are no doubts that I firmly believe in this

Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Loans to finance our nations students? To me it seems quite like a contradiction

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Who would prefer a lifetime in debt just so you can go to school? Well, I believe that any normal person with common sense would answer nobody on the question asked

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Furthermore changes ought to be done such as give students grants instead of loans

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: What is more important is that Sweden as a country would become more internationally successful, which could enhance our economic status both abroad and perhaps even more importantly our domestic situation

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Lets take a look at the situation today: If you manage to get a job after graduation at the age of eighteen you may consider yourself very lucky and even more if you find a fulltime job

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: The main problem ofcourse is money

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: What to do? You cannot parasite on your parents for ever and you do not want to become a bag-lady

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Higher education somebody said, and you think you have the answer that solves all your problems

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: I want to become a Master of Engineering, it is an interesting and well paid job

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: In other words I will have four and half years of studies to look forward at, perhaps move to another city if required

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Now we can start to look at the expenses of the project to come

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Books and study material for four and half years, semester fees, travels, rents, and food to stay alive but these are not the only expenses because I am very sure students have a personal life too

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: In some way students need to find money to cover all these expenses

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: What the state has to offer is a grant of almost two-thousand kronors which surprisingly for them is not enough however every student I have spoken to tend to agree with me

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: What are my options now? - I could ask my parents for some money but this is not really their problem now is it? I am sure they would like to me help out and it makes me feel as if the government thinks the same but I am an adult who cannot depend on my parents all the time

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Perhaps if I took a part time job I could manage to pay my studies, and probably yes I would

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: On the other hand it says in the brochures that this education is a full time occupation so it is not reasonable to ask somebody to work more then full time just so he or she could make a living

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Our options are narrowed down to the last one, the almost obligatory loan

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: To live a decent life most students will take the highest amount allowed, five-thousand kronors which gives you a total of seven-thousand kronors

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Now everybody is happy and satisfied until the day when the state wants their money back

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: After four and a half years of full time studies ex

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: students have approximately reached a debt of twohundredthousand kronors plus interest

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: This is a debt which hardly nobody manages to pay off during their lives as taxpayers

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Luckily the debt is written off at the age of sixty-five when you retire as a pensioner

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: You wonder if this is the thanks you get from the state when you could have lived on unemployment benefits watching TV and do nothing all day long

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: In contrast I pay the highest taxes on earth, and try to pay off my loan

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: In addition I am a jackpot for the state and I don't cost a penny neither

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: To sum up I believe there should not exist such a ting as a student loan, it is only an obstacle which many people will not manage to pass

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: With the taxes of today we should not have any problem with the finance of higher education students, in longer perspective society will only gain from this system

title: Student loans: A lifetime in debt
text: Instead of a lifetime debt as a repeler we should give higher education studies the same status as a regular job and pay the seven-thousand as a kind of salary

title: Europe: Co-operation or every one for themselves?

title: Europe: Co-operation or every one for themselves?
text: To conclude this brief argumentation for a stronger European Union, with more power to affect the questions that are important for the entire continent I want to stress that "The European Union of today is a Union of sovereign states, but states with common values and goals"

title: Europe: Co-operation or every one for themselves?

Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: In this essay I will present two of the main reasons why Sweden ought to leave the European Union

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: There are, as I see it, more reasons than those two that I'm going to deal with

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: But I'll concentrate on the two that I've considered to be the most important

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Namely that, first of all the EU isn't a democratic union and second of all the countries that are supposed to work together have to little in common for them to be able to work effectively with governing the union

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Both reasons are strong reasons not to continue being part of the union although the first is the strongest reason you could ever think of

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: The threats against democracy today are many, not all of them having to do with right winged fanatics with shaved heads and kickerboots

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: No, I believe that the biggest threat today is the EU

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Because, if we examine the word, democracy stands for government by the people and/or social equality

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Now people may say a lot of good things about the EU but it is not governed by the people although I might go so far as to say that when it comes to social equality, no country that I know of has ever been equal in every sense of the word

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Therefor I will not complain about the EU not being equal

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I will instead concentrate on the government by the people part

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: The EU today is a maze of red tape and do not enter signs, how will the public ever get an insight into the decisions if the people running the EU continue trying to keep much of what they do as secret as possible

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: For example, when an official in the EU leaked information to the newspapers about corruption in the European parliament

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: The people he worked for didn't exactly congratulate him on being so honest, no, they demoted him, he got a job as a cleaner in the European parliament, needless to say he soon thereafter quit his job

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: When this question was debated many argued that the decision to replace him was the right one, because he had gone to the press and not to the parliament

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: He went to the people living in the EU and not to the people who ruled the EU because he felt that the people had a right to know first

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: That is illegal

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: But so is, I fell, corruption

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Therefor, I fell, he should have been rewarded not demoted in the way that he was

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: This is a very frightening example, and I would believe it scares a lot of people working in the European parliament and a lot of people living in the EU

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I mean who wants to start sweeping floors just because someone else on his/hers job is being dishonest and he/she can't look the other way? And who wants to be governed by people that demotes honesty? I certainly do not want either of it, and I can't believe that anyone else wants that to happen

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Now, some might argue that if you don't like the people making the decisions, you could choose not to vote for them the next time

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: But if you do not know what is going on in the parliament, How can you decide who is honest and who is not? And if you got demoted for telling the public the truth about some of your colleagues and what they did with the publics money

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: Who would dare to tell the truth? My second reason why I think Sweden ought to leave the EU is that the counties in the EU has to little in common with each other to be able to work effectively together

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I'll try to be as brief as possible when explaining this

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I'm not saying that because we have our differences we cannot work together at all in the EU, what I'm trying to say is that we cannot work the way we are doing it now

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I mean how can someone who has been living in Spain or Italy all his life fully understand the problems facing the farmers in the north of Sweden or Finland, to take one example

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: The information they get is from the Swedish/Finnish representatives and they might not know or care enough about the subject to give the full picture

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: I would have liked to develop that thought further but the essay would become altogether to long if I did so I'll try to wrap this up now

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: The two reasons I have presented are to me strong reasons for Sweden to leave the EU, but of course I'm not impossible

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!
text: If the problems that I have addressed are solved then I see no reason for us not to stay in the union, just as long as we don't join the EMU

title: Sweden, just say No to the EU!

Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: In the schools of today the Principals bear the utmost responsibility for everything that concerns the school

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: That means they have to master a vast variety things

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Being knowledgeable about economics and the preparing of budgets concerning very large amounts of money is just as important as being competent in areas like personnel administration

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It is a very hard job which means that you have to be very competent to manage it

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Therefore it would seem insane not to give future Principals the chance to assimilate this competence and at the same time give society the certainty that their principals are skilled ones

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Unfortunately no such chance or certainty is given in the schools of today

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: The vast majority of peoples that become Principals have previously worked as teachers which is very good because it gives them a first rate knowledge of the school system

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: But it in no way gives them the competence for a job equivalent to Vice Director in a major company

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: The only way to make sure that future principals really possess the competence required for the job is to introduce compulsory education

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: One of the main points of such an education would be to increase the proficiency in economics

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: If you would want a job with equivalent economic responsibilities outside the school system you would have to study at least five years at university

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Economics is a very important part of a Principal's work and when neglected a debility could dramatically reduce efficiency in the whole school

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It will cost a lot of money to educate all future principals

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: But these costs are nothing compared to the big resource losses each year caused by bad economic planing by incompetent principals

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: There are very large amounts of money pumped into the school system every year so it really lays in the taxpayers best interest to make sure that all of it is used as efficiently as possible

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: In these days of down cuts and reduced means to education it is more important then ever to have competent leaders that know how to reduce economical loses

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: There is just no room for mistakes anymore

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: The vast majority of those that become principals have no earlier experience of working with economic issues

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Obviously this is a great problem and it is also quite obvious that educating these peoples are absolutely crucial to obtain well functioning schools

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Well functioning schools does naturally not consist only of a well oiled economic machinery but of several other just as important pieces

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Personnel administration is maybe the most prominent of these

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Organizing workschedules and supervising the efficiency necessary in an organization as big as a school is one of a Principal's main purposes

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It is a very difficult duty especially if you don't have a back round in personnel administration

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Not many peoples that become Principals do

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Personnel administration is not some thing you know instinctively

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It is something you have to learn

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It is absolutely absurd to think that peoples without any relevant education or experience can perform a good job on their own

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: If I were to apply for a job as a Staff Manager on a company with about a hundred employees (the size of an average school) I would probably have to show a higher certificate in personnel administration and some good references confirming my competence to even have the slightest chance of getting the job

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Isn't it very strange that the demands are so much lower on peoples holding key positions in the schools than outside them? It is about time we raised these demands and made sure that principals do have the competence required for a directorship

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Many regard the school as some thing inferior, crippled by inefficiency and incompetence

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: In some cases my own experiences correspond very well to that picture and that makes me sad

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: The only way to turn this negative trend is to raise the standard of competence in the whole school management

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: There are shorter voluntary courses for Principals and future Principals to take and they are used to some extent

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: But the problem is not Principals that acknowledge their own deficiency and do something about it

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: It is all those incompetent Principals out there who when asked would say he/she was doing a marvelous job and that there were no faults on their behalf that are dragging the whole school system down

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Having compulsory education for peoples that want to become Principals is the only way to spare the school system these burdens that constitute a great handicap

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Of course there should be exceptions made from this compulsory education for peoples that of varying reasons already posses the competence required

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: The idea is to increase competence and improve efficiency not to scare peoples from becoming Principals

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: To educate Principals will not make the world a better place

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
text: Or maybe it will? Many peoples will at least be spared the frustrating situation of working under an incompetent Principal and see their school limp because of inefficiency; never quite reaching its full potential

title: Introduce Compulsory education for future Principals!
THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: In dictionaries the word democracy is defined as "a system of government in which everyone in the country can vote to elect its members" and as "a situation in which everyone is equal and has the right to vote, make decisions etc"

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: The word monarchy is defined as "a system in which a country is ruled by a king or a queen"

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Sweden is paradoxically both a democracy and a monarchy and the way I see it a country cannot or at least should not be both

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Some of the main arguments I have heard from people who wants to keep the monarchy are that "Sweden has always been a monarchy" and " it's such a nice tradition"

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Yes, Sweden is one of the oldest monarchies in Europe and we have a history full of fascinating stories about royal war-heroes, conspiracies and mysteriously murdered kings

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: But those arguments aren't relevant to the issue

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Today's system and society can't be compared with for example the eighteenth century and our present king Carl XVI Gustav can't be compared with Carl XII

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: The word is constantly changing and today monarchy is an old-fashioned system which doesn't fit into a democratic society such as ours

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: To use history and tradition as arguments in this serious matter is just a naive and ignorant way of trying to avoid discussion and debate

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: We have to eliminate the romantic and sentimental image that surrounds the royal family and the monarchy as a system

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: We have to look at our society from a logical and practical point of view

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: I mentioned the word democracy and it's meaning earlier

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: People around the world are fighting for it

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: People sacrifice their lives for the right to vote and choose their leaders, the right to speak their mind and the right to be treated as equals

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Democracy is not as obvious to everybody as it is for us

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: In a monarchy the regent is not elected

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: The Swedish monarch is not chosen by the people and he/she does not have the same rights as everybody else

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: He/she does not have the right to vote, he/she is not free to choose his/her religion and despite the fact that he/she is the head of our state he/she has got no political power

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: But still he/she ranks above everybody else and he/she requires to be addressed as "his/her royal highness" or "your majesty"

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: And all that because he/she was born a crown-prince/princess and through inheritance became head of a state

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Sweden is a democracy

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: The Swedish people want to be treated as equals and with respect

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Men and women, homosexuals and heterosexuals, natives and immigrants etc

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Then why do more than 70% of the Swedish population accept this kind of class-difference and segregation where a small, limited group of people are treated as if they were more important and worth more respect than the rest of the people just because they were born by royal parents? Is it because "it's such a nice tradition"? Another argument that I have come across is the royal family's popularity

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: People seem to love reading and talking about their romances and other private matters

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: The media's coverage of the royal family has increased and the interest among the population has also increased because of that

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: That argument would become very weak if today's media-situation would change and that is exactly what the royal family wants

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: They are beginning to complain about the lack of privacy and the media's interference of their lives and that is understandable

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: But their popularity depends completely on the media

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: So, is their popularity totally based on the fact that they are a never-creasing source of gossip and would they be as popular if the monarch had the power? History, tradition and popularity are all quite weak arguments pro-monarchy

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: But they are also very difficult to ignore

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: These things are deeply rooted in the Swedish people

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: We have become used to the monarchy and a habit is always difficult to break

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: But the society of today is in need of a change

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Today's monarchy is an old fashioned and reactionary system where the monarch's only function seems to be waving at people from a balcony, cutting ribbons and throwing champagne-bottles at boats

title: THE SWEDISH MONARCHY A NICE TRADITION WORTH KEEPING?
text: Do we really need that?
