Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: What was in the past something so natural for young people as wearing jeans is nowadays, wearing school uniforms is today often looked upon as something boring, old fashioned and something that deprives a child much of his or her identity

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: I disagree and think that school uniforms could serve as an effective weapon against problems related to increasingly differences in children's culture, background and social status

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: I think that there are so many good arguments for making school uniforms compulsory and I'm surprised that so few schools in Sweden today consider wearing school uniforms as an important step towards less bullying and more equality

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: I can't count the mornings during my years in school that I was standing in front of my wardrobe searching for something nice to wear

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And none of these days were any special, except for when photographs were taken

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: All the other days were just ordinary school days

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And who doesn't remember worrying about not wearing the same type of clothes as everybody else, about being too different from all the others

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: This is, I think, something that lots of children carry with them from their first year in school till they graduate

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And that is something we have to change

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: More and more Swedish schools today experience increasingly bullying often when cultural diversity grows

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: There is obviously a need among children of being a group, not just mentally but even more important, in appearance

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And what would suit better, in a world where fashion is as important as it is in society today and when the fashion trade direct more and more advertisement towards younger and younger people, than start wearing uniforms in school

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: I think this would make fashion more of a personal interest, something you as an individual could choose if you want to support or not and that would change the situation in school

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: Because someone wearing old clothes, bought five years ago and that two or three older brothers or sisters have already worn for a couple of years, is often an easy victim for bullies

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And a child being bullied for a thing like this is something that we should really make an effort to stop

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: And why not doing it in such a simple way as to introduce school uniforms

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: We live in a multi- cultural society where conflicts not seldom arise because of differences in manners and customs and but it's still our will to create an harmonious unity for children in schools and during their social activities

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: We want all to have the possibility of keeping their own traditions and culture but there is also a fight for equality, for all being the same and for all having the same possibilities

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: School uniforms would much correspond to these aims and would make it easier, especially for the most vulnerable in society

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: Unfortunately, there are still huge differences in economic status among Swedish families and this is of course followed by big differences for children in the same class

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: A single child with well- paid parents has no problems in always following the hottest trend and always having the right newest clothes and things

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: The situation for another child in the same class with three brothers and sisters and with parents earning just under the average income is of course totally different

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: This, the economic aspect, is another important one of introducing school uniforms

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: Not only would children be in need of fewer sets of clothes since there would be no difference in appearance (with ordinary clothes, both children and parents feel that the child can't wear the same clothes all the time), but it would also make it easier for a child to wear old clothes that doesn't fit an older brother or sister anymore since the style hasn't changed

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: People against school uniforms say that the uniforms make children lose their identity and that introducing school uniforms would be a bad way of trying to control things

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: But young children's identities are reflected much more in their behaviour and in what they say

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
text: Contrary to make less identity, I think that children would feel it assuring, knowing that they've all got the same clothes not depending on if they are rich or poor, black or white or if they come from two totally different countries and cultures

title: Old fashioned or the hottest trend?
Alcohol at eighteen
text: Today, in Sweden, the law allows people of eighteen to buy alcoholic beverages in restaurants, pubs, clubs and cafés, but not to buy them from state liquor shops (System Bolaget)

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: In addition, you are officially not allowed to bring in alcoholic drinks from other countries, including other countries in the EU, where these are normally available for all people over the age of eighteen

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: I find this situation ridiculous and believe that the law should be changed

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: My main and most obvious point of argumentation is to do with the hypocrisy surrounding Swedish laws regarding alcohol

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: While you are allowed to drink alcohol in, for example, pubs and restaurants at the age of eighteen you are still not deemed old enough to buy your own alcoholic drink from a shop

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: If the law allows you to buy liquor in one place it is inconsistent not to allow you to do so in another

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: As there is no law preventing eighteen-year-olds from actually drinking these beverages, there should be no other restrictions concerning alcohol

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: There is more to your eighteenth birthday than your being allowed in to the local pub

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: You are now seen as an adult

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: This means you must start paying taxes, you are responsible for your own finances and debts, you can get married and take a bank loan to buy yourself a house or flat, you can drive a car or even a truck

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: You are now old enough to face criminal charges as an adult, which means you can be sent to prison

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: You are expected to do your military service and fight for your country in times of war

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: You can get a hunting certificate which entitles you to own a rifle

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: However, you are still not allowed to buy yourself, say, a bottle of wine to go with your meal

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: In my opinion a person entitled to all these things must also be able to take responsibility when it comes to buying alcohol, after all, it takes a lot more responsibility to deal with buying a house than with buying a bottle of wine

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: The fact that you are not allowed to buy this bottle of wine yourself by no means stops you from getting hold of it in other ways

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Although it is illegal to buy alcohol for teenagers there is no shortage of people offering to do so

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: This whole business is turning people into criminals

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Most parents have at some point offered their son or daughter a glass of wine with their meal

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Take graduation, for example, a typical situation when drinking Champagne is very much part of a tradition

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: A tradition which, according to Swedish laws, could count as a criminal offence

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Another more serious point is the fact that a lot of young people are so set on getting hold of alcohol that they will drink almost anything they can get their hands on, including home-brewed liquor, also known as moonshine

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: As well as increasing this market the law is encouraging people to bring in more liquor from neighbouring countries, like from Denmark, where it is a lot easier to get hold of

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Allowing eighteen-year-olds to buy legally prepared spirits from a liquor store would to some extent decrease these trends

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: All in all I feel that this law is a bit silly

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: Prohibiting people under the age of twenty from buying alcoholic drinks from shops, but not from restaurants or pubs is definitely showing double standards

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: If being eighteen means that you are an adult and thus old enough to be held responsible for all your deeds, as is in fact expected of an adult, I believe that this responsibility should include the right to buy a bottle of wine, in a shop, for home use

title: Alcohol at eighteen
text: In my opinion a change in law might, as well as making the laws consistent, give people a healthier point of view concerning alcohol, thus, hopefully, decreasing the demand for home-brewed liquor

title: Alcohol at eighteen
The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: To be old and need special help is a common problem

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Many consider that it's the relatives' responsibility to take care of the elderly, but the elderly have the right to experience a meaningful life of their own

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Quality of life is to be with friends, take part in activities and have possibilities to improve the physical condition

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: In comparison with a living by the family the elderly will get all this and also be carefully looked after by educated staff

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Elderly people living in a block of service flats supplied by the local society have much more possibilities to develop than those who live at their family's house do

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: In the service flats they have always staff around who can help, encourage and stimulate them

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Further they have a nurse and a doctor at hand and special aid for rehabilitation

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: If something would happen when they are alone, perhaps they have fallen or need help for the lavatory they just use the alarm system

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: To feel safe and secure is important for them when they have to trust other people for living

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Other advantages in this kind of living are the possibility to take part in a lot of different activities

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: It can be a therapist who is reading aloud from newspapers or books or a local musician who entertain them

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: There are also several physical activities like the possibility to weave, to use the joiner's workshop, to take part in gymnastics or to do some assistance in the institutional kitchen

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: In a private home without all these kinds of resources it can be difficult to revitalize them and instead they will be sitting in an armchair or a rocking chair all the day long, perhaps they don't even eat

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Even though the heavy expenses for care of old people are a big part in the budget of the Society nowadays, is it cheaper to let up buildings and staff for this kind of living than put all the responsibility on the family or other relatives

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: To take care of people who are too old to look after themselves and need special help cost a lot of energy and time

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Thus it influence the family's own working hours, leisure and health

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: The result can be less employed people who are paying taxes and more people being on the sick list because of overwork

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Besides families with less salary can be forced to apply for social allowance, which means that the Society have to pay in any case and the final costs might be even bigger

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: The benefits for families with relatives looked after by the state, are to begin with, their opportunities to plan their relations with the elderly

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: They will be allowed to do what they want without having a bad conscience all the time

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Instead they can do visits when it's convenient and spend pleasant time together

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Furthermore, it's liberating for them to get away from asking other people of assistance now and then when problem comes up and they need help to look after the elderly

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: It's trying to be bound night and day without getting a chance to relax

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Finally it gives the family time to establish a social life together with friends, which is one of the necessities to reach quality of life

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: One disadvantage with elderly people living in a social institution is that a generation gap will arise

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Young people will not be accustomed with old people and perhaps distance themselves from natural contacts

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: In fact there are children who have never met an old person living in such an institution

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Another disadvantage can be the risk for the elderly to be institutionalized, for example to be depending on the daily run of things and refuse to do something else like a walk or a trip, because of the fear to miss the dinner or something like that

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: Despite of disadvantages this kind of living is mostly the best for the elderly

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: In most of the institutions nowadays the lodgers use their own furniture because it is important for them to have well-known things around like the favourite chair or a home-made rug besides all photographs of children and grandchildren

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: It's also important for them to keep their integrity, to have a life of their own without ending up in the position as a child to their own child

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
text: To be a lifelong parent is a right

title: The Society Gives Possibilities to Elderly People.
THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Most mental and physical abilities change and develop as people grow

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: It is a salient feature in our schoolsystem that a great deal is done to cultivate skills such as reading, writing and arithmetics

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: However, the development of artistic skills seems to come to a stop at quite an early age

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Many grown-ups stay at a 14-year-old-level when it comes to expressing themselves artistically

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: One of the reasons why schools favour the more theoretical subjects is that it is a prevalent notion that what you learn in arts classes is of little use in daily life, which is not true! Firstly, in art classes we deal with the fact that we live in a world where we are drowned with impressions and images such as advertisements, commercials, musicvideos and news-images from various media

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Commercials are often flashy, entertaining and inventive

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: But their purpose is to make us buy things and they do not reflect the society we live in today

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Not even the daily news gives us a correct image of the world

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: People with values, ideas and prejudice choose events and make us think that these are the major happenings in the world, when in fact these images are interpretations of what the world looks like at a certain moment

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Often newsflashes come out as objective and true, so it is vital to be critical and to understand images and their connotations and not just perceive them as the "truth"

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Moreover, according to experts' research the brain is divided into two parts - the left and the right

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Reading, writing and arithmetics are skills that are usually located in the left hemisphere of the brain

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: This part is also considered as dominant and even tends to take over tasks which are more suitable for the right side of the brain to handle

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: The right hemisphere is generally seen as the subordinate one

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: It is alotted artistic abilities, thus good at things like music, drawing, creativity and perception

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: All these knacks seem somewhat forgotten in our schools, and although they are valued they are considered to be a natural result from using ones verbal and analytical skills

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: However, I will argue that this is not the case and that we need specifically to cultivate the right side of our brain, something that is hardly possible to do during one sixty-minute-lesson of art once a week

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: People in general are of the opinion that the ability to draw or perform other artistic activities is something that a minority of people are gifted with and if you can't already draw there is no use in trying to learn

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: It is like deciding not to learn a foreign language because you can't already speak it

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Actually, the difficulty to draw lies rather in learning how to observe or being able to switch over to a particular way of seeing

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: From the time we grow up we are used to seeing things through words; a cat, an apple, a tree and so on

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Consequently, the left side of the brain is activated and tends to put things into categories and to symbolise things

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: We all have a symbol for a face, an eye or a house, but this is not what they really look like

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: To draw is a process which demands careful observation and meticulous perception of details and all kinds of information

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: This can be trained in a number of ways and the trick is to give the brain a task which the left hemisphere either doesn't know how to perform or doesn't want to perform

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: This demands a lot of concentration

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: For example, you can study an up-side-down-image without having seen it the correct way first and try and draw what you see

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: When you focus on how the lines meet instead of what the image actually represents the left side becomes confused and since it can't compare the information to any stored memories it leaves the task to the more suitable right side

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: The majority of people are not aware of the fact that we actually neglect one side of the brain and its' capacity

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Thus, the school has a huge responsibility in conveying the importance of exploring our whole brain, not just a part of it

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: What is more, the left and the right side of the brain actually cooperate in several areas

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Psychologists, for example, use drawing as a method to release and understand hidden emotions

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: A drawing from the heart can be of immense help when solving an emotional problem and revealing what you actually feel

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: When the right side of the brain has shown the left side what the problem is the left side can solve the problem through language and logical thinking

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: In conclusion, to fully function as human beings there is a need for the revaluation of artistic skills and perception

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
text: Today the schoolsystem rewards the skills of the left side of the brain to such an extent that much of our childrens' inherent opportunities of the right side will stay undeveloped

title: THE RIGHT SIDE THERE SHOULD BE MORE EMPHASIS ON ARTISTIC SUBJECTS AT SCHOOL
Unemployd, new assisstants at schools?
text: Why should people who receive unemployment benefits be required to work in the public sector? There are three main reasons were I will argue for such a move

title: Unemployd, new assisstants at schools?
text: Firstly in the Swedish newspapers we can read almost everyday that schoolteachers, nurses, policedepartments and homes for elderly people are constatly struggling because of reductions of the employees but I will focus on the school

title: Unemployd, new assisstants at schools?

text: If your pet picks up a disease that is impossible or still difficult to cure, you bring him to the vet to give him a shot so the pet falls asleep

text: It makes me start thinking, why cannot we have the possibility to decide what to do with our own lives

title: My life, my choice
text: Many people are lying motionless in beds in our hospitals every day, where they are kept alive by machines

text: Note that no one but the patient should be able to decide such a ting

title: My life, my choice
text: No relative or close friend shall make the decision

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Silver treasures from the Viking Age (approx

title: Myth or Reality?
Myth or Reality?
text: D

title: Myth or Reality?
text: ) have always been an appreciated topic in discussions - both academical and general

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The great interest stems from the large quantity of hoards (there are for example around 700 silver hoards found only on the island Gotland), and the abundant mythical sphere which sorrounds them

title: Myth or Reality?
text: What we really know about these hoards is that they once were laid to rest in our native soil to dwell on their secrets, not willing nor able to share them with the posterity

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The mysterious hoards have especially since the 1950s stimulated many a scientist into the battle of more academical studies of the Viking Age and its silver

title: Myth or Reality?
text: A debate was thereby started, and is still going strong

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Rather a few theories have over the years risen from the ongoing debate, some, I have to say, more roted in reality than others

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The most recent - and also most successful - hypothesis claims that the hoards were hidden in contemporary houses, strictly as security rutin, that is, to hide the jewellery from potential thieves

title: Myth or Reality?
text: (A bank-box synonym) I strongly disagree with this thesis' aim, which is to generalize the reasons behind silver deposits into one simple explanation

title: Myth or Reality?
text: I think that the situation is more complex, and that there also is a possibility of a religious background to the silver hoards

title: Myth or Reality?
text: First of all, nothing contradicts the idea that it could have been religious reasons behind the treasure deposits made by the 11th century inhabitants

title: Myth or Reality?
text: On the other hand, the establishing of facts concerning silver hoards is indeed a delicate and problematic matter altogether, due to the reality that the only things we are able to state about them are the location where we find them and the material vision of them at that point

title: Myth or Reality?
text: In other words, the rest is really just a net of high qualified guessings

title: Myth or Reality?
text: What really favours my idea of religious reasons, is a well-known phenomenon in the archaeological dicipline called Odin's law

title: Myth or Reality?
text: This expression was first mentioned in an old Scandinavian saga, written in the 13th century

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Odin's law means that a person buries silver in honour of the gods (Odin etc), but the finesse with this deed is that he/she will get back the silver in the next life, and is thereby assured of affluency in a future life as well

title: Myth or Reality?
text: A god place for a sacrifice of this sort would probably not be in your house, but perhaps in the forest under a bush or stone

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Further, silver has alway been thought of as having magical power

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Many myths and legends have as a result of this come to surround the silver tresures, proclaiming them to be the source of many strange events, even after the Viking Age

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Silver was also said to be capable of protecting and effecting your life and destiny in different kind of ways

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The very same thing was ascribed to dead ancestors

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Their graves were therefore placed close to their progeny's settings, clearly recognizable in the landscape as small mounds or monuments

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Imagine then if you buried silver in a forefather's grave, that would give you dubbel protection (against demons and generally nasty people)! Silver items have been found in earlier graves, which eliminates the possibility of them being grave gifts

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Obviously, old sagas don't prove a thing, exept perhaps that people in past times were able to make up exciting stories based on vivid imagination

title: Myth or Reality?
text: This may be true, but I would here like to apply the proverb; no smoke without fire

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The trubble is that we can not figure out exactly what our ancestors' religion included or how it was carried out

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Beliefs about magic posessed by silver could very well have been an important factor in their supposed metaphysical world, and that the rich silver hoards we find today actually is the vestiges of their religious exercises

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Critics might say: why base an argumentative essay on nothing more established than a mere guess (or a hunch in my case)? Personally, I think it is more than a guess

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The fact that we have found silver hoards in environments other than in contemporary houses, speaks for itself

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Hiding silver for thieves must be, I agree, most convenient in your house, not in the woods where the groth is nothing but stabil, and can after a while effectively prevent you from ever finding your silver again by changing appearance

title: Myth or Reality?
text: The question which follows so naturally runs; how will you then explain the hoards found in the forests, nowhere near a house, especially not one from the 10th and 11th centuries? Lingering manifestations from a religion dead long ago, I say

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Conclusively I would like to say that there is certainly not one single explanation one can apply on every silver hoard

title: Myth or Reality?
text: Each one has its own, individual story, but I find it very likely that many of them were put away from the eye of time with religious overtones

title: Myth or Reality?

text: It does also seem paradoxical that a country, which has been governed by social democrats, almost without interruption, through a century, is so fond of monarchy

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: For what is it exactly about the royal family that we are supposed to respect? How have they contributed to improving the human condition? Is it their durability? Namely, that they are capable of sitting on the throne for years without once going to the toilet during a public engagement? No seriously, something is rotten in the state of Sweden when we are expected to bow to them, and address them by titles, simply because they were born with the name of Bernadotte

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: One more reason to abolish the monarchy is that it is expensive

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: The 40 million SEK a year it costs to maintain a monarchy could certainly be used in better ways

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: However, it would be wrong to confine criticism of the royals to economic matters

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: Such a discussion misses the point about the political role that the royal family still plays in Sweden

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: And it is that role which provides the best reasons for abolishing the monarchy altogether

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: Anybody who believes in basic democracy should be in favor of abolishing the monarchy, along with all of its hereditary privileges and powers

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: Having said this, it is of course also important to keep the question of royalty in perspective

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: The monarchy is symbolic of much that is wrong with Swedish society, but it is not the cause of the problems

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: But calling for the abolition of the monarchy could also help to sweep away some of the double standard circulating in Sweden

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: There are a lot of people who say they sympathize with the social democratic ideology

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: They are in favor of a classless society and are pro democracy, but at the same time wishes to maintain the greatest reminder of Sweden as an at one point truly undemocratic country: the monarchy

title: Monarchy - An Expensive Anachronism
text: There is no place for a monarch in a democratic and modern country like Sweden - set Victoria free!

title: A world without television
text: Could you manage without your TV? Would your life be over if you had to get rid of it? You may feel that way, but, as I see it, it is not so

title: A world without television
text: I want to, through my arguments and through this essay, show you that it is possible and even good to live without a TV

title: A world without television
text: However, nowadays it is hard to find a home without a TV and it seems to be one important part of a good standard of living

title: A world without television
text: For many people, watching TV is a natural daily thing as well as working, going to school, spending time with family and friends

title: A world without television
text: Being aware of all this, I still maintain that it is possible to live your life without a TV, I do nowadays

title: A world without television
text: I think it is even good for you and you will benefit from it

title: A world without television
text: Let me tell you why through my three arguments and my conclusion

title: A world without television
text: My first argument is about economy

title: A world without television
text: The possession of a TV will increase your costs

title: A world without television
text: First of all you need to have a television set

title: A world without television
text: A new one costs several thousand Swedish crowns

title: A world without television
text: Furthermore you have to pay about 400 Swedish Crowns each quarter of a year, just for the possession of it

title: A world without television
text: Other expenses that might occur are for example: if the TV gets out of order it needs to be repaired

title: A world without television
text: If you then do not have a warrant, the repair will probably cost you a lot of money

title: A world without television
text: A TV costs you money, but that is not all

title: A world without television
text: My second argument is that I think it can take more things away from you, like your imagination and your creativity

title: A world without television
text: Let me explain this further

title: A world without television
text: When you watch TV you are stuffed with a large number of images, fast clips and a lot of information, not always good information

title: A world without television
text: It is like you do not have to think of your own

title: A world without television
text: Everything is given to you

title: A world without television
text: I think this is bad, especially for young children that watch TV too much

title: A world without television
text: As a consequence of this, it is not strange that they might find school and reading boring because life outside the TV-screen can not be as intensive as the fast clips on MTV or on another channel

title: A world without television
text: Moreover, by the fact that TV does not trigger their imagination or encourage them to think for themselves, I feel that they are not given the possibility to fully develop their own thinking in order to become strong individuals

title: A world without television
text: As I just explained, I am of the opinion that TV can kill your imagination, in addition, watching TV can also be a time-killing occupation, which leads to my next argument

title: A world without television
text: My third and final argument is that the TV does take valuable time away from you, that you instead could spend on something else or with someone else

title: A world without television
text: In today's society, we all complain about that there is never enough time

title: A world without television
text: We rush to work and to school, we rush home, we have to cook, clean, wash, do this and that

title: A world without television
text: Still we can spend hours in front of the TV

title: A world without television
text: Maybe some of you may say that it is a way to relax, to forget all the 'musts' of our lives

title: A world without television
text: I understand that, but I think that there are other ways of relaxing, like for example taking a walk, alone or with someone, reading a book or just spending time with the ones you love

title: A world without television
text: Look at Sweden today, so many relationships do not last because we do not take the time to cherish them

title: A world without television
text: I do not say TV is all to blame for this, but I do say that the time we spend on watching TV we could spend as quality time with our family or with our friends

title: A world without television
text: It is a good start for making the world a better place

title: A world without television
text: Now you have read my three arguments concerning life with or without a TV

title: A world without television
text: I have claimed that the TV does take money, imagination and time away from us

title: A world without television
text: If you turn this around: a life without a TV, compared to other people, will give you money to spend on something else, hopefully restore your imagination and creativity to make life more fun and last, but not least, it will give you time that you can give yourself and other people

title: A world without television
Abolishing of the death penalty
text: In a lot of countries the death penalty is still in use

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Many people are of the opinion that the death penalty is needed as the only right sentence for exceptionally cruel crimes

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I do not share that opinion, mainly because I do not think that any person has the right to kill another human being

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: There are many reasons why I think the death penalty should be abolished

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I will now put my arguments forward and explain why I feel the way I do

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: To begin with I don't think that any person should have the right to execute anyone

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: We are all human beings

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: An execution, or a state killing, is colder and more premeditated than any of the crimes committed by the convict

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Even if a person has committed terrible crimes, and doesn't deserve to live because of all the horrible things he or she has done, it isn't right for any other person to take a life

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: If we use the death penalty for people who have murdered others, we are as bad as they are

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: It's not excusable to commit dreadful crimes but neither to execute the one who has

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Nobody, either individually nor as a society representative, has the right to take another man's life, even taken in to account the seriousness of his guilt

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I understand people, who are victims of cruel crimes wanting to see their attacker executed

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I also understand relatives of the victim feeling that way, but I do not think that the death penalty is the right solution

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: When a person has committed a murder or anything like that, I totally agree on that he or she should be put away behind bars to pay for what they have done

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Secondly I think that a person who has committed a dreadful crime should be locked up for life

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I think it's too easy for murderers to get away with being executed, because then they aren't being punished for life like the victim and its relatives

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: The truth and the remorse remain even when an exceptionally dangerous killer is executed

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Also I think it would be better trying to re-educate and rehabilitate the convict morally and humanly

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: What rehabilitation will be possible towards a dead man? Thirdly the death penalty is the ultimate cruel and inhuman punishment existing and it violates the right to life

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Not to mention that an execution is an act of violence, and violence tends to provoke violence

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: You can't prevent killing by killing

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: I also think it is the duty of every state to protect the life of all persons within its jurisdiction without exception

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Executions, whether by governments or others, are equally unacceptable

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Abolition of the death penalty is necessary for the achievement of declared international standards

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: People who support the death penalty are saying that it is too costly for the taxpayers to have the criminals in prison

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: The critics, on the other hand, have mentioned the economy as a strong reason for abolishing of the death penalty

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Economic studies have shown that it is a very costly procedure to execute people, the way they do in the US for example

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: The cost isn't only financial, the imposition and infliction of the death penalty is brutal to all that are involved in the process

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: My final argument is based on the fact that innocent people could be executed and that is a risk we cannot afford to take

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: There is always a possibility of miscarriages of justice

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: The possibility of killing an innocent person alone justifies the abolition of the death penalty

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: Sometimes people are falsely accused of crimes they haven't committed

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: It has happened that innocent people have been executed

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: We are depending on judicial system; a mistake made by an inexperienced lawyer, for example a minor delay in producing evidence for the defence, can cost the defendant's life

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
text: This we cannot allow

title: Abolishing of the death penalty
BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: When did Sweden become a country like any other country in the world? When did we first start to worry about locking our doors while going away to the shop? When did we for the first time feel that we had to take an extra look over our shoulder while walking the dog, to make sure that their would be no one behind us? How did this happen to us in this country that used to be known as one of the greatest and safest countries in the world, and why did this happen? These are questions that one can ask himself when it's over and too late, but yet we have time to change things, it's not all over

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: My belief is that we have to start this process by looking closer into the school system, after all that is where it all starts

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: Recently we've been given shocking news about the situation in Swedish schools, both from media, like newspapers and television, but also locally through neighbours and local authorities

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: It's when it comes down to this level that people starts to wake up and realise that something isn't right

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: What is wrong with schools in Sweden today, and what can be done about the problems? Some people believe that the solution is to upgrade the laws that involves the way schools are ran today

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: To increase the personal safety people are suggesting to have metal detectors be installed in certain schools or let guards patrol the school area to make sure that everyone is safe within the premises

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: I don't think that is the right way to handle the problem

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: We have to believe that it's possible to go back in time and find an explanation to when and why things started to drift out of hand

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: I believe that we make an enormous mistake if we go down the same road as they have done in the US to solve problems, a system where peoples integrity is more or less gone and where the school has given up it's responsibilities to policemen, who now are responsible for the safety and well-being of the students

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: I do understand that school-boards in schools with difficulties do have to put up with an enormous amount of criticisms from parents and society

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: They are more or less forced to do something radical about the problems, as quick as possible

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: I also think that now is the time to stop and think not only one time but twice, before we make a mistake that will leave scars in our nation for ever

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: So now I've said that the easy solution is not always the best one

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: That was very easily said, but to give examples to what could be done instead is not as an easy task

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: I'm not saying that I know the best way to tackle the problem, but what I do know and what I think is the elementary answer to all the questions that are related to this problem, is that we have to start believing in the goodness of people again

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: Once we start trusting each other we can start solving the problems we have

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: To do something like installing metal detectors to make sure that no one goes to school carrying a gun or a knife might solve an indirect problem, that some students for different reasons, actually pass the school entrance with some kind of weapon on them

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: On the other hand it certainly will not treat the heart of the problem

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: If you can't trust, you will not be trusted, in this case only held back

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: To sum up the content of my message I would like to emphasise upon how important I think it is to not be influenced to much by other countries

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: We who live together in this country have to build up the kind of trust that we used to hold for each other, starting with that we have to feel good about ourselves

title: BOY SHOT DOWN IN SCHOOL!
text: Tell me, who would feel good about themselves if they weren't trusted?

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: The stressful and competitive society of today, is a battle-field unlike any other we have seen in history this far

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Due to the higher grade of early specialisation, and the information technology revolution, demands are made on our civilisation like never before

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: For example, today there is a need in the corporate world for everything to be done by the minute, or rather a day or two in advance

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: When we first started to realise that only by having a thoroughly rested body, with no distracting ailments, would we be able to cope with such requests and the hectic society, we saw a boom in the field of ergonomic design - as was the case in the end of the twentieth century

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: However, that was only the first few staggering steps towards a more humane situation

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: I would in this essay, in an additional move to improve these conditions, like to argue that everybody should be entitled to a bed adjusted to their individual ergonomic and orthopaedic needs - even to the extent that it should be made compulsory

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: If we look at the world today, we see two widespread national illnesses of which we have not seen much before, namely stress and tension

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Although these conditions may not qualify as diseases, their consequences might very well indeed, and though there may be many ways of avoiding them theoretically, the best way practically is to get good and sufficient sleep

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: It is psychologically proved that lack of sleep impair the span of concentration, makes you easily irritated and can even lead you to misinterpret sensory impressions

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Nevertheless when your body is relaxed and you are rested you get a lighter disposition, you become more resistant to stress and it is less likely you will suffer from any kind of irritating ailments

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: All of this is thus achievable with a bed which provides you with satisfactorily sleep

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: What is more, an orthopaedic bed will lessen back aches and relieve already sore backs, and this, too, will heighten your ability to deal with the contemporary society

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Since back aches are hard to escape due to the fact that evolution has not yet completely adjusted our body structure to an upright position, a supporting bed would provide against this totally unnecessary frustration, which could lead to a graver condition later in life

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Even critics would agree with me, I am sure, that having an ergonomic bed would reduce such occupational injuries as stress, tension and back aches since there is proof of this in a number of independent surveys

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Nevertheless, they would claim that although this is a wonderful idea, it could never become compulsory

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: For example, how would it be implemented? Should there be a law to it, not only putting restrictions to our freedom of choice, but to the market's as well

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Besides such a law would be impossible to uphold

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Furthermore, there would be no economic ground for it

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Of course, the idea of a law prohibiting the use of a bad bed is absurd

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: But a reform towards a better bed for everybody is still a possibility

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Simply, if the government would make a calculation of what was to be gained from the decreasing expenses in physiotherapy, sickness benefits and early retirement pensions, this money could be used to encourage the bed-making industry to introduce a standardisation of bed designs

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: That could then be used by the public medical service, which would receive government funding too, to help you to find out which kind of bed suited you the best, together with your medical examinations

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: This would leave the market free to compete beyond the standardisation, and it would make it easier for you to find an orthopaedic bed adjusted to you

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: In conclusion, we have to do what we can to reduce the present amount of stress, tension, ailments and back aches we are facing

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: As we learn that this could be done with a reform based on government funding to the bed-making industry and the public medical service, which would make it easier for you to get a bed adjusted to your individual needs by way of a standardisation of bed designs, we should do all we can to implement this idea

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
text: Not to would be absolutely neglectful to a society in need, and all would gain if the world of tomorrow was a more harmonic place than the hectic one of today

title: On Why Everybody Should Have a Bed Adjusted to Their Individual Ergonomic and Orthopaedic Needs
Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Surely all of us have been struck by stress and exhaustion sometime during our lives

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: But these phenomena are getting more and more common and overwhelming

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Many people today suffer a great deal from stress and from getting burned out

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: These problems do not only concern adults, but also many schoolchildren

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Fortunately there are ways to help prevent these so called national diseases

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: For example, one easy way is through massage, which is a well-known means of relaxing and recovery

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: This method has been used by people in centuries

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: My opinion is that every employee should be offered free massage as a part of their working benefits

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: And since other forms of healthcare are free for people under the age of twenty, massage should be free for them as well

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Thus how can everyone benefit from this idea, and how will this improve our everyday lives? To begin with, more and more people are having difficulties with performing a good job at their work, because of factors such as stress and exhaustion

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: This is not hard to understand since our society is a rapidly developing one, and as a result the demands on our efficiency increase

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Many of us work so hard that we are totally exhausted when we come home

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: As if this is not enough there are a number of duties at home that many of us have to deal with after work

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: There is simply no time for relaxation

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Now, if everyone were to be offered free massage, say once a week at the least, we would become much more relaxed and as a result we would be able to work more effectively

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Less people would suffer from stress and, in addition to this, our working environment would become more pleasant and more people would enjoy going to work

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: However, I do not believe that adults are the only group in need of relaxation in the form of massage

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Schoolchildren also suffer a lot from stress and this puts a strain on their concentration and learning ability

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Massage, as we all know, not only makes us feel more relaxed, but it increases our blood circulation, and makes us feel more alert

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Through massage we can concentrate better and we become more active

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: This will, in the long run, lead to better educated children, and they will not forget what they learn at school

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Of course these massage treatments have to be financed

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Nevertheless, in my opinion there will be no big problems financing this project, since the effects of the massage treatments in themselves will be moneysaving

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: This money can then be used to pay for the expenses

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: The companies will save money because they will have employees who are more relaxed and therefore can concentrate better and work more effectively

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: They will do a better job in less time! Furthermore, people will have a better health, and there will be less people on the sick- leave

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: Thus, the government will not have to pay as much sick benefits, and could instead use this money to sponsor the project

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: To sum up, I would like to se free massage in the form of a working benefit, because I believe that this would be one great step in the right direction in order to improve peoples health, both physically and mentally

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: What we need today is a form of relaxation in our stressful lives

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: As we all know, when we feel relaxed we see the good things in life, we are nicer to one another, and we feel good about our selves

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: For instance, the body as well as the mind will get stronger, and as a result of this we will be able to resist diseases and handle difficult situations a lot better

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
text: In addition to this we will perform better at work and at school

title: Why we should introduce free massage as a working benefit
A modern monarchy
text: Sweden has been a monarchy for a very long time

title: A modern monarchy
text: In fact, our history is to a large extent connected with the different kings and queens that have ruled the country

title: A modern monarchy
text: Even though the boundaries have changed several times due to wars, conflicts and unions our monarchy has persevered

title: A modern monarchy
text: The role of the monarch has however seen a huge transformation over the last two hundred years and the powers of Gustav II Adolf or Karl XII cannot be compared with those of the present king Carl XIV Gustav

title: A modern monarchy
text: The parliament is today the main legislative and ruling body with the government being the second with the Supreme Court being separated from political influence

title: A modern monarchy
text: It does not deal with political matters or interfere with the laws passed by the parliament

title: A modern monarchy
text: The powers listed above all belonged to the monarch a little longer than two hundred years ago

title: A modern monarchy
text: The king or queen was the lawmaker, commander of the armed forces and the main ruler of the nation

title: A modern monarchy
text: Swedish kings are famous of their warfare with neighbouring counties and battles throughout northern Europe in the 17th and 18th centuries, however, the situation is completely different today where the monarch's powers have been reduced to none as a consequence of the parliamentary movements of the 19th century

title: A modern monarchy
text: In 1809, the first reform took place when dissatisfied generals set the king aside and the monarch's powers were reduced and shared with different ruling bodies

title: A modern monarchy
text: Fifty years later a reform was passed that changed the foundation of the class-based parliament to a parliament based on income and property

title: A modern monarchy
text: The monarch's power was not changed dramatically during these years but a process had started that would eventually relieve the monarch of his former duties

title: A modern monarchy
text: The new form of government had come to grow strong and it was now the parliament that became the new ruling body in domestic and international matters

title: A modern monarchy
text: As the parliament took over the responsibility of ruling the country it was expected of the monarch to stay neutral and not to express himself in political matters

title: A modern monarchy
text: Despite this fact king Gustav V chose in 1914 not to agree with the existing prime minister, as a result of this controversy the prime minister resigned

title: A modern monarchy
text: Since then there have been no further utterances in political matters form the monarch and we expect the him to keep a low profile, even in non-political matters even though it is claimed by some people that the present king and queen are strong leaders of opinion in different matters such as hunting and child-pornography

title: A modern monarchy
text: The king and queen of Sweden are today merely representatives of the country who appear on official state visits and also travel around the world creating good relations with other countries and putting Sweden on the map

title: A modern monarchy
text: There is no power at all to talk about and the monarch simply receives a salary that will enable him to carry out his duties as a representative of our country

title: A modern monarchy
text: The opposition, the critics of the monarchy, claim that it is old-fashioned and not equivalent with the modern society and also that it is expensive for the taxpayers

title: A modern monarchy
text: I don't find this sort of criticism very well considered

title: A modern monarchy
text: Firstly, Sweden is a very old country and the monarchy is a big part of our history as well as our identity

title: A modern monarchy
text: I think it is quite unique living in a country where we still have a monarchy because there are not many countries that are monarchies today

title: A modern monarchy
text: When it comes to the question of democracy and the modern day society the monarch does not have any political power as seen above, all those powers have been taken away from the monarch and been put in the hands of the people

title: A modern monarchy
text: This fact shows henceforth that a monarchy really can exist within the boundaries of a modern democracy

title: A modern monarchy
text: Secondly, the argument saying that it is expensive to uphold and support the royal family's living expenses does not seem to hold ground

title: A modern monarchy
text: Compared to other expenses it is a very small fraction of the tax money that is paid

title: A modern monarchy

title: A modern monarchy

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: The ongoing debate, on nuclear power vs

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: natural sources of power has never been such a burning issue as today

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: This question concerns all of us and it is our responsibility to raise people's awareness of this topic

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Is nuclear power really the only and the safest way to get power (electricity) to the homes and industries of Sweden? Should we, in the 21th century rely on such an insecure source of power? Are we ready to risk our children's life getting electricity? Are the Swedish nuclear power stations really as secure as we are told? While presenting the pros and cons of different sources of power, I will state my side in the debate and try to answer these burning questions

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: In this essay I will argue against the use of nuclear power, in all-current methods of using

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: It might have been the best alternative at the time it was introduced, and voted for, but today we have developed more modern and environment-friendly ways of providing for Sweden's, and other countries, need for electricity

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: We are told that nuclear power is the most safe, cheap and reliable way of keeping Sweden running

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: But is it really so? If you see to the side effects, nuclear power is very much a defective source of power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: First of all, nuclear fission in itself is a very insecure and inadequate method of gaining power (electricity); every single fission is indeed an uncontrolled explosion

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: For example, a computer programmer agency got commissioned to investigate the weaknesses of the safety system at a nuclear power station in Sweden and after some research they came up with some five-hundred different situations were the safety system would be inadequate and brake down

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Secondly, there is the contaminated nuclear waste to take care of and store for hundreds of years in the bedrock

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Everything that has contact with the toxic waste must be stored and kept safe form leakage

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: The thought of what could happen if the toxic waste got into our ground water or spread in the nature is disastrous, not to mention that the staff working with nuclear waste is at constant risk of nuclear radiation, which is a source of diseases and health problems

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Finally, the economic issue is indeed important, and the price of electricity needs to be questioned

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Nuclear power is a very expensive way of getting power; nuclear power stations are expensive to built, the Uranium that is most commonly used for nuclear fission is expensive, and you can only use it once

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: A way of financing this expensive source of power could be for Sweden to raise the price for electricity, as we are one of, not to say the, cheapest country for electric support

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: With a way of choosing the source from which the electricity to your home came, it is likely that many Swedes would be ready to pay a little more (that means a little more than 0

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: 50kr/kWh) to be able to dismantle some nuclear power stations

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: I would, that's for sure, and I'm sure many with me

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: However, rather than raising electricity prices, an important step would be to investigate our need for electricity

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: If every sixth Swede turned off one lamp in every room he/she did not use at the moment, we could be able to shut down two of four nuclear reactors at Oskarshamn nuclear power station

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: The consumers' money could then be used to build up alternative power stations

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: For as the situation is today, Sweden is not ready to just shut down the nuclear power stations

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Our alternatives are not good enough yet

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: The time of transmission will probably take many years, but the sooner we begin, the sooner we can turn to a totally changed system

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Besides considering saving electricity, we need to develop alternative energy sources

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Natural sources of power like wind, water and sun, are good alternatives

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: At first, water power does to some extent disturb the natural balance of a river, but it can naturally never be compared with the destruction that might be caused by nuclear power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: The supply of hydroelectric power depends on the annual precipitation - rain or snow, and to be depending on the capricious weather is always a risk, and therefor an argument against the use of hydro power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: But it is time introduce the best part of nature power recourses; they are all a complement to each other

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: That is to say, you need rain to get electricity from water power, but when it doesn't rain, it will, probably and hopefully, be sunshine, which means that we can gain power (and electricity) from the sun, which is indeed an inexhaustible source of power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: To just rely on sun power is nothing that we should consider in Sweden, because of the cold and mostly rainy summers, but as a complement, it is both environmentally friendly and costly effective

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Wind power is also an inexhaustible source of power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: A common argument against the introduction of wind power is that the tall pylons destroy the landscape, but we have to ask ourselves if not that is a reasonable price compared to the shape and condition the landscape would be in if there were a nuclear power station brake down

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: Moreover, none of those natural sources of power; water, sun and wind, is one by one an alternative to nuclear power, but if we complemented, and developed, all those three together, would more or less cover the energy that we today gain from nuclear power

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: To sum up, while we cannot just dismantle all the nuclear reactors at once, we have to gradually decrease the use of nuclear energy

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
text: We have to be ready to work and pay for a future without nuclear power stations, even if the work just might be turning off lamps, TV sets, and toasters, knowing that the extra money saved is for building an alternative power future

title: NUCLEAR POWER vs. NATURAL SOURCES OF POWER.
