title: The distribution revolution
text: A burning issue in the world of music distribution today is the existence and usage of the computer program Napster

title: The distribution revolution
text: With the help of this program it is possible to share music files with other users of the program

title: The distribution revolution
text: The company behind Napster provides a database in which the users can search for what they want to download

title: The distribution revolution
text: The download itself is preformed only between two ordinary computers connected to the Internet

title: The distribution revolution
text: As the music is shared between private computers it's hard to control if the copyright of the music is violated

title: The distribution revolution
text: This is where the record companies come in

title: The distribution revolution
text: They don't like the fact that their music is freely available on the Internet without their control

title: The distribution revolution
text: To protect their interests several record companies has filed a lawsuit against Napster in order to get the service stopped

title: The distribution revolution
text: It has not been stopped yet but Napster is probably going to get stopped in the near future

title: The distribution revolution
text: I don't think the record companies are doing exactly the right thing

title: The distribution revolution
text: I think that record companies should collaborate with Napster and use their technology as a new way to distribute music

title: The distribution revolution
text: My main argument for that the record companies should use this new technology is that it's impossible to stop this development with music sharing on the Internet

title: The distribution revolution
text: The Napster community has today about 51 million registered users and if it's suddenly stopped it will result in a huge demand for something to replace it

title: The distribution revolution
text: Programs with almost the same functionality as Napster already exist and it probably wouldn't take long before Napster would be fully replaced

title: The distribution revolution
text: Because of the reason that I just mentioned it's better to cooperate with Napster than just to stop it

title: The distribution revolution
text: I'm not saying that the illegal copying of copyright protected music is going to end but this is a very good opportunity for the music industry to reduce it and at the same time make profit form it

title: The distribution revolution
text: As Napster is such a widely spread program it makes it a good foundation for use as a new distribution channel

title: The distribution revolution
text: Napster would naturally have to be transformed into a membership-based community where the members either pays a membership fee or pay for the music that they download

title: The distribution revolution
text: Another side of the issue is the users, which I haven't mentioned yet

title: The distribution revolution
text: They will be forced to pay for something that previously has been free of charge

title: The distribution revolution
text: Naturally quite a lot of the users will stop using the service, but the ones who are willing to pay will see that it's not only a bad thing that they have to pay

title: The distribution revolution
text: If the users are forced to pay I think it's the record companies responsibility to make all their music available on the service

title: The distribution revolution
text: Then the users would have a much bigger selection of music that always would be available for download

title: The distribution revolution
text: In addition to that the sound quality of the music would be better as the only one who would publish music on the service would be the record companies which has access to better equipment than an ordinary home user

title: The distribution revolution
text: The scenario that I have pictured is actually beginning to happen

title: The distribution revolution
text: The big German media conglomerate Bertelsmann, of which a main part is the big BMG record company, has signed an agreement with Napster

title: The distribution revolution
text: This agreement is aiming towards a membership based community just as the one I have been discussing, but how this is going to be designed has not yet been decided

title: The distribution revolution
text: If this agreement becomes a success then Bertelsmann will drop the lawsuit that they currently have against Napster

title: The distribution revolution
text: I hope that all the other record companies will follow Bertelsmann's example and join them and Napster to create a community where all music is available for download

title: The distribution revolution
text: This would in the beginning only be a complement to how music is distributed today, but I believe that it's one step forward towards a different way to distribute music in the future

title: The distribution revolution
SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Imagine a night out, you have had a few drinks, done a bit of dancing and are now in bed ready to go to sleep feeling quite satisfied with the evening

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: When you wake up next morning with (probably) a hangover and a smell in your hair, your clothes and bed, witch can be defined as tobacco smell

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: , you do not feel as glamorous as you did last night

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: The hangover is (of course) your own fault, but the smell if you are a non-smoker? Is it really reasonable that other people has the power to endanger your health? Should you not be the one to decide about your own future? The right thing to do would be to ban smoking at night clubs and pubs all over the country

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: This might not be an easy task, so my aim would be the nations in Uppsala, since I know that it is possible

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: The main reason to why there should be no- smoking at the nations, is the danger that comes along with smoking

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: To begin with; smoking can give you several diseases, the most severe one being cancer

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Secondly there is always the danger of fire

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Most of the nations are situated in old buildings where the risk of fire are great

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Just by flicking your cigarettebump not put out onto a couch or the floor, etc

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: could start a fire, and with alcohol, often consumed on a night out, comes poor judgment so this happens very often

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Surely the nations are provided with fire- extinguishers but that is not enough protection, since the nations usually are overcrowded on a Friday night

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Of course this is something the nations should take under consideration anyway and not just with the smoking issue

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Furthermore what happened to the free will? It is said that each and every person has their own free will to make a decision, but this is not the case here

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Diseases through smoking does not just affect smokers, but also non- smokers, who through inhaling the smoke from others can just as easily and sometimes easier get cancer or asthma This fact makes my statement even clearer thinking of the people working for instance behind the bar, waitresses, waiters and DJ's even

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Although they might be smokers themselves they should be able to decide how much they want to smoke and the fact is that someone has to work in order to keep the place running

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Let us not forget another important group of people- the people who is allergic to smoke and those with severe asthma

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: These people cannot go to these, places in order to stay healthy and alive and this is all because of the smoke which is not fair

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Why should smokers make other people, although not on purpose, stay away? Oddly enough people continue to smoke even though they know that they are endangering themselves and others

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Fortunately there is a solution to everything and of course to this problem as well

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: To begin with, why not just prohibit smoking at the nations? It has been tested in several cities for example Umeå, Eskilstuna and Västerås and it has worked out beautifully

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Norrlands nation in Uppsala has actually banned smoking in some parts of the building so that is a step in the right direction

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Disregarding this as a solution at least have one room where people can smoke and no one else had to suffer

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: Although this solution might not be perfect at least it is better then the current situation

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: A few days ago my friends and I went to Stockholms nation to have lunch and left after an hour with the stench of tobacco in our hair and clothes even though neither one of us smoked during that time

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: The smell was probably there from the night before or even earlier

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: However, we still want to go back in spite of the smell, because the food is nice The question is, would someone allergic to smoke or with asthma be able to have lunch there if only just for an hour? In short banning smoking at the nations can only have a positive outcome regarding the health situation for non- smokers, which includes staff, visitors and of course for the smokers as well

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
text: I myself tend to smoke a cigarette or two sometimes on a night out and would be more than happy to smoke it outside

title: SMOKING OR NON-SMOKING?
An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: In a time when racism is growing amongst young people and words such as social Justice seems erased from our vocabulary it is of great importance to look at what role the school plays in the discussion and creation of attitudes

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I believe the schools can and should play a more active part in bringing back social Justice on the agenda

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: What I am proposing is therefore the introduction during the last year of junior high school of a compulsory stay in a foreign country

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I suggest that all Swedish high school students spend approximately one semester in a foreign country preferably outside of Western Europe, taking part in social projects such as building schools, helping with irrigation projects, working with the homeless or in women shelters etc

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I suggest this project be funded with tax money

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: This idea might at first seem overly radical and somewhat unrealistic

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: But I will here argue that the tax money spent on this project will pay back greatly maybe not so much in economical terms but in terms of gains for both the individual as well as for the society at large

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: With the risk of sounding as a hopelessly outdated idealist I will not start with the economical gains (if there indeed are any) or the gains for the society but with how the individual student would benefit from this proposal

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: The first individual gain that come to mind is the students own personal growth

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: What better way to learn about yourself, learn responsibility and improve your self confidence than to travel and meet people from other cultures? I believe it is the schools ambition not only to teach the students facts but also other skills such as being able to take initiatives and ability to work in a group

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: This is an area where, judging from the present school debate, schools seem to have difficulties and are in need of new ideas

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I believe a compulsory semester spent abroad is an innovating idea worth trying

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Apart from being exposed to a different culture the students would also be exposed to a different language

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Today when a lot of stress is being put on language skills in order to get a job, I see it as a good investment both for the individual and for the society at large to early on learn high school students to communicate in a second language

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Therefore I think it is essential to make sure everybody gets this opportunity

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I will now move on to look at what positive effects this suggested proposal would have on society

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: One of the major gains that come to mind is that it would help in the fight against racism

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: This is of course only a speculation from my side but I see it as a rather logic result if people at an early age were to be confronted with other cultures and social situations

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Not only do I think it would be an efficient way to meet the growing racism but it would hopefully also raise the awareness of the importance of social Justice

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I am by no means suggesting this to be the only solution but I do think it to be one way of dealing with two substantial problems our society is struggling with today; racism and a lack of interest in global social Justice

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Looking beyond our own country it would also be beneficial for the receiving country, taken that the students would not merely be spectators but would actively be taking part in the different projects

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Since everything today in order to be trustworthy has to be measured in economic terms I will venture to speculate that this idea is not only good for the individual and for society at large but can also be economically sustainable

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: As good as every day we read about the scarcity of qualified teachers, the physical decay of our school buildings and the lack of updated schoolbooks

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: With a large number of students (everyone in their final semester of junior high school) abroad every year the demand for schoolbooks and teachers would decrease

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: There would of course still be a need for some teachers to keep in contact with the students while abroad and to follow up at home coming

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: But the actual number of teachers could be reduced

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: It is also my conviction that this last "abroad spent" semester would be a good incentive for students to finish junior high school and therefore the number of dropouts would be reduced

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: Which apart from being good for the individual students also would have a good effect on the economy since more people would complete their studies and be more attractive on the job market

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: As you have seen I believe there to be many good reasons, both for society and for the students to introduce a compulsory stay abroad for the students in junior high school

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: I have even suggested in what way this could have a beneficial effect on the economy, even though my lack of economic training makes these assumptions rather speculative

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
text: But I believe that even if we, in order to implement this idea, would have to increase the taxes both the society, the school and the students have enough to gain that it would be well worth it

title: An idea on how to bring social justice to the school curriculum
CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Capital punishment is used in some parts of the United States, and in a number of other countries around the world

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: In these countries the state is legally killing people who have committed murders

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: In other words, killing a person is legal if the person in question has killed another person

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: I would say that it is not morally defensible to kill a person because of a crime he or she has committed

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Instead of executing murderers I think that it is our duty to help them

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Those who need societys help the most are those who commit crimes

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: They would not commit murders if they were not mentally ill in some way

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: If they are mentally ill, they need our help and we should not kill them

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Some criminals may have had a very problematic and complicated childhood

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: They may have been badly brought up and so commit crimes

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Everyone has a history that makes him or her to do such bad things to others

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: No human being kills another without such reasons

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: I can not believe that a normal person just walk along the streets and shoots someone just like that

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Murderers, who do this, are ill in some way and we should help these people instead of punishing them

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: I do not say that a problematic past or being mentally ill is an excuse for committing a crime, but that they need our help

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: There is a proverb, which says: "Love me the most, when I deserve it the least, for that is when I need it the most"

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: If we help criminals to get on the right track, they might change

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Everyone can change for the better and the least we can do is give him or her the chance

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: We must help them get on the right track and not execute them before they are given the chance

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Everyone has the right to live

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: The right to live is the most fundamental human right of all and you can not legally take this human right away >from any person

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: You can not say that anyone deserves to die, no one does

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: We all have the right to live, no matter who we are

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: As I said, I really think that every person can change for the better

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: Bur of course, taking care of all prisoners will cost society a lot of money

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: But is not human life worth more than money? I think that a human life is worth so much, no matter what! The persons who get executed have committed murder themselves, but still you cant execute the person because of this

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT
text: If society had offered them help earlier they might not have killed anyone at all

title: CAPITAL PUNISHMENT

Time for a law against Tobacco
text: One of the most powerful methods of getting rid of dangerous threats to society, is to make laws for protective purposes

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: For instance, we have laws to make traffic safer, and we have laws that forbids ordinary people from carrying guns

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: We also have laws that makes a clear statement regarding narcotics

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: And yet we have no law protecting us from the most popular, and therefore perhaps the most dangerous drug of them all, and that is of course tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: There can be little doubt, that a law that makes the sale and use of every form of tobacco illegal, is of the utmost importance

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: It is a well known fact that tobacco is hazardous to people's health

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: But there is not only the commonly known riscs of cancer and severe heart conditions that should cause worry

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: I once saw a list of the damage that tobacco may inflict upon a human body

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: At least a hundred deceases were listed, and almost every part of the body was mentioned

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Furthermore this list showed that tobacco is not only causing physical illness

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: A person might also get various psychical disfunctions from using tobacco, such as sleeping problems and depression to mention a few

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Needless to say perhaps, the healthproblems that comes from tobacco, are not of the kind that can be cured overnight

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Every year more people die from deceases directly related to using tobacco, than from anything else

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: And before they die these thousands of people suffering from tobaccorelatd deseases occupies already overcrowded hospitals, making it not only a healthissue, but a financial matter, as well

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: There is of course a considerable amount of taxmoney to be saved here

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: At the present, we have an ongoing debate over the shortage of healthcare money, and still we allow a great deal of that money to be eaten up by costs caused by tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: This gives us an equation that is easy enough to solve

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: With a law against tobacco there would be no such expense

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: And we all know that the money is well needed elsewhere in a country's healthcare system

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: We also have endless expensive goverment campaigns advicing us not to use tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Again money that could be easily saved simply by a law prohibiting tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: There are people arguing that the state actually have a taxincom on tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Such arguments are of course complete and utter nonsense

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: The money the state gain on taxes from the sale of tobacco may be the equivalent of a mere few percent of what the tobacco is costing in healthcare

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Finally I would like to emphazise on the thought of our forthcoming generations, and their growth in a society were a drug like tobacco, that really has no purpose apart from being a danger to their health, still has not been made illegal

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: A common answer to the question, why a person is using tobacco, is simply that the person for some reason once tried it and from that day is unable to stop

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: That only shows that by passing a law that bans tobacco, we can take away the possibility of that fatal first try to be exposed to our children

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Every day passing is another day, where thousands of young people take up "the bad habit"

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: And every such day should be considered a failure on our part, in terms of showing proper respect to the next generation

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: The next generation deserves this law

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Arguments concerning health and finance clearly says that we should have one

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: So let us not hesitate any longer

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Let us in the name of common sense, by law state that we have understood the folishness of allowing tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: Let us show the responsibility one has the right to expect from a civilized society

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
text: It is, and has been ever since tobacco was first tried by man, time to pass the law that bans tobacco

title: Time for a law against Tobacco
STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Society has failed of taking satisfactory care of the elderly

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Municipals are not able to provide its old inhabitants apartments and even as I write, some five thousand of them stand without proper living arrangements

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: It's also known that this group is steadily growing in our society

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: More and more money and recourses are needed

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: At the same time the number of those being in working life declines

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: I think that the best solution to this problem is that families themselves provide care for their older members, at least in some extent

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Would you like to spend the last years of your life sitting in a closet-size room, only having the next bingo session to look forward to? I assume you wouldn't

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: This is however reality for many elderly today

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Growing old is something one almost ought to feel ashamed of

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Perhaps if we spent more time with the elderly and took care of them ourselves, maybe then we would once again learn not to be afraid of ageing and instead we might, just might, realise that good things also come with it, namely experience and wisdom

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: The benefits this would bring to the elderly themselves are easily imagined

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Every one of us has the basic need for feeling useful, preferable to some other human being

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Grandma could assist in some lighter every-day chores, such as minding her grandchildren

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: It would certainly be nice for her to now that someone still needs her and enjoys her company

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: A very nice side effect of this would be the children then being able to stay a little longer in a warm and safe home environment instead of being sent away to day care institutions as early as at the age of one, not to mention all those things they could learn from the older generation

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Let's return to the closet-sized rooms and the obvious lack of something to occupy the residents with

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: I'm sure that those who work at these institutions and homes are very capable craftsmen and that they do the best they can

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: They are indeed able to provide for their customers basic needs

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Still, there simply isn't enough time for social interaction such as sitting down for a talk, mainly due to the lack of staff

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: To make it possible for families to have their parents at home, the time spent at work ought to be shortened

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Regarding the situation today there isn't even enough time to spend with ones children after a long eight- hour day at work

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: If this was done there would automatically be more openings in the job- market and unemployment figures would go down

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Society would save that huge amount of money that goes to maintain all these institutions and could preferably transfer some of the saved money to the equally huge healthcare costs

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: As long as any diseases don't trouble the old member of the family, care at home is to prefer, but professionals of course better give medical care

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: As I have shown so far there are great benefits both individually and socially

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: I'm sure though, that you already have formed some counter arguments in your mind

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: One of them might be how we are supposed to manage economically if we only work six hours per day? With more mouths to fill there has to be an increase in income as well

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: But then again, one might wonder if we really need to consume as much as we do today? Couldn't we try lowering our cost of living? Surely it's not all that important to have the most expensive house, car and so forth? I'm well aware of that in order to make these suggestions come true we would probably have to go some hundred years back in time

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: We have somehow lost our feeling of belonging together as families

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: Partly I would like to blame the society we are living in for that; the haunt for money and success has made us greedy and egoistic

title: STATE CARE CANNOT REPLACE FAMILY CARE
text: However it's extremely important for preserving humanity that we once again start taking care of each other!

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Just a couple hundreds of the 90 000 people who die in Sweden every year donate their organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: This has lead to longer queues and time of waiting for transplantation of organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: And it is calculated that 15-20 percent of those on the waiting list will die before a suitable organ becomes available

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: As a solution to this problem are some scientists working on research on xenotransplantation, which means to move organs between different species

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: In this case to move organs between pigs and human beings

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: The thought is to gene manipulate pigs to get them suitable as donors of organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: So in future transplantations it would be possible to replace human tissue with pigs' cells and organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: But unfortunately there are to many risks and much unfairness connected with it

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Therefor I think xenotransplantation is not the right solution on the lack of organs and it should not be developed either

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: First of al I am against the development of xenotransplantation because it entails risks such as transmission of unknown viruses and other infectious diseases from the pigs to the human body

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Of course, some complications could be acceptable though the receiver of a pig organ may get higher life quality, but an infection could be contagious for the people nearest the patient and possibly even to society

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Therefore I would prefer another solution to the lack of organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Public information about the lack of organs and discussions about the consequences of xenotransplantation would help

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: I think a lot of people would change their mind if they were informed

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: The problem with the lack of organs should also be presented for children at school so that they from an early age get used on the thought of donating their organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: That way more people would be ready to donate their organs and such need for organs that exist today would not exist

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Another reason to why I am against the development of xenotransplantation is the thought on how xenotransplantation could be used by the companies that have spent money on research on xenotransplantation

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: I mean that those who are going to be the sellers of the gene manipulated pig organs will try to make big business on them without carrying on the ethical consequences

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: One must realise that those companies are not charitable institutions, and that it is therefore possible that poor people in the third world would not have access to these organs, which would lead to an increase of injustice in our world

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: This make me wonder: Who would indeed be most benefited by xenotransplantation? Would it be the patient, the seller, the whole society or no one? I think the most benefited would be the seller and the less the patient, by being infected by viruses or similar diseases, and society with the increasing of injustice as a consequence of the greed of the sellers

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: In this case xenotransplantation does not fill any function and should not therefore exist

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Another thing that makes me critical against xenotransplantation is that the "donator-pigs" are exposed to a lot of pain

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: They suffer when they are used as patients in experimental transplantations with a lot of medicine that restrains the immune defence

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: Even genetical operations are painful for the pigs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: The pigs used for xenotransplantation are not able to live a happy pig life either, as they are not allowed to root on the ground

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: I believe that humans do not have the right to change animals' genes and living conditions to get them suitable as donors of organ

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: We must think about how we would like to be treated ourselves and we should stoop utilising animals the way xenotransplantation does

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: What I have mentioned above are some of the reasons why I do not like to see xenotransplantation as the solution to the lack of organs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: The development of xenotransplantation is too dangerous since pig's viruses could infect people

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: It is unfair since the sellers' greed would lead to an increase of injustice in our world

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: It is unfair, also, because it causes much pain for the pigs

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION
text: I believe that if people who do not want to donate their organs reflect on their decision and change their mind it would be easier to stop the development of xenotransplantation and all the bad consequences of it

title: NO TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF XENOTRANSPLANTATION

title: Why we need students grants
text: Being a student in Sweden means going through some hard financial years

title: Why we need students grants
text: The choice one makes though, does not only mean temporarily less income, but also a huge rate of the future income directly drawn to pay off the loans for years to come

title: Why we need students grants
text: This paper will deal with the problems connected to the issue

title: Why we need students grants
text: Firstly I will discuss the troublesome fact that a degree does not guarantee neither getting a job, nor an improved wage if one gets a job

title: Why we need students grants
text: Secondly I think that Sweden ought to show appreciation to those who are eager to learn, and realise how important it is to keep our intellectuality high

title: Why we need students grants
text: The third topic will be not risking people overworking themselves by having jobs and studies at the same time

title: Why we need students grants
text: I will sum up by presenting a possible solution to the following problem with people misusing the system if grants were to be introduced

title: Why we need students grants
text: I will start with what I hold as the most important reason of having students' grants

title: Why we need students grants
text: People who want money do best not going to the university at all

title: Why we need students grants
text: During the years of studies one has to cope with a lot less than what one could get at a low-paid job

title: Why we need students grants
text: Getting into the market after university studies, there is little difference in salary between jobs, which demand a degree and those not

title: Why we need students grants
text: Of course there are well-paid professions, but the overall difference is negligible compared with the situation in other countries

title: Why we need students grants
text: Including the loans, you end up at about the same level if you have a long education

title: Why we need students grants
text: This leaves us the reasonable conclusion that many of those studying, do it for pleasure and personal development, since the money profit is a joke

title: Why we need students grants
text: I do not imply that these are insufficient reasons, but I do feel that there ought to be some difference on the pay roll

title: Why we need students grants
text: I also think it is important to show the awareness of the advantages of an educated nation

title: Why we need students grants
text: If Sweden wants to stay at the top of the world in economic and social domains, we need to keep up intellectually with the other industrial countries

title: Why we need students grants
text: I think this involves increasing the salaries too, but I will not get into that discussion here

title: Why we need students grants
text: Giving people a chance to get an education without ending up in a difficult situation financially, should make a significant difference in itself

title: Why we need students grants
text: A risk that follows the economic disadvantage of being a student is the fact that many students both work and study at the same time

title: Why we need students grants
text: I can assert that it is rather tough to keep up in school, and in the acquiring of knowledge one does not have the time to become at all full-filled

title: Why we need students grants
text: I know that there are lots of students having problems grasping what they should in their education, and of course, having an income has to be the main issue

title: Why we need students grants
text: As a last topic, I would like to come with a suggestion how to solve one of the greatest problems of having students' grants instead of loans; removing the risk of people taking advantage of the system

title: Why we need students grants
text: If there were no demands on the students, many people would probably just fool around studying a little bit of this and a little bit of that

title: Why we need students grants
text: To make this impossible, there could be loans from the beginning, that were to be written off when a student got a degree

title: Why we need students grants
text: There could be a certain amount of terms paid for, as there are today, so that it would be impossible to stay in the system forever

title: Why we need students grants
text: For those never reaching a degree, the loan would stay as a good reason not to give up

title: Why we need students grants
text: Today if you move abroad and do not pay tax in Sweden, they do not draw money to pay off your loan

title: Why we need students grants
text: I suggest the opposite; those who take their knowledge away from the country, would have to pay for their studies even if they have received a degree

title: Why we need students grants
text: Why should the state pay for something that is not of use to the country? To sum up, I feel that Sweden would benefit in many areas by removing the only reason there is not to study: the loans

title: Why we need students grants
text: I think it is of great importance to keep developing your intellect to be able to develop psychologically

title: Why we need students grants
text: I therefore think Sweden would do best realising the need to show appreciation of those interested in getting an education by making the studies free with grants

title: Why we need students grants
text: To restrict the use of the grants, both to sift out the ones not serious in their studies and to reduce the expenses, I suggest that only those actually reaching a degree get the grants

title: Why we need students grants
text: To keep the country with what I reckon is one of the most aware people on earth, I think education has to be a central tool, available to everyone

title: Why we need students grants
Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: During the last couple of years there have been discussions concerning separate classes for boys and girls in junior high school

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: This means that there would be one class for girls and one class for boys at each level

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Despite this separation they will work according to the same curriculum and have the same subjects

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Is this a good or a bad idea? Will this give them a true picture of what today's society look like? I believe that separate classes take away many important things from the students

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: One of these things is different experiences and with that I mean different ways of looking at things

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Boys and girls have various experiences due to the way they have been brought up and the way they have been treated by the people around them

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Some children have good memories and some have bad memories but the thing is that the memories, or experiences, are not the same

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Why is this so important then? Well, I believe that this divergence gives the lessons a bit of nuance

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Boys and girls can contribute with their own views and thoughts, their own peculiarities

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: The teacher can use these differences during the lessons as examples or maybe as a good basis in a class discussion where the students learn that there are differences between boys and girls but they also learn that they are all equally important in the classroom and also in our society

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: For many years we have been striving for an equal society and we are on our way towards this goal

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: To join in on this journey you have to be able to co-operate with both males and females without any prejudice

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Do you think this is possible if you have been attending a school in which boys and girls are separated thus taking away your chances to learn how to function in a situation which requires that kind of social competence? I would not think so

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: I think that a school like this teaches the students that it is right to separate girls from boys, that there are differences between the sexes in the way they learn things

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Maybe the students get the idea that, for example, boys are better than girls, that this is the reason why they are separated

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Thoughts like these would only take us back in time and not forward towards the equal society that we are working so hard to achieve

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: So why do some people advocate for this separation? They say that there are a lot of advantages with this kind of class

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: We all know that boys and girls are not the same

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: There is no point in denying that

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: The spokesman for this type of school says that the students are given more time to develop and find themselves

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: The boys do not have to "show off" in front of the girls

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: Instead they can put all of their time and effort into the schoolwork

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: The girls are also looked upon as winners in this system

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: They do not have to compete with the boys for the teacher's attention and they are given more room

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: What I have to say about the arguments for this kind of school is that I do not believe all of them

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: You develop into one person in the all-girls class and then, when you go out into the society, you have to change into another person who can work with both sexes

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: And who says that a little competition between boys and girls is bad? I believe that competition is necessary for everyone in order to turn them into strong human beings

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: If I were to give my opinion today on this question about separate classes for boys and girls at junior high school I would be against it

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: I feel that mixed classes have more to offer and teach students

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: They give them the opportunity to learn how to co-operate with people of the opposite sex

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
text: There is also a competition in the mixed classes that encourages students to develop and improve themselves and that is what school is all about, I believe

title: Girls and boys in separate classes?
The Elderly Need the State
text: When a person gets older it is important for him or her to have someone to rely on

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: About a hundred years ago it was rather obvious that the family should take care of the elderly

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Today, however, the situation is completely different because in our society it is difficult for us to look after the elderly

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: So, we need help and this is where the state comes in

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: There are many reasons why a family cannot take care of the elderly

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Firstly, most families have small children to look after and they need all the time and attention they can get from their parents

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: We all know that children can be very exhausting and demanding, which makes it impossible to concentrate on another person

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: If you are not fully dedicated to give the old people your help, it can end with disaster

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Also, it can be even more difficult to take care of a person if he is handicapped and cannot manage by himself at all

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It is a responsibility that few people can handle

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Secondly, most adults today need to work to make a living; therefore they cannot stay at home as often as they did before

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Their work takes all of their time

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Also, one thing that has changed radically if you look back in time is that some people have to travel a long time to their work

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Maybe they have to work in a big city, or in another country

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: When they finally come home in the evenings they are often exhausted and need to rest

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: For an elderly person to ask for help can be difficult because he might feel that he is in the way

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: In such situations it is easy to start a quarrel, which can lead to anger between the family members

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Such a relationship can be very stressful but above all not healthy

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: The elderly need someone who has time to spare

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: At an old people's home they often get the opportunity to relax, which means a lot to them

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: As mentioned before, it is a huge responsibility to take care of another human being

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Imagine if something would happen

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: The elderly need special care, and you cannot offer that at home

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It would be so much easier for you and them, but above all, safer if they stayed somewhere where help is close

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: The state can offer special care, which the elderly need so much

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: They have people who are educated and want to help the person that you love so much

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Also, they have all the special equipment needed

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Several old people's homes today can offer excellent medical care

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It is something that they did not have a hundred years ago, and we should take advantage of that

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: There are so many people who care about the elderly and they can offer so much more

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: People can argue that families do not care about their elderly if they send them to a home with strangers

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: But it is actually the other way around

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: If you know in your heart that you cannot offer someone else your help, the best thing you can do is to let someone else step in

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It is also important that the family tries to visit as often as they can because it means the world to old people

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Regularly visits can actually improve the relationship between the elderly and the family

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It is mentally hard for the elderly to know that they are dependent on someone else to survive because people do not want to rely on others

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: In other words, it is important to know that you can manage on your own, without causing concern for your family

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: We all need to feel independent in some way, both young and old

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: This is the way people work

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: To keep your head high and not give up are many people's philosophy today

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: All in all, elderly people need someone who can give them full attention, but most families today cannot do that

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: Therefore, it is not wrong to let the state take over

title: The Elderly Need the State
text: It is for the best, not only for you but for the elderly too

title: The Elderly Need the State
Should the family look after the elderly?
text: It has been proposed that the family, and not the state, should look after the elderly

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: It seems like a natural thing to do when you first think about it

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: The children would grow up with their grandparents nearby and perhaps the understanding between old people and youths would improve

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: However, the society we live in today can not really be called natural and such heroic deeds do no longer fit in with people's lifestyles

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: It sounds to me just like a way for the state to save money and place a lot of extra work on the already overworked people and therefor I'm against it

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: The main reason why I don't agree with the proposition is that people in today's society have enough problems to worry about

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Many men and women already feel overloaded with work, which includes both of them managing a full-time job, taking care of a home, a relationship and possibly of children

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Apart from these chores, time has to be set aside for personal well-being in order to prevent collapses and other stress symptoms that are increasingly common these days

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Therefore, I see no possibility in managing yet another task, especially one that demands a lot of work and responsibility

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Taking on the responsibility to care for one's elderly means that geographical mobility is out of the question, something that many people, including me, saw as an advantage when joining the European Union and a matter of personal freedom

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Due to their task and their conscience, people would have to live their lives in the same geographical area as where they grew up

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Let us look at an example: Ellen is a Swedish girl and an only child from a small village in the south of Sweden called Nättraby

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: She wants to become a French teacher and to improve her French she goes off to work in Paris for a year

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: While waiting tables in Paris she falls in love with Guillaume, a French cook

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: She decides to stay, marries Guillaume and they later have twins

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: When the children have reached the age of 10, her mother dies and her retired father has difficulties taking care of himself due to back problems

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Since Ellen is the only family left and the family carries the responsibility to care for the elderly in Sweden, she feels obligated to return to Nättraby to look after her father

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: I strongly oppose the idea that Ellen or any other people would have to choose between their present family on one hand and the parents on the other, when there are state institutions with adequately educated staff to do so

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: If the state leaves it entirely up to families to look after their elders, what happens to the elders who don't have a family? Because of expenses of having children many women prefer to get a good education leading to a well-paying job first

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: This may mean they do not have children at all since there is not enough time to find a life partner and have babies before the menopause

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Many women and men are also unable to have children due to physical or even mental reasons

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Thus, there would have to be some sort of institution anyway to take care of the childless elders

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
text: Institutions for the elderly is something we can not live without in our modern and demanding society

title: Should the family look after the elderly?
What about Systembolaget?
text: I thought it would only be fair to give my opinion on the much discussed topic of "Systembolagets" significance, both considering the issues of alcoholism and free enterprise

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Since the discussion is so hot right now, it is in everyones interest, to speak their piece

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: So in the name of democracy I will give you my piece

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Systembolaget is a great institution in Swedish culture, it has existed since somewhere around the 1950s and has always been trustworthy

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: I believe that keeping Systembolaget would be a favour to the Swedish nation, and to its inhabitants

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: First of all I'd like to point out that hardly anywhere in the world, one can purchase alcohol with such great variety of quality spirits, beer or/and wine, as one can in the Swedish Systembolaget

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: This is so, due to the fact that Systembolaget is a monopoly franchise with a lack of competition

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The market is then friendly and withholds great variety

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The prices, however, can be thought of as to excessive

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: I very much feel that it is the price one simply has to pay, if the high quality is to be maintained

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: I understand people who don't agree with me, people who argues that alcohol should be sold in ordinary stores

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: It is a fine democratic thought

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: It wouldn't be better though

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Think about it

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Wouldn't liquor become a commodity like any other? I believe it would

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: And by becoming that impoverish the market so almost only standard brands would be available

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The lesser quality alcohol would be more common and even cheaper, and the alcohol of higher quality would be noticeably more rare, and naturally a lot more expensive

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: That's not the way I like it

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Look at Denmark there they have very few brands in their supermarkets

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Cheap? Yes!, good? Not particularly, not at all in the same league as the Swedish Systembolagets

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: If we move forward to the question of whether alcoholism would increase if we turned to an open market or if, in fact, there would be less problems caused by alcohol in a more open environment

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: I don't think things would change at all

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Why would they? The core of the problem of alcoholism lies in the fact that alcohol exists

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Also that it's been drunk for thousands of years

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: It has been in our culture as long as we can date it back

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The only thing that could decrease the consumption of alcohol is if it was made illegal

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: But then again that would never happen, since such a large amount of tax-money comes from Swedens strict alcohol policy

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The whole Swedish society would anyway protest such a motion and it would never win any political ground

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: If anything should be done about Systembolaget it is having it open on Saturdays

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: That is tried in chosen towns, but I think they should just keep it open in all towns

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Otherwise Systembolaget is open Monday to Friday

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: People who don't like Systembolaget often uses that argument to make a point

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The argument being, of course, you can not go there everyday, which restricts your individual rights, or something like that

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: I find that being a big heap of horse-manure, if you excuse the metaphor

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Anyone with at least intelligence below average can plan the week so that time is set aside for the purchase of alcoholic beverages

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: 559 As a final subject there is the moonshine

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Do people drink moonshine, well of course they do

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Is that related to Systembolaget by any chance? In some aspects it is

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: However, I think that is the same as with the issue of alcoholism, it wouldn't really matter either way

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Hearing me say that it wouldn't matter either way, may cause you to think "then set it free, you have no argument"

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Look then to my first argument and you will see that it is the only vital argument in this entire affair

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: The restaurants, night-clubs, pubs and such will still be there

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: When you conclude this it's fairly easy to see where the common sense is

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: In this case of course, in the keeping of Systembolaget

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Seeing it as an undemocratic institution can never be done with seriousness

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: With some planing you will have time to buy what you need during ordinary open-hours

title: What about Systembolaget?
text: Finally, the variety of flavours and brands will be uncompromised by the greed of, as the Americans say, the almighty dollar

title: What about Systembolaget?
THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: In the last couple of years you might have heard a lot about school gymnastics, whether we should or should not have it as a compulsory subject on the schedule in the Swedish comprehensive schools

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: When the schools unfortunately have been forced to do severe cut-downs amongst teachers and classes (mostly by economic reasons), it seems like gymnastics has been one of the most frequent classes to be cancelled

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Most people have argued for the importance of maintaining this subject in school, but still there has been a few people claiming that compulsory gym-classes are unnecessary and a waste of pupils time

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Today's living is far different from the way earlier generations have lived their life

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Physic work were part of the ordinary day for them through labour and other daily occupations

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Today it is not so, we live a far more lazy and convenient life and the food we eat contains about three times more fat than our ancestor's food did

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: As a result of this people have grown fatter and fatter through the years

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Studies have shown that children in the ages of seven to twelve who do not have gymnastics at their timetable in school suffer a greater risk of being overweight when the are grownups, than children who do have gymnastics

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: It is also important to remember that it is in the early years of living you lay the grounds for a good condition

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: And often also the basis for a great interest! Because it is supposed to be fun too! Many children find their future hobby during gym-class

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: In fact, a study amongst children in the ages of thirteen to eighteen a couple of years ago revealed that sports (school's gym-class included) made the pupils feel healthier both physical and psychological

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: The test revealed that the students that were active in some kind of sport, believed that without their hobby they would probably be more interested in 'bad things', such as smoking and drinking

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: The test also revealed that girls of this age rather would date someone who was active in some kind of sport than someone doing such 'bad things'

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: As I said, schools have been forced to do rather big cut-downs, and people supporting cut-downs made in the gymnastics lessons in the schedule often claim that this is the least important part of the school system

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Most of them feel that this physical part of the education is rather important, but also that it would be an even greater loss if we removed, for example, mathematics or biology from the schedule

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Another argument is that in today's society you can find a local Gym almost anywhere, that parents should be responsible for their children visiting such places on a regular basis

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: (Like a school subject, but without any supervising teachers and no grades

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: ) I think most parents will strike out this suggestion as soon as they have found out about the prices most these places have got

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: It is a well known fact that a whole week of sitting still and studying can make anyone crazy

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: By Wednesday no one will have the strength of focusing and concentrating

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: The pupils performances in the ordinary school subjects will get worse and worse and of course the average grade will drop to a lower level

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: If there should be a government decision to solve this problem, that only some schools should keep gym-classes in their education, pupils in the schools without it, would suffer from it

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Their final grades would probably be much lower than pupils from the schools with maintained gymnastics and they would have a much harder time competing into higher education

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: I think we have got evidence enough that a removal of gymnastics classes from the compulsory schedule would be a great mistake, and that pupils would get affected by it in the long run

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Therefore it is necessary to find alternative ways to solve this money problem

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Maybe the compulsory schools should not be totally free anymore and there should be a small fee for every student

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS
text: Or maybe it is up to the government to reconsider how they distribute the tax payers money

title: THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL GYMNASTICS

SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Why do we still have a monarchy in Sweden? We claim that our country is democratic, that our country in fact is one of the most democratic countries in the world, but still we keep this antiquated institution - a royal family, set apart from the rest of the people, which creates an aberration in the Swedish democratic system

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Monarchy should therefore no longer have a place in the Swedish society

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: True, the royal family has a strong support in our country

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Many are those who rise to defend monarchy and the usefulness of having the royal family to represent our country, both as PR-ambassadors to other countries, as well as in internal ceremonies

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: They immediately point to the facts that monarchy has a long tradition in this country, and that the king and queen also fill a function as symbols for Sweden, which is very good for public relations with the outside world

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: They also mention that we are also one of the few remaining monarchies in the world, and thus very exotic for foreigners that visit us

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: The regent should furthermore not be seen as a threat to democracy, as he/she does not wield any true power, but merely fills a representative role

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: It is true that the regent does not have any executive power any more, but still, the monarchic concept is not democratic, and therefore, Sweden is not a fully democratic nation

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: The opportunity to become regent and head of state is limited to a very few, and it is not likely that anyone outside the family of Bernadotte will ever fill the position as regent of Sweden

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: A more democratic Sweden would open the possibility to become head of state to a far greater number of its citizen than today's monarchy does

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Monarchy is also undemocratic for the royal family

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: The heir to the throne has hardly any choice than to accept to reign, and has hardly any opportunity to live a normal life, without any attention from the media to everything that he or she does

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: And is the royal family of Bernadotte truly traditional? They are not even of noble blood in the traditional sense of the word

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: They descend from a commoner whom formerly had the position of one of Napoleon's generals, and not from Gustav Vasa, the founder of the Swedish country, whom all other Swedish royalty descended from since his time

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: And Vasa was no mere warlord, but of a Swedish noble family, one of those from which the ranks of the chosen kings of Svea and Goetha realms were drawn for hundreds of years before the unification and liberation of Sweden, making his family's claim to the throne truly traditional

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: As for the aspect of the royal family's effect on public relations, and other's opinion of Sweden, what would be the result if something really scandalous happened within the royal family? What if the king was found to be a pederast, or if the princess Madeleine happened to stab somebody to death at a rave party? The name of the royal family would then be tainted, and I can well imagine what effect it would have on other countries' opinion and relations with us, if we continued to send our royal represents around the world after such events

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Sweden needs another system of government, as it should be impossible for a country to be both monarchy and democracy, as these two are fundamentally different

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: A democracy should hold for everybody the opportunity to become head of state, and not hinder its citizens from the possibility to become what they want, no matter if they are royal family or not

title: SWEDEN IS NOT YET A TRUE DEMOCRACY!
text: Does not the position as, say, president fit this description much more than that of royal regent? Would it not be an good thing to one day see the daughter of a pizza cook become head of state, instead of the descendant of a general, whose father was a lawyer

CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: Have you ever thought about, just like I have, why we in Sweden have the odd legislation that people are allowed to buy alcohol in a bar the day they are 18 years old but they have to wait until they are 20 to go to "Systembolaget"? it is very difficult for me to see the reasons why there should not be a change in the law in order to let people from the age of 18 buy alcohol at "Systembolaget"

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: To obtain alcohol is not a problem when you are 18 years old

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: Almost everyone knows someone who is 20 and, even though it is unlawful to peddle liquor, it is not seen as a big crime if the one you peddle to is over the age of 18

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: Many of my friends had parents who acted like peddlers after the day their sons and daughters were 18 years old, some of them even before that

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: The parents did this because they in this way thought they had control over how much their children drank

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: I also think they tried to tell their children that they trusted them and show them that they, in the eyes of the parents, had came a piece of the way to the life as an adult

text: Another reason for parents buying alcohol to their children is the fear of illicitly distilled liquor

text: This is another motive for me to say that the age limit for buying alcohol at "Systembolaget" should be lowered

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: It is easy to get hold of home distilled liquor and if the youths can not obtain alcohol from "Systembolaget", which most of them prefer even though it is more expensive, they turn to the dangerous illicitly distilled liquor

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: I return to what I mentioned in the beginning of the essay; that people in the ages between 18 and 20 are permitted to go to a bar and have a drink, but are not allowed to go to the state liquor shop to buy a few beers

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: I think the main reason to why we have not already got a change in the law is the fear that youths will easier be able to obtain alcohol if the age limit would be lower

text: This might be true, to get alcohol is already quite easy for many youths

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: Nevertheless, I do not think a change in the law would make any major differences in the possibilities for the young people to obtain alcohol

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: Furthermore I do not think that youths will drink more than they do today

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
text: There are not many countries with so many car accidents involving drunk drivers as there are in the United States

title: CHANGE THE AGE LIMIT FOR BUYING ALCOHOL
